D13a.en: At 18, he dropped out of St. John's University in his sophomore year to find work.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他在圣约翰大学二年级上学期辍学，另谋生路。

D18.en: At 18, he earned commission selling coal as a runner, 8.4 silver cents per ton.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他当跑街卖煤，每吨赚八钱四分银子佣金。

D15.en: At 17-18, he ranked among the top in track and field competitions.
D15.zh: 17到18岁时，他田径比赛名次数一数二。

D7.en: At 18, he walked into the principal's office and stammered, 'My mom disagrees!'
D7.zh: 18岁时，他走进校长办公室，嗫嚅地说：“我妈不同意！”

M3.en: He admired Fan Wenzheng and took benefiting people's livelihood as his duty.
M3.zh: 他仰慕范仲淹，以裨益民生为己任。

M7.en: He considered benefiting people's livelihood as his own responsibility.
M7.zh: 他以裨益民生为己任。

M4.en: He devoted his life to industrial construction due to concern for the nation's decline.
M4.zh: 因忧国势衰微，他一生致力于工业建设。

D13a.en: He studied for imperial exams as a child and only passed the first level near age 30.
D13a.zh: 他幼年学习科举，近三十岁才考中秀才。

D18.en: As a small child, he liked to buy cooked eggs from street vendors with a few coins from his mother.
D18.zh: 他幼时喜欢向母亲要一两文钱去买龙潭熟蛋吃。

D2.en: At 15, he studied hard under his elder brother's strict discipline and greatly improved his writing.
D2.zh: 他15岁时在长兄严厉督责下日夜用功，文思大进。

D3.en: At 16, he heard lectures on geomancy and began to study it, which later helped him avoid being misled by vulgar practitioners.
D3.zh: 他16岁时听人讲堪舆学，开始窥其门径，后来游览山川察看风水，不被庸师所误。

D12.en: At 9, he lived in a school in Tianjin and did all the cleaning himself.
D12.zh: 他9岁时在天津读书，自己打扫书房。

D15.en: At 16, he walked over 100 li to Guichi for the county exam and passed.
D15.zh: 他16岁时徒步百里到贵池府城参加童子试并考中。

M2.en: He attributed his willingness to live frugally and work hard for national rejuvenation to Confucian education.
M2.zh: 他甘于清贫、不避艰辛、以民族复兴为职志，实为儒家教育所致。

D7.en: At 17, he joined the Tongmenghui with revolutionary enthusiasm.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他满腔革命热情地加入了同盟会。

D2.en: In noisy, poor conditions, he finished all math courses in half a year and studied English hard to read foreign books.
D2.zh: 在环境嘈杂、住宿简陋的情况下，他半年内学完数学课程，并刻苦攻读英语。

D12.en: As a small child, he helped his parents with housework and took care of his younger brother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他就帮父母分担家务，砍柴担水，照管弟弟。

D11.en: At 15-16, he wrote solution books for algebra, trigonometry, and analytic geometry under the name Lu Si, filing for publication.
D11.zh: 15到16岁时，他编写了代数、三角、解析几何习题解答书，以卢思名义立案准备出版。

D9.en: He held local and central administrative posts for over ten years starting at age 18.
D9.zh: 他从18岁起担任地方和中央行政职务累计十余年。

M1.en: At 18, he entered the University of Hong Kong with excellent grades, majoring in science.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他以优异成绩考入香港大学，专修科学。

D7.en: At 13, he tearfully vowed to his mother to become successful and wealthy within 10 years to show up his fair-weather relatives.
D7.zh: 13岁时他抹着眼泪向母亲发誓，10年内要出人头地赚大钱，给翻脸不认人的亲戚看。

D2.en: At 13-14, he changed his habits, studied hard, and within half a year ranked first in Class D, winning a scholarship.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他一改贪玩习惯，发奋用功，不出半年在D班考第一，获得奖学金。

D12.en: As a small child, his father built a seaside villa named after his English name Stanley Lodge.
D12.zh: 幼年时，父亲为他建造了一幢以他的英文名Stanley Lodge命名的度假别墅。

D13b.en: He spent his childhood in Zhuangshi Town and attended Ye's Zhongxing School.
D13b.zh: 他在庄市镇度过童年，就读于叶氏中兴学校。

D4.en: He quickly mastered basic filming techniques and soon handled advanced camera moves.
D4.zh: 他很快掌握了基本拍摄要领，不久就能掌握高难拍摄动作。

D9.en: He helped distribute films and learned filmmaking at Tianyi Film Company while still a student.
D9.zh: 他在天一影片公司协助影片发行并学习拍摄电影，当时还是中学生。

D7.en: He told his elder brother he wanted to change his name because his original name was ugly.
D7.zh: 他对大哥说想改名，因为原来的名字难听死了。

D2.en: He started as a regular employee at Tianyi, doing logistics and odd jobs.
D2.zh: 他初进天一被当成普通员工，干后勤和打杂。

D11.en: At 13, he announced his new style name: Renleng, styled Yifu.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他宣布自己的号：仁楞，号逸夫。

M8.en: As a child, he loved reading books.
M8.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

M7.en: He said that as future masters, they should serve the country with passion and knowledge.
M7.zh: 他说，作为未来的主人翁，当以热血报国，以知识救国。

D14.en: At 10-12, as a flag bearer, he marched holding the school flag at a 45-degree angle.
D14.zh: 10到12岁时，他作为旗手昂首挺胸，擎着校旗以45度角行进。

D13b.en: At 13, he became an intern in the insurance department of a British firm.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他进英商安利洋行保险部做实习生。

D5.en: At 13, he left home alone and someone promised to put him on a boat to Hankou.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他独自出门，有人拍胸脯保证送他上到汉口的船。

M1.en: At 13, he said, 'I'll make a career and come back to see you!'
M1.zh: 13岁时，他说：‘等我干出一番事业来，就回来见您！’

D9.en: At about age 12, he was chosen by his teacher to be the flag bearer for the school's military band at a sports meet.
D9.zh: 大约12岁时，老师选中他担任学校运动会军乐队的旗手。

D7.en: At 13-14, he told his mother, 'I want to go to Hankou to study with Dad.'
D7.zh: 13到14岁时，他对母亲说：‘娘亲，我想到汉口念书，和爹在一起。’

D2.en: At 13-14, he felt school in Ningbo couldn't satisfy his curiosity, as it taught mostly classics far from modern life.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他觉得在宁波读书不能满足好奇心，学校教的四书五经离生活太远。

D3.en: At 12-15, he often sat by the window watching ships and wondered about the outside world.
D3.zh: 12到15岁时，他常坐在窗前望着商船和江水，想知道外面的世界。

D12.en: At 15, he told his mother, 'I'm as tall as you, I'm an adult. I can wash and cook.'
D12.zh: 15岁时，他对母亲说：‘我跟你一般高了，我是大人了，会洗衣服做饭。’

D8.en: He immediately stepped forward to clarify the situation and corrected the 'wrong case'.
D8.zh: 他当即站出来说明情况，纠正了那个‘错案’。

M4.en: At about 9, he vowed to build Taiwan's largest hospital to treat all, especially the poor.
M4.zh: 大约9岁时，他立志要建一所台湾最大的医院，救治所有人，特别是穷苦病人。

D13b.en: At 7, he was sent by his parents to Xindian National Elementary School.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他被父母送到新店国民小学就读。

D18.en: At 15, with 200 yuan borrowed from his father, he opened a rice shop in Chiayi, starting his business.
D18.zh: 15岁时，靠父亲借来的200元，他在嘉义开了一家米店，迈出事业第一步。

D5.en: At 15, he resolved to leave home and make his way in the world.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他立志要出外闯天下。

D15.en: As a small child, he sometimes walked 20 li of mountain road to see a movie.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他有时要走20里山路去看一场电影。

D4.en: At 16, he and his brothers picked out杂物 from the rice before selling it.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他和兄弟在闲暇时把米里的杂物拣出来再卖。

D9.en: At 16, after the Spring Festival, he took his two younger brothers to Chiayi to run the rice shop.
D9.zh: 16岁那年春节刚过，他带着两个弟弟南下嘉义，为米店奔波。

D6.en: At 15-16, while working at the rice shop, he built good relations with regular customers.
D6.zh: 15到16岁在米店打工时，他和顾客搞好关系，渐渐熟识。

D3.en: At 15, he observed his boss's bookkeeping and cost accounting, a habit that later benefited his own business.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他边收拾边观察老板如何记账和核算成本，这个习惯让他后来开店时受益匪浅。

D12.en: As a small child, he followed his mother to pick up fallen wood and coal from passing carts to sell or use for fuel.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他常跟着母亲到台车道旁，捡拾掉落的木材或煤块，好的拿去卖钱，差的带回家生火。

D1.en: At 15, he passed the county exam and became a government student.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他考取附学生员。

D11.en: At 12, he began learning examination poetry and prose, and sometimes went to Xiting Poetry Society to compose poems.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他开始学习试帖和制艺，有时被带到西亭诗社分题作诗。

D4.en: At 11, he followed his father's order to weed cotton fields with hired workers.
D4.zh: 11岁时，他奉父命随雇工锄棉田草。

D7.en: At 15, he used a false identity as Zhang Yucai from Rugao and passed exams to become a government student.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他冒籍如皋张氏，改名张育才，应考取附学生员。

D2.en: At 15, he passed the county exam and ranked 26th among the new government students.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他经院试取中第26名附学生员。

D12.en: As a small child, he received traditional education aimed at becoming an official through imperial exams.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他接受以科举致仕为宗旨的旧式教育。

D13a.en: At 18, he reported himself and requested to be stripped of his scholar status and return home.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他自行检举，请求褫衣顶归原籍。

D8.en: At 18, he was promoted to manager.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他被提拔为经理。

M3.en: At age 5-10, he greatly admired loyal and patriotic people.
M3.zh: 5到10岁时，他对精忠报国的人敬佩不已。

M6.en: At age 5-10, his ideal was to become an educator.
M6.zh: 5到10岁时，他的理想是当教育家。

D17.en: At age 13-14, he cared for his father in hospital, visiting daily and helping with meals before school.
D17.zh: 13到14岁时，他在医院尽心伺候父亲，每天服侍早饭后再上学，放学后汇报学业。

M2.en: He always remembered his father's stories about prodigies who lost their talent through laziness.
M2.zh: 他始终记得父亲讲的关于神童因不勤勉而才尽的故事。

D20.en: At 18, he became department manager; two years later, general manager.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他成为部门经理；两年后，升为总经理。

M4.en: At 11, he felt a surge of patriotic passion.
M4.zh: 11岁时，他心中涌起拳拳爱国热情。

D10.en: At 12, he often sat on his bed hugging his knees, staring at the deep night sky outside the window.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他常常抱着腿坐在床上，凝望着窗外深沉的夜空。

D1.en: At 14, after losing his father, he endured hunger and cold, went door to door for work, faced rejections, but never gave up.
D1.zh: 14岁丧父后，他食不果腹，寒冷难忍，挨家挨户找工作，吃闭门羹，但始终不肯放弃。

D14.en: Between 14 and 18, he worked hard, studied diligently, and remained optimistic about life.
D14.zh: 14到18岁间，他勤奋工作，努力学习，对生活充满乐观。

D13a.en: At 14, he declined his uncle's offer to support his schooling, choosing to work to support his family.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他谢绝了舅舅资助他读书的好意，决定辍学谋生养家。

D18.en: At 13, he came up with the idea of buying used textbooks from secondhand bookstores.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他想出购买旧教材的办法，从旧书店买来用。

D5.en: At 18, he left a hardware factory to join a small plastic belt factory in a remote area.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他离开五金厂，去了一家偏离闹市区的塑胶裤带制造公司。

D11.en: Between 5 and 10, he often hid in a small study after school, reading books with great enthusiasm.
D11.zh: 5到10岁时，他放学后常躲在小书房里如醉如痴地读书。

M1.en: His sales were seven times those of the second-place salesman.
M1.zh: 他的销售额是第二名的7倍。

D15.en: Between 14 and 15, he woke up before 5 a.m., carried heavy teapots, and worked 16 hours a day.
D15.zh: 14到15岁时，他每天不到5点起床，拎着沉重茶壶，一天工作16小时。

D4.en: At 17, he carried a heavy bag and traveled door-to-door on foot to sell his goods, walking over ten li to save money.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他背着大包挨家挨户推销，为了省钱，十来里路都靠走。

D9.en: At 18, he became a department manager, and two years later, general manager of the plastics company.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他成为部门经理，两年后升任塑胶公司总经理。

D6.en: At 12-13, his cousin Zhuang Yueming taught him Cantonese, and he soon could communicate with Hong Kong locals.
D6.zh: 12到13岁时，表妹庄月明教他广州话，他很快就能和香港人交流。

D2.en: Even after working 14 hours a day, he studied secondary school courses at night and set three alarms to ensure punctuality.
D2.zh: 甚至在工作14小时后，他仍坚持夜间自修中学课程，并准备了3个闹钟保证上班不迟到。

D3.en: At 13, he bought books on lung disease treatment and care, and was shocked to find he had all the symptoms.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他买了肺病治疗和护理的书，看完吓一跳，因为所有症状自己都有。

D12.en: At 14, as the eldest son, he left school and shouldered the family's burden.
D12.zh: 14岁时，作为长子，他离开学校，挑起了全家重担。

D7.en: At 14, he stopped grave diggers and said, 'Don't dig; I heard everything. Consider the money my charity.'
D7.zh: 14岁时，他阻止挖坟的人说：“不要挖了，我全听到了，钱当我施舍。”

D1.en: At 10, he helped his father sell leftover goods to restart their business after a robbery.
D1.zh: 10岁时，他帮父亲变卖剩余物资，重新开业。

D18.en: At 12, he washed old banknotes with bleach, coated them with egg white, ironed, and pressed them to look new.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他把旧纸币泡在漂白粉水里洗刷，涂上蛋白，熨平压好。

D5.en: At 10, he quietly escaped from a crowd of robbers, running for his life.
D5.zh: 10岁时，他悄悄从贼匪中逃出去，拼命奔跑。

D4.en: At 12, he learned to assay, refine, and melt gold, becoming head trader at Tianbaorong Gold Shop.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他学会看金、化金、熔金，出任天宝荣金铺头柜。

D9.en: At 15, his father entrusted him to manage both the bank and gold shop in Shunde.
D9.zh: 15岁时，父亲把顺德永生银号和天宝荣金铺交给他掌管。

D3.en: At 8-12, he was taught a Confucian passage and quickly applied it to real-life examples.
D3.zh: 8到12岁时，他学完一段古文后，很快思考出生活实例。

D12.en: At 6, he learned business after school at his father's gold and silver shop.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他课余到父亲金铺银号学习从商。

D13b.en: At 18, he consummated his marriage with the boss's daughter Zhou Cuiying, becoming the son-in-law.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他与老板的女儿周翠英圆房，正式成为东家女婿。

D13a.en: At 18, he married Zhou Cuiying in a wedding ceremony.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他与周翠英洞房花烛，结为连理。

D4.en: At 15-16, he had a brainstorm and approached someone, saying Chow Tai Fook could exchange at the fairest rate.
D4.zh: 15到16岁时，他灵机一动，上前搭讪，说周大福金铺可兑换，价格最公道。

D9.en: At 17-18, before completing 3 years, he was promoted to manager of the gold shop, handling daily operations.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他未满3年就荣升为金铺掌管，负责日常经营。

D6.en: At 15-18, he was diligent but bold, going out to stroll and inspect shops without the boss's permission.
D6.zh: 15到18岁时，他勤勤恳恳却胆大包天，未经老板同意就去逛街看铺。

D2.en: At 14-15, he started as a handyman, arriving early to sweep, dust, empty spittoons, and clean toilets.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，他从杂役干起，每天早早赶到金铺，扫地、抹灰、倒痰盂、洗厕所。

D3.en: At 16-17, he inspected shops every day before work and gained something each time.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，他每天上班前都要看铺，每天都有收获。

D12.en: Before turning 15, he began his apprenticeship.
D12.zh: 不满15岁时，他就开始了学徒生涯。

M7.en: At age 12-14, he considered himself a future master of China in a great era.
M7.zh: 12到14岁时，他自诩为大时代的中国未来主人。

M2.en: At 16-17, his father convinced him that writing would not make a living, so he gave up his dream of becoming a writer.
M2.zh: 16到17岁时，父亲告诉他写作会饿肚子，他打消了当作家的念头。

D20.en: At 13-14, he co-founded a wall newspaper called 'Jian Bao' and wrote for the literary section.
D20.zh: 13到14岁时，他和同学合办壁报《健报》，负责文艺栏并每周写一篇稿。

D13a.en: At 17, his father's comment that writing would make him hungry ended his dream of being a writer.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，父亲一句“会饿肚子”打消了他的作家梦。

D5.en: At 11-12, he traveled from Xuzhou to Luoyang using various transport and walking across the front line.
D5.zh: 11到12岁时，他从徐州到洛阳，用各种交通工具并步行穿越前线。

D11.en: For six or seven years as a child and teen, he foolishly dreamed of writing as a lifelong career.
D11.zh: 有六七年的幼少年光阴，他痴心妄想以写作为终身工作。

M1.en: At 12, he did well in a summer class and was admitted to the second year of junior high.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他读南开暑期班成绩很好，被保送为初二正式学生。

D15.en: At 12, he became physically stronger after entering middle school.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他进了中学后体质渐渐壮实起来。

D9.en: At 13-14, he co-founded a school wall newspaper and wrote a weekly column for it.
D9.zh: 13到14岁时，他和同学合办壁报《健报》，每周负责文艺栏并自己写一篇稿。

D7.en: At 12-14, he sneaked out after lights-out for snacks, got caught, and was publicly disciplined.
D7.zh: 12到14岁时，他熄灯后偷溜出校园吃宵夜被训导主任抓住，记小过并公布，之后不敢再犯。

D3.en: As a small child, he read the entire Children's Library series before finishing elementary school.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他在小学毕业前读完了整套《儿童文库》。

D12.en: Before age 18, he fled 3 times, lived in 6 cities, and attended 10 schools.
D12.zh: 18岁前，他逃难3次，住过6个城市，换了10个学校。

D19.en: At 17, he handled all bookkeeping and settlement.
D19.zh: 17岁时，他负责记账和结账。

M1.en: At 15, he thought students should learn skills to become teachers.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他认为学生要学本事，将来可做先生。

D4.en: At 13, he enjoyed doing handicrafts with knives and drills.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他喜欢做手工，刀钻齐全。

D2.en: At 16, he studied diligently, improved in bookkeeping and silver inspection, and wrote the shop sign and spring couplets.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他学业认真，记账看银有进步，并写了店招牌和春联。

D3.en: At 16, he was taught to look at distant green or roof tiles to improve eyesight, and his nearsightedness was cured in half a year.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他每天早晨望远处青绿或屋瓦，半年后近视好了。

D18.en: At 6, he learned to make paper money, and neighbor children came to learn from him.
D18.zh: 6岁时，他学扎黄钱，邻居小孩来跟他学。

D1.en: He worked days, studied nights, and spent Sundays at Zhejiang University library.
D1.zh: 他白天工作，晚上学习，每个周日都去浙江大学图书馆。

D18.en: At 13, he used his pedicab to serve as an English-speaking guide for foreigners.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他蹬三轮车为外国人当英语导游。

D11.en: He immersed himself in martial arts novels as a child.
D11.zh: 他小时候沉浸在武侠小说的浪漫世界中。

D9.en: He created a cohesive bond among a select group of peers from an early age.
D9.zh: 他从小就能在一群同龄人中建立紧密关系。

D7.en: He got angry but became more determined to pass math after a setback.
D7.zh: 他感到愤怒，但更坚定了要通过数学考试。

D6.en: He was seen as a stubborn, naughty, incorrigible rebel who needed discipline.
D6.zh: 他被视为固执、调皮、不可救药的叛逆者，需要反复管教。

D2.en: At 16, he took study courses in the summer of 1980, bicycling between home and school.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他1980年夏天参加学习课程，骑车往返于家和学校之间。

D3.en: In 1979, he practiced English with foreign tourists on the streets of Hangzhou.
D3.zh: 1979年，他在杭州街上找外国游客练英语。

D12.en: At 18, he pedaled a heavy tricycle every day to earn one yuan, rain or shine.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他不管刮风下雨还是烈日炎炎，都用力蹬着那辆笨重的三轮车，每天赚一元钱。

D5.en: He was badly wounded several times, once needing stitches without anesthesia.
D5.zh: 他多次受重伤，有一次没有麻醉就缝了针。

D1.en: At 17, he challenged rural soldiers to a wheat-cutting contest, bleeding but refusing to give up.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他和农村兵摆擂台割麦，双手磨出血也不肯服输。

D13b.en: At 17, he joined the army and became a truck driver in the air force logistics system.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他入伍，在空军后勤系统当汽车兵。

M1.en: At 17, he challenged rural soldiers to a grass-cutting contest, refusing to accept being slower.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他不服气农村兵割得快，和他们摆擂台比赛。

D12.en: At 17, he joined the army as a truck driver in the air force logistics.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他入伍在空军后勤当汽车兵。

D15.en: At age 5, he guarded the flag for his team and planted it first on the hilltop.
D15.zh: 5岁时，他负责看管旗帜，并第一个把旗插到山头上。

D6.en: At 7-9, he stole melons with village kids, hid from the guard, and returned with a shirtful, becoming the leader.
D6.zh: 7到9岁时，他和山里孩子偷瓜，躲过看瓜人，背回一口袋香瓜，成了孩子王。

D11.en: As a teenager, he gathered kids to play soccer and tell stories on weekends.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他周末常带一群孩子踢球、摆龙门阵。

D16.en: At 15, he loved being the center of attention, persuading audiences that his side was right.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他喜欢成为焦点，说服听众自己是对的。

D13b.en: At 15-16, he dropped out of high school after his sophomore year.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁时，他高中二年级后辍学了。

D5.en: At 15-16, he was eager to become an ambulance driver for the Red Cross in WWI.
D5.zh: 15到16岁时，他渴望在第一次世界大战中为红十字会开救护车。

D11.en: As a child, he imagined situations and how he would handle them.
D11.zh: 小时候，他想象各种情况以及自己会怎么应对。

D15.en: As a child aged 7-12, he played grand neighborhood games in the alley behind his house.
D15.zh: 7到12岁时，他在屋后小巷里玩盛大的邻里游戏。

D7.en: At 17, he talked his parents into letting him join as a Red Cross ambulance driver.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他说服父母让他加入红十字会当救护车司机。

D6.en: At 18, he and Ethel were almost an item in the group from the start.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他和Ethel几乎从一开始就是团体里的一对。

D12.en: He prided himself on being able to sweep, clean, and make beds as well as anyone.
D12.zh: 他为自己能像任何人一样扫地、打扫和铺床而感到自豪。

D18.en: At 14, he opened the Ray Kroc Music Emporium in 1916.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他于1916年开设了Ray Kroc音乐商店。

D9.en: At 14, he worked in a tiny music store he started with two friends.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他在和两个朋友开的小唱片店工作。

D11.en: He read Puyi's autobiography and let his imagination run wild.
D11.zh: 他看了溥仪写的《我的前半生》就浮想联翩

D7.en: At 17, he was declared unqualified because his uncle was a 'rightist'.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他因舅舅是右派分子而被宣布为不合格

D2.en: At 17-18, he resolved to 'train his worker-peasant feelings' by carrying a manure bucket door to door during winter break.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他决心苦练工农感情，寒假里背着粪桶挨家挨户淘粪

D3.en: As a teenager, he was more interested in national affairs than in books.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他对国家大事的兴趣比书本更大

D1.en: At 16-17, he hid a broken arm from his leaders, compensating with strength and flexibility.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他隐瞒了胳膊骨折，靠力量和灵活性掩盖缺陷。

D13b.en: At 12-14, he did not apply for the Communist Youth League, focusing on studies instead.
D13b.zh: 12到14岁时，他没有申请入团，而是专心学习。

D4.en: In his 20s, he learned and taught typesetting, proofreading, printing, binding, and stencil cutting.
D4.zh: 20多岁时，他从头学习并教授排版、校对、印刷、装订和刻蜡版。

D9.en: He applied for professional title evaluation through the institute to help young employees.
D9.zh: 他通过院里申报了职称评定，帮助年轻员工考取级别。

D7.en: He defied his father by abandoning the old system to make his own way.
D7.zh: 他为了和父亲赌气，抛弃旧体制，要靠自己闯出一番天地。

D6.en: He became close friends with several girls, who treated him like a parent and confided secrets to him.
D6.zh: 他和几个女孩子成了好朋友，她们把他当家长，常对他说悄悄话。

D3.en: As a teenager, he read and discussed books like Fan Wenlan's history and Stalin's philosophy.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他和别人一起读书讨论，读了范文澜的《中国通史简编》等。

D18.en: At 16-18, he learned to roll cigarettes from locals in Yan'an, who said it was to relieve fatigue.
D18.zh: 16到18岁时，他在延安跟老乡学会了卷烟抽，老乡说这是为了解乏。

M5.en: At 9, he felt deep embarrassment from an incident he did not soon forget.
M5.zh: 9岁时，他因一件事感到非常难堪，久久不能忘怀。

M7.en: At 9, he devised a circus parade and enlisted Ruth and neighborhood children to help.
M7.zh: 9岁时，他设计了一场马戏游行，并邀请Ruth和邻居孩子们帮忙装饰花车。

D20.en: At 18, he established Iwerks-Disney Commercial Artists in January 1920.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他于1920年1月创立了Iwerks-Disney Commercial Artists。

M4.en: As a small child, he duplicated political cartoons from Appeal to Reason.
M4.zh: 幼年时，他临摹了《诉诸理性》上的政治漫画。

D10.en: At 8-14, he always awoke in panic, fearing he would be late for school.
D10.zh: 8到14岁时，他总是在恐慌中醒来，担心上学迟到。

D14.en: He induced other students to wear funny costumes and repeat jokes he copied from vaudeville comics.
D14.zh: 他让其他学生穿上滑稽服装，重复他从杂耍喜剧里抄来的笑话。

D13a.en: His mother forged his name on a passport application, and he altered his birthdate to read 1900.
D13a.zh: 他母亲在护照申请上伪造了他的签名，而他把自己的出生年份改成了1900年。

D11.en: He was interested in cartooning, but his drawings were uninspired.
D11.zh: 他对漫画感兴趣，但他的画缺乏灵感。

M1.en: He attended evening classes three times a week at the Chicago Academy of Fine Arts.
M1.zh: 他每周三次去芝加哥美术学院上夜校。

D15.en: He competed on the school's seventy-pound relay team in 1917.
D15.zh: 1917年，他参加了学校的70磅接力赛。

D9.en: He impulsively suggested they go into business together.
D9.zh: 他一时冲动，提议他们一起创业。

D6.en: He spent more time at a friend's house than at his own.
D6.zh: 他在朋友家待的时间比在自己家还多。

D2.en: He never left his drawing board, not even taking a break until lunch.
D2.zh: 他从不离开画板，甚至不休息直到午饭。

D3.en: He began an intensive self-education in animation, using the Slide Co. as his school.
D3.zh: 他开始了对动画的密集自学，把Slide公司当作学校。

D18.en: He aged helmets with quick-drying shellac, earning five francs each.
D18.zh: 他用快干虫胶给头盔做旧，每个赚五法郎。

D8.en: In high school, he challenged his electronics teacher by catching him in errors.
D8.zh: 高中时，他当面指出电子学老师的错误并挑战他。

D14.en: He was born on February 24, 1955, in San Francisco, California.
D14.zh: 他于1955年2月24日出生在加利福尼亚州旧金山。

D13b.en: Within weeks of his birth, his mother signed over custody to Paul and Clara Jobs.
D13b.zh: 他出生几周内，母亲将他交给Paul和Clara Jobs抚养。

D13a.en: At 17, he dropped out of school, got a tuition refund, but remained on campus.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他退学并拿回学费，但仍留在校园。

D18.en: At 17-18, he decided to sell his IBM Selectric typewriter to raise cash.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他决定卖掉IBM Selectric打字机来换钱。

D5.en: At 17, he began lifelong experiments with compulsive diets, eating only fruits and vegetables.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他开始终身尝试强迫性饮食，只吃水果和蔬菜。

D11.en: He created light shows for parties using lasers and mirrors attached to speakers.
D11.zh: 他和朋友用激光和音箱上的镜子为派对制作灯光秀。

M1.en: At age 9-10, he scored at the high school sophomore level.
M1.zh: 9到10岁时，他考到了高中二年级的水平。

D7.en: At 17, he refused to go to his assigned classes and went to the ones he wanted.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他拒绝上分配的课，去上自己想上的课。

D2.en: As a teenager, he could spend endless days designing circuit boards for various gadgets.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他能花无数天为各种小玩意设计电路板。

D3.en: At 17-18, he started with two-day fasts and tried to stretch them to a week or more.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他开始两天禁食，并试图延长到一周或更久。

D12.en: At 17-18, he had his first work experience.
D12.zh: 17到18岁时，他有了第一次工作经历。

D15.en: At 14, he switched from swimming team to water polo because practice took too much time.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他因游泳队训练太耗时，转而参加水球。

M5.en: At 16, he and Allen ran a meaningless endless loop to spite the computer center.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他和Allen运行了一个无意义的死循环来报复计算机中心。

D8.en: At 16, he arranged English class sections so all the smartest kids were in his session.
D8.zh: 16岁时，他把所有最聪明的孩子都安排到他所在的英语班。

D19.en: At 16-17, he shepherded the scheduling program for two more years.
D19.zh: 16到17岁时，他又花了两年时间维护排课程序。

D20.en: At 13-14, he and Paul Allen made most of the entries.
D20.zh: 13到14岁时，他和Paul Allen完成了大部分条目。

M4.en: At 17, he jumped at TRW's offer for paid job with PDP-10 access.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他抓住TRW的机会，得到有薪工作并可以随意使用PDP-10。

D10.en: At 16-17, he broke down in tears.
D10.zh: 16到17岁时，他崩溃大哭。

D1.en: At 17, after Evans died, a shaken Gates asked Allen to help with the scheduling project.
D1.zh: 17岁时，Evans去世后，受震动的他请Allen帮忙做排课项目。

D13a.en: At 18, he obeyed his parents and postponed plans to start a software company with Paul Allen.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他听从父母，推迟了与Paul Allen创办软件公司的计划。

D5.en: At 13-14, he accessed accounting files where passwords were stored as a challenge.
D5.zh: 13到14岁时，他把访问存有密码的会计文件当作挑战。

D11.en: At 7-8, he read the entire encyclopedia from beginning to end.
D11.zh: 7到8岁时，他把百科全书从头到尾读了一遍。

M1.en: At 9-10, he said, 'I want to get back to division because last year I was the best.'
M1.zh: 9到10岁时，他说：‘我想回到除法，因为去年我是最好的。’

D15.en: At 17-18, he water-skied fearlessly, executing turns and jumps on one ski.
D15.zh: 17到18岁时，他勇敢地滑水，单脚完成转弯和跳跃。

D4.en: At 16, he and Paul Allen set out to build a computer using Paul's idea.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他和Paul Allen按照Paul的想法着手制造一台电脑。

D9.en: At 18, he and Allen seriously discussed forming their own software company.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他和Allen认真讨论创办自己的软件公司。

D7.en: At 13-14, he sneaked out of the house to take a bus to C-Cubed for more programming.
D7.zh: 13到14岁时，他偷偷溜出家，坐十分钟公交去C-Cubed继续编程。

D6.en: At 18, he played duplicate bridge, chess, and hung out arguing in dorms till late.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他打桥牌、下棋，在宿舍争论到深夜。

D12.en: At 16, he spent three weeks in spring as a legislative page in Olympia, Washington.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他春天在华盛顿州奥林匹亚市当了三个星期的立法议会侍者。

D14.en: At 17-18, he landed leading roles in two school plays.
D14.zh: 17到18岁时，他在两部校园戏剧中担任主角。

D13b.en: At 16, he served as a page from July 17 to August 18.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他从7月17日到8月18日担任议会侍者。

D17.en: At about age 7, he had his wound checked and cleaned daily for three weeks.
D17.zh: 大约7岁时，他每天被检查并清洗伤口，持续了三周。

M4.en: At 13, he heard of wonders at Philadelphia and resolved to leave the farm for new power.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他听说费城的奇迹，决心离开农场投身新动力领域。

D10.en: At 12, he was emotionally devastated by his mother's death, perhaps the most traumatic event of his life.
D10.zh: 12岁时，母亲去世让他精神崩溃，这可能是他一生中最痛苦的事。

D1.en: At 16, he worked for half an hour and cleared a trough for a heavy unit to slide.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他干了半小时，清出一条槽让重物滑下。

D13a.en: As a small child, he was sensitive to his parents' wishes and felt how much he meant to them.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他对父母的意愿很敏感，并意识到自己对他们的重要性。

D5.en: At 17, he was seriously injured when a horse ran away and collapsed the manure wagon he was driving.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他赶的粪车因马受惊逃跑而翻车，他受了重伤。

D9.en: At 13-16, he attended county fairs with his father, brothers, and sisters.
D9.zh: 13到16岁时，他和父亲、兄弟姐妹一起参加县集市。

D7.en: At 16, he left the farm to find his future in Detroit without informing anyone.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他没告诉任何人就离开农场去底特律寻找未来。

D2.en: At 7, he trooped off to the one-room Scotch Settlement School.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他去了只有一间教室的苏格兰移民学校。

D3.en: At 13, he encountered a portable steam engine, which influenced him.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他遇到了一台便携式蒸汽机，这影响了他。

D15.en: Between ages 13-16, he skated in winter with Edsel and others, learning early and becoming proficient.
D15.zh: 13到16岁间，他和Edsel等人冬天滑冰，学得早且很熟练。

D4.en: At 13, he and Henry stumbled across a self-powered steam engine on a trip to Detroit.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他和Henry在去底特律的路上偶然发现了一台自行蒸汽机。

M6.en: He started fantasizing about colonizing other planets in high school.
M6.zh: 他在高中时开始幻想殖民其他星球。

D20.en: He and Kimbal were chatting during a class break when Wood interrupted them.
D20.zh: 他和Kimbal课间聊天时被Wood打断。

D16.en: At 16, he railed against fossil fuels in favor of solar power.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他抨击化石燃料，支持太阳能。

D14.en: Its coverage in the magazine netted him five hundred dollars.
D14.zh: 杂志报道让他赚了500美元。

D13b.en: He wanted to avoid joining the military despite South Africa's requirement.
D13b.zh: 尽管南非要求服兵役，他想避免参军。

D18.en: At about age 10, he dabbled in selling Easter eggs door-to-door.
D18.zh: 大约10岁时，他尝试挨家挨户卖复活节彩蛋。

D11.en: At 13 in South Africa, he taught himself to code and wrote the video game Blastar.
D11.zh: 13岁时在南非，他自学编程并编写了游戏Blastar。

M1.en: At age 8, he would stand by his father's chair and argue his case repeatedly.
M1.zh: 8岁时，他会站在父亲椅子旁反复争辩。

D4.en: At about age 11, he created his own chemical compounds and put them in canisters.
D4.zh: 大约11岁时，他自制化合物并放入罐中。

D9.en: At about age 11, he spearheaded work with homemade explosives and rockets.
D9.zh: 大约11岁时，他带头制作炸药和火箭。

D7.en: At about 8-10, he told the Dungeon Master he was the bad guy, and they killed him.
D7.zh: 大约8到10岁时，他对地下城主说他是坏人，然后他们杀了他。

D6.en: He immersed himself in Dungeons & Dragons with his brother and cousins.
D6.zh: 他和弟弟及表亲们沉浸在龙与地下城游戏中。

D3.en: At 9, he became hooked on coin-operated video games in motel lobbies.
D3.zh: 9岁时，他迷上了汽车旅馆大厅里的投币式电子游戏。

D12.en: At 17, he did chores on a Saskatchewan farm.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他在萨斯喀彻温省的一个农场做杂务。

D8.en: He won a bike race but got angry when the others cheated and hit them.
D8.zh: 他自行车比赛领先，发现别人作弊后气得打了他们。

D5.en: At age 8, he was determined to get a motorcycle.
D5.zh: 8岁时，他决心要一辆摩托车。

M7.en: He tried to reunite his family on the Greek mainland.
M7.zh: 他尽力将家人团聚在希腊本土。

M2.en: At 18, he was determined to build a business as successful or more than his father's had been in Smyrna.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他决心建立一家比父亲在士麦那的生意更成功或至少同样成功的企业。

D1.en: His first task was to try to free his father from prison.
D1.zh: 他的首要任务是设法将父亲从监狱中解救出来。

D13a.en: At 15-17, he was prohibited by his father from car or boat rides and faced stern discipline.
D13a.zh: 15到17岁时，他被父亲禁止乘车或乘船，并受到严厉管教。

D18.en: He started his career with sixty dollars.
D18.zh: 他带着六十美元开始了自己的职业生涯。

D5.en: He borrowed money for a one-way ticket to Buenos Aires.
D5.zh: 他向家里借钱买了去布宜诺斯艾利斯的单程票。

M1.en: He was determined to make it on his own.
M1.zh: 他决心靠自己成功。

D15.en: He became school champion at rowing.
D15.zh: 他成为了学校划船冠军。

D4.en: He signed on as a deckhand.
D4.zh: 他签约当了甲板水手。

D9.en: He assumed duties with seriousness and dedication.
D9.zh: 他认真投入地承担了职责。

D6.en: He often met a favorite girl, bought her sweets, and walked her home.
D6.zh: 他常去见一个喜欢的女孩，给她买糖，送她回家。

D2.en: At 17, he took an intensive three-week course on the inner workings of the telephone company.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他参加了为期三周的密集课程，学习电话公司的内部运作。

D3.en: He worked on his Spanish, read newspapers, and listened in on business calls.
D3.zh: 他学西班牙语，看报纸，偷听商务电话。

D12.en: He accompanied his sisters to school and brought them home each day.
D12.zh: 他每天陪妹妹们上学，放学再接她们回家。

D4.en: At 10-14, he dismantled an electric bell and rigged a local telegraph system to a friend's house.
D4.zh: 10到14岁时，他拆了一个电铃，然后给邻居朋友家装了一个本地电报系统。

D19.en: At 17, he counted his money and organized his collections.
D19.zh: 17岁时，他数钱并整理他的收藏品。

M3.en: At 10, he knew he was in the presence of a big shot in Sidney Weinberg's office.
M3.zh: 10岁时，他在Sidney Weinberg的胡桃木镶板办公室里意识到自己面对的是个大人物。

M7.en: At 17-18, he wrote an article titled 'Buffett Lashes Marshall Plan'.
M7.zh: 17到18岁时，他写了一篇题为《Buffett Lashes Marshall Plan》的文章。

D16.en: At 15, he argued convincingly in favor of absurdities like eliminating income taxes.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他令人信服地为荒谬观点辩护，比如取消所得税。

D14.en: He wore a raccoon fur coat to high school football games just for fun.
D14.zh: 他穿着浣熊毛皮大衣去高中足球赛，只是为了好玩。

D13a.en: He moved back to Omaha to live with his grandfather and aunt.
D13a.zh: 他搬回奥马哈，和祖父及姑姑同住。

D5.en: He briefly ran away from home to Hershey, Pennsylvania at age 13.
D5.zh: 他13岁时短暂离家出走，去了宾夕法尼亚州赫希。

D11.en: He was obsessed with Al Jolson and played his records day and night.
D11.zh: 他痴迷于Al Jolson，日夜播放他的唱片。

M1.en: He took extra credits and graduated in 2.5 years instead of three.
M1.zh: 他多修学分，用两年半毕业，而不是通常的三年。

D15.en: He took one boxing lesson at age eight and never put on gloves again.
D15.zh: 他八岁时上了一节拳击课，再也没戴过手套。

D4.en: He formed Wilson Coin-Operated Machine Co. with a friend during high school.
D4.zh: 他和朋友在高中时成立了Wilson投币机公司。

D9.en: He convinced the route manager to give him The Westchester route.
D9.zh: 他说服路线经理把The Westchester路线给了他。

D7.en: At 10, he collected and wore dozens of Willkie–McNary buttons on his shirt.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他收集了几十枚Willkie–McNary徽章，别在衬衫上。

D6.en: At 10-11, he enjoyed spending time with adults and being vied over by his uncles.
D6.zh: 10到11岁时，他喜欢和大人待在一起，被叔叔们争相讨好。

D3.en: At 14-15, he discovered Bob Hoffman and his magazine Strength and Health in search of technique.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，为了寻找技巧，他发现了Bob Hoffman和他的杂志《Strength and Health》。

D17.en: At 7-9, he rescued Bertie's doll from a wastebasket and returned it to her.
D17.zh: 7到9岁时，他从废纸篓里救出Bertie的娃娃并还给她。

D1.en: He practiced sending a bolo ball flying off his paddle a thousand times.
D1.zh: 他练习让波洛球从木拍上飞出去一千次。

D10.en: At age 8-10, he went home crying after an incident that made him think about colors.
D10.zh: 他八九岁时哭着回家，这件事让他开始思考颜色。

D1.en: He was miserable but stayed because he was ashamed to face his parents.
D1.zh: 他很痛苦，但因为羞于见父母而留了下来。

D13b.en: At 15, he went to Tokyo to become an apprentice at Art Shokai.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，他去东京在Art Shokai当学徒。

D13a.en: At 18, he agreed to stay another year as an apprentice out of appreciation.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他出于感激同意再当一年学徒。

D18.en: At 16, he used a salvaged motorcycle with a sidecar to carry passengers and made money.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他骑着一辆修好的摩托车带侧斗载客赚钱。

D5.en: He often skipped classes to lie on a hill and watch the sky.
D5.zh: 他经常逃课，到学校后面的山坡上躺着看天空。

D4.en: As a small child, watching the motor became his favorite pastime.
D4.zh: 幼年时，看发动机成了他最喜欢的消遣。

D7.en: At 17, he rode a bike without lights and was hailed by a policeman.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他骑没灯的车被警察叫住。

D2.en: After six months of baby-sitting, he was ordered to assist with auto repairs.
D2.zh: 当了六个月保姆后，他被命令去帮忙修车。

D12.en: He grew up in a subsistence economy where food and clothing were all-critical.
D12.zh: 他在自给自足的经济中长大，吃穿都是大问题。

D14.en: At 8, he made aviation goggles from cardboard and bamboo propellers, terrorizing the village as Niles Smith.
D14.zh: 8岁时，他用纸板做航空护目镜，在自行车前装竹制螺旋桨，扮成Niles Smith在村里横冲直撞了一个月。

D15.en: At 16, he hopped into a car and drove through streets clogged with people fleeing.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他跳进一辆车，在挤满逃难人群的街道上穿行。

D10.en: At 12-13, he begged Godai for a 10% discount, breaking into tears in his eagerness.
D10.zh: 12到13岁时，他哭着恳求Godai同意打九折。

D13a.en: At 15, he sent himself a fake telegram saying his mother was ill to get home.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他给自己发假电报说母亲生病，需要回家。

D18.en: At 9, he was sent to Osaka to work 16 hours a day and live with his employer.
D18.zh: 9岁时，他被送到大阪，每天工作16小时，与雇主同住。

D4.en: At 16, he shoveled dry cement into a receptacle and pushed it along a track.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他把干水泥铲进容器，然后沿轨道推车。

D9.en: At 18, he acted as foreman wiring the Ashibe Theater, in charge of three teams.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他担任阿部剧院的布线工头，负责三个技术团队。

D2.en: At 9-10, he spent entire days polishing with rough stalks until his hands were scratched and swollen.
D2.zh: 他9到10岁时，为了最好质量，整天用粗秆子打磨，直到手被划伤肿胀。

D1.en: At 12, he decided to compete in cycling contests sponsored by The Osaka Shimbun.
D1.zh: 12岁时，他决定参加大阪新闻社赞助的自行车比赛。

D14.en: At 9, he felt immense satisfaction hearing Mr. Murakami's praise when he would win.
D14.zh: 9岁时，听到Murakami先生表扬他获胜，他感到无比满足。

M7.en: As a teenager, his boarding house became the headquarters of the Junagadh Students' League.
M7.zh: 十几岁时，他住的宿舍成了Junagadh学生联盟的总部。

M1.en: At 16, he was fired with ambition never to depend on anyone.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他燃起雄心，永不依赖他人。

D15.en: At 18, he won a bet by diving off a ship and swimming to shore through waters with occasional shark attacks.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他打赌赢了，从船上跳下游泳到岸边，游过偶尔有鲨鱼出没的水域。

D7.en: As a teenager, he organized functions like saluting the national flag, taking risks.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他组织活动，比如向国旗敬礼，冒了很多风险。

D6.en: He found accommodation in a boarding house funded by the Modh Bania caste.
D6.zh: 他在一个由Modh Bania种姓资助的寄宿公寓找到了住处。

D2.en: He read from The Times of India and wrote a summary in English, passing the test.
D2.zh: 他阅读《印度时报》并用英语写摘要，顺利通过了测试。

D3.en: At Bahadur Kanji school, he was infected by the independence mood.
D3.zh: 在Bahadur Kanji学校，他被独立情绪感染了。

D8.en: At 16, he argued with the police commissioner to defend Shah.
D8.zh: 16岁时，他和警察局长争论以保护Shah。

M4.en: At 15, he sent someone to pass on information to the Indian union.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他派人去印度联邦传递信息。

D13b.en: As a child, he was taken back by his natural parents against his will.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他被亲生父母违背意愿带回去。

D5.en: At 14, he was threatened with arrest and expulsion for political speeches.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他因政治演讲被威胁逮捕和开除。

D12.en: At age five, he was given in adoption to the childless Raja of Manda.
D12.zh: 五岁时，他被亲生父母过继给没有孩子的Manda土王。

M6.en: He dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers, unlike his own.
M6.zh: 他梦想着这样一个军人、冒险家家庭，与他自己的家庭截然不同。

D11.en: As a small child, he dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers unlike his own.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他梦想着一个与自己家庭不同的军人冒险家家庭。

M1.en: At 6-11, he was an excellent pupil, top in all subjects including PE.
M1.zh: 6到11岁时，他是个优秀的学生，所有科目包括体育都名列前茅。

D15.en: At 16, he rode competitively.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他参加马术比赛。

D2.en: At 18, while his friends partied, he worked hard in preparatory classes.
D2.zh: 18岁时，当他的朋友们在里尔狂欢时，他在预科班里努力学习。

D3.en: As a small child, he dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers unlike his own.
D3.zh: 幼年时他梦想一个与自家不同的军人冒险家家庭。

D19.en: At 15, he made his first balance with 40 shares of Banamex worth 6,600 pesos.
D19.zh: 15岁时，他做了第一笔结算，持有40股Banamex股票，价值6600比索。

D20.en: At 16-17, he was asked to administer family matters.
D20.zh: 16到17岁时，他被要求管理家庭事务。

D18.en: From age 12, he bought stock in the National Bank of Mexico.
D18.zh: 从12岁起，他就购买墨西哥国家银行的股票。

M1.en: At 15-16, he duplicated his capital by May.
M1.zh: 15到16岁时，他在5月前使资本翻倍。

D4.en: At 10, he accompanied his father to the historical center to learn the ropes.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他陪父亲去历史中心帮忙并学习门道。

D6.en: As a child, he played football and baseball with friends in the park.
D6.zh: 他小时候在公园里和朋友们踢足球、打棒球。

D2.en: At 12, he opened his first checking account and started buying shares in the National Bank.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他开了第一个支票账户，并开始购买国家银行的股票。

D15.en: As a teenager, he was very good at running the ball in American football.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他非常擅长美式橄榄球的持球跑动。

D20.en: She served as publicity director of the Biology Club, the Math Bulletin, the Art Committee, and the Forum.
D20.zh: 她担任过生物俱乐部、数学公报、艺术委员会和论坛的公关主任。

D14.en: As a child, she loved making up everyone, combing her sister-in-law's hair.
D14.zh: 小时候，她喜欢给所有人化妆，给嫂子梳头。

D13b.en: She attended P.S. 14 from kindergarten in 1913 until graduating 8B in June 1921.
D13b.zh: 她从1913年幼儿园起就读于P.S. 14，直到1921年6月从8B班毕业。

D9.en: She headed the General Organization.
D9.zh: 她领导了总组织。

D6.en: As a teenager, she was publicity director for several school clubs.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，她担任多个学校俱乐部的宣传主任。

D2.en: She peddled his beauty products and learned hands-on facial massage from him.
D2.zh: 她推销他的美容产品，并向他学习手部面部按摩技巧。

D3.en: She learned the technique of hands-on facial massage from him.
D3.zh: 她向他学习了亲手面部按摩的技巧。

M3.en: He was inspired by the exploits of those pilots.
M3.zh: 他被那些飞行员的壮举所激励。

D14.en: He took pleasure in writing and may have revealed some talent at it.
D14.zh: 他喜欢写作，可能在这方面展现了一些天赋。

D5.en: He said he likes everything on the edge, living dangerously.
D5.zh: 他说他喜欢一切在边缘、危险地活着。

D4.en: He took something apart and had surplus parts when reassembling.
D4.zh: 他拆开东西，装回去时多了几个零件。

D3.en: He read several books on aviation and was fascinated by the story of Aero-Postale.
D3.zh: 他读了几本关于航空的书，被Aero-Postale的故事迷住了。

D10.en: He used to glare at men who stared at his mother, feeling they insulted her.
D10.zh: 他过去常怒视盯着他母亲看的男人，觉得他们在侮辱她。

D11.en: He complained to his mother that if she had married his father earlier, he could have been a fighter pilot.
D11.zh: 他向母亲抱怨，如果她早点嫁给他父亲，他就能当战斗机飞行员了。

D14.en: In junior high, she won the trophy for best typist in her class.
D14.zh: 初中时，她赢得了班级最佳打字员的奖杯。

D5.en: At 17, she got married.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她结婚了。

M1.en: From age 7 to 17, she worked hard for straight A's or to sell the most Girl Scout cookies.
M1.zh: 7到17岁间，她努力拿全A或卖最多女童子军饼干。

D6.en: During schooldays, a friend named Tillie Bass helped shape her life.
D6.zh: 上学期间，一个叫Tillie Bass的朋友影响了她的生活。

D2.en: From age 7 to 17, after school she cleaned the house then did homework.
D2.zh: 7到17岁间，放学后她先打扫房子再做作业。

D12.en: At age 2, her father became ill with tuberculosis.
D12.zh: 两岁时，她父亲患上了肺结核。

M3.en: At 16, he admired Luciano Zuccoli for his dual careers as journalist and author.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他崇拜Luciano Zuccoli，因为他身兼记者和作家双重身份。

D14.en: At 16, his name appeared in La Gazzetta dello Sport.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他的名字出现在米兰的《体育报》上。

D11.en: At 16, he wrote a letter chronicling a soccer match between Modena and Inter.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他写了一封信，记述了摩德纳对国际米兰的足球比赛。

D15.en: At 16-17, he fell hopelessly in love with motor racing and the thrills of speed.
D15.zh: 16到17岁时，他无可救药地爱上了赛车和速度带来的刺激。

D4.en: At 17, he sat behind the wheel of a newly finished car in the factory yard.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他坐在工厂院子里一辆刚完工的汽车方向盘后面。

D7.en: He admitted that his first confession was also his last.
D7.zh: 他承认自己第一次忏悔也是最后一次。

D3.en: At 11, he trekked two miles across farmland to watch his second race.
D3.zh: 11岁时，他步行两英里穿过农田去看他的第二场比赛。

D16.en: At 16, he began dedicating himself to semi-professional journalism.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他开始致力于半职业的新闻工作。

D9.en: At 16, he began an after-school internship with Provincia di Modena.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他开始在摩德纳省做课后实习。

D6.en: He and his brother had their own track and invited school friends to train on it.
D6.zh: 他和哥哥有自己的跑道，邀请同学来训练。

D2.en: At 4-7, he and his brother trained each evening on a dirt strip lined with poplars.
D2.zh: 4到7岁时，他和弟弟每天晚上在杨树成行的土路上训练。

M8.en: At 12, she lost her Catholic faith.
M8.zh: 12岁时，她失去了天主教信仰。

D17.en: At 12, she was instructed to kiss her dead mother's corpse on the lips.
D17.zh: 12岁时，她被要求亲吻死去母亲的嘴唇。

M4.en: At 12, she realized she was a person outside the secrecy of confession.
M4.zh: 12岁时，她意识到自己是一个独立于忏悔秘密之外的人。

D10.en: At 12, she was left with an undying grudge against her family.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她对家人怀有永久的怨恨。

D4.en: At age 5, she peeled damp red wallpaper off the walls.
D4.zh: 5岁时，她撕下墙上潮湿的红墙纸。

D6.en: She sat on the benches of the friendly school with the three older children.
D6.zh: 她坐在那所友好学校的板凳上，和三个大孩子在一起。

D12.en: She learned about the country and had a few years of happiness.
D12.zh: 她了解了乡村，并度过了几年快乐时光。

M3.en: At 11-12, he idolized Lindholm as his first hero on a misguided ideological path.
M3.zh: 11到12岁时，他崇拜Lindholm，视其为他在错误意识形态道路上的第一个偶像。

M4.en: At 11-12, he admired both Hitler and Lindholm.
M4.zh: 11到12岁时，他钦佩希特勒和Lindholm。

D18.en: At 15-17, he registered IKEA as a firm.
D18.zh: 15到17岁时，他注册了宜家公司。

D5.en: At 11-12, he biked to the village and put up posters, maybe trying to recruit members.
D5.zh: 11到12岁时，他骑车到村里张贴海报，可能试图招募成员。

D11.en: As a small child, he lived with two imaginary Indians, Kamfert and Schane, who shadowed him.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他与两个想象中的印第安人Kamfert和Schane生活，他们如影随形。

D9.en: For the first ten years, IKEA was a one-man company for him, with some family members.
D9.zh: 在头十年里，宜家对他来说是一个人的公司，虽然有一些家人参与。

D7.en: As a small child, he experienced beatings and indoctrination on his family farm.
D7.zh: 幼年时他在自家农场经历打骂和灌输教育。

D6.en: At 16, he attended a lecture by Engdahl and was greatly enthused.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他去听了Engdahl的讲座，深受鼓舞。

D3.en: As a boy, he had leafed through it but never read it.
D3.zh: 他小时候翻过那本书，但从未读过。

D12.en: As a child, he found it difficult to escape an emotional desert without lasting damage.
D12.zh: 他幼年时觉得难以逃离情感荒漠而不受持久伤害。

D13b.en: At 12, he remembers his father coming home late and announcing he had given IKEA away, but kept power.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他记得父亲深夜回家，宣布已将宜家赠与他人，但保留了决策权。

M4.en: At 17-18, he wanted to fly a bomber over Germany to take revenge on Hitler.
M4.zh: 17到18岁时，他想驾驶轰炸机飞越德国向希特勒复仇。

D16.en: He joined the debating team sponsored by his Latin teacher.
D16.zh: 他加入了拉丁老师Virgil Parks赞助的辩论队。

D1.en: At 17-18, he applied for a scholarship and was crushed when he didn't get it.
D1.zh: 17到18岁时，他申请了奖学金，没拿到时很受打击。

D14.en: As a teenager, he got pretty good at doing the shag and the lindy hop.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他跳沙格舞和林迪舞跳得相当好。

D13b.en: He was not allowed to have a credit card or charge anything.
D13b.zh: 他家里不允许任何人拥有信用卡或赊账。

D18.en: At age 10, he and his friends offered to carry shoppers' bags for tips.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他和朋友们用红 wagon 帮顾客送袋子赚小费。

D5.en: At 17-18, he was classified 4F, a medical deferment, so he couldn't join the Air Corps.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他被定为4F（医疗延期），无法加入航空队。

D11.en: At 12-13, he developed a passion for jazz and swing.
D11.zh: 12到13岁时，他对爵士和摇摆乐产生了热情。

M1.en: At age 11, he was determined to be the captain.
M1.zh: 11岁时，他决心要当队长。

D4.en: As a teenager, he started playing the tenor sax.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他开始吹奏次中音萨克斯。

D9.en: At 15, he decided to enter the auto business after Eddie convinced him.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他在Eddie的劝说下决定进入汽车行业。

D7.en: At 9, he got into fights with kids who called him names.
D7.zh: 9岁时，他和骂他的孩子打架。

D2.en: He worked hard on train trips to places like Norfolk, Charlotte, Atlanta, and Jacksonville.
D2.zh: 他努力出差，坐火车去诺福克、夏洛特、亚特兰大和杰克逊维尔等地。

D3.en: At 17-18, he developed an interest in engineering and looked into colleges.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他对工程学产生了兴趣，并考察了几所大学。

D17.en: His mother treated his wart with drugstore remover and a carving knife over weeks.
D17.zh: 他妈妈用药店买的药水和雕刻刀治好了他脚上的疣。

M2.en: He told someone about playing Ping-Pong with his father as a boy and never beating him.
M2.zh: 他告诉别人小时候和父亲打乒乓球，从未赢过。

D15.en: He lost count of races run in flats or spikes modified by Bowerman from 1955 to 1959.
D15.zh: 1955到1959年间，他记不清有多少次穿着Bowerman改装的跑鞋比赛。

D7.en: As a boy, he played Ping-Pong with his father and felt pain from never beating him.
D7.zh: 他小时候和父亲打乒乓球，因从未赢过而痛苦。

D6.en: In high school, he was second-fastest in Oregon, staring at Grelle's back for four years.
D6.zh: 高中时他是俄勒冈州第二快的跑者，四年里一直盯着Grelle的背影。

D2.en: At 15, he went out for track and found he could run, and no one could take that away.
D2.zh: 15岁时他参加田径队，发现自己能跑，而且没人能夺走这个。

D12.en: As a child, he essentially had servants in the servants' quarters.
D12.zh: 他小时候在仆人区，实际上有仆人伺候。

D19.en: He rearranged all the furniture in a room to confuse the cleaner.
D19.zh: 他重新摆放房间里的所有家具，让清洁工感到困惑。

D20.en: He soon became the leader of a school gang.
D20.zh: 他很快成了学校帮派的头目。

M4.en: He had strong anti-British sympathies.
M4.zh: 他强烈同情反英情绪。

D16.en: He could recite whole scenes of dialogue.
D16.zh: 他能背诵整段对话。

D10.en: He tensed up and would have gone for him if I hadn't been there.
D10.zh: 他绷紧了身体，如果我不在，他肯定会冲上去。

D1.en: He would never admit defeat but kept fighting until Unicorn gave up.
D1.zh: 他从不认输，一直打直到Unicorn放弃。

D13a.en: His father barred him from taking part in the sequel to My Son A-Chang.
D13a.zh: 他父亲禁止他参演《我的儿子阿昌》的续集。

D18.en: He got a job there, even though it meant getting up well before daylight.
D18.zh: 他在那里找了份工作，尽管这意味着天不亮就得起床。

D5.en: At 13-14, he would never acknowledge defeat or admit his gang had been beaten fairly.
D5.zh: 13到14岁时，他从不承认失败或承认自己的帮派被打败。

D11.en: He imitated Errol Flynn in Robin Hood.
D11.zh: 他模仿Errol Flynn在《罗宾汉》中的表演。

M1.en: He feared being beaten and desired to be the best.
M1.zh: 他害怕被打败，渴望成为最强者。

D9.en: At 18, he knew that working out in public would attract more interest.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他知道在公共场合锻炼会吸引更多兴趣。

D7.en: At 12-13, he became the leader of a school gang with strong anti-British sympathies.
D7.zh: 12到13岁时，他成为一个有强烈反英情绪的学校帮派头目。

D6.en: At 18, he suggested they start an informal martial arts group to work out together.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他建议成立一个非正式武术小组一起练习。

D2.en: At 18, he absorbed everything he was shown, so sifu Mark accelerated his training.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他吸收了所有教给他的东西，所以师父Mark加快了他的训练。

D12.en: At 18, he worked as a junior waiter, doubling as a busboy during busy shifts.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他做初级服务员，忙时还兼做勤杂工。

M5.en: At 17, she refused a film role, insisting she could only play a student.
M5.zh: 17岁时，她拒绝了一个电影角色，坚持只能演学生。

D8.en: At 17, she refused a role over the phone, saying she could only play a student as a high school graduate.
D8.zh: 17岁时她在电话里拒演，说刚高中毕业只能演学生。

M6.en: At 17, she felt fate destined her for film.
M6.zh: 17岁时，她觉得命运安排她走上电影这条路。

D17.en: At 18, her mother told her she hoped she would always be happy.
D17.zh: 18岁时，母亲对她说希望她以后都这么快乐。

M2.en: At 18, her family opposed her film career, causing her mother to be bedridden for three days.
M2.zh: 18岁时，家人反对她进入电影圈，母亲为此卧病三日不起。

M4.en: At 18, she was curious about filmmaking and refused to give it up.
M4.zh: 18岁时，她对拍电影充满好奇，怎么也不肯放弃。

D16.en: At 17, she refused a role, saying she could only play a student as a high school graduate.
D16.zh: 17岁时她拒绝了一个角色，说自己刚高中毕业只能演学生。

D10.en: At 18, she cried when her hair was cut short for a film.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她因拍戏被剪短头发而哭肿了眼睛。

D14.en: At 17, she played a teenage girl in the film 'Outside the Window'.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她饰演了《窗外》的少女。

D13b.en: At 18, her mother signed her first film contract for NT$10,000.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，母亲代她签了第一张电影合约，片酬一万元。

D18.en: At 18, right after signing the contract, she went shopping at a boutique in Ximending with a friend.
D18.zh: 18岁时，签完约当天她就和张俐仁去西门町逛委托行。

D5.en: At 18, she was curious about filmmaking and refused to give it up despite her parents' objections.
D5.zh: 18岁时，她对拍电影充满好奇心，怎么也不肯放弃。

M1.en: At 17, as a senior in high school, she felt anxious and lost about her future while classmates prepared for college exams.
M1.zh: 17岁高中三年级时，她为前途感到焦虑和迷惘。

D15.en: At 17, she was very thin and small, weighing less than 100 pounds.
D15.zh: 17岁时，她又瘦又小，还不到一百磅。

D4.en: At 18, on the first day of shooting, her hair was cut short above the ears, making her cry until her eyes swelled.
D4.zh: 18岁拍摄第一天，她的头发被剪到耳朵之上，她哭得眼睛都肿了。

D7.en: At 18, she refused to give up acting despite her parents' strong opposition.
D7.zh: 18岁时，她不顾父母反对，坚决不肯放弃拍电影。

D6.en: At 17, she walked on the bustling Ximending streets with two classmates.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她和两位同学走在最热闹的西门町街头。

D12.en: At 17, after failing the college entrance exam, she attended cram school at night.
D12.zh: 17岁时，她没考上大学，晚上到台北车站对面的补习班上课。

M7.en: At 16, she was recommended to perform at a charity gala in Singapore for disabled children.
M7.zh: 16岁时，她由中广公司推荐，赴新加坡参加总理夫人主持的伤残儿童慈善晚会义演。

D17.en: At 17, she went to Hong Kong and raised funds with her sweet smile.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她专程赴港，展开甜甜的笑容，善款便滚滚而来。

M2.en: As a small child, her mother proudly exclaimed, 'That's my daughter!'
M2.zh: 幼年时，她母亲自豪地喊：‘那是我女儿哎！那是我们邓家的女儿哎！’

D20.en: At age 10-11, she played the lead role in a class play.
D20.zh: 10到11岁时，班上演话剧，她演女主角。

M4.en: At 11, she won first place in a Huangmei opera song contest on the radio.
M4.zh: 11岁时，她参加中华广播电台举办的黄梅调歌曲比赛，以一曲〈访英台〉获得冠军。

D16.en: At age 10-11, she represented her class in many speech and recitation contests.
D16.zh: 10到11岁时，她代表班上参加很多次演讲比赛、朗诵比赛。

D10.en: At age 6-11, she cried bitterly when teased by classmates but silently endured.
D10.zh: 6到11岁时，她为同学的捉弄哭得很伤心，却默默忍受下来。

D1.en: At age 10-11, she won first place school-wide and then county-wide in a competition.
D1.zh: 10到11岁时，她不负众望过关斩将，得到全校第一，又代表芦洲国小抱得全县第一的大奖回来。

D14.en: At 17, she performed in Hong Kong for the first time and sold White Flower Oil for charity.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她首次赴港演出，在香港工展会义卖白花油助学。

D13b.en: At 12, she failed to enter her ideal public school but was admitted to a prestigious private girls' school.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，她小学毕业没考上理想的公立学校，反而考上很不容易考进去的私立金陵女中。

D13a.en: At 16, she made the painful decision to drop out of school to sing.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她痛苦地决定休学唱歌。

D18.en: At 17, she won first place in a charity sale in Hong Kong, becoming the youngest 'White Flower Oil Charity Queen'.
D18.zh: 17岁时，她参加香港工展会白花油慈善义卖，以第一名成为最年轻的'白花油义卖皇后'

M1.en: At 13, she won a singing competition with the song 'Picking Red Water Chestnuts'.
M1.zh: 13岁时，她参加歌唱比赛以《采红菱》获得冠军

D15.en: At age 4-5, she was taken to learn ballet at the only dance studio in Pingtung.
D15.zh: 4到5岁时，她被带去屏东唯一的舞蹈社学芭蕾舞。

D4.en: At 17, she wore an apron and cooked tangyuan for a show in Hong Kong.
D4.zh: 17岁时，她穿上围裙当厨娘，在香港卖汤圆作秀

D9.en: At 18, she switched record labels and toured Southeast Asia for a year.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她跳槽到丽风唱片，在东南亚巡回演唱一年

D7.en: As a small child, she silently joined her brothers when they were punished to kneel.
D7.zh: 幼年时，她默默跑去和受罚的兄弟一起跪

D6.en: As a child, she had a group of close friends, about ten boys and girls.
D6.zh: 小时候，她有一批死党好友，十来个男男女女

D2.en: She studied French in France, attending class, hiring a tutor, and repeatedly listening to tapes alone for hours.
D2.zh: 她在法国学法语，按时上课，请家教一对一，课后反复听录音带，一学就是半天。

D3.en: At 16, she was captivated by French and wanted to learn it well.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她被法语迷住，想好好学会这个语言

D12.en: At 14-15, she had no teenage years and was entangled with malicious rumors about pregnancy and abortion.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，她没有少女时代，被怀孕、堕胎等恶毒谣言纠缠。

D5.en: At 8, she quietly went to the front lines to perform for troops.
D5.zh: 8岁时，她悄悄到前线做劳军演出

M4.en: At about 17, he joined the Left-Wing Dramatists League with two friends.
M4.zh: 17岁左右，他和两个朋友一起加入了左翼剧联。

D11.en: As a teenager, he improvised on stage, sticking his legs straight up when his stage wife pushed him down.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他在台上即兴发挥，被“妻子”按倒时把两条腿直挺挺竖起来。

D15.en: At 8, he learned martial arts, dabbling in all 18 weapons.
D15.zh: 8岁时，他学拳术，十八般武艺都学了点。

D4.en: At 13-15, he handled lighting, sets, directing, and acted all roles himself.
D4.zh: 13到15岁间，他自己搞灯光、布景、导演，各种角色都尝试过。

D9.en: At 18, he went with Wang Weiyi to direct a play at Shanghai Women's Commercial College.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他跟王为一去上海女子商业学院排戏。

D6.en: He frequently participated in Left-Wing Drama League activities.
D6.zh: 他经常参加左翼剧联的活动。

D2.en: At 15-17, he watched good foreign films many times to study them.
D2.zh: 15到17岁间，一部好外国影片他往往看好多遍。

D3.en: At 14, he was already a seasoned Peking opera fan.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他已经是看京戏的老资格了。

D20X.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing Company gate and went to the No. 2 glass studio for filming.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，到二号玻璃棚拍戏。

D17.en: At 17, she gazed at the child in her mother's arms and felt love well up inside her.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她注视着母亲怀里的孩子，心中爱意油然而生。

M2.en: At 7, she vowed in her heart to repay her mother's hard work in raising her.
M2.zh: 7岁时，她心底发愿要报答母亲的养育之恩。

D20.en: At 16, she walked into Mingxing Film Company and went to the No. 2 glass studio for filming.
D20.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，到拍戏的二号玻璃棚去。

M4.en: At 17, she resolved to dedicate herself to art and film for the suffering and justice, like her predecessor.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她暗下决心：也要为受苦的人们、为了正义，献身给艺术、给电影。

D10.en: At 18, she involuntarily thought of death, feeling it better to die than live in such pain.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她不由自主地想到了死，觉得与其痛苦地活着，还不如死了好。

D14.en: At 16, wearing mourning clothes, she recalled her father's funeral and a humiliation, and tears flowed.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她穿着孝衣，想起童年送葬和受辱经历，悲从中来，泪水涟涟。

D13a.en: At 18, after careful thought, she wrote a sincere job application letter to Dazhong Baihe.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她深思熟虑后，给大中华百合写了一封言辞恳切的应聘信。

D18.en: At 17-18, she first played a supporting role in Zhang Zhiyun's film, then a lead in Chen Kengran's debut.
D18.zh: 17到18岁间，她先在张织云主演的《战功》中当配角，后在陈铿然的《秋扇怨》中出演主角。

M1.en: At 8, she vowed to study hard to live up to her mother's hopes.
M1.zh: 8岁时，她发誓要好好学习，不辜负母亲的苦心。

D4.en: At 17, after finishing 'Blood and Tears Monument', she immediately joined the filming of 'Yang Xiaozhen'.
D4.zh: 17岁时，拍完《血泪碑》后，她马不停蹄地加入了《杨小真》的拍摄。

D9.en: At 18, at 'Dazhong Baihe', she quickly became a lead, starring in three films in late 1928.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她到“大中华百合”后很快挑起大梁，1928年底一连主演了《珍珠冠》、《劫后孤鸿》和《情欲宝鉴》三部影片。

D6.en: At 18, upon hearing of her classmate Yang Biruo's death, she was so grieved she couldn't lift her head.
D6.zh: 18岁时，听到同学杨碧若牺牲的噩耗，她痛苦得头也抬不起来。

D2.en: At 8, she studied diligently, memorized the classics, and wrote beautiful characters.
D2.zh: 8岁时，她学习用功，背熟了《三字经》、《千字文》，字也写得很秀气。

D3.en: At 17, between filming, she toured Putuo's scenic spots and felt awe for Buddha in the grand temples.
D3.zh: 17岁时，拍片之余她饱览普陀胜景，在寺庙大殿上心中充满对佛祖的敬畏。

D12.en: As a small child, her mother carried her burning with fever to the doctor on a tram, the first time.
D12.zh: 幼年时，母亲抱着发高烧的她第一次坐电车去看医生。

D7.en: At 17, she declared, 'No matter his attitude, I am determined to have this child.'
D7.zh: 17岁时，她说：“不管他的态度如何，我是要定了这个孩子了。”

D1.en: At 13, she begged Yan Yungao to teach her; he refused, so she peeked through a crack in his door to learn secretly.
D1.zh: 13岁时，她求严云高教，严云高不教，她就透过门缝偷看偷学。

D14.en: At 10-12, while selling rice at the market, her singing drew crowds, selling her rice fast and at good prices.
D14.zh: 10到12岁时，在集镇卖米，她歌声一响，买米人涌来，米卖得快价钱好。

D13b.en: At 8, her family gave her away to the Chen family as a child bride.
D13b.zh: 8岁时，家里把她送给陈姓人家做童养媳。

D13a.en: At 8, she was given as a child bride but soon ran back home.
D13a.zh: 8岁时被送做童养媳，但不久她就跑回来了。

D18.en: From 7 to 11, she herded cattle, cut grass, did farm work, was a child bride at 8, studied half a year at 9, and sold rice at 11.
D18.zh: 7到11岁间，她放牛、割草、干农活，8岁当童养媳，9岁读书半年，11岁贩米挣钱。

D5.en: At 18, she was detained by a local militia leader, forced to be his concubine, beaten, and attempted suicide by swallowing gold.
D5.zh: 18岁时，她被自卫队大队长扣押逼做小老婆，受尽毒打，吞金自杀被救。

D11.en: At 13, she made up a song using a familiar folk tune and her own lyrics, singing about her hard life.
D11.zh: 13岁时，她用桐城民歌调子编词唱道：“天上打鼓四门开……”

M1.en: At 8-10, after failing at song duets, she decided to learn, determined not to be defeated.
M1.zh: 8到10岁时，对歌卡壳受窘后，她拿定主意学，不怕学不会。

D4.en: At 7, she listened, savored, and imitated folk songs, feeling fresh and amazed.
D4.zh: 7岁时，她倾听、品味、琢磨，模仿着学起民歌。

D6.en: At 15, she went to Anqing and joined Ding Yongquan's Huangmei opera troupe.
D6.zh: 15岁时，她到安庆找到丁永泉父女，加入了黄梅调剧团。

D2.en: At 14-15, she practiced vocal and basic skills daily from dawn, persisting through all seasons.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，她每天天不亮就喊嗓子，练四功五法，夏练三伏，冬练三九。

D3.en: At 13, she learned a difficult aria by listening a few times, while others needed many tries.
D3.zh: 13岁时，她听了几遍就会唱别人学十几遍都学不会的《撇荠菜》花腔。

D12.en: At 11, she started selling rice with women, walking over ten li of mountain roads early each morning.
D12.zh: 11岁时，她开始跟妇女们贩米，天天起早赶露水集，跑十几里山路。

M2.en: At 16, she resolved to study hard to repay her parents.
M2.zh: 16岁时，她决心一定要学出个名堂来报效父母。

D14.en: At 16, she entered the exam room with judges including Hong Shen.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她走进考场，主考官一字排开，有洪深等人。

D18.en: At 16, she defied family opposition and went to Shanghai to enter the Chinese Film School.
D18.zh: 16岁时，她不顾家人反对，只身赴上海考进中华电影学校。

D5.en: At 16, she and a friend hailed a taxi and asked the driver to teach them to drive.
D5.zh: 16岁时，她和同窗拦住出租车，请求司机教她们开车。

D11.en: At 16, she looked out the window and got the idea to use 'Butterfly' as her name.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她望向窗外春光，灵感闪现，决定用蝴蝶做名字。

M1.en: At 18, she set a goal to catch up with stars like Zhang Zhiyun, Yang Naimei, Xuan Jinglin, and others.
M1.zh: 18岁时，她立下目标，要赶上张织云、杨耐梅、宣景琳等红星。

D6.en: She was invited by Xu Qinfang to star in the film 'Autumn Fan Resentment'.
D6.zh: 她受徐琴芳邀请主演《秋扇怨》。

D2.en: She worked diligently on films, spending almost every day under the lights at Tianyi.
D2.zh: 她认真拍片，几乎天天在水银灯下度过。

D3.en: She saw an ad for the Chinese Film School and felt a flash of inspiration.
D3.zh: 她看到中华电影学校招生广告，眼前一亮。

D12.en: She decided to learn a skill to support her family as the eldest daughter.
D12.zh: 她决定学一门技艺为父母分忧。

D1.en: At 17, she wiped Zhang's tears through an empty frame, causing a retake.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她用手绢为张拭泪，却从空镜框中擦进去，导致重拍。

D8.en: He kicked out his foot and snapped a comic away, sliding it across the polished floor.
D8.zh: 他一脚踢出，把漫画书踢飞，滑过光滑的木地板。

M6.en: At 7, he felt that a place was paradise, brought up on stories of swordsmen and warrior-monks.
M6.zh: 7岁时，他觉得那里是天堂，因为他从小听侠客和少林武僧的故事长大。

D17.en: He punched someone in the shoulder and went to comfort Little Brother.
D17.zh: 他打了某人的肩膀，然后去安慰小弟弟。

M2.en: At 7, his father told him he was going to work in Australia to give him things he liked.
M2.zh: 7岁时，他父亲告诉他要去澳大利亚工作，好给他想要的东西。

D10.en: He stood with his mouth open, feeling a sweet, pleasant pain rising from his belly.
D10.zh: 他张着嘴站着，感到一种从肚子升起的甜蜜又愉快的痛。

D13b.en: At 10, his parents let him go to opera school without much difficulty.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他父母没太费劲就让他去了戏校。

D13a.en: He decided to leave the school, believing he could do better.
D13a.zh: 他决定离开学校，相信自己能做得更好。

D18.en: He found his first acting role in Education of Love at age 11.
D18.zh: 他11岁时在《爱的教育》中找到了第一个角色。

D5.en: At 12-13, he was forbidden by his master to hang around the shower entrance.
D5.zh: 12到13岁时，他被师父禁止在淋浴间入口处闲逛。

D11.en: He played alone, imagining himself as General Kwan Kung or the Monkey King.
D11.zh: 他独自玩耍，想象自己是关公或孙悟空。

M1.en: He knew his destiny was more than a crowd extra or stunt performer.
M1.zh: 他知道自己的命运不只是当群众演员或特技替身。

D15.en: He was an adventurous youth, so they let him go to opera school at ten.
D15.zh: 他是个爱冒险的少年，所以十岁时被允许去戏曲学校。

D9.en: He joined the Seven Little Fortunes Opera group at age 7-8.
D9.zh: 他7到8岁时加入了七小福戏班。

D6.en: At 16, he went to a disco and showed off for girls.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他去迪斯科舞厅向女孩们炫耀。

D2.en: At 7-17, he practiced five or six hours straight with barely a break.
D2.zh: 7到17岁间，他连续练习五六个小时，几乎不停歇。

D3.en: At 12, he developed an interest in martial arts and studied various styles.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他对武术产生兴趣，学习了多种流派。

D12.en: At 14-17, he worked as a junior stuntman at Shaw Brothers and other studios.
D12.zh: 14到17岁间，他在邵氏等片场当小武行。

D20.en: At 10, Sammo Hung became 'Big Brother' because he was the oldest.
D20.zh: 10岁时，洪金宝因为年纪最大成了‘大哥’。

D8.en: At 16-17, she slapped a man across the face.
D8.zh: 16到17岁时，她扇了一个男人一耳光。

M3.en: As a teenager, she became crazy about Hideki Saijo.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，她疯狂迷上了西城秀树。

D17.en: Between ages 10 and 15, she began adopting stray dogs.
D17.zh: 10到15岁间，她开始收养流浪狗。

D20.en: At 14-15, as vice president, she organized a fan club gathering.
D20.zh: 14到15岁时，她作为副会长办了歌迷会聚会。

M4.en: At 16-17, she flew to Japan with family to support Hideki Saijo and gave him a gold medal.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，她与家人飞往日本为西城秀树捧场，并送上一块金牌。

D10.en: After Huang Zhan left, she burst into tears and collapsed onto the bed.
D10.zh: 黄沾出门后，她哇地一声哭倒在床上。

D14.en: At 4.5, she began singing on stage in lounges and clubs.
D14.zh: 四岁半时，她开始在酒廊、歌厅走台唱歌。

D13a.en: At 14-15, unable to bear school, she chose to drop out.
D13a.zh: 14到15岁时，她不堪忍受学校而选择了退学。

D18.en: At about 11, to save money, she and her sister became the main performers.
D18.zh: 大约11岁时，为了节省开支，她和姐姐成了主要演出人员。

D5.en: At 17, she insisted on going to Japan for a concert despite her fiancé's objection.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她坚持去日本看演唱会，不听未婚夫劝告。

D11.en: She loved the stage because it gave her freedom to perform.
D11.zh: 她喜欢舞台，因为可以尽情发挥表演。

M1.en: At 17-18, she won the New Talent Singing Championship.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，她获得新秀歌唱大赛冠军。

D7.en: As a small child, she preferred learning from teachers in her mother's troupe and playing with male relatives.
D7.zh: 幼年时，她更喜欢跟母亲戏班的老师学艺，和舅父表哥玩耍。

D6.en: At 17, her sister signed her up for the New Talent Contest.
D6.zh: 17岁时，姐姐帮她报名参加新秀大赛。

D2.en: Even when asked to sing songs she disliked, she tried her best to sing well, thinking, since I'm singing, why make a sad face?
D2.zh: 就算人家点到她不喜爱的歌，她都会尽力去唱好，她想，既然唱，何必苦口苦面呢！

D3.en: At 4-5, she watched intently when outside teachers taught at her mother's school.
D3.zh: 4到5岁时，她津津有味地看校外老师授课。

D12.en: At 5-15, she spent her days rigidly: school, practice, stage, sleep.
D12.zh: 5到15岁时，她每天刻板地度过上学、练歌、登台、睡觉。

M8.en: At 17, she described love for one's country as a kind of faith.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她认为家国之爱是一种信仰。

D8.en: At 15, she quickly rose to become the leading actress through hard work.
D8.zh: 15岁时，她辛苦繁忙地拍片，很快升为第一女主角。

D19.en: At 17, she meticulously researched the pronunciation of lyrics, consulting dictionaries and teachers.
D19.zh: 17岁时，她考究歌词字音，查字典、问老师，决定唱法。

M7.en: At 12, she was chosen to lead the patriotic song 'National Glory' in an anti-Japanese performance.
M7.zh: 12岁时，她被选为抗日歌舞剧终场爱国歌曲《民族之光》的领唱。

M6.en: At 13, she decided on a path toward singing.
M6.zh: 13岁时，她为自己决定了一条通向歌唱的道路。

D17.en: As a small child, she cared for her ill foster mother, doing all household chores and giving her earnings.
D17.zh: 幼年时，她帮养母洗脸熬粥，挣钱全交养母，端茶倒水洗衣擦身。

M2.en: At 17, she could not abandon the foster mother who raised her.
M2.zh: 17岁时，她抛不下养她长大的养母。

D20.en: At 16, she toured West Lake in Hangzhou, where she improvised the song 'Boat Lovers' and later recorded it.
D20.zh: 16岁时，她游览杭州西湖，即兴创作了歌曲《扁舟情侣》并录成唱片。

M4.en: At 13, she sang the line 'fight the enemy on the battlefield' with great vigor, stirring the audience.
M4.zh: 13岁时，她唱到“与敌人周旋于沙场之上”时特别带劲，观众沸腾喝彩。

D10.en: At 17, she feared grasping a fleeting thought and suffering endless grief, struggling as if drowning.
D10.zh: 17岁时，她生怕抓住某一刹那的思绪，然后尝尽百遍万遍的悲痛，像在水中挣扎般难以呼吸。

D1.en: At 11, she practiced tirelessly, inspired by Li Lili's thousand falls and rises.
D1.zh: 11岁时，她练功毫不吝惜汗水，以黎莉莉跌倒爬起逾千遍为榜样。

D14.en: At 12, she substituted for the absent lead Wang Renmei in a New Year's show.
D14.zh: 12岁时，主演王人美未到场，她顶替上台救场。

D13b.en: At 14, a young boss adopted her as his goddaughter, and it became fact.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，一位青年老板认她做干女儿，后来成了事实。

D13a.en: At 18, she nodded to try a period film, saying she was willing to broaden her acting range.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她点头同意尝试古装片，说愿意开拓戏路子。

D18.en: Around 11, she gave most of her money to her foster mother and saved a little.
D18.zh: 约11岁时，她把大部分钱交给养母，只留下一点点攒起来。

D5.en: At 17, she prepared to join the Shanghai National Salvation Drama Troupe under progressive influence.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她在进步力量影响下，准备参加上海救亡演剧队。

D11.en: At 13, her diary sentences subtly revealed rippling emotions.
D11.zh: 13岁时，她日记中看似普通的句子，隐秘地流露出涟漪般的情意。

M1.en: At 14, she ran to radio stations daily and practiced singing day and night for a competition.
M1.zh: 14岁时，她每天跑电台卖力演唱，又抓紧时间不分昼夜练唱。

D15.en: At 17, she walked to the deck and gazed over the rail during her first sea voyage.
D15.zh: 17岁时，她第一次在大海上旅行，走到甲板上凭栏远望。

D4.en: At 12-14, she begged Sister Fatty to teach her piano and finished all basic exercises in under two years.
D4.zh: 12到14岁时，她恳求胖姐姐教她弹钢琴，两年不到弹完了全部基本练习曲。

D9.en: At 16, she defied her studio's refusal and went to Hangzhou to perform.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她不顾公司不批准请假，义无反顾去了杭州参演。

D6.en: At 15, she imitated a puppet's voice and said 'Sister Fengmei, welcome' while holding a doll.
D6.zh: 15岁时，她拿着布娃娃模仿小木偶的声音说：“凤妹姐姐欢迎你。”

D2.en: At 11, she secretly watched others play piano and asked Sister Fatty questions.
D2.zh: 11岁时，她每天悄悄站到钢琴边看别人弹琴，还向胖姐姐问这问那。

D12.en: At 9-10, she dropped out after third grade and worked odd jobs with her foster mother.
D12.zh: 9到10岁时，她辍学后跟着养母去打零工，挑起了生活重担。

D13a.en: At 13, his master Rong Diexian refused a redemption offer from Luo Yinggong, seeing his future as promising.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他的师父荣蝶仙拒绝了罗瘿公的赎身提议，认为他前程远大。

D15.en: In his teens to mid-20s, he danced, skated, swam, rode horses, shot, and rode trendy bikes and motorcycles.
D15.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他跳舞、溜冰、游泳、骑马、射击，还骑凤头牌自行车和摩托车。

D4.en: As a child to teen, he did handicrafts like making wooden props, painted faces and scenery, and assembled crystal radios.
D4.zh: 幼年到十几岁时，他做手工（木刀枪、标子旗等），画脸谱、画布景，装矿石收音机。

D6.en: He invited classmates to watch operas, cheered loudly, and became a fan.
D6.zh: 他邀同学看戏，高声吆喝，成了捧角儿的。

D12.en: At 7-9, he attended three different schools in less than two years, and his father sighed he wasn't cut out for study.
D12.zh: 7到9岁时，他上了不到两年学，换了三个学校，他父亲摇头叹气说不是块读书的料。

D14.en: At 18, he first felt fate's power on stage, believing drama chose him.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他第一次站上舞台，面对聚光灯和黑暗中的观众，感到是戏剧选择了他。

D11.en: At 9, he wrote a script and directed classmates; when boys refused to play girls, he cross-dressed himself.
D11.zh: 9岁时，他编写剧本导演同学表演，男生不愿演女生，他就自己反串。

D7.en: At 15, he told his father he didn't like anything and wanted to be a director; they laughed but he meant it.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他对父亲说：“我都不喜欢，我想当导演。”大家一笑置之，但他讲的是实话。

D6.en: At 16-18, he joined the choir in high school.
D6.zh: 16到18岁时，他参加高中合唱团。

D12.en: At 10, on his second day at a new school, he was slapped by the teacher for poor exam results and had to say thank you.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他转学第二天因考试不佳被老师打耳光，还要鞠躬说谢谢老师。

D6.en: As a small child, he saw his father drink, tap a stick, sing, and dance.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他爸一喝酒，拿个棍一敲，就唱起来，就跳舞。

D2.en: At 6-12, he got many perfect scores and was a top student favored by teachers.
D2.zh: 6到12岁时，他好多个双百，学习成绩特别好，老师特别喜欢他。

D3.en: As a teenager, he learned trumpet and classical music, gaining texture and ensemble skills like a Westerner.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他学吹号做古典音乐，虽然做得不好，但给了他质感、合奏和听，像普通西方人接受的训练。

D12.en: At 3-5, he once took a bus alone and transferred, scaring his parents who thought he was lost.
D12.zh: 三到五岁时，他有一次自己坐公共汽车还倒车，把父母吓坏了，以为他丢了。

D13b.en: At 13, he told his father he wouldn't go to college, but would take a technical secondary school exam.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他告诉父亲自己不打算上大学，要考中技。

D12.en: At 16, he began to consciously make himself a tool by copying big-character posters for others.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他通过给别人抄大字报，有意识地让自己工具化。

M7.en: His mother was a pianist who sacrificed her art for marriage.
M7.zh: 他母亲是为婚姻牺牲艺术的钢琴家

M2.en: He noted his father was an incorruptible scholar-official, supported by his mother.
M2.zh: 他提到父親書生從政，兩袖清風，母親全心全意支持。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote a piece, submitted it, and saw it published in the United Daily News supplement.
D11.zh: 14歲時，他寫了一篇〈兒歌〉寄出，一週後看到它變成鉛字登在聯副。

M1.en: He studied ballet to learn from the best teacher.
M1.zh: 他为了跟最好的老师学芭蕾。

D4.en: Lin Hwai-min spent two years persuading factories to produce Taiwan's first stage plastic flooring.
D4.zh: 林怀民花两年说服工厂制作台湾第一代舞台塑胶地板。

D16.en: At 11, he introduced unfeline comic business, sniffing at a dog and raising a leg.
D16.zh: 11岁时，他表演了不像猫的滑稽动作，嗅一只狗并抬起后腿。

D10.en: At 14, he cried when he heard he was late for the matinée.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他听说自己赶不上日场演出时哭了起来。

D1.en: At 18, he depended on handouts from his brother for several months.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他靠哥哥的接济过了好几个月。

D14.en: At 14, he received praise for his wonderfully clever acting as Billy.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他因扮演Billy时极其聪明的表演而受到表扬。

D13b.en: At 12, his father died.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他的父亲去世了。

D5.en: At 18, he obtained a trial booking without pay in a small theater off Mile End Road.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他在迈尔恩德路旁的小剧院获得了一次无薪试演。

D11.en: At 16, he played Billy the pageboy.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他扮演了侍童比利。

D15.en: At 15, he marched in front of a fancy dress ball procession playing a bugle.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他走在化装舞会队伍前面吹号。

D9.en: At 15, he led a fancy dress ball procession playing a bugle and was given 10 shillings by the sports committee.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他带领化装舞会队伍吹号，体育委员会给了他10先令。

D6.en: As a small child, he attended school on various occasions.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他多次上学。

D2.en: At 17, he practiced for hours in front of a mirror to get the moves down pat.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他对着镜子练习几个小时，直到动作完美。

D3.en: At 7, he learned to write his name.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他学会了写自己的名字。

D12.en: At 9, he was sent to Norwood Schools with his brother Sydney.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他和哥哥Sydney被送到Norwood学校。

M3.en: At 12-14, she wrote an essay on Abraham Lincoln, her hero.
M3.zh: 12到14岁时，她写了一篇关于亚伯拉罕·林肯的作文，林肯是她的英雄。

M6.en: At 18, she joined the church.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她加入了教会。

D17.en: At 17, she loved to babysit, bathing, cleaning, playing, and reading to children.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她喜欢当保姆，给孩子们洗澡、洗衣服、陪他们玩、给他们读书。

D10.en: At 12, she was depressed after an unhappy stay with the Martin-Monroe family.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她在马丁-门罗家过得不开心，之后明显抑郁。

D14.en: At 17, she loved to dance, cut in from man to man, giggled, and gyrated tirelessly.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她喜欢跳舞，不断插到不同男人之间，咯咯笑，不知疲倦地扭动。

D13b.en: At 16, she saw an arranged marriage as the only alternative to another foster home or the orphanage.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，她觉得包办婚姻是避免再次寄养或回孤儿院的唯一选择。

D13a.en: At 15, she had a wedding arranged by her aunt in a friend's home.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，她在姨妈安排下在朋友家举行了婚礼。

D18.en: At 18, she worked 10 hours a day at Radioplane Co.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她每天在Radioplane公司工作10小时。

D5.en: At 11, Doc Goddard tried to force himself on her while drunk.
D5.zh: 11岁时，醉酒的Doc Goddard试图对她施暴。

D11.en: At 12-15, she wrote articles for the school newspaper, including one on the ideal dream girl.
D11.zh: 12到15岁间，她为校报撰稿，包括一篇关于理想梦中女孩的文章。

M1.en: At 15, she was described as 'A for Ambitious'.
M1.zh: 15岁时，她被形容为“A代表有抱负”。

D15.en: At 12, she received athletic awards from Los Angeles City Schools.
D15.zh: 12岁时，她获得了洛杉矶市立学校的体育奖。

D7.en: At 12, when the principal warned her pants were immodest, she substituted a tight skirt.
D7.zh: 12岁时，校长警告她裤子不端庄，她换上了紧身裙。

D6.en: At 14, she went to the beach with a boyfriend, wearing a skimpy bathing suit for the first time.
D6.zh: 14岁时，她第一次穿暴露泳衣和男友去海滩。

D2.en: At 18, she tried to be outstanding at work and won a gold medal from Radioplane.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她努力在工作中表现出色，并获得了Radioplane颁发的金牌。

D3.en: At 14, she stared at the ocean, seeing gold, lavender, blue, and foaming white.
D3.zh: 14岁时，她凝视着大海，看到金色、薰衣草色、蓝色和泡沫般的白色。

D20X.en: At 18, she felt angry but, after thinking calmly, did not force others to follow her will.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，她很生气，但冷静一想，就不强人所难了。

M7.en: She believed leaving the collective would be like a lone ship risking shipwreck.
M7.zh: 她认为倘若离开了同仁的事业，民族的利益，擅自出走，那恰如孤帆离开了航道，难免会触礁沉没。

M6.en: She loved movies and longed to return to the screen.
M6.zh: 她酷爱电影，多么渴望能重返银灯生活。

M2.en: At 18, she said we should be loyal to the country and filial to our ancestors.
M2.zh: 18岁时，她说我们应该忠于国家，对祖先尽孝道。

D20.en: She became a standing committee member of the newly formed Film Workers Association.
D20.zh: 她成为新成立的电影工作者协会的常务委员。

M4.en: At 12-13, she joined left-wing drama groups and performed in plays by Tian Han.
M4.zh: 12到13岁时，她参加左翼戏剧团体苞莉芭剧社等，演出田汉编剧的话剧。

D16.en: As Yulili, she denounced traitors before execution, saying patriots ordered her actions.
D16.zh: 她饰演的郁丽丽临刑前对着殷贼啼血陈词，说是反对汉奸、不愿做亡国奴的人指使她干的。

D10.en: She choked up and tears welled in her eyes.
D10.zh: 她哽咽了，泪水止不住涌上眼眶。

D1.en: At age 9-10, she studied hard day and night and caught up with her schoolwork.
D1.zh: 她犟劲一来，每天起早贪黑，一遍遍念、记、写，一个学期后功课赶上来了。

D14.en: She played lead roles in multiple plays in 1938.
D14.zh: 她饰演了《夜光杯》中的郁丽丽、《黑地狱》中的金姑娘等女主角。

D13b.en: She reported the letter to Shen Fu and Chen Baichen immediately.
D13b.zh: 她马上禀告了沈浮、陈白尘。

D18.en: At age 11, she joined progressive theater activities under leftist guidance.
D18.zh: 她刚踏上社会，就在左翼骨干组织下参加了许多进步演剧活动。

D5.en: At age 11, she entered an acting school and played a maid in a silent film, starting her career.
D5.zh: 她考入联华影业公司演员养成所，在无声影片《故宫新怨》中扮演小丫头，开始艺术生涯。

D11.en: As a child, she learned martial arts and practiced breathing exercises, imagining she could spit out a sword.
D11.zh: 她看了武侠小说后拜师习武，每天放学去学武术，深夜在院中练吐纳法，幻想嘴里能吐出剑来。

D4.en: She, a non-smoker, bought packs of cigarettes and practiced smoking in front of a mirror for a role.
D4.zh: 从不吸烟的她整包买香烟，对着镜子不断练习拿烟、吸烟的姿势。

D9.en: At age 17, as a lead actress, she immersed herself in rehearsals from morning till night.
D9.zh: 她是主要演员，一排起戏来就一头埋进去，从早到晚奔忙在排练场上。

D2.en: At 18, she practiced Bengbeng opera singing repeatedly until she got it right.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她一句一句学蹦蹦戏，一直练到够味儿。

D12.en: At 13-14, she became poor, took charge of the household, cooked for her father, and made shoes for her brother.
D12.zh: 13到14岁时，她家变穷了，她当家了，给父亲做饭送饭，给哥哥做布鞋。

M1.en: At 17, she rose instantly and surprisingly did well, replacing Hu Die as the top star.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她即时崛起，居然不错，代替胡蝶坐上了第一把交椅。

M8.en: He performed with such intensity that the audience sat in stunned silence at the final curtain.
M8.zh: 他表演得如此投入，以至于谢幕时观众惊得鸦雀无声。

D8.en: He would touch a peer's face and goad them, persisting even when told to stop.
D8.zh: 他会摸同龄人的脸挑衅，即使对方叫他停也不罢休。

M6.en: Perhaps this gave him his first real glimpse of what his prodigious talent could achieve.
M6.zh: 也许这让他第一次真正看到自己非凡的才华能取得什么成就。

D17.en: He dug up a buried pet and brought it back inside, then dug it up again after his father reburied it.
D17.zh: 他挖出被埋的宠物带回屋里，父亲重新埋了之后他又挖了出来。

D1.en: At 17, he tried to take classes seriously but was discouraged by how far behind he was.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他试图认真上课，但发现自己落后太多而感到沮丧。

D13b.en: As a teenager, he earned only six credits out of a possible fifteen at Libertyville High.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他在Libertyville高中只修了15个学分中的6个。

D13a.en: At 17, his father decided to send him to military school at Shattuck.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他父亲决定送他去沙特克军校。

D18.en: At 13-14, someone tried to sell him a revolver.
D18.zh: 13到14岁时，有人想卖给他一把左轮手枪。

D11.en: At 17, he showed a love of Shakespeare but changed his lines.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他表现出对莎士比亚的热爱，但会改台词。

D15.en: As a teenager, he got a string of Es in gym, which required little more than attendance.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他在体育课上拿到一连串E，那门课只要出勤就行。

D4.en: As a teen, he often went through all the traps, springing them.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他经常检查所有陷阱，把它们触发。

D9.en: At age 5-6, he marshaled his younger sister to join him in antics for attention.
D9.zh: 5到6岁时，他带领妹妹一起搞怪以吸引注意。

D7.en: At 18, he laughed and refused to pay when confronted by Roberts.
D7.zh: 18岁时，面对Roberts的质问，他笑着拒绝付款。

D6.en: At age 4-5, he went to see housewife Isabel Kratville across the street.
D6.zh: 4到5岁时，他去找对面的家庭主妇Isabel Kratville。

D12.en: He was born just before midnight on April 3, 1924, in Omaha, Nebraska.
D12.zh: 他于1924年4月3日午夜前出生在内布拉斯加州奥马哈。

D10.en: At 18, he found his date with Mario in the backseat of his father's Cadillac.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他发现他的约会对象和Mario在他父亲的凯迪拉克后座上亲热。

M5.en: At 12, she felt too grown up to sit on a school bench.
M5.zh: 12岁时，她觉得自己太大，不该再坐在学校板凳上。

M8.en: At 12, she watched the coming and going of the clouds as her geography.
M8.zh: 12岁时，她看云彩的来去，那就是她的地理。

D8.en: At 15, she told a friend: keep away from my girl friends, as I did from yours.
D8.zh: 15岁时，她对朋友说：离我的女性朋友远点，就像我离你的那样。

M7.en: At 12, she would join one of the clusters at the street corners.
M7.zh: 12岁时，她会加入街角的一个小团体。

D17.en: At about 13, she took charge of a big gray cat named Mirre while its owner had the flu.
D17.zh: 大约13岁时，她负责照顾一只名叫Mirre的大灰猫，当时它的主人得了流感。

M2.en: At 12, she was the youngest of three children, and the family got on well.
M2.zh: 12岁时，她是三个孩子中最小的，一家人相处融洽。

M4.en: At 12, she would study photographs outside movie houses.
M4.zh: 12岁时，她会研究电影院外的照片。

D10.en: At 15, she wept so much during communion that her face became blotchy.
D10.zh: 15岁时，她在领圣餐时哭得太厉害，脸都花了。

D1.en: At 17, she learned three scenes for the test in scarcely three weeks.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她在不到三周内学会了测试的三个场景。

D18.en: At 14, she earned seven crowns a week soaping customers' faces in a barbershop.
D18.zh: 14岁时，她在理发店给顾客脸上抹肥皂，每周挣七克朗。

D11.en: At 14, she thought she could speak to Mr. Ranft about it because he had five other theaters.
D11.zh: 14岁时，她觉得可以跟Ranft先生谈，因为他还有五家剧院。

M1.en: At 12, she could only think how lovely it would be at their little place by Lake Årsta.
M1.zh: 12岁时，她只想在Årsta湖边的那个小地方该有多好。

D6.en: She was the accepted center of their little group at the café where they lunched.
D6.zh: 她成了他们午餐咖啡馆小团体的公认中心。

D2.en: She would have to learn the technique, if she was to be able to swim in these new waters.
D2.zh: 如果她要在这些新水域中游泳，就必须学习技巧。

D3.en: She could never understand that those splotches and lines could be mountains, rushing rivers or glittering lakes.
D3.zh: 她始终无法理解那些斑点和线条能代表山脉、奔流的河流或波光粼粼的湖泊。

D12.en: She used to walk the two and a half miles from her home to the theater to save the carfare.
D12.zh: 她常常从家步行两英里半到剧院，以省下车费。

M3.en: At 11, she idolized Fonteyn as a role model in style and ballet.
M3.zh: 11岁时，她崇拜Fonteyn，视其为风格和芭蕾的榜样。

D17.en: At 5-6, she embarrassed her mother by trying to pick babies out of prams.
D17.zh: 5到6岁时，她试图从婴儿车里抱出婴儿，让母亲难堪。

M4.en: At 13, she risked safety by giving private dance recitals to raise money for resistance workers.
M4.zh: 13岁时，她冒着危险举办私人舞蹈演出，为抵抗运动工作者筹款。

D10.en: At 5-6, she often wept in secret to avoid being scolded.
D10.zh: 5到6岁时，她常偷偷哭泣，以免被责骂。

D1.en: At 16, she had jaundice, anemia, and edema; the war's end saved her life.
D1.zh: 16岁时，她患上黄疸、贫血和水肿；战争结束救了她一命。

D13a.en: At 11, her mother enrolled her in school as Edda van Heemstra, not Audrey Ruston.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，她母亲用Edda van Heemstra的名字给她注册入学，而非Audrey Ruston。

D18.en: At 13, she gave private dance recitals to collect money for resistance workers.
D18.zh: 13岁时，她举办私人舞蹈演出，为抵抗运动工作者筹款。

D5.en: At 16, she went into Arnhem for food, saw a patrol, and hid in a cellar.
D5.zh: 16岁时，她进入阿纳姆找食物，看到巡逻队，躲进废墟地下室。

D15.en: As a child, she was thrown by a pony and dislocated her collarbone, making her uncomfortable with horses.
D15.zh: 小时候，她被小马摔下来，锁骨脱臼，从此对马感到不安。

D4.en: She attended ballet lessons in Folkestone.
D4.zh: 她在福克斯通上芭蕾课。

D9.en: At 14, she began teaching younger children.
D9.zh: 14岁时，她开始教更小的孩子。

D7.en: At 15, she saw her uncle being taken away by the Gestapo; he was later shot.
D7.zh: 15岁时，她目睹舅舅被盖世太保带走，后来他被枪决。

D6.en: She and a few other students risked their safety by presenting private dance recitals.
D6.zh: 她和几个其他学生冒着危险举办私人舞蹈演出。

D2.en: Her mother enrolled her in the ballet class at the Arnhem Conservatory.
D2.zh: 她母亲给她报了阿纳姆音乐舞蹈学院的芭蕾课。

D3.en: She was chosen to present bouquets to de Valois and Fonteyn, and had met Fonteyn backstage before.
D3.zh: 她被选中向德瓦卢瓦和芳婷献花，之前曾在后台见过芳婷。

D12.en: She lived a one-meal-a-day regime with her family.
D12.zh: 她和家人每天只吃一顿饭。

D8.en: At age 6-8, she fired children in the cast whose parents complained about the ticket charge.
D8.zh: 6到8岁时，她开除了那些父母抱怨票价的儿童演员。
