D17.en: At 13, she cut down and tried to revive a hanged person.
D17.zh: 13岁时，她割断绳子并试图救活一个上吊的人。

D20.en: She and her siblings performed Beauty and the Beast for charity.
D20.zh: 她和兄弟姐妹演了《美女与野兽》做慈善。

M4.en: The $60 they earned went to buying a phonograph for the Navajos.
M4.zh: 他们赚的60美元给纳瓦霍人买了留声机。

D1.en: As a child, she was determined to stand out from her brother's shadow and please her father.
D1.zh: 小时候，她决心从哥哥的阴影中脱颖而出并取悦父亲。

D14.en: At 6-8, she played the lead in a children's production of Bluebeard, wearing a blue-stained beard.
D14.zh: 6到8岁时，她在儿童版《蓝胡子》中扮演主角，戴着染成蓝色的胡子。

D13b.en: As a small child, she was spanked, plunged into cold showers, and forbidden candies.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她被打屁股、冲冷水澡、不准吃糖。

D13a.en: At 14, her father agreed she could withdraw from school.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，她父亲同意她退学。

D18.en: For years she refused to have a refrigerator, insisting on her old icebox.
D18.zh: 她多年不肯买冰箱，坚持用旧冰柜。

M1.en: From ages 4 to 10, she was stimulated to run, swim, bike, and play tennis better than others.
M1.zh: 4到10岁时，她被激励跑步、游泳、骑车和打网球，要比别人更好。

D15.en: She learned golf at age 5 and competed in state championships as a teen.
D15.zh: 她5岁学高尔夫，十几岁时参加州锦标赛。

D7.en: From ages 4 to 10, she chose to wear her elder brother Tom's castoffs.
D7.zh: 4到10岁时，她选择穿哥哥Tom的旧衣服。

D3.en: At around 16, she developed lifelong Anglophilia from reading Wow.
D3.zh: 大约16岁时，她通过阅读Wow培养了对英国的终生热爱。

D8.en: At 17-18, he lashed out at Jeanetta for breaking up his friendship.
D8.zh: 17到18岁时，他因为Jeanetta破坏了他的友谊而对她发火。

D19.en: At 18, he faked documentation when he couldn't find evidence for a point.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他找不到证据就伪造文件。

M3.en: He began social and intellectual climbing by emulating James DeWeerd.
M3.zh: 他通过模仿James DeWeerd开始了社会和智力上的攀升。

D16.en: At 14, he challenged a boy to a fistfight and was suspended from school.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他向一个男孩挑战拳击，被学校暂时停学。

D10.en: At 14, he threw a temper tantrum, accused her of sabotaging his prize, and ignored her for weeks.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他大发脾气，指责她破坏他的奖品，几周不理她。

D1.en: At 18, he insisted on the integrity of the Dickens text and refused to cut the speech in the final round.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他坚持狄更斯文本的完整性，拒绝在决赛中删减演讲。

D14.en: He dropped his surname and billed himself as Byron James on the program.
D14.zh: 他去掉姓氏，在节目单上自称Byron James。

D13b.en: At 18, he registered for summer and fall classes and would take an English exam.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他注册了夏季和秋季课程，并要参加英语考试。

D13a.en: He openly defied his father's expressed repugnance for dramatics.
D13a.zh: 他公然违抗父亲对戏剧表演的厌恶。

D5.en: He had a predisposition for speed and brinkmanship, ignoring his bad short-sight.
D5.zh: 他偏爱速度和冒险，不顾自己严重的近视。

D11.en: He made a little cardboard theater with puppet figures for impromptu skits.
D11.zh: 他做了一个小纸板剧院，用木偶即兴表演短剧。

M1.en: The judges awarded him the title and entered him for the National contest.
M1.zh: 评委授予他头衔，并让他参加全国比赛。

D15.en: He scored forty points in three games, the highest individual total of the tournament.
D15.zh: 他在三场比赛中得到四十分，是赛事个人最高分。

D4.en: Before his tenth birthday, he learned to drive and service the tractor.
D4.zh: 十岁前，他学会了开拖拉机并维修它。

D9.en: As an adolescent, he was the kingpin in all plays at his small high school.
D9.zh: 青少年时期，他是小高中所有戏剧的核心人物。

D7.en: He refused to wear the customary coat, white shirt and tie expected of entrants.
D7.zh: 他拒绝穿参赛者应穿的常规外套、白衬衫和领带。

D6.en: He volunteered at registration, ushered, and took tickets at dances.
D6.zh: 他自愿在注册处帮忙，引导观众，并在舞会上检票。

D12.en: At age 10, he was given a horse on condition he care for it himself.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他得到一匹马，条件是他自己照顾它。

D2.en: At age 5, he caught and tossed a ball vigorously and asked for a basketball.
D2.zh: 5岁时，他用力接球和扔球，还问人要篮球。

D19.en: He amassed a collection of relics he kept for life.
D19.zh: 他收集了一批纪念品，并终生保留。

M3.en: He studied others' dress, table manners, gestures, and accents.
M3.zh: 他研究别人的穿着、餐桌礼仪、手势和口音。

M7.en: He refused tip money from soldiers out of patriotism.
M7.zh: 出于爱国心，他拒绝收士兵的小费。

D20.en: He performed in Pender's Knockabout Comedians troupe.
D20.zh: 他在Pender的滑稽杂技团表演。

D10.en: At 10, he spent nights alone staring at a photo of Elsie, weeping and praying for God to watch over her soul.
D10.zh: 10岁时，他独自在夜里看着Elsie的照片哭泣，祈祷上帝守护她的灵魂。

D14.en: At 16, he made his US debut as a stilt-walker in Good Times.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他作为踩高跷者在《Good Times》中首次在美国登台。

D13a.en: At 9, his strict mother disappeared from his life.
D13a.zh: 9岁时，他严厉的母亲从他的生活中消失了。

D5.en: At 18, he was keen to stay on in New York.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他渴望留在纽约。

D11.en: At 11, he had his first experience of theatre.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他第一次体验了戏剧。

M1.en: At 11, he won a scholarship to Fairfield Grammar School.
M1.zh: 11岁时，他获得了费尔菲尔德文法学校的奖学金。

D15.en: At 12, he found training as an acrobat fun and a release.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他觉得杂技训练很有趣，是一种释放。

D4.en: At 8, he met an electrician working as a part-time assistant in his school lab.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他遇到了一位在学校实验室做兼职助手的电工。

D7.en: At 14, he was suddenly expelled from Fairfield on 13 March 1918 for an undocumented reason.
D7.zh: 14岁时，1918年3月13日，他因未记录的原因突然被费尔菲尔德学校开除。

D6.en: At 18, he began to frequent the National Vaudeville Artists Club on West 46th Street.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他开始常去西46街的国家杂耍艺人俱乐部。

D3.en: At 13, he eagerly asked to be taken backstage to see the theater's modern switchboard and lighting system.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他急切地请求被带到后台看剧院的现代配电盘和灯光系统。

D19.en: He learned chess from his father, which became his favorite game and lifelong pleasure.
D19.zh: 他父亲教他下棋，这成了他最喜欢的游戏和终生的乐趣。

D17.en: At 14, he fished a three-year-old boy out of a lake after the child fell off a dock.
D17.zh: 14岁时，他从湖里救起一个从码头落水的三岁男孩。

D18.en: At 16-18, he worked as a model, usher, and vendor.
D18.zh: 16到18岁间，他做过模特、引座员和小贩。

M1.en: At about 18, he was very ambitious, talented, and very good in the play.
M1.zh: 18岁左右，他非常有抱负、有才华，在剧中表现出色。

D4.en: At 13-14, he built a makeshift clubhouse in the woods where friends spent the night.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他在树林里搭了一个临时俱乐部，朋友们在那里过夜。

D9.en: At 14, he became a leader of the Seneca Point Gang.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他成了Seneca Point帮的头儿。

D7.en: At 18, he said, 'I'm leaving this fucking place for good!'
D7.zh: 18岁时，他说：‘我他妈要永远离开这鬼地方！’

D2.en: At 17, he got A's and B's to show he could do well if he wanted.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他得了A和B，以证明只要他想就能做好。

D13a.en: At 18, he chose the sea instead of working in a shipyard.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他选择了出海，而不是去船厂工作。

D15.en: At 14, he handled boats with skill that delighted Belmont.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他驾船技术娴熟，让Belmont很高兴。

M8.en: At 15, she was confirmed as a Lutheran.
M8.zh: 15岁时，她接受了路德宗的坚信礼。

D19.en: As a teenager, she sang to soldiers at train stations and hospitals during WWI.
D19.zh: 十几岁时，她在火车站和医院为士兵唱歌。

M3.en: At age 10-12, she was ecstatically devoted to Henny Porten, Germany's first great movie star.
M3.zh: 10到12岁时，她狂热崇拜德国第一位电影巨星Henny Porten。

M7.en: As a teenager, she conducted potato and charcoal drives and sang at railroad stations and military hospitals.
M7.zh: 十几岁时，她组织土豆和木炭募捐，在火车站和军医院唱歌。

D17.en: At 13, she attached a black band to her left sleeve and wore only black or grey.
D17.zh: 13岁时，她在左袖上别了黑纱，只穿黑色或灰色衣服。

D10.en: At 15, she was confirmed as a Lutheran, which seemed shockingly tardy to the Catholic cook.
D10.zh: 15岁时，她作为路德宗信徒接受了坚信礼，这在天主教厨师看来晚得惊人。

D14.en: At 12, she tucked her violin under her arm after achieving her goal: a first audience.
D14.zh: 12岁时，她把小提琴夹在胳膊下，实现了目标：第一次有了观众。

D13b.en: At 15, she was confirmed as a Lutheran, the usual age for a Lutheran girl.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，她作为路德宗信徒接受了坚信礼，这是路德宗女孩的通常年龄。

D13a.en: At 17, she would not graduate or take final exams, choosing to pursue music instead of academics.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，她不会毕业或参加毕业考试，选择追求音乐而非学业。

D11.en: As a child, she named herself Marlene, as family memory and documents prove.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她给自己取名Marlene，家族记忆和文件为证。

D15.en: As a teenager, she spent evenings at railroad stations singing soldiers off to war or at military hospitals singing them to peace.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，她晚上在火车站唱歌送士兵上战场，或在军医院唱歌送他们安息。

D9.en: She divided her time diligently and precociously shrewdly.
D9.zh: 她勤奋而精明地分配时间。

D12.en: Her father died, presumably in 1907.
D12.zh: 她父亲去世了，大概在1907年。

D6.en: She entwined her arm with Hilde Sperling's.
D6.zh: 她挽着希尔德·斯珀林的手臂。

D19.en: At 8, he built a huge wall chart tracking every British ship afloat daily.
D19.zh: 8岁时，他制作了一张巨大的墙图，每天记录几乎每艘英国船只的位置。

D16.en: At 7, he astonished his family by reciting most of England's train schedules from memory.
D16.zh: 7岁时，他凭记忆背诵出英格兰大部分火车时刻表，令家人惊讶。

D13a.en: He briefly served as an altar boy.
D13a.zh: 他短暂地当过辅祭男孩。

D18.en: At about 9, he was allowed to deliver fruit and vegetables by horse-drawn cart around Epping.
D18.zh: 大约9岁时，他被允许用马车在埃平地区运送水果蔬菜。

D11.en: At 6, one of his earliest memories was a play he saw in 1905.
D11.zh: 6岁时，他最早的记忆之一是1905年看的一部戏。

M1.en: At 11, the Distinction List named A. Hitchcock third among six with general honors in form 1B.
M1.zh: 11岁时，优秀名单上A. Hitchcock在1B班六名获得综合荣誉的学生中排名第三。

D4.en: At 16, he designed an advertising layout for institutional wiring.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他设计了一个宣传机构布线的广告版面。

D2.en: At 7, his parents failed to enroll him in school at the prescribed age.
D2.zh: 7岁时，父母没有按规定送他上学。

D3.en: At 17-18, he frequented art galleries and museums, entranced by French moderns.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他常去美术馆和博物馆，对法国现代派作品着迷。

D12.en: At 14-16, he may have left school to help support his family.
D12.zh: 14到16岁时，他可能离开学校去帮助养家。

D13b.en: At 11, his first day at Saint Ignatius was a Wednesday, senior boys admitted earlier.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他在圣依纳爵的第一天是星期三，高年级男生已提前入学。

D5.en: At 18, he signed up for a cadet regiment of the Royal Engineers in 1917.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他于1917年报名参加了皇家工兵学员团。

D7.en: At 10, his father withdrew him from school after he complained about meals.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他抱怨学校伙食，父亲便让他退学了。

D14.en: He played the drums in a swing band.
D14.zh: 他在摇摆乐队里打鼓。

D4.en: He received a Graflex professional camera from Jacques on his thirteenth birthday.
D4.zh: 他13岁生日时从Jacques那里收到一台Graflex专业相机。

D9.en: He took each of his friends aside and gave them directions privately.
D9.zh: 他把每个朋友拉到一边，私下给他们指示。

D6.en: He was a member of the school's photography club.
D6.zh: 他是学校摄影俱乐部的成员。

D2.en: He bought the record and played it continuously until his sister Barbara broke it in anger.
D2.zh: 他买了那张唱片，不停地播放，直到他姐姐Barbara生气地把它摔坏。

D3.en: He taught himself how movies were made by reading film theory books and filling them with notes.
D3.zh: 他通过阅读电影理论书籍并在上面做满笔记，自学了电影制作。

M6.en: At 17, he saw Olof Molander's production of A Dream Play and decided his path.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他看了Olof Molander导演的《一出梦的戏剧》，决定了自己的道路。

D10.en: He had a violent temper and was sometimes soft and sensitive.
D10.zh: 他脾气暴躁，有时又柔软敏感。

D13a.en: At 18, he failed the Latin preliminary, his first act of rebellion against his father.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他拉丁语预考不及格，这是他反抗父亲的第一枪。

D18.en: He swapped half his army of lead soldiers for a precious contraption.
D18.zh: 他用一半的铅兵换了一个珍贵的装置。

D5.en: He went to Germany for the first time on an exchange visit in 1934.
D5.zh: 1934年他第一次去德国做交换访问。

D11.en: At 12, he and his sister performed a fantasy called When the Ice Troll Melts.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他和妹妹表演了一出幻想剧，叫《冰妖融化时》。

D15.en: He says, "I've grown so tall I can't keep my balance any more."
D15.zh: 他说：“我长太高了，再也保持不了平衡。”

D4.en: He sacrificed most of his tin soldier army in a swap.
D4.zh: 他牺牲了大部分锡兵来交换。

D9.en: He directed plays at Master Olofs Garden, including Strindberg's Lucky Per's Journey.
D9.zh: 他在Master Olofs Garden导演了戏剧，包括斯特林堡的《幸运的佩尔之旅》。

D6.en: He had a rewarding and liberating relationship with a girl in her mid-teens.
D6.zh: 他和一个十五六岁的女孩有一段有益且解放的关系。

D2.en: He was made to go to church every Sunday to hear his father preach.
D2.zh: 他每个周日都被迫去教堂听父亲布道。

D3.en: He returned to the gallery week after week, following each production with score in hand.
D3.zh: 他每周都回到剧院，手里拿着乐谱跟着每一场演出。

D12.en: He was born with a caul over his head, a sign of future success, and was a Sunday child.
D12.zh: 他出生时头上覆有胎膜，这是未来成功的征兆，而且他是个周日孩子。

M4.en: At 16, he was heavily indoctrinated about the Nazi cause.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他受到关于纳粹事业的强烈灌输。

D20X.en: He returned to Rimini and finished high school there.
D20X.zh: 他回到里米尼并在那里完成了高中学业。

M6.en: He acted on his resolve to become a journalist and went to Florence.
M6.zh: 他下定决心成为记者，去了佛罗伦萨。

M2.en: His father insisted he become a lawyer, while his mother wanted him to be a doctor.
M2.zh: 他父亲坚持要他当律师，而他母亲想让他当医生。

D20.en: He and painter Demos Bonini opened a portrait shop named Febo.
D20.zh: 他和画家Demos Bonini开了一家肖像店，取名Febo。

D16.en: He kept torturing himself, saying he would go back to Milan, but his resolve to stay in Rome was stronger.
D16.zh: 他不断折磨自己，反复说要回米兰，但留在罗马的决心更坚定。

D10.en: He kept torturing himself, saying over and over, 'I'm going back to Milan.'
D10.zh: 他不断折磨自己，反复说：“我要回米兰。”

D1.en: He kept writing sketches and doing illustrations, sending them to publications with little success.
D1.zh: 他坚持写幽默小品和画插图，投稿给几家刊物，但很少成功。

D14.en: At 15, he made other boys laugh by doing impressions of him.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他模仿那个人逗其他男孩笑。

D13b.en: He joined a fascist youth group and wore the required uniform.
D13b.zh: 他加入了一个法西斯青年团体并穿上了规定的制服。

D13a.en: At 18, he was allowed to go to Rome on condition that he enroll in law school and live with his aunt.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他被允许去罗马，条件是必须读法律系并住在姑妈家。

D5.en: He sometimes spent the night on a park bench despite having a comfortable bed at home.
D5.zh: 他有时在公园长椅上过夜，尽管家里有舒适的床。

D11.en: He published his first cartoon in the Florentine weekly 420.
D11.zh: 他在佛罗伦萨周刊《420》上发表了第一幅漫画。

D15.en: He stole a chicken from a local colonel and heisted a bird cage and sacks of flour.
D15.zh: 他偷了当地上校的一只鸡，还偷了一个鸟笼和几袋面粉。

D4.en: He opened a portrait shop named Febo with painter Demos Bonini in Palazzo Malatesta.
D4.zh: 他和画家Demos Bonini在马拉泰斯塔宫开了一家肖像店，取名Febo。

D9.en: He embarked on a business project with a friend, trying to earn money from their art.
D9.zh: 他和一个朋友一起创业，试图靠艺术赚钱。

D7.en: As a boy, he saw a slide of a naked girl slipped in among others.
D7.zh: 他小时候看到有人把一张裸女幻灯片混进其他幻灯片里。

D6.en: At 16, he went camping in Verrucchio with the scouts and GIL.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他和童子军以及GIL一起去韦鲁基奥露营。

D2.en: At 18, his apprenticeship under Nerbini let him learn from within a publishing house.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他在Nerbini手下当学徒，得以从内部了解出版社的运作。

D12.en: At 16, he couldn't pay his bill and ran out; she told him he could eat there anyway.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他付不起账单跑出去，她追上去说他可以免费在那里吃。

D3.en: At 18, he enrolled in law school at the Università di Roma and planned his move to the capital.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他进入罗马大学法学院并计划搬到首都。

D20X.en: He began by reading comics, saw too many movies, and watched too much television.
D20X.zh: 他从小开始看漫画，看了太多电影，也看了太多电视。

D19.en: At 16, he broke down the film and hung shots on his homemade cutting rack, taping descriptions and locations onto each scene.
D19.zh: 16岁时，他把胶片拆开，把镜头挂在自己做的剪辑架上，在每个场景上贴上描述和位置。

D17.en: He spent his childhood in a house with three younger sisters and a mother who played concert piano.
D17.zh: 他的童年是在一个房子里度过的，有三个妹妹和一位弹奏音乐会钢琴的母亲。

M4.en: He was scared and angered by being treated differently because of his religion.
M4.zh: 他因宗教受到区别对待，感到害怕和愤怒。

D10.en: He stuck tape to his nose and forehead, hoping it would develop a tilt.
D10.zh: 他把胶带贴在鼻尖和额头上，祈祷鼻子能变歪。

D1.en: He was always the last picked for games; nobody wanted jerky little Steven.
D1.zh: 他玩游戏总是最后一个被选，没人想要讨厌的小史蒂文。

D14.en: His first feature-length movie, Firelight, was shown at the Phoenix Little Theater.
D14.zh: 他的第一部长片《火光》在凤凰城小剧院放映。

D13b.en: He graduated from Saratoga High School.
D13b.zh: 他从萨拉托加高中毕业。

D13a.en: He was bar mitzvahed in an Orthodox synagogue at age 13.
D13a.zh: 他13岁时在东正教犹太教堂举行了成人礼。

D18.en: He worked in the cafeteria to earn pocket money.
D18.zh: 他在自助餐厅打工赚零花钱。

D11.en: As soon as he had a train set, he repeatedly recreated the crash.
D11.zh: 他一得到火车模型，就反复重现撞车场景。

M1.en: At 15-16, he made his first fully scripted movie, Escape to Nowhere, and won First Prize at the Arizona Amateur Film Festival.
M1.zh: 15到16岁时，他制作了第一部完整剧本的电影《无处可逃》，并在亚利桑那业余电影节上获得一等奖。

D15.en: In a school footrace, he came second last, just ahead of a handicapped boy.
D15.zh: 在学校赛跑中，他跑了倒数第二，只比一个残疾男孩快。

D7.en: At 16-17, in fury at a neighbor's slurs, he smeared their windows with peanut butter.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他因邻居的辱骂而愤怒，用花生酱涂抹了他们的窗户。

D6.en: At a retrospective of USC graduate films, he got to know the more social of the film students.
D6.zh: 在南加州大学毕业生电影回顾展上，他结识了那些更善于社交的电影学生。

D2.en: At 12-14, he began to study the clarinet and march in the school band.
D2.zh: 12到14岁时，他开始学习单簧管并参加学校乐队游行。

D3.en: As a small child, he found movies, especially Disney cartoons, emotionally overwhelming.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他觉得电影，尤其是迪士尼动画，在情感上令人难以承受。

M6.en: At around 12-13, he began to hear the calling to become a priest.
M6.zh: 12到13岁时，他开始听到成为神父的召唤。

D14.en: At 8 or 9, he decided he wanted to be a priest.
D14.zh: 8或9岁时，他决定要成为神父。

D13a.en: In 1960 at 17, he told his parents he wanted to be a teacher and applied to NYU.
D13a.zh: 1960年他17岁时，告诉父母他想当老师，并申请了纽约大学。

D18.en: At about 14-17, he saved up ten dollars to buy The Film Till Now by Paul Rotha.
D18.zh: 14到17岁间，他攒了十美元买了Paul Rotha的《电影至今》。

D11.en: At 6-13, his apartment window was a frame where he watched a living Italian neorealist film.
D11.zh: 6到13岁间，他公寓的窗户成了画框，他观看一部活生生的意大利新现实主义电影。

D4.en: At 8-13, he copied comics, a key step in his filmmaking development.
D4.zh: 8到13岁时，他临摹漫画，这是他电影生涯的关键一步。

D9.en: At around 8-12, he often served at the 5:00 a.m. mass and received special training for Saturday funeral masses.
D9.zh: 8到12岁时，他经常在凌晨5点弥撒中服务，并接受特殊训练担任周六葬礼弥撒的祭坛男孩。

D2.en: At 8-9, he committed to serve as an altar boy during visitations.
D2.zh: 8到9岁时，他承诺在守灵期间担任辅祭。

D3.en: At age 6, he heard his cousin shout that the screen was bigger than the whole house.
D3.zh: 6岁时，他听到表哥大喊屏幕比整个房子还大。

D16.en: At around 11-12 in 1953, Principe told him this man could never have been a priest.
D16.zh: 1953年他11到12岁时，Principe告诉他这个人不可能成为神父。

D5.en: As a teen, he was sent ahead to provoke a target, then stepped back for others.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他被派去挑衅目标，然后退后让别人动手。

D15.en: As a teen, he was small and sickly, took punishment, and earned acceptance.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他瘦小多病，忍受了惩罚，赢得了认可。

D7.en: As a small child, he learned the Sicilian code of silence from his father.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他从父亲那里学到了西西里的缄默法则。

D8.en: He took his roommate's chocolate without permission.
D8.zh: 他未经允许拿走了室友的巧克力。

M6.en: He was fanatically devoted to the idea of the theatre.
M6.zh: 他狂热地投身于戏剧理想。

D20.en: At 18, he wrote to say the summer festival would feature three plays, one directed by him.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他写信说夏季戏剧节将上演三部剧，其中一部由他导演。

D10.en: He claimed he had never known stage fright until that night.
D10.zh: 他声称直到那晚才第一次感到怯场。

D1.en: He was very upset to have lost.
D1.zh: 他对输掉比赛非常沮丧。

D14.en: At 11-12, he directed and performed in Richard III, Julius Caesar, and Androcles and the Lion.
D14.zh: 11到12岁时，他导演并演出了《理查三世》《尤利乌斯·恺撒》和《安德罗克勒斯和狮子》。

D13b.en: He was born on May 6, 1915, weighing ten pounds.
D13b.zh: 他于1915年5月6日出生，重十磅。

D13a.en: He continued to pose as a would-be painter.
D13a.zh: 他继续假装自己是个想当画家的人。

D18.en: At 18, he wrote pulp fiction.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他写了通俗小说。

D5.en: At 16, the Gate directors took a gamble hiring him for a pivotal role.
D5.zh: 16岁时，Gate剧院导演冒险让他担任关键角色。

D11.en: At 17, he co-wrote a play, Marching Song, about John Brown and freeing slaves.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他与人合写了一部关于约翰·布朗和解放奴隶的戏剧《进行曲》。

M1.en: At 18, he scored a hit as Mercutio in Katharine Cornell's Romeo and Juliet.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在凯瑟琳·康奈尔制作的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》中饰演茂丘西奥大获成功。

D15.en: At 17, he came out with a wound that tended to travel.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他带着一个伤口出来，这个伤口会移动。

D4.en: As a child (age 11-15), he supervised building a larger marionette theater.
D4.zh: 11到15岁时，他监督建造了一个更大更华丽的木偶剧院。

D9.en: At 11, he was cast as leading man and supervised his leading lady's makeup.
D9.zh: 11岁时，他担任男主角并指导女主角的化妆。

D7.en: At 16, he said, 'Why should I waste time teaching professors what I know?'
D7.zh: 16岁时，他说：“我为什么要浪费时间教那些教授我所知道的东西？”

D2.en: At 4-9, he began a lifelong habit of voluminous, unsystematic reading.
D2.zh: 4到9岁时，他开始了大量但不成体系的阅读习惯，并持续一生。

D3.en: At 17, he recorded his progress on a play, showing his developing grasp as a writer and editor.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他记录了自己在剧本上的进展，展现了他作为作家和编辑的成长。

D17.en: He worried constantly about her because she had grown much weaker.
D17.zh: 他总为她担心，因为她身体越来越弱。

D8.en: He imperiously gave the start signal for the quartet, and everything continued as if nothing had happened.
D8.zh: 他傲慢地发出了四重奏的开始信号，一切就像什么都没发生一样继续下去。

D14.en: He made his opera debut among amateurs in a challenging opera: Arrigo Boito's Mefistofele.
D14.zh: 他在一群业余爱好者中首次登台演出歌剧，演的是很有挑战性的Arrigo Boito的《梅菲斯特》。

D13a.en: He earned a teaching diploma but found the idea of teaching unappealing.
D13a.zh: 他拿到了小学教师资格，但觉得教书没意思。

D7.en: At twelve, he boldly defended the great tenor from the Marche, even knowing the man was in poor physical shape.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他大胆地为那位来自马尔凯的男高音辩护，尽管他知道那人身体状况很差。

D6.en: As a boy, he joined his father in the Società Corale Gioachino Rossini choir.
D6.zh: 他小时候和父亲一起加入了Gioachino Rossini合唱团。

D12.en: He says his family was poor; his father was a baker, so they had food but no money for singing lessons.
D12.zh: 他说家里很穷，父亲是面包师，有饭吃但没钱上声乐课。

D5.en: He saw the exciting insurance job as full of unpredictability and uncertainty.
D5.zh: 他觉得保险工作虽然刺激，但充满变数和不确定。

M8.en: At age 5, his mother held daily Bible studies for him and his siblings.
M8.zh: 他5岁时母亲每天为他与兄弟姐妹上圣经课

D8.en: At 9, he refused, and Joseph smacked him across the face.
D8.zh: 9岁时，他拒绝了，约瑟夫打了他一巴掌。

D20.en: At 17, his vote was just one of six in group decisions with his brothers and father.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他的投票只是兄弟和父亲六人中的一票。

M4.en: At 9, he said, 'I was just watchin' you 'cause you're so good. How do you do that?'
M4.zh: 9岁时，他说：“我只是在看你，因为你太棒了。你怎么做到的？”

D16.en: At 9, he was an accomplished mimic, sounding as smooth as a grown-up.
D16.zh: 9岁时，他擅长模仿，声音像大人一样流畅。

D14.en: As a child, he would go out in public wearing a hairy gorilla head mask.
D14.zh: 小时候，他会戴着毛茸茸的大猩猩头套出门。

D11.en: At 12-14, he drew animals and monsters in class instead of paying attention.
D11.zh: 12到14岁时，他在课上画动物和怪物，没注意听讲。

M1.en: At 16, he was as unhappy as the rest of his family about what was happening at Motown.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他和家人一样对Motown发生的事情感到不满。

D15.en: At 11, he was hailed as the most naturally commanding child performer since Sammy Davis, Jr.
D15.zh: 11岁时，他被誉为自Sammy Davis, Jr.以来最具天赋的儿童表演者。

D9.en: His father arranged a press conference to announce the family's new affiliation with CBS.
D9.zh: 他父亲安排了一场新闻发布会，宣布全家与CBS的新合作。

D7.en: At 17, he made it clear he did not want to participate, but was outvoted.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他明确表示不想参加，但被投票否决。

D6.en: At 13, his brothers jumped on him, tickling him and wrestling him to the ground.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他的兄弟们扑到他身上，挠他痒痒，把他摔倒在地。

D2.en: At 7, he rehearsed for hours with the group until they heard a familiar car pull up.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他和团队排练了好几个小时，直到听到熟悉的汽车声。

D3.en: At 11, from Diana Ross he got a sense of style and an appreciation of power.
D3.zh: 11岁时，他从Diana Ross那里学到了风格感，也懂得了欣赏权力。

M6.en: He went door-to-door even at the peak of his fame.
M6.zh: 他在成名巅峰时还挨家挨户传教。

D13b.en: At age 5, he walked with his family to Kingdom Hall every Sunday as his mother asked.
D13b.zh: 5岁时，他每周日按母亲要求穿好衣服和家人一起走到王国聚会所。

M6.en: At 18, she believed there was a place for her in show business.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她相信演艺界有她的位置。

D16.en: At 18, she bluntly told another actress she could use her help, and it was appreciated.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她直率地告诉另一位女演员她可以帮忙，对方很感激。

D10.en: As a small child, she was extremely tense, nervous, sensitive, and vulnerable, filled with anxiety and fear.
D10.zh: 幼年时，她极度紧张、神经质、敏感脆弱，充满焦虑和恐惧。

D14.en: At about 12, she auditioned for and got a role in a local musical revue by the Masonic Club.
D14.zh: 大约12岁时，她试镜并获得了共济会俱乐部当地音乐剧的一个角色。

D13a.en: At 17-18, she quit her job, returned home, and enrolled as a bleached blonde sophomore at Jamestown High School.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁时，她辞掉工作回家，染成金发以二年级生身份入读詹姆斯敦高中。

D18.en: At 18, she returned to New York where Hattie Carnegie gave her a job that financed more auditions.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她回到纽约，Hattie Carnegie给了她一份工作，资助她进行更多试镜。

D11.en: At 11, she imagined she was a glamorous woman named Sassafrassa.
D11.zh: 11岁时，她想象自己是一个名叫Sassafrassa的迷人女人。

M1.en: At 18, she moved an audience to laughter and appreciation, just like her hero Julius Tannen.
M1.zh: 18岁时，她让观众发笑并欣赏，就像她的偶像Julius Tannen一样。

D4.en: At 18, she posed as the Chesterfield Cigarette Girl.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她扮成切斯特菲尔德香烟女郎。

D6.en: At 18, she was talented and had attended drama school; they were casting Within the Law.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她有才华并上过戏剧学校；他们正在为《法律之内》选角。

D2.en: At 9, she studied singing, piano, and dancing at Chautauqua Institute.
D2.zh: 9岁时，她在肖托夸学院学习唱歌、钢琴和舞蹈。

D3.en: At 9, she read magazines about theater personalities.
D3.zh: 9岁时，她看关于戏剧人物的杂志。

D12.en: At 10, she worked leading a blind man for 50 cents a week.
D12.zh: 10岁时，她领盲人走路，每周赚50美分。

D17.en: At 12, she ran with two pails of water to a train wreck, thinking a tramp might need it.
D17.zh: 12岁时，她提着两桶水跑向火车事故现场，以为可能有流浪汉需要水。

D5.en: At about 12, her partner threw her so hard across the boards that her shoulder was dislocated.
D5.zh: 大约12岁时，她的搭档把她狠狠摔在台上，导致她肩膀脱臼。

D8.en: At 16-17, he was a master of the put-on with a current of hostility.
D8.zh: 16到17岁时，他擅长装模作样，带着一股敌意。

M3.en: At 14, Little Richard's example inspired him to play piano again.
M3.zh: 14岁时，Little Richard的榜样激励他重新弹钢琴。

M7.en: At 16-17, he always went with daughters of miners, farmers, and workers.
M7.zh: 16到17岁时，他总和矿工、农民、工人的女儿们在一起。

D17.en: At 16, he spent a lot of time at Echo's home playing with her young nephew.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他常在Echo家陪她的小侄子玩。

D20.en: He provided the trio with its idiosyncratic impetus.
D20.zh: 他给三人组提供了独特的动力。

M4.en: At 17, he made an audacious move, a raised digit to the student council.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他做出大胆举动，对学生委员会竖中指。

D10.en: At about 17, his eyes widened with surprise when Echo returned his bracelet in the hall.
D10.zh: 约17岁时，Echo在走廊还他手链，他惊讶地睁大眼睛。

D1.en: He teamed up with a disc jockey and reserved the Hibbing Armory.
D1.zh: 他和一个唱片骑师合作，预定了希宾军械库。

D14.en: He performed on a television talent show broadcast on Channel 9 in the Twin Cities.
D14.zh: 他在双子城第9频道播出的电视才艺秀上表演。

D13b.en: At 18, he enrolled at the University of Minnesota in the Twin Cities.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他进入明尼苏达大学双城分校。

D13a.en: He made the senior honor roll in 1959 along with Wichman and Wilcox.
D13a.zh: 1959年他和Wichman、Wilcox一起上了高年级荣誉榜。

D5.en: He told my brother he'd played with Conway Twitty, so he got the job without auditioning.
D5.zh: 他跟我哥说他和Conway Twitty一起演出过，所以没试镜就得到了那份工作。

D11.en: He invented an impromptu word game called Glissendorf to confuse innocent third parties.
D11.zh: 他发明了一个即兴文字游戏叫Glissendorf，用来迷惑无辜的第三方。

M1.en: He was anxious to put together anything that could complement his music.
M1.zh: 他急于凑齐任何能配合他音乐的东西。

D15.en: He used to sing like Jerry Lee Lewis and play piano while standing up.
D15.zh: 他过去唱歌像Jerry Lee Lewis，还站着弹钢琴。

D4.en: He decided to take further lessons under Clarabelle Hamilton.
D4.zh: 他决定跟Clarabelle Hamilton继续上课。

D9.en: He put together a short-lived combo called Elston Gunn and the Rock Boppers.
D9.zh: 他组了一个短命的组合叫Elston Gunn and the Rock Boppers。

D7.en: At 15-17, he snapped back at his parents, 'The honor roll isn't everything.'
D7.zh: 15到17岁时，他对父母顶嘴说：‘光荣榜不代表一切。’

D2.en: At 17, after losing the Chords, he picked up the guitar, determined not to be walked out on again.
D2.zh: 17岁时，失去Chords乐队后，他拿起了吉他，决心不再被人甩掉。

D3.en: At 17, he and a friend visited a guy and spent many hours talking with him.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他和一个朋友去拜访了一个人，并花了很多时间和他交谈。

D12.en: At 7, he carved his initials B. Z. next to the wall-mounted telephone.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他在墙上的电话旁边刻下了自己名字的首字母B. Z.

D8.en: At 16, he fought a fellow usher he suspected was a stool pigeon and got fired.
D8.zh: 16岁时，他因怀疑另一名引座员是告密者而与之打架，随后被解雇。

D20.en: At 13-15, he and others formed a football team.
D20.zh: 13到15岁间，他和别人组建了一支足球队。

D1.en: From the first quavering notes of the first song he sang, it was obvious that there was something different about him.
D1.zh: 从他唱的第一首歌的第一个颤抖的音符开始，就明显看出他有些与众不同。

D14.en: At 12, he brought his guitar to school from the very start of the school year.
D14.zh: 12岁时，他从学年一开始就把吉他带到学校。

D13b.en: At 16, he quit high school in the middle of his second year.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他在高二中途辍学。

D18.en: As a kid, he always had to climb the fence.
D18.zh: 小时候，他总是得爬篱笆。

D5.en: At 18, he almost drove to Hernando, Mississippi to get married.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他差点开车去密西西比州的Hernando结婚。

D11.en: At 10, he dreams of being Slim, wearing a western shirt with sparkles and a scarf.
D11.zh: 10岁时，他梦想成为Slim，穿着带亮片的西部衬衫，脖子上系着围巾。

M1.en: At 18, he was visibly proud that he had made it through.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他为自己挺过来了而明显感到自豪。

D9.en: At 18, he had a tryout at a club called the Hi-Hat on South Third.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他在South Third的Hi-Hat俱乐部试演。

D7.en: At 17, he made a ringing declaration of independence through his dress, hair, and demeanor.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他通过穿着、发型和举止宣告独立。

D6.en: At 15, he and four other boys became well known for their concerts on Market Mall.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他和另外四个男孩因在Market Mall的音乐会而闻名。

D2.en: At 15, he sat alone on the steps at night, fingering chords softly in the dark.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他晚上独自坐在台阶上，在黑暗中轻轻拨弄和弦。

D3.en: At 10, he listened to the Opry every Saturday night.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他每周六晚上听Opry。

D12.en: At 18, he talked about finding a job to buy a house for his mother.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他谈到找工作给妈妈买房子。

D19.en: He collected old rent ledgers from East and West villages and ancestral temples, detailed in his book 'Ren Geng Suo Yan'.
D19.zh: 他收集了东西庄和祠堂的旧租簿，详情载于其《任耕琐言》中。

D13b.en: As a teenager, he obediently studied calligraphy from Wang, Zhong, Yan, and Liu as his elders instructed.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他听从长辈要求，学王、学锺，学颜、柳。

D13a.en: At 17, he obeyed his father's order and continued studying at the academy.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他遵父命，仍就读书院。

M1.en: At 13, he returned to his ancestral home Shexian with his father to take the child examination and ranked among the top.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他随父回原籍歙县应童子试，名列前茅。

D15.en: At 15, he learned martial arts from his clan, enjoying sword fighting and horse riding.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他跟族人学拳术，喜欢击剑、骑马。

D4.en: At 11, he secretly took out his father's seal collection and spent a month carving over ten seals in Deng Shiru's style.
D4.zh: 11岁时，他趁父亲外出，偷偷取出印谱，花一个月临刻了邓石如的篆印十多方。

D7.en: At 7, he and his brother detained a senior painter to beg for guidance, showing a copied treatise.
D7.zh: 7岁时，他和弟弟苦留倪先生，拿出抄来的《逸甫论画》求教。

D2.en: At 12, he borrowed a landscape album and artist biographies from Qi Yin, studying them day and night.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他从祁寅家借来山水画册和画家生平图书，日夜钻研。

D3.en: He studied tradition by browsing, close reading, and copying, comparing works across eras and artists.
D3.zh: 他通过浏览、细读和临写三种方法研究传统，并比较不同时代和画家的作品。

D12.en: At 17, after his family's businesses failed, he obeyed his father and continued studying at an academy.
D12.zh: 17岁时，家道中落，他遵父命仍就读书院。

D13b.en: At 10, he married Chen Chunjun by his parents' order.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他奉父母之命和陈春君结婚。

D18.en: As a small child, he gathered firewood to sell for family income.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他上山打柴卖钱贴补家用。

D11.en: At 8, he became fascinated with drawing, copying a fisherman and various subjects.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他对画画产生浓厚兴趣，常撕写字本画钓鱼老头等。

D7.en: At 6, he refused to go see an official, and his mother praised his spirit.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他拒绝去看官，母亲夸他有志气。

D2.en: At 9-11, he reviewed old lessons and practiced calligraphy daily despite busy chores.
D2.zh: 9到11岁间，他忙里偷闲温习旧书，每天临摹大楷字帖。

D3.en: At 8, he climbed a stool to trace a Lei Gong portrait onto thin paper, achieving an exact copy.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他搬高凳取下雷公像，用薄纸勾影，画得一模一样。

D12.en: At 8, after recovering from illness, he stopped schooling to help with household chores due to poverty.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他病好后因家贫辍学，留家帮忙做事。

D10.en: At 16, he screamed, red-faced, cursing his godfather with gutter talk from Hoboken.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他尖叫着、脸通红、青筋暴起，用霍博肯街头脏话骂他的教父。

D11.en: At 16, he got it into his head that he would prefer to be a sportswriter.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他执意认为自己更想当体育记者。

D7.en: He got arrested for shooting out streetlamps with a BB gun.
D7.zh: 他因用BB枪打坏路灯被捕。

D2.en: Week in and week out, he did the vocal exercise 'Let us wander by the bay' in all twelve keys.
D2.zh: 他每周都做声乐练习，用所有十二个调唱“让我们在海湾漫步”。

D3.en: He learned to look at the lyric on paper and think about why the writer chose those words.
D3.zh: 他学会看着纸上的歌词，思考作者为什么选那些词。

D13b.en: He was named after his godfather, Frank Garrick.
D13b.zh: 他随教父的名字取名Frank Garrick。

M5.en: She took to heart that Kambanis made more of a hit than she did.
M5.zh: 她心里很在意Kambanis比她更受欢迎。

D8.en: She jumped at him and scratched his face with both hands.
D8.zh: 她扑向他，用双手抓他的脸。

M6.en: She saw her musical talent as a destiny and a duty, not just a means to earn a living.
M6.zh: 她认为自己的音乐天赋不仅是谋生和证明自身价值的手段，更是一种使命和责任。

M2.en: Her mother coerced her to work due to money shortage.
M2.zh: 她母亲因缺钱强迫她出去工作。

D20.en: She registered as an American citizen at the U.S. consulate general.
D20.zh: 她在美国总领事馆注册为美国公民。

M4.en: She must have seen the advantages of maintaining a good relationship with the Casa d'Italia.
M4.zh: 她一定看到了与Casa d'Italia保持良好关系的好处。

D16.en: She would pick up her part and devour it and almost immediately sing it note for note.
D16.zh: 她会拿起自己的部分，迅速消化，然后几乎立刻一个音符不差地唱出来。

D10.en: She whirled a cat by its tail and flung it out over the fire escape.
D10.zh: 她抓住猫的尾巴甩了几圈，然后把它扔出防火梯。

D14.en: At 15, she gave her first performance of Cavalleria Rusticana at the Olympia Theater on 19 March 1939.
D14.zh: 15岁时，她于1939年3月19日在奥林匹亚剧院首次演出《乡村骑士》。

D13b.en: At 16, she had to redo all compulsory subjects, which annoyed her.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，她不得不重学所有必修科目，这让她很恼火。

D13a.en: At 15, she stayed only a year at the National Conservatory, then went to the Athens Conservatory with de Hidalgo.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，她在国家音乐学院只待了一年，之后随德·伊达尔戈去了雅典音乐学院。

D18.en: At 17-18, the money she earned was the only income for her household.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，她挣的钱是家里唯一的收入。

D11.en: She was given a chance to escape to the worlds of her romantic imagination.
D11.zh: 她有机会逃入浪漫想象的世界。

M1.en: At 16, she came second out of thirteen candidates with marks of 1-2 and 1.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她在13名考生中排名第二，成绩为1-2和1。

D9.en: She sang in at least ten student concerts and one live radio broadcast.
D9.zh: 她至少参加了十场学生音乐会和一次现场广播演唱。

D2.en: She was in the advanced class of de Hidalgo at the school of singing, as per examination records.
D2.zh: 根据考试记录，她在德·伊达尔戈的高级声乐班学习。

D3.en: She read the notes by bringing the music very close to her face while holding her glasses.
D3.zh: 她拿着眼镜，把乐谱凑到脸前看音符。

D12.en: At thirteen, she left New York with her mother and sister and traveled to Greece.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她随母亲和姐姐离开纽约前往希腊。

D7.en: Alexander went out of his way to upset and irritate her.
D7.zh: 亚历山大故意惹她生气，刺激她。

D19.en: At 18, he read the Shixue Hanying to learn poetry rules.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他读《诗学涵英》学习写诗规则。

M7.en: He hung the national flag at the entrance of Huangang Temple and inside Baimei Garden.
M7.zh: 他在环巩庵门口和百梅园内升挂了国旗。

D17.en: At 18, he felt sympathy and often pleaded for mercy for someone.
D17.zh: 18岁时，他顿生同情，常为之求情讨饶。

M2.en: As a child and young man, he said Dafengtang was his family's hall name, not just his studio.
M2.zh: 他说大风堂是张家的堂名，而非他的画室名。

D20.en: He often collaborated with a close friend in Beiping, dining and traveling together daily.
D20.zh: 他在北平时常跟一位密友合作，日夕游宴。

D10.en: At 17, he felt sad hearing that a man he knew was loyal and not a bad person.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他听了很难过，觉得那人讲义气，不是坏蛋。

D1.en: At 17, he begged a man to intercede for releasing Fan Tianyou.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他求人出面讲情，要求放樊天佑回去。

D13b.en: At 17, he was forced to become a bandit's scribe, nicknamed 'Black Pen Master'.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他被逼上梁山，当了土匪的黑笔师爷。

D13a.en: At 16, he obeyed his father and elder brother's order to leave Neijiang and attend junior high in Chongqing.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他奉父兄之命离内江，到重庆求精中学读初中。

D5.en: At 18, he thought everyone, being poor students, had nothing to fear from bandits and decided to risk going home.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他认为大家都是穷学生，不怕土匪抢，决定冒险回家。

M1.en: At 16, he performed well in school, often praised by teachers for calligraphy and Chinese.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他学习成绩颇佳，写字和国文常得老师夸奖。

D15.en: At 16, he often swam across the Jialing River with classmates in hot weather.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他天热时常与同学结伴横游嘉陵江。

D9.en: He wrote a letter asking for help, saying it would actually save his own life.
D9.zh: 他写了一封家信求援，说这实际上也是救他的命。

D7.en: At 18, he refused and insisted on going home to continue studying.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝并坚持回家继续念书。

D6.en: Zhang Daqian had various friends who provided him with various kinds of help or services.
D6.zh: 张大千有各式各样的朋友，为他提供各式各样的帮助或服务

D2.en: Before age 16-17, he learned from his mother and sister, laying his painting foundation.
D2.zh: 十六七岁前，他受母亲和姐姐教导，奠定绘画基础。

D3.en: At 14, he had no interest in math and still liked painting the most.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他对数学没兴趣，最喜欢绘画。

M5.en: At 17, he boasted that the Kang family would not let bandits kill him to save face.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他夸口说康家不会让土匪砍他的头，因为要顾面子。

D20.en: At 17, he co-founded Shanghai Academy of Fine Arts and served as its principal.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他与乌始光等创办上海图画美术院，任校长。

D14.en: At 10, he wrote a large couplet in public at a school performance.
D14.zh: 10岁时，他在游艺会上当众写大字对联。

D18.en: At 15, with his uncle and cousin's support, he opened a drawing school in Changzhou, teaching clan siblings.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他在姑父和表兄支持下，在常州开办图画传习所，来学者皆族中兄妹。

D4.en: At 14, he copied a road with trees and houses, drawing it farther than his teacher, earning praise.
D4.zh: 14岁时，他临摹一条马路，画得比老师更远，受周湘夸奖。

D9.en: At 16, he proposed writing a school founding declaration to explain their mission.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他提议写办学宣言，让社会各界了解办学宗旨。

D2.en: At 13, he bound his school essays into a volume titled 'Liu Pan Ce Lun'.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他将书院所作策论装订成册，自题《刘槃策论》。

D3.en: At 9, reading the Analects, he found contradictions and questioned his teacher.
D3.zh: 9岁时，他读《论语》，感到矛盾，便问塾师。

D12.en: At 17, he defended his school as his own creation and declared he would donate it to the state.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他称上海美专是自己艰苦缔造的，并说早想无偿捐献给国家。

D13a.en: At 15, he blew out the candle and left to ask his father to break his unhappy shackles.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他吹灭红烛，拉开门走出去，要请父亲帮他解开不幸的枷锁。

M5.en: At 15, he and a friend wanked together as an act of rebellion and mutual showing off.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他和朋友一起手淫，作为反叛和互相炫耀的行为。

M8.en: At 14, he wrote his first completed song, 'I Lost My Little Girl,' partly as a diversion from his mother's death.
M8.zh: 14岁时，他写完了第一首歌《I Lost My Little Girl》，部分是为了从母亲去世的创伤中转移注意力。

D17.en: At 8-12, he cycled daily to buy hake for his pets.
D17.zh: 8到12岁时，他每天骑车去给宠物买鳕鱼。

D20.en: At 16, he was the undisputed leader because he knew one more chord than the rest.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他是无可争议的领袖，因为他比别人多知道一个和弦。

M4.en: At 17, he felt his poor sight no longer marked him as a nerd, thanks to Buddy on the charts.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他觉得自己的差视力不再让他显得像个书呆子，因为Buddy上了排行榜。

D16.en: At 18, he rounded on Thelma in front of several students and was crushingly rude to her.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他在几个学生面前斥责Thelma，对她极其粗鲁。

D10.en: At 17, he lay with his head in her lap all day, too numb to speak or move.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他整天把头枕在她腿上，麻木得说不出话也动不了。

D1.en: At 18, he and his trio approached promoters for work as a nonpercussive group.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他和他的三人乐队去找演出商，想作为一个无打击乐的组合演出。

D14.en: At 16, he gave the Quarry Men's first public performance at a street carnival in Liverpool.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在利物浦街头嘉年华上做了Quarry Men的首次公开演出。

D13b.en: At 16, he left Quarry Bank for art college.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他离开Quarry Bank去了艺术学院。

D5.en: At 15-16, he rarely fought, mostly backing off when fists were near.
D5.zh: 15到16岁时，他很少真打架，快动手时多半就退了。

D11.en: At 16-17, he wrote 'One After 909' during songwriting sessions.
D11.zh: 16到17岁时，他在写歌会上写了《One After 909》。

M1.en: At 16, he entered several skiffle contests with The Quarry Men but failed to win or place.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他随采石工人乐队参加了几次斯基弗尔比赛，但未能获胜或获得名次。

D15.en: At 16-17, he and other musicians took off shoes and climbed into an empty tub.
D15.zh: 16到17岁时，他和几个乐手脱了鞋爬进空浴缸里。

D9.en: He cycled over Allerton golf course and told Paul McCartney he wanted him in the group.
D9.zh: 他骑车穿过Allerton高尔夫球场，告诉Paul McCartney他想让他加入乐队。

D2.en: At 10-15, he was good at English, art, and French, awful at arithmetic, and made little effort.
D2.zh: 10到15岁时，他擅长英语、美术和法语，算术很差，而且不怎么用功。

D12.en: At 4, he was handed over to the care of his Lennon relatives for the one and only time.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他唯一一次被交给列侬家的亲戚照顾。

D6.en: At 15-16, Rod Davis became a firm friend of his and the banjoist in his first musical group.
D6.zh: 15到16岁时，罗德·戴维斯成了他的密友，也是他第一个乐队的班卓琴手。

D20X.en: He placed all his paintings before his children, let them choose one as a memento, and donated the rest.
D20X.zh: 他把自己的画作全部放在孩子面前，让他们选一幅作纪念，其余全部捐赠。

M3.en: He regarded Lu Xun as his spiritual pillar.
M3.zh: 他把鲁迅当作精神支柱。

M6.en: He decided to go his own way, to paint oils that the Chinese people would love, and to blaze that trail with his own feet.
M6.zh: 他下决心走自己的路，要画出中国人民喜爱的油画来，靠自己的脚去踩出这样一条路。

M2.en: At 17, he worried about bringing pain to his family with his future.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他担心自己的前途会给家人带来痛苦。

M4.en: He encountered the Hangzhou Art Academy and fell madly in love with fine arts.
M4.zh: 他接触到了杭州艺专，疯狂地爱上了美术。

D1.en: He passed the exam, but unexpectedly fate would lead him into a bewildering starry sky.
D1.zh: 他考上了，却不意将被命运之神引入迷茫的星空。

D13a.en: At about 11, he no longer wanted to be a village teacher and aimed to enter Wuxi Normal School.
D13a.zh: 大约11岁时，他不愿在农村当初小教员，想进省立无锡师范。

D11.en: He was very happy and often fantasized about meeting an immortal who would teach him magic.
D11.zh: 他高兴极了，也常常幻想能遇到神仙指点法术。

D15.en: As a small child, he learned a crude dog paddle in a calm creek, managing only 4-5 meters.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他在农村学过一点土法游泳，被讥为狗爬水，只能在平静的小河里爬四五公尺。

D4.en: At age 10-11, he conspired with classmates to steal ginkgo nuts during a stormy night.
D4.zh: 10到11岁时，他串通同学趁狂风暴雨之夜偷白果。

D9.en: At age 10-11, he colluded with classmates to steal ginkgo nuts.
D9.zh: 10到11岁时，他串通同学偷白果。

D7.en: At 17, he defied his father's opposition to becoming a painter, driven by beauty's allure.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他违背父亲反对，被美诱惑而坚持学画。

D6.en: At 17-18, he worked night shifts with a friend.
D6.zh: 17到18岁时，他和德群总加夜班。

D2.en: At 11-13, he boarded at school, with his family selling grain and pigs to afford fees.
D2.zh: 11到13岁时，他住校，家里粜稻卖猪凑学费。

D3.en: As a small child, he watched his teacher draw, using a paper roll as charcoal and licking ink brushes.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他常看缪老师用纸卷当炭条作画，舔笔染黑嘴唇。

D12.en: At 12, he understood his father's frugality and refused to pay for a better room after being bitten.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他深知父亲挣钱艰难，被咬后也不肯加钱换房。

D4.en: At age 7-8, he started learning to draw by copying Jieziyuan Huapu and helping his grandfather carve patterns on stone tablets.
D4.zh: 七八岁时，他照着《芥子园画谱》学画，还帮祖父磨凿子、递榔头，看祖父在石碑上刻图案。

D9.en: At age 15-18, he co-founded the Tanli Poetry Society with classmates and served as vice president.
D9.zh: 十五到十八岁时，他与同学组织“探骊诗社”，任副社长。

D2.en: At age 7-8, he skillfully copied Jieziyuan Huapu, a book his father used for learning painting.
D2.zh: 七八岁时，他用巧手描摹《芥子园画谱》，那是他父亲学画时用过的书。

D3.en: At age 15-18, his favorite after-school reading was Zhao Ming Wen Xuan.
D3.zh: 十五到十八岁时，他课余最喜欢读《昭明文选》。

D12.en: He was born in 1900 into a poor peasant family in Meixian, Guangdong; his grandfather and father were stonemasons.
D12.zh: 他1900年生于广东梅县一个贫苦农民家庭，祖父是石匠，父亲也做石匠。

M4.en: At 17, he joined patriotic students in anti-imperialist demonstrations and fundraising on the streets.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他和其他爱国学生一起上街举行反帝示威游行和募捐活动。

D7.en: At 18, he declared, 'Even if I go hungry, I will still learn to paint!'
D7.zh: 18岁时，他说：‘就是饿肚皮，我也要学画！’

D6.en: At 16-18, he joined the school band and performed in cities like Changzhou and Wuxi.
D6.zh: 16到18岁间，他参加了学校乐队，到过常州、无锡等城市公演。

D2.en: At 16-18, he woke an hour early daily for two years to practice calligraphy.
D2.zh: 16到18岁间，他每天早起一小时，临家庙碑、写颜楷大字，持续了两年多。

D3.en: As a small child, his grandfather's paint box sparked his interest in painting.
D3.zh: 幼年时，祖父的大画箱触发了他对绘画艺术的兴趣。

D12.en: As a small child, his grandmother and mother pasted matchboxes for meager piecework wages.
D12.zh: 幼年时，祖母和母亲糊火柴盒以换得微薄的计件工资。

D19.en: He treasured this oil painting and preserved it intact for decades.
D19.zh: 他十分珍惜这件油画，将它完好地保存了几十年。

D18.en: He made charcoal portraits for a friend's relative, earning two or three yuan each time to barely get by.
D18.zh: 他为一位同乡画炭像，得到满意后又被介绍给别人，半年多里靠画炭像挣两三块钱度日。

D4.en: He repaired a photo studio's backdrop and then painted a new one using soot and glue.
D4.zh: 他修好了照相馆的布景，又用烟子调胶画了一幅新布景，画面很有意境。

D12.en: At 12-13, after his mother died and his father fell ill, he faced the need to support himself.
D12.zh: 十二三岁时，母逝父病，他面临自立谋生的选择。

D5.en: He dodged a knife attack and pinned the attacker's hand against a pole until he dropped the knife.
D5.zh: 他躲开扒皮的刀，把对方的手按在电线杆上磨得鲜血直流，刀掉在地上。

D17.en: As a teenager, he ran while his mother did laundry by the river.
D17.zh: 十几岁时，他跑步，妈妈在河边洗衣服。

D20.en: At 16, he was selected for the city propaganda team, basically like a theater troupe.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他入选了市宣传队，基本上像一个剧团。

D10.en: Later, seeing the Boston Symphony Orchestra perform in China surprised and hurt him.
D10.zh: 后来见到波士顿交响乐团到中国演奏，让他很惊讶也很受伤。

D11.en: He felt everything he was interested in as a youth converged to serve his becoming an artist.
D11.zh: 他年少时感兴趣的东西最终都汇流成力量，为他成为艺术家服务。

D15.en: He was a young man who practiced martial arts and loved painting.
D15.zh: 他是一个好练武术、爱画画的青年。

D6.en: At 16, he was selected for the city propaganda team and thus avoided being sent to the countryside.
D6.zh: 16岁时他入选了市宣传队，没有上山下乡。

D3.en: He played the violin to understand the West through that window.
D3.zh: 他拉琴就是想透过这扇窗子去理解西方。

D14.en: He made martial arts films and also played the violin.
D14.zh: 他拍过武打片，也拉过小提琴。

D13a.en: His violin teacher gave him an ultimatum: choose between martial arts and violin.
D13a.zh: 他的提琴老师给他下最后通牒，要他在武术和小提琴中选一个。

D18.en: At 7, he went herding cattle with a farmhand for nearly ten days.
D18.zh: 7岁时，他跟帮工去放牛快十天了

D5.en: As a teenager, he loved hiking alone and explored dozens of hills near his hometown.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢独自登山，家乡几十个山头差不多全让他踏遍了

M1.en: At 18, he wrote passionately about his concerns for the nation and his ambition to revive Chinese painting.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他奋笔疾书，写下忧国忧民的真情实感和振兴民族绘画的抱负

D15.en: At 18, he walked over 600 li to Hangzhou, taking a boat or train for less than a third of the way.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他步行六百多里到杭州，乘车搭船的不到三分之一

D4.en: At 14-15, he made clay seals by kneading fine clay, drying, carving, and firing them.
D4.zh: 14到15岁时，他找来上好粘土，做成印坯，晒干刻成印章，再煅烧坚实

D7.en: At 11-12, when punished for drawing, he never cried, just rubbed his swollen palm.
D7.zh: 11到12岁时，因画画被打手心，他从不落泪，只用另一只手揉着

D2.en: As a teenager, he stayed up every night writing, painting, and carving until the lamp oil ran out.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他每晚在油灯下写字作诗、画画刻章，不点完一灯油不上床。

D3.en: At 11-12, he drew figures and flowers on paper, copied novel illustrations, and memorized temple decorations.
D3.zh: 11到12岁时，他在纸片上画小人花鸟，临摹小说插图，心记祠庙门窗上的山水人物

D12.en: At 18, his father told him to quit school and farm.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他父亲让他别读书了，回家种田。

D17.en: At 17-18, he extended a helping hand and covered all expenses for two people without hesitation.
D17.zh: 17到18岁时，他主动伸出援手，二话没说就承担起两个人的全部费用。

D1.en: He stayed at school hoping to continue his studies.
D1.zh: 他没有走，希望留在学校能有机会继续学业。

D13a.en: He nervously showed his father the expulsion notice.
D13a.zh: 他忐忑不安地拿出学校的开除通知给父亲。

D18.en: As a child, he ate melons for free and spat out seeds for the seller.
D18.zh: 他小时候白吃瓜，把瓜子吐出来给卖瓜人。

D5.en: He resolutely chose the hardest school with the best faculty.
D5.zh: 他毅然选择最难考、师资最强的学校。

D11.en: He found Wang Xizhi's cursive script copybook and eagerly copied it.
D11.zh: 他在父亲书房找到王羲之的《草诀歌》帖，如饥似渴地临摹。

D15.en: He was obsessed with basketball, playing even by moonlight until silence.
D15.zh: 他迷上篮球，晚上有月亮也继续打，直打到万籁寂静。

D4.en: He used his father's money to buy a large box of paint instead of food or daily necessities while fleeing.
D4.zh: 他把父亲给的钱买了一大箱颜料，而不是食物或日用品，跟着流亡。

D9.en: His PE teacher urged him to apply to a sports school in Shanghai.
D9.zh: 体育老师动员他报考上海的体育专科学校。

D7.en: He signed a statement drafted by senior students criticizing the principal.
D7.zh: 他签署了学兄们起草的批评校长的声明。

D6.en: He and a friend often went to a tavern to drink huadiao wine together.
D6.zh: 他和朋友经常结伴去小酒馆喝花雕酒。

D3.en: He eagerly took the calligraphy manual back to his room and started practicing.
D3.zh: 他如获至宝地把《草字诀》抱回房间，迫不及待地研墨展纸练习。

D13b.en: He came to the United States in 1935.
D13b.zh: 他于1935年来到美国。

D11.en: As a small child, he believed he could hear bamboo growing in nearby groves.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他相信自己能听到附近竹林中竹子生长的声音。

M1.en: He was one of the better students.
M1.zh: 他是成绩较好的学生之一。

D15.en: He and friends played enough billiards in local halls to become accomplished players.
D15.zh: 他和朋友们在本地台球厅打了很多台球，成了高手。

D6.en: He was helped by many Eastern students in a foreign student club at university.
D6.zh: 大学里有一个留学生社团，其中很多东方学生对他多少有些帮助。

D2.en: He studied at the University of Pennsylvania, MIT, and Harvard.
D2.zh: 他在宾夕法尼亚大学、麻省理工学院和哈佛大学学习。

D3.en: As a child, he studied with a tutor in the Lion Forest with family members.
D3.zh: 他小时候和家人在狮子林跟老师学习。

D12.en: He was born in pre-Communist China.
D12.zh: 他出生在共产主义之前的中国。

D13a.en: He agreed to take Oxford exams only if he could then choose where to study.
D13a.zh: 他同意参加牛津考试，但前提是之后能自己选择学习地点。

D4.en: At 13, he was responsible for preparing his mother's opium pipe.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他负责给母亲准备鸦片烟枪。

D1.en: After that, he never dared to be careless again.
D1.zh: 从那以后，他再也不敢马马虎虎了。

D13a.en: He remembers at age 5-6, after pooping, he would stick out his butt and insist on waiting for his mother to wipe him.
D13a.zh: 他记得自己五六岁时，拉完屎还撅着屁股非等娘来擦不可。

D15.en: During summer vacations, his family went to the seaside to swim; a retired naval officer taught the children underwater swimming.
D15.zh: 暑假时全家去海滨游泳，一位退休海军军官教孩子们潜泳。

D4.en: He said to someone: 'One day I'll install an electric bell for you.'
D4.zh: 他对人说：赶明儿我给您装个电铃。

D2.en: After that, he never dared to be careless again.
D2.zh: 从那以后他再也不敢马马虎虎了。

D3.en: At 14-17, he loved music, learned piano and violin, and practiced diligently with enjoyment.
D3.zh: 14到17岁时，他酷爱音乐，学钢琴和小提琴，孜孜不倦地练习并引以为乐。

D9.en: At 18, he served as captain and played first trumpet and piccolo.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他担任队长，吹第一小号，也擅长短笛。

D13a.en: At 15, he resented his mother for not letting him go to Chengdu and was even angrier about the arranged marriage.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他因不能去成都而对母亲不满，提起成亲更加恼火。

D11.en: At 6-10, he found strange antiques and paintings fascinating, secretly playing with them and doodling with a brush.
D11.zh: 6到10岁时，他觉得古怪的古董和书画很有趣，偷偷玩古董，还用毛笔乱涂乱画。

D4.en: At 6-10, he often roamed the hills with a cowherd and once fell from a bamboo pole, losing consciousness.
D4.zh: 6到10岁时，他常跟放牛的长工满山跑，一次从竹竿上摔下来，不省人事。

D9.en: At 15, he gathered all the neighborhood boys, formed sworn brotherhoods like adults, and invented games.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他把全街少年拢在一起，学大人拜把子结弟兄，变着法子玩耍。

D7.en: At 6-10, after being scolded by his teacher, he hid behind the bed curtain and poked the teacher's back with a stick at night.
D7.zh: 6到10岁时，因被教书先生责备，他夜里躲在帐子后面，用木棍戳先生的脊背。

D2.en: At 11-15, his uncle taught him classical texts and poetry, and he learned to write poems and essays.
D2.zh: 11到15岁时，傅大舅给他讲《古文观止》等，还教他作诗写文章。

D20X.en: She neither competed with nor deferred to Rivera.
D20X.zh: 她既不与Rivera竞争，也不屈从他。

M5.en: She flaunted her alegría like a peacock's tail.
M5.zh: 她像孔雀开屏一样炫耀自己的快乐。

D8.en: At 15, she stiffened and returned Lupe's stare without a word.
D8.zh: 15岁时，她僵住并一言不发地回瞪Lupe。

M3.en: At 10, she said 'Frida is the most intelligent'.
M3.zh: 10岁时，她说‘Frida是最聪明的’。

M6.en: She said 'I paint because I need to'.
M6.zh: 她说‘我画画是因为我需要’。

M2.en: She chose her birth date as 1910, the year of the Mexican Revolution.
M2.zh: 她将自己的出生年份选为1910年，即墨西哥革命爆发之年。

D20.en: At 12, she was told 'Idealists, persist'.
D20.zh: 12岁时，她听到别人说‘理想主义者，坚持’。

M4.en: At 15-18, she believed the poor were oppressed by the wealthy.
M4.zh: 15到18岁时，她相信穷人受富人压迫。

D16.en: As a teenager, she listened to earnest young orators arguing for student rights.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，她聆听热忱的青年演说家为学生权利辩论。

D10.en: At 18, she said: 'I felt a horrible pain in my waist and an anguish more terrible than you can imagine'.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她说：‘我感到腰部剧痛和难以想象的痛苦。’

D14.en: At 15-16, she said: 'My ambition is to have a child by Diego Rivera. And I'm going to tell him so someday.'
D14.zh: 15到16岁时，她说：‘我的志向是和迭戈·里维拉生个孩子。总有一天我会告诉他。’

D18.en: At 15, she won sweets from peddlers by coin toss and never lost.
D18.zh: 15岁时，她用小贩的硬币投掷赢糖果，从未输过。

D5.en: She soaped the stairway descending from the amphitheater stage where he was painting.
D5.zh: 她在他画画的露天剧场舞台下方的楼梯上涂了肥皂。

D11.en: At about 17, she developed a personal emblem.
D11.zh: 大约17岁时，她设计了一个个人标志。

M1.en: At 15, she began attending an elite preparatory school.
M1.zh: 15岁时，她开始上一所精英预科学校。

D15.en: She had a smashed pelvis that led to miscarriages and at least three therapeutic abortions.
D15.zh: 她的骨盆碎裂，导致多次流产和至少三次治疗性堕胎。

D4.en: Her mother rigged up an easel so she could paint while lying in bed.
D4.zh: 她母亲搭了一个画架，让她能躺在床上画画。

D2.en: As a small child, she learned to sew, embroider, cook, and clean.
D2.zh: 幼年时，她学会了缝纫、刺绣、做饭和打扫。

D3.en: At 15, she read constantly, from Dumas to Mariano Azuela, the Bible to Zozobra.
D3.zh: 15岁时，她不断阅读，从大仲马到Mariano Azuela，从圣经到Zozobra。

D13a.en: At 16, she said: 'This morning I took communion and prayed to God for all of you.'
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她说：‘今早我领了圣餐，为大家祈祷。’

D6.en: She said, 'I want you to come, that is to say you and the boys.'
D6.zh: 她说：“我希望你来，就是说你和那些男生。”

M4.en: At 17, he said he was more Communist than ever.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他说他比以往任何时候都更共产主义。

D10.en: At 12-18, he threw himself from a first-floor window to avoid something, almost killing himself.
D10.zh: 12到18岁间，他从一楼窗户跳下以躲避某事，差点摔死。

D14.en: At 15, he always carried a cane topped with a golden double-headed eagle.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他总带着一根顶端有金色双头鹰的手杖。

D13b.en: He said he could not understand why it was really necessary.
D13b.zh: 他说他实在不明白这有什么必要。

D5.en: As a teenager, he hurled himself off staircases from a considerable height while peers watched.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他在同伴注视下从相当高的楼梯上跳下。

D11.en: At 16, he stared at scratches on bare walls and composed allegorical pictures with his left hand.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他盯着光墙上的划痕，用左手想象性地构图寓言画。

M1.en: At 13, he received a Special Merit certificate for his performance at the Municipal Drawing School.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他因在市立绘画学校的表现获得特别荣誉证书。

D15.en: At 12, he spent much time on top of a squat tower at the Pichots' home.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他大部分时间待在Pichot家矮塔的顶上。

D4.en: At 17, he let his hair grow long and had sideburns.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他留了长发和鬓角。

D9.en: As a small child, Dalí's home hosted lively discussions by cultural leaders.
D9.zh: 幼年时，达利家中常有文化领袖热烈讨论。

D7.en: At 17, he said 'Spain is shit, both the Government and the people.'
D7.zh: 17岁时，他说‘西班牙是狗屎，政府和人民都是。’

D6.en: At 18, he accompanied Buñuel on forays through nocturnal Madrid.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他陪布努埃尔在夜间的马德里游荡。

D3.en: At 15, he recorded in his diary the death of Renoir, calling him one of the best French Impressionists.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他在日记中记录了雷诺阿的去世，称他无疑是最好的法国印象派画家之一。

D12.en: As a small child, he was baptized Salvador Felipe Jacinto in the parish church of Saint Peter.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他在圣彼得教区教堂受洗，取名Salvador Felipe Jacinto。

D18.en: At age 4-6, he tried to buy her from her mother with his piggy-bank savings.
D18.zh: 4到6岁时，他试图用存钱罐里的钱把她从她母亲那里买来。

D20X.en: He was solitary and entirely possessed by his art.
D20X.zh: 他独自一人，完全沉浸在自己的艺术中。

M5.en: He proclaimed himself the peer of celebrated figures by doing their portraits.
M5.zh: 他通过为知名人物画像来宣称自己是他们的同辈。

M8.en: He said, 'I could have stayed there forever drawing without a stop.'
M8.zh: 他说：‘我可以永远待在那里不停地画。’

D8.en: He accepted Sabartés' adulation as his due and caricatured him.
D8.zh: 他接受Sabartés的崇拜视为理所当然，并把他画成讽刺画。

M3.en: At 17-18, he wanted to conquer life like Achilles.
M3.zh: 17到18岁时，他想要像阿喀琉斯一样征服生活。

M7.en: He saw fulfilling dreams as a point of family honor and a debt for financial sacrifices.
M7.zh: 他认为实现梦想是家族荣誉，是对经济牺牲的回报。

M6.en: He felt that to conquer life, one must look death in the face and dominate it through painting.
M6.zh: 他感到要征服生活，就必须直面死亡并通过绘画支配它。

M2.en: He signed his works with variations of his name: P. Ruiz Picasso, P. R. Picasso, Picasso.
M2.zh: 他在作品上签名时使用名字的变体：P. Ruiz Picasso、P. R. Picasso、Picasso。

D20.en: At 16, he described himself on official forms as a pupil of Don Antonio Muñoz Degrain, not his father.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他在官方表格上称自己是Don Antonio Muñoz Degrain的学生，而非他父亲。

D10.en: At 14, he displayed a phobic reaction to illness that lasted his whole life.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他对疾病表现出恐惧反应，这种反应持续了他一生。

D13b.en: As a small child, his parents sent him to the more up-to-date Colegio de San Rafael after he recovered.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他病愈后父母送他去了更现代的Colegio de San Rafael。

D13a.en: At 18, he did not reenroll at La Lonja but produced academically perfect drawings.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他没有在La Lonja重新注册，但创作了学术上完美的素描。

D18.en: At 18, he tried doing illustrations for magazines to finance his Paris trip.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他尝试为杂志画插图以资助巴黎之行。

D5.en: At 17-18, he left home for a few weeks and moved into the brothel where Rosita del Oro worked.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他离家几周，搬进了Rosita del Oro工作的妓院。

D11.en: At 17-18, he drew an amorous couple with the encounter of faces left blank.
D11.zh: 17到18岁时，他画了一对情侣，脸部相遇处留白。

M1.en: At 18, he believed he could do better than Casas, having had the run of his studio.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他相信自己能比Casas做得更好，因为他曾自由使用Casas的画室。

D15.en: At 13-14, he courted a girl with considerable ardor and some success.
D15.zh: 13到14岁时，他热烈追求一个女孩并取得了一些成功。

D4.en: At 16, he was well enough to sit up in bed and sketch the floor of his room.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他身体好转到能坐起来画房间的地板。

D2.en: At 7-9, he agreed to go to school only on condition that he could draw as much as he liked.
D2.zh: 7到9岁时，他同意去上学，条件是到了学校可以想画多少就画多少。

D3.en: At 16, he declared he wouldn't follow any particular school.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他宣称自己不追随任何特定流派。

M2.en: At 11, his father had good sense and his mother irrationality.
M2.zh: 11岁时，他父亲明智而母亲不理性。

M4.en: At age 11, he abandoned his early Catholicism and adopted the conventional atheism of his peers.
M4.zh: 11岁时，他放弃了早期的天主教信仰，接受了同龄人常见的无神论。

D13b.en: At age 11, he entered San Carlos as a full-time pupil.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他进入圣卡洛斯学院成为全日制学生。

D13a.en: At 11, after a short trial, he was allowed to leave military school and attend San Carlos full-time.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，经过短暂尝试，他被允许离开军校，全职就读圣卡洛斯学院。

D11.en: At age 18, he drew a little treescape sketch on the back of something, a small gesture of independence.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他在某物背面画了一幅小树景素描，作为一个小小的独立姿态。

M1.en: At 18, he graduated from San Carlos and won a bursary to study in Europe.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他从圣卡洛斯毕业，获得奖学金去欧洲学习。

D2.en: At 11, he started attending the Academy of San Carlos art school.
D2.zh: 11岁时，他开始就读于圣卡洛斯国立艺术学院。

D4.en: At 6, he seized a priest's candlestick and drove him out of the church.
D4.zh: 6岁时，他抓起神父的烛台，把他赶出了教堂。

M7.en: At 16-18, he took political stances and fought as a young rebel.
M7.zh: 16到18岁时，他采取政治立场，像年轻叛逆者一样战斗。

M4.en: He mainly intellectualized his anger about injuries as political dissent.
M4.zh: 他主要将自己对伤害的愤怒理性化为政治异议。

D10.en: At 17, he felt emptiness and despair, making him vulnerable to depression.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他感到空虚和绝望，这使他容易抑郁。

D13b.en: At 18, as a scholarship recipient, he was denied the right to live in a dormitory.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，作为奖学金获得者，他被剥夺了住校的权利。

D11.en: He kept a notebook of poems, a story, and a play in Hebrew.
D11.zh: 他保留了一个笔记本，用希伯来语写了一些诗、一个故事和一个剧本。

M1.en: He went to Yale hoping to become a labor lawyer.
M1.zh: 他进入耶鲁大学，希望成为一名劳工律师。

D15.en: He liked to hike through forested hills and look out across the valley.
D15.zh: 他喜欢徒步穿越森林覆盖的山丘，爬到山顶眺望山谷。

D4.en: He had his first exposure to art.
D4.zh: 他第一次接触艺术。

D7.en: He was singled out for religious education and grew to despise it.
D7.zh: 他被单独挑出来接受宗教教育，并逐渐对此感到厌恶。

D2.en: From age 10 to 18, he was a diligent student in Portland.
D2.zh: 10到18岁间，他在波特兰是个勤奋的学生。

D3.en: As a teenager, he avidly read Greek classics and theater.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他如饥似渴地阅读希腊经典和戏剧。

D12.en: At 12-15, he took a Dramatic Art course in high school.
D12.zh: 12到15岁时，他在高中选修了戏剧艺术课。

D13a.en: He left Portland with friends to begin study at Yale.
D13a.zh: 他与好友Aaron Director和Max Naimark离开波特兰，前往耶鲁大学学习。

D5.en: He sailed second class on the SS Czar from Libau to Brooklyn.
D5.zh: 他乘坐SS Czar号二等舱从Libau前往布鲁克林。

D8.en: He signed a friend's yearbook 'For more and better arguments.'
D8.zh: 他在朋友的年鉴上签名'为了更多更好的争论。'

M3.en: He idolized his brother Charles and wanted to do what he did.
M3.zh: 他崇拜他的哥哥Charles，想效仿他。

M6.en: At 16, he visited Ojai, read theosophical tracts, and shared findings with his father.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他去了Ojai，读了神智学著作，并与父亲分享心得。

D20.en: He and another prodigy began holding meetings.
D20.zh: 他和另一个天才开始举办会议。

D10.en: At 18, he argued with others about who was doing more work on the two-man saw.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他和别人争论谁在双人锯上干得更多。

D1.en: After weeks of trying to fix a Model T, he and Sande left Riverside.
D1.zh: 他花了几周时间试图修好一辆福特T型车，然后和Sande一起离开了Riverside。

D13b.en: At age 6, his father LeRoy sold the family farm against his better judgment.
D13b.zh: 6岁时，他父亲LeRoy违背本意卖掉了农场。

D18.en: At 18, he thought he might make a little book money.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他觉得自己可能赚点书钱。

D11.en: As a child, he built a rowboat inspired by Huck Finn.
D11.zh: 他小时候受哈克贝利·费恩启发，造了一艘划艇。

D15.en: At 10, he killed an animal on the run.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他在奔跑中杀死了一只动物。

D4.en: He got up a group and arranged a furnace to fire their stuff.
D4.zh: 他组织了一个小组，安排了一个窑炉来烧制他们的东西。

D7.en: At 17, he flouted the school's course requirements and dress code.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他无视学校的课程要求和着装规定。

D6.en: At age 10, Sande wrote him a letter.
D6.zh: 10岁时，Sande给他写了一封信。

D2.en: At 16-18, he first began to draw in Schwankovsky's class.
D2.zh: 16到18岁时，他在Schwankovsky的课上第一次开始画画。

D3.en: As a small child, he waited eagerly in the roadside ditch every day for his car's noise.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他每天急切地等在路边的沟里，听他父亲汽车的声音。

M7.en: At 13-18, he began to write poetry in Russian and hazily considered an artistic path as escape.
M7.zh: 13到18岁间，他开始用俄语写诗，并模糊地考虑艺术道路作为逃离。

M6.en: At 13-18, he hazily considered an artistic path as escape from his parents' constrained lives.
M6.zh: 13到18岁间，他模糊地考虑艺术道路作为逃离父母受限生活的途径。

M2.en: He naturally turned from honoring his parents to sentimentalizing them.
M2.zh: 他自然地从尊敬父母转向了将他们情感化。

M4.en: At 13, he abandoned religion, enjoying munching a stolen apple on Yom Kippur.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他放弃宗教，在赎罪日偷吃苹果享受破戒。

D10.en: From 1903 onward, he suffered prolonged, frequent periods of depression.
D10.zh: 从1903年起，他长期频繁地经历抑郁期。

D1.en: From 13 to 18, he repeated a year and studied five years without graduating.
D1.zh: 13到18岁间，他留级一年，学了五年却没毕业。

D14.en: At about 18, he already had paintings hanging on the walls of his parents' home.
D14.zh: 大约18岁时，他已有画作挂在父母家的墙上。

D13b.en: As a small child, he rose daily at six to go to the prayer house.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他每天六点起床去祈祷堂。

D11.en: He repeatedly repainted a scene that had become commonplace to him.
D11.zh: 他反复重画那个对他来说已经变得平常的场景。

D15.en: He attended cheder from age 3-4 to 13 during long winters.
D15.zh: 他在漫长的冬天里，从三四岁到十三岁去上犹太小学。

D4.en: As a child, he went to the marketplace in a cart with his uncle Neuch.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他坐牛车和舅舅Neuch一起去集市。

D7.en: At 13, he abruptly abandoned religion, never to return.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他突然放弃宗教，再也没有回头。

D6.en: As a child, he spent much of his time on the streets like most poor children.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他像大多数穷孩子一样，大部分时间在街上度过。

D3.en: In childhood, he learned to read Hebrew, but his real first language of culture was Russian.
D3.zh: 童年时，他学会了读希伯来语，但他真正的文化第一语言是俄语。

D12.en: As a child, he almost ceaselessly helped his mother in her activities.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他几乎不停地帮助母亲做各种事情。

D2.en: At 13-14, he began training with forty-pound weights to build muscles like his father.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他开始用四十磅的哑铃锻炼，想练出像父亲那样的肌肉。

D10.en: He was long, skinny, awkward, and grimacing.
D10.zh: 他又高又瘦，姿态笨拙，表情恐惧。

D13b.en: He began formal art training at the Correspondence School of Illustrating.
D13b.zh: 他开始在函授插画学校接受正规艺术训练。

D13a.en: His parents paid the bills and conditioned the choice.
D13a.zh: 他父母付了钱并决定了选择。

D11.en: He made a pen-and-ink sketch of Charles Dana Gibson.
D11.zh: 他画了一幅Charles Dana Gibson的钢笔素描。

M1.en: At 18, he persuaded his parents to let him transfer to a more famous school.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他说服父母让他转学到一所更著名的学校。

D15.en: At about twelve, he shot up to over six feet.
D15.zh: 大约12岁时，他突然长到超过六英尺。

D4.en: He wrote a letter to his mother on July 26, 1900.
D4.zh: 1900年7月26日，他给母亲写了一封信。

D9.en: At 17, he served on the advertising committee.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他在广告委员会任职。

D3.en: At 13, he may have been motivated to try pen and ink by reading illustrated magazines.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他可能因为阅读插图杂志而受到启发尝试使用钢笔和墨水。

D12.en: As a teenager, he used to help out in the store after school.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他放学后常在店里帮忙。

D17.en: He was raised by his sister, according to his son Pierre.
D17.zh: 他由姐姐抚养长大，据他儿子Pierre说。

D10.en: He had regular bouts of illness that put him to bed for up to eight weeks.
D10.zh: 他经常生病，不得不卧床长达八周。

D13a.en: He obediently returned to school for two more years.
D13a.zh: 他顺从地回到学校又读了两年。

D18.en: He taught Amélie to shoot and to think for herself.
D18.zh: 他教阿梅莉射击，也教她独立思考。

D11.en: He planned a theatrical production about the eruption of Vesuvius with Léon.
D11.zh: 他和Léon计划了一出关于维苏威火山爆发的戏剧。

D15.en: At 9-10, he slipped on grease and mud outside her house and got injured.
D15.zh: 9到10岁时，他在她家外面的油脂和泥巴上滑倒受伤。

D4.en: As a small child, he climbed it; as a young man, he painted it.
D4.zh: 幼年时他爬过它，年轻时他画了它。

D9.en: At 17, he proposed spending a year in Paris studying for preliminary law exams.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他提议在巴黎待一年准备初级法律考试。

D12.en: At 18, he was photographed with his mother by his uncle at Ypres.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他和母亲在伊普尔被叔叔拍照。

D14.en: At 15, he directed a play using cut-out cartoon characters as actors.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他用剪下的卡通人物当演员导演了一出戏。

M3.en: He sought to emulate, never to imitate.
M3.zh: 他追求效仿，从不模仿。

D16.en: He said, 'I'd give my entire lifework for one conversation.'
D16.zh: 他说：“我愿意用我全部的作品换一次对话。”

D11.en: At 7, he made his earliest drawings, inspired by fairy tales and illustrated art books.
D11.zh: 7岁时，他画了最早的作品，灵感来自童话和插图艺术书。

M1.en: At 17, his ability sprang into being with such authority that it could brook no admonition or advice.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他的能力突然显现，权威到不容任何告诫或建议。

D4.en: At 4, the cave beneath a rock became his principal hiding place and sanctum.
D4.zh: 4岁时，岩石下的洞穴成了他主要的藏身地和圣地。

D2.en: He attended a boarding school at Schiers, where he had the use of a small studio.
D2.zh: 他在席尔斯上寄宿学校，在那里他有一个小工作室可用。

D3.en: At 16, he was especially impressed by Tintoretto and Giotto's Arena Chapel frescoes.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他特别被丁托列托和乔托的竞技场礼拜堂壁画所打动。

D9.en: He arranged for Giacometti to meet the magistrate in charge of Caroline's case.
D9.zh: 他安排贾科梅蒂去见负责卡罗琳案件的法官。

D8.en: In high school, he pointed at Bill Shaffer and said 'Hee hee hee hee hee hee.'
D8.zh: 高中时，他指着Bill Shaffer说‘嘻嘻嘻嘻嘻嘻’。

D19.en: At age 8, he mailed a dime to Shirley Temple's fan club and received a signed photo, his most cherished possession.
D19.zh: 8岁时，他寄了一角钱给Shirley Temple的粉丝俱乐部，收到一张签名照，成为他最珍爱的藏品。

M2.en: At 13-17, his aim was to fulfill his father's prediction and go to college.
M2.zh: 13到17岁时，他的目标是实现父亲的预言，上大学。

D10.en: At 13-17, he worried a lot and the stress affected his appearance.
D10.zh: 13到17岁时，他非常焦虑，压力影响了他的外貌。

D1.en: He fought out of his background through his art and talent.
D1.zh: 他通过自己的艺术和才华摆脱了出身背景。

D14.en: His drawings won him his first solo exhibition at Tech.
D14.zh: 他的画作为他赢得了在Tech的首次个展。

D13a.en: He clung to his mother's skirts, begging to stay home; she said he was too young.
D13a.zh: 他抓住母亲的裙子，求她让他待在家里；母亲说他太小了。

D11.en: He dreamed of being a movie star in Hollywood.
D11.zh: 他梦想成为好莱坞电影明星。

M1.en: He was awarded the Leisser Prize for best summer work by a sophomore.
M1.zh: 他获得了Leisser奖，表彰大二学生的最佳暑期作品。

D15.en: As a child, he would collapse at the slightest contact during street hockey games.
D15.zh: 他小时候打街头曲棍球时，一碰就倒。

D4.en: He watched cartoons and drew a lot from them.
D4.zh: 他看卡通片，从中获得灵感画了很多画。

D7.en: He was so mischievous they had to tie him to the bedpost when babysitting.
D7.zh: 他太调皮了，保姆只好把他绑在床柱上。

D6.en: He went to Skibo, the hangout for art students at Tech, and held court there.
D6.zh: 他去了Skibo，那是Tech艺术学生的聚集地，并在那里成为焦点。

D3.en: He drew compulsively, constantly, and amazingly, piling drawings all over his room.
D3.zh: 他不停地画，画得惊人，画作堆满了整个房间。

D12.en: He moved into bed with his mother in the dining room while his father slept upstairs.
D12.zh: 他搬到餐厅和母亲同睡，而父亲睡在楼上。

D13b.en: He was christened and confirmed within weeks of birth.
D13b.zh: 他出生后几周内接受了洗礼和坚信礼。

M8.en: At 14, he wrote in his diary on 15 November 1878.
M8.zh: 14岁时，他于1878年11月15日在日记中写道。

D19.en: At 14, he noted in his diary on 15 November 1878.
D19.zh: 14岁时，他在1878年11月15日的日记中写道。

M6.en: He had a vision in Saint-Cloud.
M6.zh: 他在圣克卢看到了一个幻象。

D16.en: He cut in on conversations with ironic, self-mocking comments and concise paradoxes.
D16.zh: 他会突然插话，用讽刺自嘲的评论和简洁的悖论来点明讨论。

D10.en: He persistently tried to come to terms with the memory of his dying mother.
D10.zh: 他不断试图接受母亲临终的记忆。

D1.en: Over the winter and spring he produced many fresh, non-photographic pictures, mostly landscapes.
D1.zh: 在冬春两季，他画了许多清新的画，主要是风景，没有一张是照片式的。

D13b.en: He was brought up to respect his superiors and lead a truly Christian life.
D13b.zh: 他从小被教育要尊重长辈，过真正的基督徒生活。

D18.en: He sent three of his smaller works to an auction house where they sold off a whole lot of paintings.
D18.zh: 他把自己三幅较小的作品送到拍卖行，那里卖掉了一大批画。

M1.en: At 18, he came top of freehand drawing.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在自由手绘中得了第一名。

D4.en: At 18, he finished a picture for his aunts.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他完成了给姑姑们的画。

D9.en: He had a studio on the fourth floor of 'Pultosten', opposite the Storting.
D9.zh: 他的工作室在'Pultosten'大楼四楼，正对着议会大楼。

D6.en: As a small child, he played on the floor with his cousin Ludvig using a wooden train he had made.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他和表弟Ludvig在地板上玩他做的木头火车。

D2.en: At 18, he attended drawing school fairly regularly since the end of August.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他从8月底开始比较规律地去绘画学校。

D3.en: At 17, he started studying art history in the mediocre national collection.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他开始在平庸的国家收藏馆学习艺术史。

D13a.en: At 17, his mother got him out of technical school to develop his artistic potential.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他母亲让他从技术学校退学，以发展他的艺术潜力。

D4.en: At 16, he made his own violin, understanding its principles without instruction.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他自制了一把小提琴，无师自通其原理。

D2.en: At 18, he entered the Academy at Bucharest on scholarship and made a muscular study used as an anatomical model.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他靠奖学金进入布加勒斯特美术学院，创作了一幅肌肉研究作品，后被用作解剖模型。

D12.en: As a boy, he was apprenticed to a carpenter and cabinet-maker.
D12.zh: 他小时候给木匠和家具匠当学徒。

D7.en: At 11, he left his family definitively after running away twice before.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他离家出走，此后再未归家。

M7.en: He spent summers doing arduous labor on the family farm.
M7.zh: 他夏天在家庭农场做艰苦的体力劳动。

D10.en: He slid backward down the attic stair believing himself dying.
D10.zh: 他以为自己快死了，从阁楼楼梯上滑下来。

D1.en: He earned low grades and was a recurrent dropout.
D1.zh: 他成绩差，经常辍学。

D13a.en: He fought his father in the stable and held him down until he promised to leave him alone.
D13a.zh: 他在马厩里和父亲打斗，按住父亲直到他答应不再管他。

D5.en: At 16-17, he had the temerity to drop in unannounced on Richard Morris Hunt.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，他冒昧地未经介绍就拜访了Richard Morris Hunt。

D11.en: He liked to dream, shut away in a world of his own devising.
D11.zh: 他喜欢做梦，躲在自己创造的世界里。

D15.en: As a young athlete, he could easily walk 20 miles a day.
D15.zh: 年轻时，他一天能轻松走20英里。

D4.en: As a small child, he played with Froebel building blocks.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他玩过福禄贝尔积木。

D7.en: At 16-17, he presented himself to Frank Furness and said he came to enter his employ.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他出现在Frank Furness面前并告知他前来求职。

M2.en: She was supposed to make herself forgiven for being a girl.
M2.zh: 她应该为自己是个女孩而求得原谅。

D10.en: She felt humiliated by his teasing, ashamed not to be the boy he always wanted.
D10.zh: 她因他的取笑感到羞辱，为自己不是他想要的男孩而羞愧。

D11.en: She molded a figure of her father from bread and spit, then cut off its limbs.
D11.zh: 她用面包和口水捏了一个父亲的雕像，然后切掉了四肢。

M1.en: She was an overachiever.
M1.zh: 她是个成绩优异的人。

D4.en: She heated glass cups to create a vacuum and placed them on her mother's back.
D4.zh: 她加热玻璃杯制造真空，然后把杯子放在母亲背上。

D7.en: Sometimes she countered her father's displays by throwing a tantrum.
D7.zh: 有时她通过发脾气来对抗父亲的表现。

D2.en: At 10, she worked like a dog.
D2.zh: 10岁时，她拼命工作。

D3.en: At 10, she became an expert at drawing legs and feet.
D3.zh: 10岁时，她成了画腿和脚的高手。

D12.en: At 10, she helped in the atelier and attended private school in Paris.
D12.zh: 10岁时，她在工作室帮忙，并在巴黎上私立学校。

M3.en: At 14, he zealously fastened onto Charles L'Eplattenier as a hero figure.
M3.zh: 14岁时，他狂热地崇拜Charles L'Eplattenier这个英雄人物。

D13b.en: At 14, he was enrolled in the Ecole d'Art at La Chaux-de-Fonds as a first step towards watch engraving apprenticeship.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他进入拉绍德封的艺术学校，作为钟表雕刻学徒的第一步。

D13a.en: At about 14.5, he left traditional secondary school to go to the School of Applied and Industrial Arts.
D13a.zh: 大约14岁半时，他离开传统中学，进入应用与工业艺术学校。

D11.en: As a child, he drew constantly after school and used his mother's clothesline to dry watercolors.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他放学后不停地画画，还用母亲的晾衣绳晾干水彩画。

M1.en: At 16, he hammered a repoussé copper relief of Dante, winning the school's highest prize.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他锤制了一幅但丁的铜浮雕，获得了学校最高奖。

D15.en: As a child, on hikes that tested his family to the maximum, his father brought them to ravishing vistas.
D15.zh: 童年时，在让家人筋疲力尽的远足中，他父亲带他们去看迷人的景色。

D9.en: At 17, he designed his first building, Villa Fallet, construction began in spring 1906.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他设计了第一栋建筑Villa Fallet，1906年春开工。

D7.en: At 14, he left his seat in an unwarranted and noisy manner.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他无故大声离开座位。

D2.en: Around 1905, he lived with his parents and Albert in the family apartment in La Chaux-de-Fonds.
D2.zh: 约1905年，他和父母及Albert住在拉绍德封的家庭公寓里。

D8.en: At 16-18, she wondered if it was all right for her to think for others and get them to do what she wanted.
D8.zh: 16到18岁时，她怀疑自己替别人思考并让他们做自己想做的事是否合适。

D1.en: At 17, she determinedly went along with the class to anatomy class to become an artist.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她坚定地跟着全班去上解剖课，要成为艺术家。

D18.en: As a child and young adult, she did commercial illustration work.
D18.zh: 幼年和青年时期，她做过商业插画工作。

M1.en: At 17-18, she was fifth in her class in December, seventh in January, and first in February.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，她12月班级第五，1月第七，2月第一。

D4.en: At 15, she looked at a plant with the idea of painting it for the first time.
D4.zh: 15岁时，她第一次看着植物想要画它。

D9.en: At 18, she was art editor of the yearbook and illustrated it with ink-and-white pen drawings.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她是年鉴的美术编辑，并用黑白钢笔画为其插图。

D2.en: At 11, she began taking drawing lessons at home with her two younger sisters.
D2.zh: 11岁时，她开始在家和两个妹妹一起上绘画课。

D6.en: She co-founded a Kappa Delta sorority chapter with a classmate.
D6.zh: 她和同学一起创办了Kappa Delta姐妹会分会。

D13a.en: He attended vocational school at age 12.
D13a.zh: 他12岁时上了职业学校。

D2.en: At about 13-15, he attended the Spenrathschule trade school for two years.
D2.zh: 大约13到15岁时，他在Spenrathschule职业学校上了两年学。

D3.en: He stood in awe of the powerful piers and the octagonal dome they support.
D3.zh: 他敬畏地注视着那些有力的支柱和它们支撑的八角形穹顶。

D12.en: At 12, he attended vocational school.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他上了职业学校。

D18.en: At about 10-13, he lettered stones while his brother carved and sisters finished them.
D18.zh: 大约10到13岁时，他负责在石头上写字，哥哥雕刻，姐姐们做收尾工作。

D16.en: At 17-18, he quoted Taine to his grandfather: 'One doesn't ripen, one merely rots away in places.'
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他引用泰纳对祖父说：‘人不会成熟，只会腐烂。’

D10.en: As a small child, he would roll on the ground or bang his head against the wall when annoyed.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他生气时会在地上打滚或拿头撞墙。

D7.en: As a child, he was convinced that man invented God and would never budge.
D7.zh: 童年时，他坚信是人创造了上帝，并且绝不改变。

D6.en: In the early 1930s, he met his second Central European emigrant on the terrace of the Dôme.
D6.zh: 1930年代初，他在多姆咖啡馆的露台上遇到了第二位中欧移民。

D2.en: As a child, he applied himself to art with complete discipline and devotion.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他全身心投入艺术，充满自律和奉献。

D3.en: At 17, he spent most of his time devouring books in the chief supervisor's den.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他大部分时间都在主管的密室里埋头读书。

D11.en: At 16, he painted L'Eglise de Guermantes in oil on cardboard.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他用油画在纸板上画了《盖尔芒特教堂》。

D1.en: At 12, he was accepted by Castleford Secondary School on his second attempt.
D1.zh: 12岁时，他第二次尝试被卡斯尔福德中学录取。

D13b.en: As a small child, he was forced to take violin lessons against his will.
D13b.zh: 他幼年时被迫上小提琴课，尽管他抗议。

D13a.en: At 17, he was assigned as a student teacher to his old elementary school.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他被分配到母校小学做实习教师。

D5.en: At 18, he left home for London to enlist in a regiment, carrying addresses of suitable regiments.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他离家去伦敦参军，带着几个合适兵团的地址。

M1.en: At 18, he came third in the General Musketry course, qualifying as a first-class shot.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在通用步枪射击课程中排名第三，成为一等射手。

D4.en: At 15, he carved 'SCOTT SOCIETY 13' into an oak board.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他在橡木板上刻下'SCOTT SOCIETY 13'。

D9.en: At 15, he was asked to make a notice-board for the school Scott Society.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他被要求为学校Scott Society制作一块告示板。

D7.en: At 18, he was rejected by the Artists' Rifles and the Inns of Court OTC as too short.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他因太矮被艺术家步枪团和律师学院军官训练团拒绝。

D6.en: At 12-15, he was in the Blue house at school.
D6.zh: 12到15岁间，他在学校的蓝队。

D2.en: At 18, he started a correspondence drawing course but got stuck in lesson six.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他开始了绘画函授课程，但在第六课卡住了。

D3.en: At 13, he drew grotesque corbels for pleasure.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他为了乐趣画了一些怪异的牛腿。

D12.en: At 13, Moore's brother Raymond contributed to family income as a teacher.
D12.zh: 13岁时，Moore的哥哥Raymond当教师补贴家用。

M8.en: At 15-16, he felt so saintly during his confirmation that he floated four feet off the ground.
M8.zh: 15到16岁时，他在坚信礼上感觉自己圣洁得飘离地面四英尺。

D7.en: At 10, he fled when his father went to get a stick to beat him.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他趁父亲去找棍子时夺门逃走。

D2.en: At 10, his exam scores far surpassed the second-place student, earning teachers' praise.
D2.zh: 10岁时，他考试成绩远超第二名，深得老师夸奖。

M7.en: He decided to start a newspaper to enlighten the people and promote their strength.
M7.zh: 他决意创办报纸以开民智、宣民力。

D20.en: He and others decided to use the type to publish a Chinese newspaper.
D20.zh: 他和黄胜等人议决利用这副铅字出版中文报纸。

M4.en: He decided to start a newspaper, perhaps to enlighten the people and promote their strength.
M4.zh: 他决意创办报纸，可能是为了开民智、宣民力。

D13a.en: His father wanted him to study classics but gave up after he resisted, and found another path for him.
D13a.zh: 他父亲想让他苦读经书，但他不上路，父亲只好另为他指点出路。

D5.en: He was kidnapped by bandits, then persuaded a cook and escaped together.
D5.zh: 他遭土匪绑架，后说服伙夫，联袂逃脱。

D6.en: At 14, he was warmly received by Rev. Brien and introduced to St. Paul's College.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他受到白利安牧师热情接待，被介绍到圣保罗书院就读。

D2.en: At 13, he walked with an old man on a church path, reciting strange sounds aloud.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他跟着一位老人在教堂小路上边走边背诵，发出怪声。

D12.en: He was born in a grocery store in Singapore on July 20, 1842.
D12.zh: 1842年7月20日，他在新加坡一家杂货铺里出生。

D18.en: In his spare time, he co-founded Zhongwai Xinbao, the first daily newspaper in China.
D18.zh: 他暇时与友人创办《中外新报》，中国自此有日报。

D10.en: Pale and trembling, she wriggled into a cupboard to breathe.
D10.zh: 她脸色苍白、颤抖着钻进壁橱才能呼吸。

D14.en: She danced naked before her aunts and cousins in the parlor.
D14.zh: 她在客厅里当着姨妈和表姐妹的面裸体跳舞。

D18.en: She enlisted a cousin as business manager and charged admission.
D18.zh: 她让一个表亲当经理，并收取门票。

D5.en: She met up with a girlfriend and fled all the way to Tokyo.
D5.zh: 她和一个女性朋友会合，一路逃到了东京。

D11.en: She found her unique method: ink paintings of dots and pen drawings of cellular forms.
D11.zh: 她找到了自己独特的方法：用墨画点，用笔画细胞形状。

D4.en: She enjoyed smashing window panes and mirrors with a rock or hammer.
D4.zh: 她喜欢用石头或锤子把窗玻璃和镜子砸得粉碎。

D7.en: At 5-10, she was beaten nearly unconscious by her mother for her actions.
D7.zh: 5到10岁时，她因自己的行为被母亲打得几乎失去知觉。

D2.en: At 17-18, she scrupulously painted each tiny bump on the rinds of her subjects morning, noon, and night.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，她一丝不苟地日日夜夜画下她所画对象果皮上的每个小凸起。

D3.en: She has been painting, sculpting, and writing for as long as she can remember.
D3.zh: 她从记事起就一直在画画、雕塑和写作。

D13a.en: She kicked my palette across the room; we sometimes grappled physically.
D13a.zh: 她把我的调色板踢到房间那头，我们有时还会扭打在一起。

M8.en: He gave no press interviews from 1940 until the last decade of his life.
M8.zh: 他从1940年起直到生命最后十年才接受媒体采访。

M7.en: He designed the cover drawing for the magazine POETRY (LONDON).
M7.zh: 他为《POETRY (LONDON)》杂志设计了封面画。

M6.en: He had a sense of his own destiny and a towering ambition to be an artist.
M6.zh: 他有一种使命感，并有成为艺术家的巨大抱负。

M2.en: He never knew anyone who wanted to cut themselves off from their parents to such a degree.
M2.zh: 他从未见过有人如此刻意与父母断绝关系。

M4.en: At 15-16, he signed a protest in support of Republican Spain on Regent Street.
