M4.zh: 15到16岁时，他在摄政街签名支持西班牙共和国的抗议活动。

D10.en: At 18, he had scabies from dirty clothes and had to shave from head to foot; his balls stung.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他因脏衣服得了疥疮，不得不从头到脚剃毛，睾丸刺痛。

D14.en: At 17, he showed off at the Café Royal, pre-amorous adventures.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他在皇家咖啡馆炫耀，这是恋爱冒险之前的事。

D13b.en: At age 11, his mother took him to England in September 1933.
D13b.zh: 1933年9月，他母亲带他去了英格兰。

D13a.en: As a teenager, he sidelined his father but still asked him for money.
D13a.zh: 十几岁时，他冷落父亲，但仍会向他要钱。

D18.en: At 12, he filched candied fruits on a boat and used the phrase 'Ma mère attendra plus tard' to avoid paying.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他在船上偷蜜饯，并用‘我妈妈会晚点来’这句话逃避付钱。

D5.en: At 13, he used to jump on people, grab their necks until they couldn't breathe, then run off.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他常跳到别人身上，掐住脖子直到他们无法呼吸，然后跑开。

D11.en: At 17, he saw his Düreresque self-portrait drawing published in Horizon.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他看见自己最像丢勒的自画像发表在《地平线》上。

M1.en: At 15, he won with an underwater scene and almost sold it.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他凭一幅水下场景获奖，还差点卖掉了它。

D15.en: As a small child, he skated and competed with his brothers in long-jump at home.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他滑冰，并和两个兄弟在家里的花园比赛跳远。

D4.en: At 16, he carved an attractive frog and polished its alabaster back.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他雕刻了一只漂亮的青蛙，并打磨了它的雪花石膏背部。

D9.en: In his early adulthood, he was taken up by London's intellectual elite.
D9.zh: 青年时期，他被伦敦的知识精英所接纳。

D3.en: At 17, he read Ulysses and imagined a dejected Molly Bloom.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他读了《尤利西斯》，想象出一个沮丧的莫莉·布鲁姆。

D12.en: At 16-17, he felt he had to pull away from his mother.
D12.zh: 16到17岁时，他觉得自己必须离开母亲。

D6.en: At 18, he took her to the Dorchester Hotel to show her off.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他带她去多切斯特酒店炫耀她。

M8.en: At 17, he made nature his religion, with Yosemite as his temple.
M8.zh: 17岁时，他把自然当作自己的宗教，以约塞米蒂为殿堂。

D19.en: As a small child, he assembled meticulous displays of bugs impaled on pins.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他精心排列用大头针钉住的虫子标本。

M6.en: At 10, he was more interested in what he could discover for himself.
M6.zh: 10岁时，他更感兴趣的是自己能发现什么。

D16.en: At 15, he was such a chatterbox that he would surely...
D16.zh: 15岁时，他话多得肯定……

D13a.en: At age 12, after his final expulsion, he ended his classroom education and studied at home.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，最后一次被开除后，他结束了学校教育，在家学习。

D18.en: Hoping to profit, he offered prints of the children in three sizes.
D18.zh: 希望能赚钱，他提供了三种尺寸的儿童照片。

D5.en: He slipped and slid down a sixty-degree slope, pulling Holman with him.
D5.zh: 他滑倒了，沿着60度斜坡滑下，把Holman也带了下去。

D11.en: He wrote romantic poetry full of nature imagery and spiritual yearning.
D11.zh: 他写浪漫主义诗歌，充满自然意象和精神渴望。

M1.en: At 17, he entered a print in a competition and received an honorable mention.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他提交了一张照片参赛，获得了荣誉奖。

D15.en: At 16, he and his uncle left on a full day's hike to Mount Starr King.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他和叔叔出发去斯塔尔金山徒步一整天。

D4.en: At 9, he enjoyed clamming, crabbing, and playing with baby chicks.
D4.zh: 9岁时，他喜欢挖蛤蜊、捉螃蟹，和小鸡玩。

D9.en: He attended two camera-club meetings, which he found impossibly boring.
D9.zh: 他参加了两次摄影俱乐部会议，觉得无聊透顶。

D7.en: At 15, he sneaked out at night, forcing his mother to block the tent door with a chair.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他晚上偷溜出去，母亲不得不用椅子挡住帐篷门。

D6.en: He took time off from his duties at the LeConte lodge to join an excursion.
D6.zh: 他请假离开LeConte旅馆的职责，参加了一次远足。

D2.en: At 18, his energies were focused on technical mastery of photography.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他的精力集中在掌握摄影技术上。

D3.en: At 18, he began thinking about photography's expressive potential from a pictorialist viewpoint.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他开始从画意摄影的角度思考摄影的表现潜力。

D12.en: At 14-15, he found part-time employment with a photo finisher in San Francisco.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，他在旧金山一家照片冲印店找到了兼职工作。

D17.en: At 9, he was taught his letters.
D17.zh: 9岁时，他被人教认字。

D16.en: At 17, he argued with the rector.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他和校长争论。

D10.en: At 16, he was overcome with terror at the thought of making a speech.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他一想到要演讲就惊恐万分。

M1.en: At 16, he won a gold medal for pre-eminence in all his studies at the University of the Public Schools in Louisville.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他在路易斯维尔公立学校大学因所有学业优异获得金牌。

D7.en: At 8, he and a friend did not scream, aware their parents would not approve of their antics.
D7.zh: 8岁时，他和一个朋友没有尖叫，知道父母不会赞同他们的恶作剧。

D2.en: At 14, he studied French, Latin, Chemistry, German, Algebra, Composition, Trigonometry, focusing on Mathematics and Languages.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他学习法语、拉丁语、化学、德语、代数、作文、三角学，主修数学和语言。

D12.en: As a child, he accompanied his father on grain-buying trips in the Ohio valley with his brother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他和哥哥阿尔弗雷德一起陪父亲去俄亥俄河谷买粮食。

D13b.en: At 12-13, his teacher threatened punishment if anyone spoke German with him.
D13b.zh: 12到13岁时，他的老师威胁说，如果有人跟他说德语，就会惩罚那个人。

D5.en: At 16, he had to get himself to Dresden and into school on his own.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他得自己前往德累斯顿并入学。

D11.en: At 16, he began a diary detailing the ship, crew, and passengers.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他开始写日记，详细记录船、船员和乘客。

D19.en: At 8, he noted his father was a skilled accountant.
D19.zh: 8岁时，他提到父亲是熟练的会计。

D17.en: He participated in a charity school and got in touch with life.
D17.zh: 他参加义校，接触生活。

D20.en: At 17, he won the election for student council president at Chongde Middle School with underground party support.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他在地下党支持下胜选，担任崇德中学学生自治会主席。

M4.en: He joined the student movement after reading posters and progressive books.
M4.zh: 他通过看大字报和进步书刊加入了学生运动。

D10.en: He felt regret for living comfortably under Japanese rule during the war.
D10.zh: 他遗憾自己在日本统治下过着安逸生活。

D1.en: He made up missed exams over several semesters after transferring.
D1.zh: 他转学后陆续补考了第一学期缺考的五门课程。

D14.en: At 16-18, he self-directed and performed a play called 'Sheng Guan Tu' on campus.
D14.zh: 16到18岁时，他在校园里自导自演了一部叫《陞官图》的话剧。

D13b.en: His parents were engaged at age 2 due to local custom of intermarriage.
D13b.zh: 他父母在两岁时因亲上加亲风俗订婚。

D13a.en: He followed his father to Beijing in 1938.
D13a.zh: 他1938年随父亲来到北平。

D18.en: He saved meal money to buy law books, bringing back four cases.
D18.zh: 他省下饭钱买法学书，回国时带了四箱。

D5.en: He was too scared to go out, not knowing the situation outside.
D5.zh: 他胆子小，不敢出去，不知道外面情况。

D11.en: He enjoyed stargazing with adults identifying constellations.
D11.zh: 他晚上乘凉时看星星，大人教他认星座。

D15.en: He played soccer daily and was selected for the Yenching University team.
D15.zh: 他每天踢球，被选入燕大校足球队。

D9.en: At 18, he actively signed up for the school protection committee at Yenching University.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他积极报名参加燕京大学的护校委员会。

D12.en: At 15-18, he lived at school and managed boarders' meals through self-governance.
D12.zh: 15到18岁时，他住在学校，参与自治活动管理寄宿生伙食。

M8.en: At 9, he witnessed the grand funeral of Wu Peifu, a rare spectacle in Beijing.
M8.zh: 9岁时，他目睹了吴佩孚的盛大葬礼，这在北平极为罕见。

D19.en: He listed every book he read, with title and author, through 1904.
D19.zh: 他列出了他读过的每一本书的书名和作者，直到1904年。

D16.en: At age 8-14, he was forever being shushed at the table and forever breaking out again.
D16.zh: 8到14岁时，他总是在餐桌上被要求安静，又总是忍不住再次开口。

D10.en: He suffered agonies concerning this indecent lack of privacy.
D10.zh: 他为这种不体面的隐私缺失而痛苦。

D14.en: At age 10-14, he performed with levity and gusto in his attic room.
D14.zh: 10到14岁时，他在阁楼房间里轻松而充满热情地表演。

D13b.en: He went to Harvard at age 16 because it was the rule.
D13b.zh: 他16岁上哈佛，因为这是规矩。

D13a.en: At 10, he was sent to school under Epes Dixwell.
D13a.zh: 10岁时，他被送去跟Epes Dixwell上学。

M1.en: At 17, he ranked 22nd in a class of 99 at the end of his freshman year.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他在大一结束时在99人的班级中排名第22。

D7.en: At 17-18, he did not embrace exercise because the Autocrat recommended it.
D7.zh: 17到18岁时，他没有因为独裁者的推荐而接受锻炼。

D12.en: At 7, he sat frozen on the steps, wondering why they hadn't told him when she would be hanged.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他僵坐在台阶上，想知道为什么没人告诉他她何时被绞死。

D3.en: As a teenager, he read voraciously, moving from classical to modern literature.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他废寝忘食地阅读，从古典文学走进近代文学世界。

M7.en: At 15, he volunteered to join the Chinese Socialist Youth League, vowing to fight for communism.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他志愿加入中国社会主义青年团，愿为共产主义事业奋斗终生。

D20.en: At 15, he founded the first Socialist Youth League branch in Wuxi County and was elected secretary.
D20.zh: 15岁时，他建立了无锡县第一个社会主义青年团支部，并被选为团支部书记。

M4.en: At 16, he was elected the first Party branch secretary of Wuxi's first Party organization.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他被推选为无锡第一个党组织的第一任党支部书记。

D5.en: At 17, he volunteered to go to Shanghai during summer break to experience the workers' movement.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他主动申请利用暑假到上海亲身体验工人运动。

D9.en: At 17, he led Wuxi's Party and Youth League organizations in propaganda activities among the masses.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他领导无锡的党团组织到群众中进行宣传鼓动。

D7.en: At 18, he was the first to openly criticize Ren Zhuoxuan's erroneous leadership.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他首先站出来对任卓宣的错误领导提出看法。

D3.en: As a child and teenager, he loved music and performed in school concerts multiple times.
D3.zh: 幼年时他便喜欢音乐，中小学时多次举行音乐演出。

D12.en: At 6-10, his mother made him finish calligraphy practice before bed.
D12.zh: 6到10岁时，他母亲每天看着他写完描红和仿字本才让他睡觉。

D17.en: At 15, he had typhoid fever and was nursed by her for 20 days until she collapsed.
D17.zh: 15岁时，他得了伤寒，她照顾了他20天，直到她累倒。

D10.en: He cried until she retracted her statement and promised to live on.
D10.zh: 他哭到她收回那句话并承诺活下去才停。

D14.en: He burst into laughter with his hands holding his lower jaw.
D14.zh: 他双手托着下巴大笑起来。

D13a.en: At age 6, his parents engaged him to another child.
D13a.zh: 6岁时，他父母给他定了娃娃亲。

D18.en: He used to filch dimes from his mother's hoard to buy kites.
D18.zh: 他常从母亲私房钱里偷一两角钱买风筝。

D5.en: He tried to light a bottle with a match; it burst but he wasn't hurt.
D5.zh: 他试图用火柴点燃瓶子，瓶子炸了但他没受伤。

D11.en: At age 12, he felt as though he had beaten Confucius by three years.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他觉得自己好像比孔子早了三年。

M1.en: He would be the first honor man for six terms consecutively.
M1.zh: 他会连续六个学期成为第一名荣誉生。

D15.en: He was a champion at kicking shuttlecocks in secondary school.
D15.zh: 他在中学时是踢毽子冠军。

D4.en: He tied a rope between a pillar and a millstone and walked on it with a pole.
D4.zh: 他在柱子和磨盘间系了根绳子，拿根竿子走上去。

D9.en: At 17-18, he convinced his brother to divide the household and property.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他说服哥哥分家分财产。

D7.en: He proposed dividing the property and separate kitchens, which his brother saw as a family revolution.
D7.zh: 他提议分家并分开做饭，这在他哥哥听来像是一场家庭革命。

D2.en: He always stood at the top of the class in physics.
D2.zh: 他的物理成绩总是全班第一。

D3.en: He read Herbert Giles' version to see how the same things were presented in English.
D3.zh: 他读了Herbert Giles的译本，想看看同样的事情用英语怎么表达。

D12.en: His father died when he was ten.
D12.zh: 他十岁时父亲去世了。

D20.en: At 18, they voted him president of the Lyceum.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被选为Lyceum的主席。

D16.en: At 17, he gave his graduation oration 'Labor vs. Capital' as class president and valedictorian.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他作为班长和毕业生代表发表了毕业演讲《劳工与资本》。

D14.en: As a teenager, he received invitations to speak at social gatherings in Frewsburg and neighboring towns.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他受邀在Frewsburg和邻近城镇的社交聚会上演讲。

D5.en: At 12-14, he poured a dipper of water out the window when the teacher turned her back.
D5.zh: 12到14岁时，他趁老师转身把一瓢水泼出窗外。

M1.en: As a teenager, he signed up for the debate team that competed with other schools.
M1.zh: 十几岁时，他报名参加了与其他学校比赛的辩论队。

D15.en: As a small child, he hiked with his dog or rode a horse on adventures by himself.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他独自带着狗徒步或骑马探险。

D4.en: As a small child, he slept in the stalls with horses, rubbed them down, and helped train them.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他睡在马厩里，给马擦洗，并帮助训练它们。

D9.en: At 16, he led a meeting of the county Democratic Party.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他主持了县民主党的一次会议。

D7.en: At 18, he ignored his father's objections and accepted Frank Mott's offer.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他不顾父亲反对，接受了Frank Mott的邀请。

D6.en: At 17, he was glad to find his new school had a debate club, the Lyceum.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他很高兴发现新学校有一个辩论俱乐部Lyceum。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to study an extra year at the high school in Jamestown.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他决定在Jamestown的高中多读一年。

D3.en: At 6-14, he read his way through most of the five hundred books in Frewsburg's library.
D3.zh: 6到14岁间，他读完了Frewsburg图书馆五百本书中的大部分。

D12.en: At 5-14, he collected wood, churned butter, weeded the garden, and fed the chickens and hogs.
D12.zh: 5到14岁间，他捡柴火、搅黄油、给花园除草、喂鸡和猪。

M4.en: She felt growing alienation from religious practice and a new steeliness in her resolve.
M4.zh: 她感到对宗教实践越来越疏远，决心也变得更加坚定。

D10.en: She and her father were engulfed by waves of inconsolable grief.
D10.zh: 她和父亲被无法安慰的悲伤浪潮淹没。

D14.en: She was chosen as one of four students to speak for her eight hundred classmates.
D14.zh: 她被选为四名学生代表之一，为八百名同学发言。

D5.en: She smoked her first cigarette at thirteen when dared to inhale without coughing.
D5.zh: 她十三岁时第一次抽烟，被激将吸入而不咳嗽。

M1.en: She won the English Scholarship Medal, a New York State scholarship, and was slated to speak at the Round Table Forum of Honor.
M1.zh: 她获得了英语奖学金奖章、纽约州奖学金，并计划在荣誉圆桌论坛上发言。

D15.en: As an adult, she would come to love riding and waterskiing.
D15.zh: 成年后，她喜欢上了骑马和滑水。

D4.en: At 10, she climbed garage roofs as her next caper.
D4.zh: 10岁时，她爬车库屋顶作为下一个恶作剧。

D7.en: At 17, she felt a keen sense of guilt for putting her own needs first.
D7.zh: 17岁时，她因把自己的需求放在首位而感到强烈的内疚。

D12.en: At 17, her mother died of cancer after a four-year struggle.
D12.zh: 17岁时，她母亲在与癌症抗争四年后去世。

D11.en: At 16-17, she was called camp rabbi at summer camp.
D11.zh: 16到17岁时，她在夏令营被称作营队拉比。

D19.en: As a small child, he filled his schoolbooks with careful notations and questions.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他在课本上工整地记下问题和定义。

D18.en: At 9-12, he delivered the Daily Californian and sold ice successfully.
D18.zh: 9到12岁时，他送《加州日报》并成功卖冰。

D5.en: As a teenager, he played football despite his mother's fear of him getting hurt.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他不顾母亲担心受伤，继续打橄榄球。

M1.en: At 18, he made the all-city team and had an impressive college career.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他入选全市代表队，大学表现也很出色。

D15.en: At 17, he tried out for the university baseball team but did not make it.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他参加大学棒球队选拔但没选上。

D4.en: He worked as a mechanic's helper in the Southern Pacific roundhouse during summers.
D4.zh: 他暑假在南太平洋铁路的圆形机车库当机械工助手。

D9.en: He served as secretary at the organizational meeting and carried a union card for life.
D9.zh: 他在组织会议上担任秘书，并终身持有工会会员卡。

D6.en: He was invited to join La Junta and happily did so.
D6.zh: 他被邀请加入La Junta，并欣然接受了。

D2.en: He learned to play the clarinet and play it well.
D2.zh: 他学会了吹单簧管，而且吹得很好。

D3.en: At 17-18, he set out to become the lawyer that Johnson was.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他立志成为约翰逊那样的律师。

D13b.en: As a small child, teachers tied his left hand to force him to write with his right.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，老师把他左手绑起来强迫他用右手写字。

D17.en: He pleaded for milk to care for a cat.
D17.zh: 他央求母亲给牛奶来照顾猫。

M2.en: He was inspired by his mother's ambition to excel.
M2.zh: 他受到母亲追求卓越的激励。

D16.en: He and his father kept one-upping each other.
D16.zh: 他和父亲不断互相超越。

D1.en: He quit the team to focus on schoolwork, then rejoined and was reelected captain.
D1.zh: 他退队专注学业，后重新加入并再次当选队长。

D13b.en: He was required to learn a section of the Constitution as punishment.
D13b.zh: 他被罚背诵宪法的一部分。

D18.en: At 7, he worked for ten cents a day plus all he could eat.
D18.zh: 7岁时，他打工每天赚10美分加管饱。

D5.en: As a small child, his family was full of strong people not intimidated by violent segregation.
D5.zh: 幼年时，他的家庭充满不惧暴力隔离的强者。

D6.en: At 14-16, he won election to the student council and treasury committee.
D6.zh: 14到16岁时，他当选学生会和财务委员。

D2.en: At 16-17, he was never late to school and absent just one day.
D2.zh: 16到17岁时，他上学从不迟到，只缺勤一天。

D3.en: At 16-17, he turned to Uncle Fee to see the wider world of Baltimore politics.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，他找Fee叔叔了解巴尔的摩政治和公司。

D16.en: At 16, he came top in English and mathematics.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他英语和数学考了第一名。

D1.en: At 15, he had to study advanced mathematics on his own when teachers left.
D1.zh: 15岁时，老师走了，他只好自学高等数学。

D13b.en: He joined the Royal Engineers under compulsory service.
D13b.zh: 他在强制兵役下加入了皇家工兵部队。

M1.en: At 15, he worked hard for a university scholarship in mathematics.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他努力争取大学数学奖学金。

D6.en: At 9, he earned a free place at Andover Grammar School after exams.
D6.zh: 9岁时，他通过考试获得安多弗文法学校的免费名额。

D2.en: At 16, he sat for the Oxford and Cambridge examination.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他参加了牛津剑桥入学考试。

D12.en: At 17, he managed on £120 a year but watched every penny.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他靠120英镑年收入勉强度日，但每一分钱都精打细算。

D20.en: He was elected president of the Mask and Bauble club in the middle of his sophomore year.
D20.zh: 他在大二中期被选为Mask and Bauble俱乐部的主席。

M4.en: He rejected liberalism and followed the 1952 presidential campaign of Republican Dwight D. Eisenhower.
M4.zh: 他拒绝自由主义，关注1952年共和党人德怀特·艾森豪威尔的总统竞选。

D16.en: He gravitated toward the Philodemic Society, the oldest collegiate debating society in the country.
D16.zh: 他倾向于加入Philodemic Society，这是全国最古老的大学辩论社团。

D1.en: At 14-15, he won a full scholarship to Xavier High School after a failure led to a better opportunity.
D1.zh: 14到15岁时，他在一次失败后获得了更好的机会，赢得了Xavier High School的全额奖学金。

D14.en: He became president of the dramatic society and starred in school plays, including the lead in Macbeth.
D14.zh: 他成为戏剧社主席，并在学校戏剧中担任主角，包括在《麦克白》中领衔。

D13a.en: Guided by the Jesuits at Xavier High School, he decided to attend Georgetown.
D13a.zh: 在泽维尔高中耶稣会士的指导下，他决定就读乔治城大学。

M1.en: He won 19 First Honor awards, one for each marking period.
M1.zh: 他获得了19个一等荣誉奖，每个评分期一个。

D15.en: He learned to fire a rifle and march in formation.
D15.zh: 他学会了开枪和列队行进。

D9.en: He was elected president of the White Debating Society for his sophomore year.
D9.zh: 他被选为白厅辩论协会大二学年的主席。

D7.en: He deemed warrantless wiretaps a practical necessity and opposed removing executive branch control.
D7.zh: 他认为无证窃听是实际必要，反对剥夺行政分支的控制权。

D6.en: He was sent with Schmidt to the Hall of Fame Tournament at New York University.
D6.zh: 他和Schmidt被派往纽约大学参加名人堂锦标赛。

D2.en: He studied constantly and participated in every activity available to him.
D2.zh: 他不断学习，并参加了所有能参加的活动。

D3.en: He followed Attorney General Levi's actions in the process leading to John Paul Stevens's appointment.
D3.zh: 他关注了司法部长Levi在任命John Paul Stevens过程中的行动。

D12.en: He was often left in the care of one Panaro or another as a small child.
D12.zh: 他幼年时经常被交给某个Panaro照看。

D1.en: At age 10, his father enrolled him in the entrance exam for the Academic Gymnasium.
D1.zh: 10岁时，他父亲为他报名了学术文理中学的入学考试。

D13b.en: He likely received private instruction in Israelite religion during those years.
D13b.zh: 他那些年可能接受了以色列宗教的私人教育。

M1.en: He did not become a 'top student'.
M1.zh: 他没有成为“优等生”。

D6.en: He formed a lifelong friendship with someone.
D6.zh: 他与某人建立了终生的友谊。

D3.en: He did not enroll in any courses taught by Tezner during his studies.
D3.zh: 他学习期间没有注册过Tezner的任何课程。

M3.en: He learned Norwegian and traveled to Norway to meet Hamsun because he admired him so much.
M3.zh: 他因为非常崇拜Hamsun，学了挪威语并去挪威见他。

D20.en: At 15, he was president of the tenth-grade Science Club.
D20.zh: 15岁时，他是十年级科学俱乐部的主席。

D14.en: At 15, he was in the tenth-grade play.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他参加了十年级的戏剧演出。

D13b.en: At 16, he followed his father's encouragement, applied to Yale and Harvard, and was accepted at Yale.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他听从父亲鼓励，申请了耶鲁和哈佛，并被耶鲁录取。

D11.en: At 12-14, he made up mysteries with others as they went along.
D11.zh: 12到14岁时，他和别人一起即兴编造各种谜案。

M1.en: He was the top-ranked student upon graduation in 1954.
M1.zh: 他在1954年毕业时是成绩最好的学生。

D15.en: At 12-16, he shot rifles and played tennis with friends.
D15.zh: 12到16岁时，他和朋友一起射击步枪、打网球。

D4.en: At 12-14, he directed a movie made behind his house.
D4.zh: 12到14岁时，他在自家屋后导演了一部电影。

D9.en: At 14-16, he was asked by the principal to teach geometry for two weeks.
D9.zh: 14到16岁时，他被校长请去代教几何课两周。

D6.en: At 15, he was in the tenth-grade play and a member of the Projectionist Club.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他参演了十年级话剧，还是放映员俱乐部成员。

D2.en: At 15, he got an A in English and 98 or 100 in math, science, Latin, and French.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他英语期末考得A，数学、科学、拉丁语和法语得98或100分。

D3.en: At 12, he wrote two long papers by hand and wanted to type instead.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他手写了两篇很长的论文，觉得以后打字更高效。

D12.en: He was born on January 11, 1939, in New York City, an only child.
D12.zh: 他于1939年1月11日出生在纽约市，是独生子。

D13a.en: At 13, he agreed to give his train set to the Rosenberg children.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他同意把火车套装送给罗森伯格家的孩子。

D18.en: At 13, he said he had outgrown the trains, so they could be given away.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他说自己已经长大了，不再玩火车，所以可以送人。

D13b.en: He enrolled as a law student at Turin University.
D13b.zh: 他进入都灵大学学习法律。

D13a.en: He grew up in a patriotic and fascist family; his father joined the Fascist National Party in 1923.
D13a.zh: 他在一个爱国且法西斯主义的家庭长大；他父亲于1923年加入了法西斯国家党。

D11.en: He started writing poetry in 1923, when he was in the fourth year of junior secondary school.
D11.zh: 他1923年开始写诗，当时他上初中四年级。

D9.en: He tried his luck in a competition organized by the Turin University Association for student revues, together with some friends.
D9.zh: 他和一些朋友一起参加了都灵大学协会组织的学生时事讽刺剧比赛。

D7.en: At 18, he joined the Youth Vanguard at D'Azeglio Senior Secondary School.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他加入了D'Azeglio高中的青年先锋队。

D6.en: He was in the fifth year at D'Azeglio School in the 1923–4 academic year, third from the left in the second row in a class photo.
D6.zh: 1923-24学年，他在D'Azeglio学校上五年级，班级合影中第二排左起第三位。

D3.en: At 13, he vividly remembered the great Legalitarian Strike of summer 1922.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他清楚记得1922年夏天的合法罢工。

D13a.en: At 17, he deferred to his father's wishes without question.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他无条件顺从父亲的意愿。

M1.en: In high school, he took home so many academic prizes that none were left for others.
M1.zh: 高中时，他拿走了那么多学术奖项，别人都没得拿了。

D6.en: He and Charlie invited friends over for Sunday dinner.
D6.zh: 他和Charlie邀请朋友来吃周日晚餐。

D2.en: As a child, he was a bookworm, pleasing his parents.
D2.zh: 他小时候是个书虫，让父母很满意。

D3.en: At 10, he had seen police and governmental abuse.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他就见过警察和政府滥用权力。

D7.en: At 10, he witnessed his father being carried home bloodied and beaten by police.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他目睹父亲被警察打得浑身是血抬回家。

M3.en: After finishing a story about Susan B. Anthony, she decided her life course.
M3.zh: 她读完苏珊·B·安东尼的故事后，确定了自己的人生方向。

D10.en: She climbed on board a train, sat at a window waving at her parents as it moved away.
D10.zh: 她爬上火车，坐在窗边向父母挥手，直到火车驶出视线。

D1.en: At 15, she said she had a flat tire and had to change it.
D1.zh: 15岁时，她说她在罗伯斯井那边爆了胎，不得不换轮胎。

D14.en: At 16, she saw a tall, good-looking boy and asked him to dance.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她看到一个高大帅气的男孩，邀请他跳舞。

D15.en: At 15, she stood on the lug wrench and jumped to create more force.
D15.zh: 15岁时，她站在扳手上跳起来增加力量。

D4.en: As a girl, she climbed Round Mountain with her father, avoiding rattlesnakes.
D4.zh: 小时候，她和父亲爬圆山，躲开响尾蛇。

D6.en: At 16, she came back with a cute guy named Andy who had a red convertible.
D6.zh: 16岁时，她带回来一个叫Andy的帅哥，他有一辆红色敞篷车。

D2.en: She had a photographic memory and scored 148 on an IQ test.
D2.zh: 她有照相式记忆，智商测试得了148分。

D12.en: At 6, she was sent away to school in El Paso and lived with her grandmother.
D12.zh: 6岁时，她被送到埃尔帕索上学，和祖母住在一起。

M7.en: At 12, he declared that a man of ambition should take the world as his responsibility.
M7.zh: 12岁时，他说大丈夫当以天下为己任。

M2.en: At 11, he wished to become famous and accomplished to repay his parents.
M2.zh: 11岁时，他想成名成家以报答父母的愿望。

M4.en: At 14, he felt the anti-Qing ideas instilled by teachers made the world seem new.
M4.zh: 14岁时，他感到灌输的反清思想使天地为之一新。

D16.en: At 12, he ended his essay with 'A true man should take the world as his duty' to express his ambition.
D16.zh: 12岁时，他以‘大丈夫当以天下为己任’结尾，抒发自己的宏愿大志。

D10.en: At 10, he fled when his mother, angry, tried to beat him with a ruler.
D10.zh: 10岁时，母亲要打他，他夺门而逃。

D11.en: At 13, he wished to write a great novel and become a famous author.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他想将来能著一伟大小说，成一名家。

M1.en: At 8-11, he won awards in monthly exams and became famous at school for his weekly history essays.
M1.zh: 8到11岁时，他每月考试得奖，每周史论文章使他在立志小学出了名。

D15.en: At 14, he could not adapt to the intense physical training due to his thin body.
D15.zh: 14岁时，身体单薄的他无法适应学校强度很大的体育训练。

D4.en: At 14, he learned seal carving from classmates, handling the knife and brush.
D4.zh: 14岁时，他向同学学习篆刻，操刀弄笔。

D7.en: At 15, he was expelled for rebelling with classmates.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他因和同学们一起反抗而被开除。

D2.en: At 16, he trained himself by listening in class and writing down the teacher's lecture from memory, improving his memory.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他上课专心听讲硬记，课后默写老师所讲内容，训练了自己的记忆能力。

D12.en: At 10, his father died, leaving a will for him to study industrial technology and become an industrialist.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他父亲去世了，遗嘱希望他以后学工业技术，成为实业家。

D14.en: At 14, he heard a speaker whose voice entranced people.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他听到一个能用声音迷住人的演讲者。

D18.en: As a small child, his father sold linens from the family apartment and peddled door to door.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他父亲在家卖亚麻布，还挨家挨户兜售。

D6.en: As a teenager, he continued discussions outside late into the night.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他在外面继续讨论到深夜。

D3.en: As a teenager, he discovered Cooper Union's reading room and read newspapers for hours, developing interest in politics.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他发现了库珀联盟的阅览室，花数小时读报纸，培养了对政治的兴趣。

D12.en: At 12, he entered P.S. 25 while his older brothers worked to support the family.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他进入P.S. 25上学，而他的哥哥们则打工养家。

D20X.en: At 16, he wrote in his diary about his depression and desire to travel overseas.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他在日记中写道连日郁郁不快，渴望乘风破浪海外游。

M5.en: At 13-15, he took the imperial exam with a devout attitude.
M5.zh: 13到15岁间，他几乎以虔诚的心情去应试。

M8.en: At 13, he bought and was deeply impressed by Li Xiaochi's book 'Sitong Ji' about the Taiping Rebellion.
M8.zh: 13岁时，他购得李小池《思痛记》，对书中所述太平天国破坏印象深刻。

M7.en: At 15, he wrote about the call to expel foreigners.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他写下驱逐洋人在此时矣。

M6.en: At 16, he expressed desire to abandon writing and join the military overseas.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他感叹何时得乘风破浪海外游，欲投笔执戈。

D17.en: At 13-16, he had to take on the main responsibility for his family.
D17.zh: 13到16岁间，他不得不担负起全家的主要责任。

M4.en: At 15, he thought it was time to expel the foreigners.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他认为驱逐洋人的时候到了。

D16.en: At 15, he recorded in his diary that the Boxers destroyed railways and telegraph poles, calling them national troublemakers.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他在日记中记载义和拳拆毁铁路电杆，称其为国家顽民。

D11.en: At 13-14, after his younger brother died, he wrote sad poems to vent his sorrow.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，四弟去世后，他写俚词抒发悲感。

D15.en: At 15, he sat bare-chested and barefoot under the cabin.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他赤膊科头，箕踞舱下。

D4.en: At 16, he went to the market every morning in a white linen gown, carrying baskets among the vendors.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他每天早晨穿着白色夏布长衫，提着菜篮去菜市场买菜。

D9.en: At 13-16, he had to take on family responsibilities like collecting rent and paying taxes.
D9.zh: 13到16岁间，他不得不担负起全家的主要责任，收租与完粮。

D7.en: At 16, he felt depressed and wanted to leave home to travel overseas and join the military.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他连日郁郁不快，想乘长风破万里浪，投笔执戈从事域外。

D6.en: At 13-14, he had a first love with a girl called Yang Sangu.
D6.zh: 13到14岁时，他初恋的对象是杨三姑。

D2.en: At 14, he started keeping a diary on February 18, 1898.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他于1898年2月18日开始记日记。

D12.en: At 13-16, he took on family duties like collecting rent and paying taxes after his father's death.
D12.zh: 13到16岁时，父亲早逝，他担负起全家责任，收租与完粮。

D3.en: At 11-15, he read 'Jing Hua Yuan' at Sanwei Bookhouse and many novels in Hangzhou.
D3.zh: 11到15岁时，他在三味书屋读《镜花缘》，在杭州侍读时读了很多小说。

D10.en: At 14, he felt both upset and relieved imagining her tragic death.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他既觉得不快，又仿佛心里放下了大石头。

D16.en: At 14-16, she debated adults on current events and hung out with older people.
D16.zh: 14到16岁时，她与成年人辩论时事，并与年长的人交往。

D10.en: At age 8-9, she was deeply shaken by something.
D10.zh: 8到9岁时，她深受震动。

D14.en: At age 8-9, she was finally put out of the church choir.
D14.zh: 8到9岁时，她最终被赶出教堂唱诗班。

D13a.en: From age 5 to 18, she could not address adults by first names, only as Uncle, Aunt, Cousin.
D13a.zh: 5到18岁期间，她不能直呼成年人名字，只能称叔叔、阿姨、表亲。

D18.en: At 18, she was paid fifty dollars a month.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她每月得到五十美元。

D11.en: At 17-18, she wrote a poem published in her English department head's textbook.
D11.zh: 17到18岁时，她写了一首诗，被英语系主任出版在教材中。

M1.en: She won a prize for an essay on tuberculosis.
M1.zh: 她因一篇关于结核病的文章获奖。

D2.en: She learned about Negro spirituals as a black-culture treasure from her sister's attendance at St. Augustine's.
D2.zh: 她通过姐姐在圣奥古斯丁学校的经历了解到黑人灵歌是黑人文化的瑰宝。

D3.en: She was introduced to black history by Father Edwards through lectures and books.
D3.zh: 爱德华兹神父通过讲座和书籍向她介绍了黑人历史。

D12.en: She worked a youth-opportunity job with the National Youth Administration after high school.
D12.zh: 高中毕业后，她在国家青年管理局的一个青年机会项目中工作。

M8.en: At 12, she began questioning the religious beliefs she once held sacred.
M8.zh: 12岁时，她开始质疑曾经视为神圣的宗教信仰。

D17.en: At 10.5, she loved helping her mother bathe and wheel her baby sister in a buggy.
D17.zh: 10岁半时，她喜欢帮母亲给妹妹洗澡，推着她的大黑婴儿车。

D10.en: At 14, she railed against her mother after suppressing aggression all week.
D10.zh: 14岁时，她压抑了一周的怒气爆发，对母亲发火。

D1.en: At 18, she refused to drop out in ignominy and sought a respectable exit.
D1.zh: 18岁时，她拒绝不光彩地退学，拼命寻找体面的出路。

D13a.en: At 12, she squeezed her eyes shut and pretended to go along, hiding her doubts.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，她闭紧眼睛假装顺从，隐藏自己的疑虑。

D11.en: At 12, she escaped to strange and distant worlds through books for a few hours.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她通过书籍逃到陌生遥远的世界，享受几小时。

M1.en: At 12, she raced through correspondence lessons, sending two envelopes weekly instead of one.
M1.zh: 12岁时，她拼命赶函授课程，每周寄出两个大信封，而不是要求的一个。

D15.en: At 10.5, she cracked her collarbone on a doorstop stick left out by a boy.
D15.zh: 10岁半时，她撞上一个男孩留下的门挡棍，摔断了锁骨。

D4.en: At 14, she designed a house, drawing walls and windows on graph paper.
D4.zh: 14岁时，她在方格纸上设计房子，画出墙壁和窗户的线条。

D9.en: At 14, she sang in the school choir and starred in plays and concerts.
D9.zh: 14岁时，她在学校合唱团唱歌，并在戏剧和音乐会中担任主角。

D7.en: As a child, she suspected prejudice when an invitation to sing was withdrawn.
D7.zh: 小时候，当唱歌邀请被无故取消时，她怀疑是偏见。

D2.en: At 10-12, she took correspondence courses from the Department of Education.
D2.zh: 10到12岁时，她上了教育部的函授课程。

D3.en: At 18, she went on a summer school trip to Algeria and Europe.
D3.zh: 18岁时，她参加了去阿尔及利亚和欧洲的暑期学校旅行。

D12.en: At 17, she quit school to run the store, care for siblings, and nurse her dying mother.
D12.zh: 17岁时，她被迫辍学，帮忙经营店铺、照顾弟妹并护理病危的母亲。

M6.en: At 15, he read Kropotkin's 'To the Youth' and first understood class oppression rationally.
M6.zh: 15岁时，他读了克鲁泡特金的《告少年》，第一次从理性上懂得了阶级压迫。

D20.en: He and other youths gathered to reprint a pamphlet and publish a manifesto, but the group was never formally established.
D20.zh: 他和几个青年人聚在一起翻印了一本小册子，发表了一篇宣言，但均社没有正式成立过。

M4.en: At 15, he declared anarchism his life and became an anarchist.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他宣布安那其主义是他的全部，从此成为安那其主义者。

D10.en: As a small child, he hid in a sedan chair bearer's bed during New Year worship, ignoring calls.
D10.zh: 幼年时，除夕敬神拜祖时他躲在轿夫的破床上，别人叫他也不应。

D1.en: At 17, he and friends distributed leaflets on May Day.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他和朋友们在五一节上街散发传单。

D14.en: He translated Turgenev's prose poem 'Threshold' many times in his life.
D14.zh: 他一生中多次翻译屠格涅夫的散文诗《门槛》。

D13b.en: At 15-16, his grandfather allowed him to take English lessons at a YMCA and hoped to get him a post office job.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁时，他祖父准许他去青年会学英文，并希望为他谋得邮局差事。

D5.en: He left his family to join the masses.
D5.zh: 他离开家庭，投身于人民群众之中。

D11.en: At 18, he published his poem 'The Cry of the Abused'.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他发表了诗作《被虐者底哭声》。

D4.en: He released chickens and played games with them every morning.
D4.zh: 他每天一早放鸡出来，赶来赶去做游戏。

D9.en: He watched children's plays and wrote a script for them.
D9.zh: 他看完孩子们自编自演的戏，还为他们编了一出剧本。

D7.en: He was silenced by his father when trying to speak.
D7.zh: 他插嘴说话，被父亲喝住。

D6.en: He gained servants' respect through frequent contact.
D6.zh: 他从小与仆人常来常往，得到他们的敬爱。

D2.en: He fell ill frequently while studying English at YMCA school in 1918.
D2.zh: 1918年他去青年会英文补习学校学英文，一个月生了三次病。

D3.en: He eagerly embraced radical new doctrines after the May Fourth Movement.
D3.zh: 五四运动后，他带着极大热诚接受激进新学说。

M4.en: At 15, she resolutely joined patriotic student demonstrations and fundraising against Yuan Shikai.
M4.zh: 15岁时，她毅然参加了爱国学生反日讨袁的示威游行和募捐活动。

D13a.en: At 11, she silently endured advice to quit school from elders who opposed girls' education.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，她沉默地忍受着长辈劝她辍学的话。

D18.en: At 11, she donated her saved ten dollars of New Year's money to Shanghai Shenbao.
D18.zh: 11岁时，她献出了自己积攒的十块压岁钱，送到上海《申报》。

M1.en: At 14, she resolved to master math and all science subjects.
M1.zh: 14岁时，她下决心要学好数学和一切数理化的功课。

D15.en: As a small child, she was taught by her father how to shoot, ride, and row.
D15.zh: 幼年时，父亲教她如何打枪、骑马、划船。

D4.en: As a small child, she knew how to operate a Mauser rifle and screw large shells into a cannon.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她知道毛瑟枪里的机关，也会将很大的炮弹旋进炮膛里。

D9.en: At 14, she proposed to her uncle and first teacher that she go to school.
D9.zh: 14岁时，她向舅舅杨子敬提出要去学校上学。

D7.en: At 7-11, when her mother tore her book, she calmly picked up the pieces and continued reading.
D7.zh: 7到11岁间，母亲撕了她的书，她不哭不叫，走过去拿起撕成两段的书接着看。

D6.en: At 11, she gradually adapted and made many close girlfriends, including Wang Shiying.
D6.zh: 11岁时，她逐渐习惯了，并交上了许多要好的小女朋友，包括王世瑛。

D2.en: At 14-18, she spent most of her time on math and science homework.
D2.zh: 14到18岁间，她一天到晚做功课，花时间最多的是数理化。

D3.en: At 5, she sat alone on the stone steps staring at the sea for three hours.
D3.zh: 5岁时，她一个人呆呆地坐在石阶上，对着大海看了三个钟头。

D12.en: At 13, she began to understand her mother's difficulties in managing household chores.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她开始懂得母亲料理家务的难处。

D17.en: At 8, he cried in a corner for his deceased little sister, saying 'It's for my little sister!'
D17.zh: 8岁时，他躲在屋角为妹妹哭泣，说‘为妹妹啦！’

M2.en: His grandfather dreamed of him and other descendants becoming Hanlin scholars together.
M2.zh: 他祖父梦想让他和儿孙一起考取翰林。

D10.en: At 17, he felt heartbroken in a foreign land, missing home and family.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他身处异乡，思念家乡亲人，柔肠欲断。

D1.en: At 13-15, he took on family responsibilities after his father fell ill.
D1.zh: 13到15岁间，父亲病后，他挑起家中重担，出面交涉。

D13b.en: At 6, he began schooling, first with relatives, then at Sanwei Bookstore.
D13b.zh: 6岁时，他开蒙读书，先随亲戚学，后到三味书屋。

D11.en: At 13-14, he and his brother invented fairy tales about immortal mountains and giant ants.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，他和弟弟编童话，讲仙山、巨蚁、仙人炼玉。

D4.en: At 6-12, he traced all 100+ illustrations from a borrowed novel and bought drawing manuals.
D4.zh: 6到12岁时，他描下借来的《荡寇志》绣像，又买画谱一页页描。

D9.en: At 18, he sat in a classroom at another school in Nanjing, the Mining and Railway School.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他坐在南京另一所矿路学堂的教室里。

D12.en: As the eldest grandson, he had to shoulder the family's responsibilities.
D12.zh: 他是长孙，顶门立户的重担落在他肩上。

D7.en: He and classmates stormed a tutor's empty school, smashing inkstones and breaking bamboo slips in defiance.
D7.zh: 他和同学冲到私塾，打翻砚台，折断竹签，大闹一通。

M5.en: He refused to feel inferior despite others' disdain, keeping his distance from worldly wealth and honor.
M5.zh: 别人的白眼和蔑视并没有使他自卑，他对世俗的荣华富贵躲得远远的。

D8.en: At age 15, he wanted to argue but was too sad to speak.
D8.zh: 15岁时，他想辩解却悲伤得说不出话。

M6.en: He buried himself in books, his paradise, and studied hard like his mother, swallowing tears.
M6.zh: 他钻到书里去，书是他的乐园，他像母亲一样有眼泪往肚里流，咬着牙发奋念书。

D17.en: He was born without milk; his mother fed him dried steamed bun paste and prayed over him when he was sick.
D17.zh: 我生下来就没有奶吃，母亲把馍馍晾干粉碎煮成糊喂我；我多病，母亲夜间放一碗清水在窗台上祷告神灵。

D16.en: At a classmate's wedding, he improvised a couplet praising the bride and groom, which everyone admired.
D16.zh: 中学毕业那年，在同学婚礼上他即席作对子“昔传城北徐公美，今说河东柳氏贤”，大家都说对得工。

D10.en: His eyes filled with tears and his ears went numb as his heart ached for his beloved mother.
D10.zh: 他的眼里装满了泪水，耳朵失去知觉，心痛他那可爱可敬而又可怜的妈妈。

D14.en: At temple fairs, he watched his mother more than the stage performances.
D14.zh: 庙会上，他看母亲比看戏还多。

D13a.en: He did not return home for his grandfather's deathbed because of exams, arriving after the funeral.
D13a.zh: 他因期考未回见祖父最后一面，到家时已盖棺。

D18.en: At 12-15, he could not distinguish his mother's needlework from his grandfather's as the sedan chair business thrived.
D18.zh: 12到15岁间，他分不清母亲的针线活和外公的，轿庄生意越来越红火。

D5.en: At about 12, he heard that following the cobblestone road leads to the destination and waited at the stone turtle.
D5.zh: 12岁左右，他听说沿着麻石路走就能到，就在石乌龟那儿等着。

D11.en: As a small child, he spent hours making toys from cotton, thread, and cloth scraps.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他用棉花、线和布头捏小鸡小鸟，玩上半天。

M1.en: He was top in his class in this subject and often praised by teachers.
M1.zh: 他这方面成绩拔尖，常得到老师夸奖。

D15.en: At age 9-10, he tried to help with waterwheeling and got his left leg injured by the pedal.
D15.zh: 他九到十岁时试车水，被水车踏脚打伤左腿。

D4.en: From age 5-6, he helped with the whole silkworm-raising process, from ant removal to cocooning.
D4.zh: 他从五六岁起就帮忙养蚕，从掸蚁到蚕上山都会做。

D7.en: At 15-16, he cried and threatened to run away when his father forbade him from studying.
D7.zh: 15到16岁时，他因父亲不让读书而大哭，并威胁要去流浪。

D2.en: As a child, he helped his grandmother water flowers, sweep, and assist with chores.
D2.zh: 他幼年时帮祖母浇花、撮土、打下手。

D3.en: He loved reading, daydreaming, and listening to stories, often going to a teahouse for storytelling with friends.
D3.zh: 他爱看书、爱幻想、爱听故事，常和小伙伴去小茶馆听说书。

M2.en: His grandfather died crawling home after being burned by the Eight-Nation Alliance, a tragedy that greatly influenced him.
M2.zh: 祖父被八国联军炮火烧伤后爬回家结束生命，这一幕对他影响极大。

M3.en: In middle school, she particularly admired Lin Yutang as a role model.
M3.zh: 中学时代的她特别心仪林语堂，把他当作参照人物。

M7.en: She tried to introduce Chinese painting style to America.
M7.zh: 她尽量把中国画的作风介绍到美国去。

D20.en: At 11, classmates read her romance novels in bed and argued over them.
D20.zh: 11岁时，同学晚上在被窝里读她写的言情小说，还因小说起小争执。

M4.en: At 16-17, she had only one thought: escape, leave this home.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，她只有一个念头：逃出去，离开这个家。

D1.en: At 12-17, she vowed to follow his path and surpass him in fame.
D1.zh: 12到17岁时，她发愿要走他的路，并在名气上超过他。

D14.en: At 18, she published her first work 'The Genius Dream' in West Wind Monthly.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她在《西风》月刊上发表第一篇作品《天才梦》。

D13a.en: At 18, she stepped out of her father's house, never to return.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她跨出父亲家大门，永远回不了那个家了。

D5.en: At 16-17, she walked on the street, each step a kiss on the ground.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，她走在人行道上，每一步都像在地上印下一个响亮的吻。

D11.en: At 16, she published two unsigned comic poems in Guoguang teasing male teachers.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她在《国光》上登了两首未署名的打油诗，调侃男教师。

M1.en: At 5-8, she vowed to outdo her brother after being bullied by servants.
M1.zh: 5到8岁时，她从佣人那儿受气后发誓要胜过弟弟。

D15.en: She watched two movies straight after getting off the train, then went home satisfied.
D15.zh: 他们一下火车就直奔电影院，一连看了两场，她才兴尽回家。

D9.en: She wrote 'Long Live the Wife', still directed by Sang Hu.
D9.zh: 她写了《太太万岁》，仍由桑弧执导。

D7.en: At 16 in middle school, she faced punishment for a satirical poem she helped publish.
D7.zh: 16岁上中学时，她因参与发表打油诗被校长要求道歉或停办刊物。

D6.en: In her 20s, she would return from Hangzhou to Shanghai to watch the same film twice.
D6.zh: 20多岁时，她会从杭州赶回上海连看两场同一部电影。

D3.en: At 18 in 1939, she was admitted to University of London but switched to HKU due to war.
D3.zh: 1939年18岁时，她考取伦敦大学但因战事改入香港大学。

D12.en: At 11 in 1931, she wore her stepmother's hand-me-downs after her parents divorced.
D12.zh: 1931年11岁时，父母离异后她只能穿继母的旧衣服。

D10.en: At 12-15, I locked myself in the bathroom, sobbing, and swore revenge.
D10.zh: 12到15岁时，我闩上门无声抽噎，看着眼泪流下，咬牙说要报仇。

D19.en: At 18, he nearly missed trains, kept incomplete accounts, and had gaps in his diary.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他差点错过火车，账目不全，日记也有空白。

D16.en: At 18, he wrote firmly in the margin, 'No'.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他在页边坚定地写下“不”。

D10.en: As a teenager, his rapid mood swings between elation and gloom caused constant anxiety.
D10.zh: 十几岁时，他情绪在兴高采烈和忧郁之间快速波动，让人持续焦虑。

D1.en: As a child, he resolved never to lose his temper with his family, and kept that resolution almost completely.
D1.zh: 幼年时，他决心不对家人发脾气，并且几乎完全做到了。

D13b.en: At 11, he was sent to junior school by his parents.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他被父母送到初中。

D5.en: At 14, he was so distressed at Cheltenham that he contemplated running away.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他在切尔滕纳姆非常痛苦，甚至考虑过逃跑。

M1.en: At age 18, he succeeded and went up to New College in 1926.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他成功进入新学院，于1926年入学。

D15.en: At 18, he wrote to his parents about his matches with the First Rugby XV.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他写信给父母讲述他参加第一橄榄球队比赛的事。

D9.en: He became Head Boy of Bradford.
D9.zh: 他成了布拉德福德的男学生长。

D7.en: As a teenager, he demanded to know what legitimate basis Jenifer claimed for her authority over him.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他质问Jenifer凭什么对他有权威。

D6.en: As a small child, he was part of a closely knit family, seeing much of relatives.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他生活在一个亲密的家庭中，经常见到亲戚。

D2.en: At 17-18, he won a large collection of prizes in subjects from Latin Verse to Political Science.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他赢得了一大堆奖项，科目从拉丁诗歌到政治学。

D19.en: At 6, he listed subjects and hours for home study in separate columns.
D19.zh: 6岁时，他把在家学习的科目和时间分别列成表格。

M3.en: At 15, he thought of a man: 'Here is a man who embodies my ideals.'
M3.zh: 15岁时，他想：这个人体现了我理想中的样子。

M6.en: At 18, he prayed: 'May my Heavenly Father bless and sanctify me to his service.'
M6.zh: 18岁时，他祈祷：愿天父赐福并使我圣洁，为他服务。

D20.en: At 18, he was unanimously elected senior delegate to the Delta Upsilon Convention.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被一致推选为高级代表，参加Delta Upsilon大会。

D16.en: At 18, he said: 'I gradually warmed with my theme, till something gave way in my head and I ran on profusely.'
D16.zh: 18岁时，他说：我逐渐进入状态，直到脑子里好像有什么东西打开了，滔滔不绝地讲起来。

D10.en: At 18, he said: 'I never was more worked up. I talked 20 minutes, bathed in perspiration, almost wilted.'
D10.zh: 18岁时，他说：我从未如此激动。讲了20分钟，浑身是汗，几乎虚脱。

D1.en: At 16, he blotted a page, recopied it leaving out lines, got only honorable mention.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他弄脏了一页纸，重抄时漏了十几行，只得了荣誉奖。

D14.en: At 18, he was the center of a crowd of boys congratulating him on his report.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他成为一群男孩的中心，他们围着他祝贺他的报告。

D13a.en: At 14, he joined a boarding club with fifteen to twenty new friends.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他加入了一个寄宿俱乐部，和十五到二十个新朋友一起。

D18.en: At 15, he wrote a letter claiming to be a ministerial student to get a free book.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他写信谎称自己是神学生，免费得到了一本书。

D5.en: At 16, he joined a forbidden cane rush that turned into a fistfight.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他参与了被禁止的抢棍活动，演变成混战。

D11.en: At 11, he wrote a school theme about Warwick Castle after visiting it.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他参观了沃里克城堡，回校后写了一篇相关作文。

M1.en: At 18, he proposed resolutions he drafted to support Garfield.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他提出自己起草的决议以支持加菲尔德。

D4.en: At 15, he worked on geometrical inventions and was asked by a professor to create an original demonstration.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他参与了几何发明，教授要求他创造原创证明。

D9.en: At 18, he spoke in class meeting for 20 minutes against cliques, sweating heavily.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他在班会上讲了20分钟反对小团体，汗流浃背。

D7.en: At 18, he aimed to reform others' opinions using lectures and subtle English.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他意图借助讲座和巧妙的英语改变他人的观念。

D6.en: At 18, he found the fellows he met to be a fine set.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他结识了一群很棒的伙伴。

D12.en: At 14, his parents wrote to his landlady that they could not yet send his board money.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他父母写信给他的房东说还没法寄他的食宿费。

D3.en: At 18, he attended a dissection and an operation, picking up information.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他参加了一次解剖和一次手术，学到了知识。

M7.en: He believed liberty depended on free interchange of ideas, even far from prevailing wisdom.
M7.zh: 他认为自由依赖于思想的自由交流，即使远离主流观点。

M2.en: In his late teens to early twenties, he abandoned philosophy for law due to family pressure.
M2.zh: 他十几岁到二十出头时，迫于家庭压力放弃哲学转向法律。

D18.en: At 9, he eagerly told his cousin Gus about his growing stamp collection.
D18.zh: 9岁时，他兴奋地告诉表弟Gus他不断增长的邮票收藏。

D15.en: At 16, he walked over 100 miles in the Alps with his headmaster.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他和校长在阿尔卑斯山徒步超过100英里。

D4.en: At 10, he joyfully prepared the evergreen hut at the campsite.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他快乐地搭建营地里的常青树小屋。

D9.en: At 16, Learned Hand went on a European trip with family and others in summer 1888.
D9.zh: 16岁时，Learned Hand在1888年夏天与家人等去欧洲旅行。

D7.en: He rejected the view that one's acquaintances should be governed by antecedents or parentage.
D7.zh: 他拒绝接受一个人的交往应受出身或家世支配的观点。

D6.en: As a teenager, he was involved with The Harvard Lampoon.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他参与了《哈佛讽刺》杂志。

D2.en: At 17-18, he worked as hard in his first year at Harvard as at Albany Academy.
D2.zh: 17到18岁间，他在哈佛第一年像在奥尔巴尼学院一样努力。

M8.en: At 14, he clung to Jesus Christ as his counselor, guide, comforter, and father.
M8.zh: 14岁时，他紧紧依靠耶稣基督作为他的顾问、向导、安慰者和父亲。

D3.en: At 8, he read a great deal, average only compared to Samuel Hand.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他大量阅读，只是与Samuel Hand相比才显得普通。

D17.en: As a teenager, she sat with her grandmother in the afternoons.
D17.zh: 十几岁时，她下午去陪祖母坐着。

D16.en: At 14, a desperate mob surrounded her and Eddie Irizarry, the two biggest mouths, asking them to plead the class's case.
D16.zh: 14岁时，一群绝望的人围住她和Eddie Irizarry这两个最能说的人，请他们为班级辩护。

D10.en: As a small child, she cried from pain while her mother rubbed sticky salve into her skin.
D10.zh: 幼年时，她因疼痛而哭，母亲一边把黏糊糊的药膏抹在她皮肤上一边也哭。

D1.en: At 8, she decided if she needed daily shots, the only way to survive was to do it herself.
D1.zh: 8岁时，她决定如果每天都要打针，唯一的活路就是自己来。

D14.en: At 12-13, she volunteered to do the Bible reading in church to test herself.
D14.zh: 12到13岁时，她主动报名在教堂读圣经来考验自己。

D18.en: At 15, she decided to get a job.
D18.zh: 15岁时，她决定去找工作。

D11.en: As a child, she loved poems and copied them onto slips of paper to learn them.
D11.zh: 她从小喜欢诗，把它们抄在小纸片上背诵。

M1.en: At 17-18, she wanted to apply to Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Columbia, Stanford.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，她想申请哈佛、耶鲁、普林斯顿、哥伦比亚、斯坦福。

D15.en: At 5-9, she walked barefoot because her shoes were too small.
D15.zh: 5到9岁时，她光脚走路因为鞋子太小。

D4.en: At 10-12, she raced over and stuck her head out for air until Titi shooed her back.
D4.zh: 10到12岁时，她跑过去探头呼吸，直到Titi把她赶回去。

D9.en: In high school, she signed up for a self-imposed preprofessional program in public speaking.
D9.zh: 高中时，她报名参加自己强加的公共演讲预科项目。

D7.en: At 5-9, she stood outside Tío Foro's store to listen to the jukebox.
D7.zh: 5到9岁时，她站在Tío Foro的店外听自动点唱机。

D6.en: At 18 in 1972, she found herself with a group of girls in the resident adviser's dorm room.
D6.zh: 1972年18岁时，她和一群女孩在宿舍管理员的房间里。

D2.en: At 5-9, she had to make two dozen handkerchiefs a week, stitching hems and ironing them.
D2.zh: 5到9岁时，她每周要做两打手帕，缝边并熨烫。

D3.en: At 5-9, the best part of school was the library and carrying home a book.
D3.zh: 5到9岁时，学校最好的部分是图书馆和带书回家。

M4.en: At about 17, she noted that after Pearl Harbor, Puerto Rican boys joined up as soon as they were old enough.
M4.zh: 大约17岁时，她注意到珍珠港事件后波多黎各男孩一到年龄就参军。

D14.en: At 15, she joined protests, speeches, and taught abacus at a night school.
D14.zh: 15岁时，她参加游行、讲演，还在平民夜校教珠算。

D13a.en: At 18, she broke off her engagement with her older cousin.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她解除了和大表哥的婚约。

D18.en: As a small child, her father died, and her mother faced discrimination from relatives.
D18.zh: 幼年时，父亲病故，母亲遭族人白眼。

D9.en: At 15, she joined student movements and taught abacus at a night school.
D9.zh: 15岁时，她参加学生运动，并在贫民夜校教珠算。

D7.en: At 17, she withdrew from school to protest the unjust dismissal of a teacher.
D7.zh: 17岁时，她为抗议校方无理解聘教师而退学。

D6.en: At 10, she moved to Taoyuan and attended the county girls' primary school.
D6.zh: 10岁时，她到桃源县立女子小学读书。

D2.en: At 11, she studied classical texts and foreign novels with her mother after school.
D2.zh: 11岁时，她课余随母亲学习《古文观止》、《论语》等古典文学和外国小说。

D3.en: At 15, she transferred to Zhounan Girls' School and was exposed to works by Bing Xin, Zhou Zuoren, Hu Shi, and others.
D3.zh: 15岁时，她转入周南女中，接触到冰心、周作人、胡适等人的作品。

D12.en: At 18, she entered the平民女子学校 founded by Chen Duxiu and Li Da in Shanghai.
D12.zh: 18岁时，她进入了陈独秀、李达等创办的平民女子学校（上海）。

M2.en: At 18, he was deeply moved by his father's kindness and borrowing money to support his education.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他永远难忘父亲的仁慈和借债供他读书的胸怀。

D20.en: At 16, he gathered friends and classmates to imitate Fiction Monthly and start a Fiction Daily.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他集合朋友同学模仿《小说月报》办了个《小说日报》。

M4.en: At 13, he admired Shi Kefa's unyielding spirit and often visited Meihualing to pay respects.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他非常景仰史可法的坚贞不屈，常常到梅花岭畔凭吊。

D1.en: After receiving rejection letters, he didn't lose heart; at 16, he gathered friends and classmates to start a Fiction Daily.
D1.zh: 收到退稿后他不泄气，16岁时集合朋友同学办了个《小说日报》。

D13b.en: At 18, he accepted arranged marriage as natural and saw nothing wrong with it.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他觉得婚姻大事遵从父母之命是天经地义的，没什么不妥。

M1.en: At 18, he graduated with excellent conduct and grades, receiving an award from the school.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他品行与学业俱优，毕业时校方授予品学兼优奖状。

D9.en: At 16, he gathered friends and classmates to start a Fiction Daily, writing in classical Chinese and mimeographing it himself.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他集合朋友同学办了个《小说日报》，用文言写作，自己油印。

D7.en: As a small child, he thought the statue's smile was excessive and secretly made a general statue.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他觉得天官脸上笑太多，偷偷塑起一座将军像。

D2.en: After class, he often went to the small river near campus to recite texts, read English aloud, or stroll alone.
D2.zh: 课后他常到校园前的小河边背诵课文、朗读英语或独自徜徉。

D8.en: He often played the leader, serving as 'governor' seven times and collected money to prepare weapons for his gang.
D8.zh: 他常常扮演头头的角色，当过七回‘都督’，还得凑钱为本帮兄弟预备军器。

M7.en: He joined a warlord's army.
M7.zh: 他参加军阀的部队。

D17.en: He learned to weave small baskets from straw and make whistles from桐木皮 like Uncle Teng in 'Long River'.
D17.zh: 他学会用稻草编小篮子，或者像《长河》里的滕伯伯卷桐木皮做成哨子吹曲子。

M2.en: At age 6, he heard family legends exaggerating ancestor Shen Hongfu's achievements, emphasizing descendants must live up to them.
M2.zh: 6岁时，他听到家庭传说夸大沈宏富的功名，强调后代必须无愧于祖宗功业。

D16.en: When the angry owner chased them with a bamboo pole, he fled and sang folk songs to taunt the owner.
D16.zh: 当愤怒的主人拿着竹竿追来时，他飞奔而逃，还唱山歌气那主人。

D10.en: As a child, he repeatedly lied to cover up truancy, angering his father who threatened to cut off one of his fingers.
D10.zh: 他少年时一再说谎掩饰逃学，引起父亲愤怒，说再逃学说谎就要当众砍去他一个手指。

D1.en: At 6-12, he lied and played truant, causing his father to threaten to cut off his finger.
D1.zh: 6到12岁时，他因逃学说谎，父亲威胁要砍他手指。

D14.en: At 6, he was fascinated by the market fair held every five days near Fenghuang.
D14.zh: 6岁时，他迷上了凤凰西郊每五天一次的赶场。

D18.en: At 6, he learned to gamble with leftover money from buying groceries.
D18.zh: 6岁时，他用买菜剩的零钱赌骰子，后来把买菜钱全赌了。

D5.en: At 13, he and friends would row a boat downstream while cursing back at angry boat owners.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他和伙伴把船放向下游，回骂愤怒的船主。

D11.en: At 6, when punished to kneel in a corner, his imagination flew to exciting scenes he had seen.
D11.zh: 6岁时，被罚跪在角落时，他想象如生翅膀，飞到见过的动人事物上。

M1.en: At 6, he may have been pushed by his parents to strive for officialdom to compensate for his father's mediocrity.
M1.zh: 6岁时，他也许被父母推动去拼命奋斗，谋个一官半职，弥补父亲的庸碌。

D15.en: At 13, he played 'dragon-tiger fight' on the playground and raced to climb big trees.
D15.zh: 13岁时，他在操场玩龙虎斗，或和同学比赛爬大树。

D4.en: At 6-14, he loved watching various handicrafts like rope-making, weaving, and blacksmithing.
D4.zh: 6到14岁时，他喜欢看绞绳子、织竹簟、打铁等各种手工。

D9.en: At 15, he volunteered to buy dog meat with others' money, sometimes paying himself.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他自告奋勇去买狗肉，有时自己出钱。

D7.en: At 7, he resisted punishment for truancy and grew defiant, playing pranks with classmates.
D7.zh: 7岁时，他因逃学挨打罚跪，反而滋长抵抗情绪，还和学伴一起恶作剧。

D6.en: At 16-17, as a clerk, he socialized with superiors, cooking dog meat and playing the flute.
D6.zh: 16到17岁当文书时，他和高级人物打成一片，炖狗肉请上司，一起吹短箫。

D2.en: At 13, he enjoyed school reforms: less recitation, no severe beatings, and weekly holidays.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他不用成天背书，体罚废除，每七天有假日。

D12.en: At 16, he was promoted to sergeant clerk, got along with officers, and recorded confessions.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他被提升为上士文书，和官长混熟，审问犯人时记口供。

D16.en: At age 10-12, he chatted eloquently when asked about recent reading.
D16.zh: 10到12岁时，他被人问起最近读什么书，便侃侃而谈。

D13b.en: Qian Zhongshu's father Qian Jibo taught at Tsinghua for a year.
D13b.zh: 钱锺书父亲钱基博曾在清华教过一年书

D11.en: At age 6-7, he excitedly reenacted a novel's plot for his younger brother and cousins.
D11.zh: 6到7岁时，他回家后手舞足蹈地向弟弟和堂弟复述小说情节。

M1.en: At age 18, he won first place in Chinese and English in a school-wide competition.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在全校比赛中得了国文和英文第一名。

D7.en: At age 15, he freely read large amounts of popular magazines.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他借了大批《小说世界》等刊物恣意阅读。

D6.en: At ages 4-6, he followed his uncle to teahouses and storytelling sessions.
D6.zh: 4到6岁时，他跟着伯父去茶馆和听说书。

D2.en: At 15, he began systematic reading of classical anthologies during summer vacation, marking the start of his lifelong scholarship.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他暑假回家开始系统阅读《古文辞类纂》等选本，这是他一生志学之始。

D12.en: He was born on November 21, 1910, and less than a year later the Xinhai Revolution overthrew the Qing dynasty.
D12.zh: 他生于1910年11月21日，不到一岁时辛亥革命推翻了清朝。

D5.en: At age 11, he played with a slingshot in class, shooting mud pellets at others.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他上课玩弹弓，用泥丸弹人。

D4.en: At age 11, he caught small frogs in his shoes on a rainy day.
D4.zh: 11岁时，下雨天他脱了钉鞋，把小青蛙抓起来放在鞋里。

D10.en: At 12, she trembled and said 'Your pants leg' with a sense of doom.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她浑身战栗地说'你的裤腿'，感觉大祸临头。

D13a.en: At 10-12, unable to answer a question, she humbly pointed at something random.
D13a.zh: 10到12岁时，她回答不出责问，卑屈地瞎指了一个。

D4.en: At 5-10, she tied a rope to a tricycle and pulled it with her doll to grandma's house.
D4.zh: 5到10岁时，她在车头拴绳子拖着走，带娃娃去外婆家。

D7.en: At 12, she stepped forward and said 'Comrade, wait!' to a woman, then said 'Your pants leg'.
D7.zh: 12岁时，她一步上前对女人说'同志，等一等'，然后说'你的裤腿'。

D6.en: At 18, she gathered with friends in Shanghai, visiting each other and sharing music study tips.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她和一伙人聚集在上海，互相串门交流音乐学习感想。

D3.en: At 18, she went to a female classmate's home to listen to a teacher explain harmony techniques.
D3.zh: 18岁时，她去一个女同学家听老师讲和声技法。

D20X.en: At 16, while at Hangzhou Fu Middle School, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学，与张幼仪订婚。

D8.en: At 18, he reluctantly agreed to an arranged marriage due to parental pressure and the Zhang family's influence.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他因父母之命和张家的声势，不情愿地订婚并结婚。

D20.en: At 16, he was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D10.en: At 18, he was dissatisfied with the marriage but went through with it due to parental pressure.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他对这桩婚姻不满意，但碍于父母之命还是订婚并结婚了。

D14.en: As a teenager, he would unexpectedly do something light and peculiar to attract attention.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他会出其不意地做出一件很轻快奇特的事情来吸引大家的注意。

D13b.en: At 16, he was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at the prefectural school.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他在府中上学期间与张幼仪订婚了。

D13a.en: At 16, he was engaged by his parents.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他由父母做主订了婚。

M1.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while attending Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D9.en: As a teenager, he often served as class monitor because he ranked first in exams.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他因为考试得第一，常当班长。

D7.en: At 18, he reluctantly agreed to an arranged marriage due to parental pressure.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他因父母之命勉强同意了一桩包办婚姻。

D6.en: At 13, he met Jiang Fucong during a memorial event for Lin Dichen at Gushan.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他在孤山凭吊林迪臣时与蒋复璁相识。

D2.en: At 16, his calligraphy showed firm purpose and direction, and natural grace from years of training.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他的书法显示出坚定的目的和方向，以及经过多年训练后的自然神韵。

D3.en: As a teenager, he was often seen reading novels printed on glossy paper instead of studying.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他平时不用功，总爱看石印细字的小说。

D8.en: At 12, she drew a caricature of Fei Xiaotong in the sand with a stick.
D8.zh: 12岁时，她用树枝在沙地上给费孝通画了个丑像。

D19.en: As a small child, she added coal to the stove without making a sound.
D19.zh: 幼年时，她加煤能不出声。

D1.en: At 9-12, she skipped grades in English, moving from the lowest class to intermediate.
D1.zh: 9到12岁时，她学英文跳班，从小班升到中班。

D13a.en: At 9, her mother told her to decide for herself about going to school.
D13a.zh: 9岁时，妈妈要她自己拿主意。

D11.en: At 16, she published two five-character ancient-style poems in the first issue of a journal.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她在第一期上发表了两首五古诗。

M1.en: At 17, she graduated a year early from high school with top scores, then ranked first in the entrance exam for Soochow University.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她提前一年毕业，成绩常列第一，在东吴大学入学考试中仍考第一。

D15.en: At 9-12, the teacher asked her to demonstrate a jumping exercise for the class.
D15.zh: 9到12岁时，老师叫她出来给大家示范跳跃动作。

D9.en: At 9-12, she led the class in gym because she understood the English commands.
D9.zh: 9到12岁时，她因听懂英语口令被老师挑中领队。

D7.en: At 16, she refused to do propaganda work, and the school did not punish her.
D7.zh: 16岁时，她拒绝去宣传，学校后来也没追究。

D2.en: At 12-16, she read widely under her father's influence, building a foundation for literary creation.
D2.zh: 12到16岁时，她在父亲影响下广泛阅读，为文学创作打下基础。

M5.en: He was robbed and went hungry for half a day, but was too ashamed to tell anyone.
M5.zh: 遭这一场抢劫，使他饿了大半天的肚子，却不敢对人言语，觉得太丢人。

D19.en: He chopped all the firewood into pieces of similar size, stacked them neatly on the steps, and sat on the stone to admire them.
D19.zh: 他用斧子把所有的柴劈碎，粗细长短差不多整齐，再一一在台阶上垒好了，然后坐在院中的捶布石上观赏。

M7.en: He tipped off Sanwa's family about a study session.
M7.zh: 他给三娃家通风报信过学习班的事。

M2.en: He had to help his father write an appeal for rehabilitation because his father's hands trembled too much to hold a pen.
M2.zh: 他得帮父亲写翻案的申诉书，因为父亲患了手颤的病，一提笔手颤得握不住笔。

D16.en: He talked a lot but kept circling around the point, and when about to hit it, he would veer off into trivial talk.
D16.zh: 他话说得多，却只是兜圈儿，眼看着要兜到圈中了，一拐又说起不咸不淡的话。

D10.en: He squatted on a grave mound and suddenly felt terrified, suspecting a female demon would emerge from a rat hole.
D10.zh: 他蹲在那坟丘上突然感到恐怖，总疑心从鼠洞里冒出一股烟，烟雾散去要现出女妖怪来。

D1.en: At about 18, his father told him he believed he would leave the mountains, and now university enrollment was opening.
D1.zh: 大约18岁时，他父亲对他说，一直认为他不该这样下去，会从山里出去，现在大学开始招生了。

D14.en: At 18, his diary was often taken and read aloud publicly, even circulated to other companies.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他的日记常被人偷去当众念，还流传到别的连队。

D13b.en: He graduated from junior high school in 1967 at age 14.
D13b.zh: 他是1967年的初中毕业生，那时14岁。

D13a.en: At 17 in 1970, his mother arranged a match with a cultured girl, but he didn't take it seriously.
D13a.zh: 1970年他17岁时，母亲托人给他说了一门亲事，对方是干部女儿有文化，但他并不在意。

D18.en: As a teenager, he and his brother dug up tree roots all day for firewood, filling three baskets.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他和弟弟刨了一天树根，弄到三背篓劈柴。

D5.en: As a teenager, he recognized a stranger as a defector and said so; his parents warned him to keep quiet.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他认出陌生人是偷渡者并说出；父母吓一跳，叮嘱他别说出去。

D11.en: As a teenager, he envied another class's big-character posters with new phrases like 'Sima Zhao's heart is known to all'.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他羡慕别班的大字报上有新词新句，比如‘司马昭之心，路人皆知’。

M1.en: At 15, he pushed himself to reach a rest stop, a habit that became his key to success.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他咬牙赶到歇脚地，这种劲头成为他成功的保证。

D15.en: At 16-18, he was stung by wasps while cutting grass, applied snot, and crossed waist-deep river with grass.
D15.zh: 16到18岁时，他割草被蜂蜇，用鼻涕涂抹，背着草篓趟过齐腰深的河水。

D4.en: At 15, he and two classmates built a three-step platform, doing good deeds like Lei Feng.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他和两个同学修了三层台阶，学雷锋做好事。

D7.en: At 14-17, he blurted out a disrespectful nickname for a teacher and was scolded by classmates.
D7.zh: 14到17岁时，他忍不住冒了一句“那田麻子”，被旁边几个人训斥。

D2.en: At 18, he sat in the front row, letting the teacher's spit land on his face without moving.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他坐在头一排，老师卷舌音的唾沫溅湿他的脸，他一动不动。

D10.en: He and his classmates were constantly anxious about masturbation during adolescence.
D10.zh: 他和同学们整天因为手淫而惶惶不可终日。

D13a.en: As a small child, he was obedient and sat still all day at kindergarten.
D13a.zh: 他小时候很听话，在幼儿园从早坐到晚不动。

D18.en: He traded a meat bun for his brother's belt for a day.
D18.zh: 他用一个肉包子交换哥哥的腰带使用一天。

D5.en: At age 8, he was tricked into going to a distant village and cried bitterly at a stranger's funeral.
D5.zh: 他八岁时被同学骗到远村，在陌生人的葬礼上号啕大哭。

D11.en: He used a whole notebook to compose Lu Xun's 'Diary of a Madman' into an unperformable musical score.
D11.zh: 他用整册作业本把鲁迅的《狂人日记》谱成了无法演唱的乐曲。

D7.en: As a child, he chased his brother with a racket and hit him in the head after being provoked.
D7.zh: 小时候，他被哥哥激怒后拿起球拍追打哥哥，飞出的球拍击中了哥哥的脑袋。

D6.en: He felt anxiety and fear with friends during adolescent enlightenment.
D6.zh: 他在青春期启蒙过程中与朋友一起感到焦灼和惶恐。

D3.en: At age 12, he and his brother got library cards and were overjoyed.
D3.zh: 他12岁时和哥哥拿到图书馆借书证，欣喜若狂。

D12.en: At 4, he began walking home alone after his older brother often forgot him and ran off to play.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他开始自己走回家，因为哥哥经常忘记他，自己跑去玩。

M8.en: She grew up in a Christian family, deeply influenced by Christianity.
M8.zh: 她生长在基督教家庭，深受基督教影响。

D14.en: At about 16, she demonstrated painting a peony in 15 minutes on educational TV.
D14.zh: 大约16岁时，她随邵幼轩上教育电视台，示范15分钟完成牡丹

D13a.en: She refused to go to school after second year of junior high, and her parents let her drop out.
D13a.zh: 她初二就不肯再去学校，父母只好让她休学。

D11.en: She signed her first self-given name ECHO in the corner of a painting.
D11.zh: 她在画右下角签下今生给自己取的第一个名字ECHO。

D4.en: At 18, she followed a friend's teacher Gu Fusheng to learn sketching.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她跟随朋友的老师顾福生学素描。

D7.en: At 12, she was hit by ink on her face, started skipping school, and dropped out.
D7.zh: 12岁时，她受墨汁涂面打击，开始逃学，休学在家。

D6.en: At 10-11, she got into a fight with a boy on a ridge after class.
D6.zh: 10到11岁时，她下课后在田埂上跟男生打了一架。

D2.en: At 13, she began practicing writing, music, and painting, and attempted suicide by stabbing.
D2.zh: 13岁时，她开始练习写作、音乐、绘画，切腹自杀获救。

D3.en: At 11.5, she secretly read Dream of the Red Chamber under her skirt in class.
D3.zh: 11岁半时，她在课堂上把《红楼梦》藏在裙子下面偷偷地读。

D5.en: At age 3, she followed a helper to Pingtung and took a fishing boat to Ryukyu.
D5.zh: 她3岁时跟着家中工人到屏东，又坐渔船去琉球。

D4.en: He learned oil painting and sculpture from artist Yun Zongying at school.
D4.zh: 他在学校跟随艺术家Yun Zongying学习油画和雕塑。

D3.en: He learned practical aspects of play production not found in books.
D3.zh: 他学到了书本上学不到的戏剧制作的实际方面。

D12.en: He performed with his younger brother for their mother.
D12.zh: 他和弟弟为母亲表演。

D19.en: He befriended two coworkers at the county cotton processing factory, knowing their powerful relatives could help him.
D19.zh: 他在县棉花加工厂结识了两名职工，知道他们长辈有权，特意团结他们。

M7.en: As a child, he joined others in tasting hundreds of plants and insects to expand the human diet.
M7.zh: 他小时候和其他人一起尝遍百草百虫，为扩充人类食谱做贡献。

D17.en: He secretly brought beans and carrots to nurse a mother rabbit after she gave birth.
D17.zh: 他偷偷弄来豆子和红萝卜给母兔子坐月子补充营养。

D20.en: He united a few close friends, formed the 'Caltrop Combat Team', and wrote its manifesto.
D20.zh: 他联合几个死党成立蒺藜战斗队，并亲自写战斗宣言。

M4.en: He privately complained to classmates that school was a prison and the teacher was like a slave owner.
M4.zh: 他私下跟同学嘀咕说学校是大监狱，老师是奴隶主。

D16.en: He shouted revolutionary slogans, calling to smash the bourgeois education system.
D16.zh: 他喊造反口号，要砸烂资产阶级教育路线。

D10.en: He often lost his resolve when seeing food, despite his mother's criticism.
D10.zh: 他常被母亲批评没志气，一见食物就忘了一切。

D1.en: He applied to join the army every year from age 18 but was rejected each time due to his family's class background.
D1.zh: 他十八岁起每年报名参军都被刷下来，因为家庭成分是富裕中农。

D14.en: At 11, he was chosen to recite from memory at a Children's Day event due to his good memory.
D14.zh: 11岁时，他因为记忆力好被选上在六一儿童节背书。

D13b.en: He spent seven years from 1966 to 1973 quietly growing up in the countryside.
D13b.zh: 他从1966到1973年七年里在田野间悄无声息地成长。

D13a.en: At about 12, he dropped out of school and stayed home.
D13a.zh: 12岁左右，他辍学在家。

D18.en: At 18 in 1973, he got a seasonal worker job at a cotton processing factory through his uncle's connection.
D18.zh: 1973年18岁时，他通过五叔的关系走后门进棉花加工厂做季节工。

D5.en: He wrote a letter to the Minister of Education, imitating the trendsetter Zhang Tiesheng.
D5.zh: 他模仿当时的风云人物张铁生，给教育部长周荣鑫写了一封信。

D11.en: At 18, he bought ink and a notebook with 50 cents from his mother and started writing a story titled 'By the Jiao-Lai River'.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他跟母亲要了五毛钱买墨水笔记本，趴在炕上开始写，文名叫《胶莱河畔》。

M1.en: At 6-12, he invented a trick to get his grandmother's reward and kept it secret even from his grandfather.
M1.zh: 6到12岁时，他为了得到奶奶的奖赏，自己发明了诀窍，连爷爷也不告诉。

D15.en: At 18 in 1973, he worked as a strong laborer digging the Jiao-Lai River with villagers.
D15.zh: 1973年18岁时，他作为壮劳力跟着村里人去昌邑县挖胶莱河。

D4.en: As a child, he invented a trick of dyeing his hands green with grass juice before catching grasshoppers.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他发明了捉蚂蚱前先用青草汁把手染绿的诀窍。

D9.en: At 10-15, he formed the 'Caltrop Rebel Squad', burned the school curriculum, and wrote a manifesto.
D9.zh: 10到15岁时，他成立了“蒺藜造反小队”，焚烧了学校的课程表，并写了战斗檄文《造反造反造他妈的反》。

D6.en: He met people from all over the country at the factory, broadening his horizons and honing his skills.
D6.zh: 他在厂里结识了来自五湖四海的人，开阔了眼界，也锻炼了自己。

D2.en: After applying to join the army every year since 1973, he finally succeeded in 1976 and became a soldier.
D2.zh: 他从1973年开始每年报名参军，终于在1976年蒙混过关，当上了解放军。

D3.en: At 11-18, after dropping out, he wandered at home, studied medicine with his great-uncle, and memorized hundreds of herb names.
D3.zh: 11到18岁时，他辍学后在家乡游荡，跟随伯祖学习医术，把几百味中药名字背得滚瓜烂熟。

D12.en: After dropping out of school at age 11-14, he herded cattle and sheep alone in distant pastures, not yet a full laborer.
D12.zh: 辍学后的头几年，他还不算整劳力，只能一个人赶着牛羊到很远的地方放牧。

D8.en: At 9-15, he directed and commanded peers like a little flock, crossing into tyranny.
D8.zh: 9到15岁时，他像指挥小羊群一样指挥同伴，并越界成为暴君。

M2.en: At 6-10, he was driven more by fear than curiosity or ambition, his parents thought.
M2.zh: 6到10岁时，他更多是出于恐惧而非好奇或野心，他父母这么认为。

D16.en: At 10-14, he could recite demanding texts like translations of Ovid and Homer.
D16.zh: 10到14岁时，他能背诵奥维德和荷马译作等艰深文本。

D13b.en: He was born at the edge of the ghetto in Prague, shortly before its ultimate demise.
D13b.zh: 他出生在布拉格犹太人区边缘，不久后该区就彻底消失了。

D5.en: At 11, he decided to risk it, thinking he could play dumb if need be.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他决定冒险，认为必要时可以装傻。

D11.en: At 12-13, he turned to writing novel-length prose, a less rule-bound pursuit.
D11.zh: 12到13岁时，他转向写长篇小说，一种不那么循规蹈矩的追求。

M1.en: At 12, he was the top pupil in his class, standing out for different reasons than before.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他是班上成绩最好的学生，但这次引人注目的原因与以往不同。

D15.en: He earned the Freischwimmer swimming certificate.
D15.zh: 他获得了Freischwimmer游泳证书。

D4.en: At 8, he went to the exhibition at least twice.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他至少去了两次展览。

D9.en: At age 11, his father Hermann talked his way out of criminal proceedings.
D9.zh: 11岁时，他父亲Hermann靠口才免于刑事诉讼。

D7.en: At 14, Czech students' procession was dispersed by mounted police.
D7.zh: 14岁时，捷克学生的游行被骑警驱散。

D6.en: He began university studies at the German University in Prague, spending time in Munich.
D6.zh: 他开始在布拉格的德国大学学习，并在慕尼黑待了一段时间。

D2.en: He said he was half delighted that he was actually studying at last, and would not come to the café that week.
D2.zh: 他说他有点高兴终于真正在学习，因此那周不会去咖啡馆。

D3.en: He had his Nietzsche summer, the major Zarathustra experience, two years earlier.
D3.zh: 他两年前经历了他的尼采之夏，即重要的查拉图斯特拉体验。

D14.en: At 13, he had to learn a Torah portion by heart and chant it before the congregation.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他必须背下一段《托拉》并在会众面前吟唱。

M5.en: At 17, he felt as if he had left a part of his moral being there.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他感觉自己把一部分道德自我留在了那里。

D8.en: As a small child, he would frighten playmates by seizing their hand and announcing his need to possess them like a tyrant.
D8.zh: 幼年时，他会抓住玩伴的手，像暴君一样宣告要占有他们，把他们吓坏。

M6.en: At 15-16, he wrote that discovering laws of human nature consoles for life's bitterness.
M6.zh: 15到16岁时，他写道，发现人性的规律可以慰藉人生的苦涩与脆弱。

D17.en: At 18, he was prohibited from bathing by his caregiver, who feared he might fall.
D17.zh: 18岁时，他被照顾者禁止洗澡，因为她担心他站不稳会摔倒。

D20.en: At 16, he and a few classmates began producing short-lived little magazines.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他和几个同学开始办一些短命的小杂志。

D16.en: At 17, still enamored of Jacques, he handed him a letter at school inviting him to have sex.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他仍迷恋Jacques，在学校递给他一封信，邀请他发生性关系。

D10.en: At 17, he wrote in a rage when his mother forbade him to see Jacques or invite him over.
D10.zh: 17岁时，母亲禁止他见Jacques或邀请他来家里，他愤怒地写了信。

D14.en: At 17, he wrote out flattering comments on the piece and had them forwarded to the author.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他写了恭维的评论并转寄给了作者。

D13b.en: As an infant, he was baptized at the parish church of Saint-Louis d’Antin.
D13b.zh: 他幼年时在圣路易-昂坦教堂受洗。

D13a.en: He told his father that anything outside literature and philosophy would be wasted time.
D13a.zh: 他对父亲说，任何文学和哲学之外的事情都是浪费时间。

D11.en: He wrote an assignment for a tutor recounting an episode from the Roman sacking of Corinth.
D11.zh: 他为家庭教师写了一篇作业，讲述罗马洗劫科林斯的一个片段。

M1.en: At age 12, he received the fifth certificate in French, fourth in Latin, and second prize in natural sciences.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他获得了法语第五名证书、拉丁语第四名证书和自然科学二等奖。

D15.en: At age eight, he tripped and broke his nose while playing on the Champs-Élysées.
D15.zh: 8岁时，他在香榭丽舍大街玩耍时绊倒摔断了鼻子。

D4.en: He could stare transfixed at a rose or anything on his wavelength.
D4.zh: 他能目不转睛地盯着玫瑰或任何与他合拍的东西。

D9.en: He and friends created a review, with him as secretary.
D9.zh: 他和朋友们创办了一份评论刊物，他担任秘书。

D7.en: He stubbornly insisted on daily outings to the Champs-Élysées to meet his sweetheart.
D7.zh: 他固执地坚持每天去香榭丽舍大街见他的新恋人。

D6.en: He wrote to his grandmother, embarrassed by how much he was eating.
D6.zh: 他写信给祖母，对自己吃那么多感到不好意思。

D2.en: As a child, I was a good boy with insatiable curiosity and a passion for learning.
D2.zh: 小时候，我是个充满好奇心和求知欲的好孩子。

D3.en: In an 1888 letter to his mother, he recalled happy days at Le Tréport enjoying the sea air and walks.
D3.zh: 在1888年给母亲的信中，他回忆起在勒特雷波尔享受海风和散步的快乐时光。

M3.en: At 18, he sent an anonymous pneumatic mail to France, proclaiming his admiration for France's books.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他给France寄了一封匿名气动信，表达对France作品的钦佩。

D14.en: At 13, he won first prize in a Hong Kong children's art competition with his painting Children's Day.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他代表学校参加全港小学生美术比赛，画作《儿童节》得了冠军。

D11.en: At 7-8, he listened to Old Jiang tell the story of Xue Rengui's Eastern Expedition, his first novel.
D11.zh: 7到8岁时，他听老央讲《薛仁贵征东》，那是他开宗明义第一本小说。

M1.en: At 15, he became the top student in his class in junior high.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他在初三成为班上第一名。

D6.en: He was closest to classmates Sun Bina, Qiu Jizi, and Yang Wenjiu.
D6.zh: 他和女同学孙碧娜、裘寄梓和男同学杨文就的关系最好。

D2.en: He worked hard on Chinese classics and hired a tutor for extra lessons in summers 1955-56.
D2.zh: 他高中三年努力学好国文，还在1955和1956年暑假请老师缪天华为自己开小灶。

D3.en: He enjoyed memorizing Sima Qian's biography of General Li, unlike many classmates.
D3.zh: 他不但不觉得默写《史记·李将军列传》是苦事，反而以此为乐。

D19.en: He drew many maps, knowing more than his geography teacher.
D19.zh: 他画了许许多多地图，他知道的比地理老师还多。

M6.en: He vaguely felt he would become a poet.
M6.zh: 他隐隐然觉得自己会成为诗人。

M2.en: His name meant to glorify China and live in auspicious light.
M2.zh: 他的名字光中，是勉励他光耀中华，祝福他生活在吉祥的光环中。

D16.en: He won first place in English composition, second in Chinese composition, and third in English speech.
D16.zh: 他夺得英文作文第一名，中文作文第二名，英文演讲第三名。

D1.en: He and his mother endured hardships to escape the occupied zone via Shanghai to Hong Kong.
D1.zh: 他和母亲历经半年波折，从沦陷区经上海乘船逃到香港。

D14.en: His cousins liked him for his ability to draw horses and door gods.
D14.zh: 表兄妹都喜欢他能画马和门神。

D18.en: He bought an old Turkish map with his meager allowance.
D18.zh: 他用微薄的零用钱买了一张旧的土耳其地图。

D5.en: He and his mother fled to a temple where Japanese troops also stayed.
D5.zh: 他和母亲逃入佛寺，日军也在那里驻扎。

D11.en: He read Chinese old novels to understand folk customs and bridge classical and vernacular Chinese.
D11.zh: 他读中国旧小说，认识民情风土，在文言与白话之间搭桥。

M1.en: He studied and drew maps so much that he knew more than his geography teacher.
M1.zh: 他神游并绘制地图，发现所知已超过地理老师。

D15.en: He couldn't be a team player but joyfully became a cheerleader.
D15.zh: 他不能成为球队队员，但兴高采烈地当啦啦队员。

D4.en: He drew maps for clumsy female classmates who asked him to.
D4.zh: 笨手笨脚的女同学常央他代绘中国全图。

D9.en: He never refused to draw maps for classmates, but always left a mistake in each.
D9.zh: 他从不拒绝代绘地图，但每张必留一个错误。

D6.en: His cousins liked him because he could draw horses and door gods.
D6.zh: 表兄妹都喜欢这位能画马画门神的小表弟。

D2.en: He recited classical prose aloud every night by lamplight, admiring its elegance.
D2.zh: 每晚就着桐油灯光，低回高亢地习诵古文，赞叹其工整典丽。

D3.en: He and a classmate tested their teacher's knowledge of classical allusions.
D3.zh: 他和同学持古文请教老师，有意考其深浅。

D12.en: His father left Nanjing without notice when the government evacuated, leaving him and his mother behind.
D12.zh: 父亲随政府西撤，未及告别，留下孤儿寡母在沦陷区。

D20X.en: He was a classmate of Haibo at Yanchuan County Chengguan Primary School, one grade above him, and Haibo often followed him.
D20X.zh: 他和海波是延川县城关小学同学，他高海波一个年级，海波常常跟随他。

D1.en: He cried to Liu Junkuan that the school had stopped accepting him, and Liu interceded with the principal to let him in.
D1.zh: 他哭着找到刘俊宽说学校不收他了，刘俊宽去县中学找校长说情，校长破例收了他。

D14.en: At 11-12, he learned tunes by whistling, winning the teacher's favor.
D14.zh: 11到12岁时，他用吹口哨学曲子，赢得老师另眼看待。

D13b.en: He was adopted by his uncle in Guojiagou Village, but without any formal ceremony.
D13b.zh: 他被过继给郭家沟村的大伯，却没有这里面的任何形式。

D18.en: As a small child around five or six, he fell into a mountain water hole while gathering firewood but miraculously survived.
D18.zh: 他五六岁时上山砍柴从高崖跌进山水窟窿，但又奇迹般地活了。

D11.en: At 11-12, he made up rhymes and stories that made people laugh, his first creative attempts.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他编顺口溜和故事逗人笑，可能是最初的创作尝试。

M1.en: At 14, he ranked second in the county exam.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他考了全县第二名。

D15.en: At 14-15, he played basketball well and became a main player on the school team.
D15.zh: 14到15岁时，他篮球打得好，成为校队主力队员。

D4.en: As a child, he devoured books like a hungry calf in a vegetable garden, refusing to leave until closing time.
D4.zh: 他像一头馋嘴牛犊闯进菜园子，拼命地啃食，不到下班关门，他绝不离开这个地方。

D9.en: At 11-12, he became class monitor due to his excellent grades and organizational skills.
D9.zh: 11到12岁时，他凭借优异成绩和组织才能当上班长。

D7.en: At age 8-9, he lost control of his emotions and fought a boy, breaking his head.
D7.zh: 8到9岁时，他控制不住情绪，和一个男孩扭打在一起，把对方头打破了。

D6.en: At 11-12, his speeches in class attracted most classmates' attention.
D6.zh: 11到12岁时，他每次在班上宣讲，总能吸引绝大多数同学的目光。

D2.en: At 8-12, he could listen to lessons for higher grades in class.
D2.zh: 8到12岁时，他一年级就能听二年级的课，三年级能听四年级的课。

D3.en: At 14-15, he befriended the school librarian and secretly borrowed banned books to read in a ravine.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，他和县中学图书馆管理员搞好关系，偷偷借出禁书，钻到校园后面的山沟里阅读。

D12.en: As a small child, he helped with chores like watching siblings, gathering pigweed in summer, and chopping wood in winter.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他就跟在大人后面干力所能及的事：照看弟弟妹妹，夏天寻猪草，冬天砍柴。

D19.en: She wrote and described the Hyde Park Gate News at age 9-15.
D19.zh: 她9到15岁时撰写并描述了《海德公园门新闻》。

D10.en: At 15, she made 'very furious and tantrumical' preparations for a wet family holiday.
D10.zh: 15岁时，她为一次潮湿的家庭假期做了'非常愤怒和发脾气'的准备。

D14.en: As a child, she was nicknamed 'the Goat' for her comical eccentricities.
D14.zh: 幼年时，她因滑稽古怪被起外号‘山羊’。

D13b.en: Her birth was registered as 'Adeline Virginia' on 16 February 1882.
D13b.zh: 1882年2月16日，她的出生被登记为'阿德琳·弗吉尼亚'。

D18.en: She projected a work titled History of Women.
D18.zh: 她计划写一部名为《女性史》的作品。

D11.en: At 9-13, she contributed to The Hyde Park Gate News (1891-1892, 1895).

D4.en: As a young girl, she learned bookbinding and did silver-point printing with Vanessa.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她学会装订，并与Vanessa一起做过银尖画印刷。

D7.en: At 15, she refused to kneel during a church service.
D7.zh: 15岁时，她拒绝在教堂礼拜中下跪。

D2.en: As a child (8-18), she was taught to talk about nerves and did so with angry impatience.
D2.zh: 8到18岁间，她被教导谈论神经紧张，并带着愤怒的不耐烦这样做。

D3.en: At 6, she wrote to her godfather asking about the Adirondacks and wild beasts.
D3.zh: 6岁时，她写信给教父询问阿迪朗达克山脉和野兽。

D1.en: At 13-14, she came top in monthly exams, beating V by 29 marks.
D1.zh: 13到14岁时，她月考得了第一，比V高出29分。

D8.en: At age 6-9, he stood confidently before the tallest boy in third class.
D8.zh: 6到9岁时，他自信地站在三年级最高的男孩面前。

D19.en: At 12, he meticulously lent money to siblings and parents, recording it in a ledger.
D19.zh: 12岁时，他像精细的会计一样慷慨借钱给兄弟姐妹和父母，并记在账本上。

M3.en: At 17, he published an article on Ibsen in the London Fortnightly Review.
M3.zh: 17岁时，他在伦敦《双周评论》上发表了一篇关于易卜生的文章。

M4.en: At 18, he admired Ibsen's reticence and refusal to engage enemies.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他欣赏易卜生的沉默和拒绝与敌人争斗的态度。

D16.en: At 18, he spoke for at least thirty minutes without notes, answering each critic.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他至少讲了半小时，不看稿子，逐一回应批评者。

D1.en: At 18, Archer read Joyce's work, noted the dedication, but returned it with critical regrets.
D1.zh: 18岁时，Archer读了乔伊斯的作品，记下献词，但附上批评意见退回。

D14.en: Between ages 5 and 12, he heard local songs sung skillfully at home.
D14.zh: 5到12岁时，他在家里听到当地歌曲被熟练演唱。

D13b.en: At age 6, he was placed in the lowest class of the Lower Line by the Prefect of Studies.
D13b.zh: 6岁时，他被学监安排到低年级最低的班级。

D18.en: At age 12, he generously lent money to siblings and parents, recording it in a notebook.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他慷慨地借钱给兄弟姐妹和父母，并记在笔记本上。

D5.en: At age 12, he and Stanislaus played truant, missing a day of school in early June 1894.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他和Stanislaus逃学，在1894年6月初旷课一天。

D11.en: At 14-16, he wrote poems and could compose music by ear, though he couldn't write notation.
D11.zh: 14到16岁时，他写诗并能凭耳朵作曲，虽然不会记谱。

M1.en: At age 11, he was stimulated by rivalry with Albrecht Connolly, a model for Heron in the novel.
M1.zh: 11岁时，他与Albrecht Connolly的竞争激励了他，后者是小说中Heron的原型。

D4.en: At age 7, he and his friends played football with Bill O'Connell's best hat.
D4.zh: 7岁时，他和朋友们用Bill O'Connell最好的帽子踢足球。

D7.en: At age 11, his father converted a property charge into a mortgage with Reuben Dodd.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他父亲将科克地产的抵押转为与Reuben Dodd的法定抵押。

D6.en: He befriended the Sheehy family during his last years at Belvedere.
D6.zh: 他在贝尔维迪尔的最后几年与希伊一家成为了朋友。

D2.en: He completed an essay on force as a pre-university exercise.
D2.zh: 他完成了一篇关于力的文章，作为大学前的练习。

D3.en: He began spending the money he received from the Fortnightly Review for his article on Ibsen.
D3.zh: 他开始花掉因写易卜生文章而从《双周评论》收到的钱。

M6.en: At age nine he found his vocation.
M6.zh: 9岁时他找到了自己的使命。

D17.en: At age 8-13, his mother took his father for treatment at the Keeley Institute near Memphis.
D17.zh: 8到13岁时，他母亲带父亲去孟菲斯附近的Keeley Institute治疗。

D16.en: He read Swinburne aloud to others in high school.
D16.zh: 高中时他大声朗读斯温伯恩的诗给别人听。

D1.en: At age four he survived scarlet fever with careful nursing.
D1.zh: 4岁时他得了猩红热，在精心护理下活了下来。

D14.en: At 13, he wandered streets wearing an old Confederate cap.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他戴着旧邦联帽在街上闲逛。

D13b.en: At his mother's insistence, he wore a canvas back brace to improve his posture.
D13b.zh: 在母亲坚持下，他穿帆布背带矫正姿势。

D13a.en: As a child, his mother remained silent during arguments with his father.
D13a.zh: 他幼年时，母亲在父亲发怒时保持沉默。

D18.en: At 17-18, he worked as a bookkeeper in his grandfather's bank.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他在祖父的银行当记账员。

D11.en: At 8-9, he showed a fertile imagination for childhood games and enlisting others.
D11.zh: 8到9岁时，他展现出丰富的想象力，设计童年游戏并拉别人参与。

M1.en: At 14-17, he played on the high school football team.
M1.zh: 14到17岁时，他参加高中橄榄球队。

D15.en: At age 10-11, he would slip out before daybreak to see fruit trains carrying strawberries north.
D15.zh: 10到11岁时，他会在天亮前溜出去看运草莓北上的火车。

D4.en: As a child, he went on hunting expeditions and experienced rites of passage.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他参加狩猎远征，经历了当时的成人仪式。

D9.en: At 17-18, he quarterbacked the high school football team in fall 1915.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他在1915年秋季担任高中橄榄球队的四分卫。

D7.en: At 12, he responded to paternal rejection by perfecting his alienation from home.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他因父亲拒绝而彻底疏远家庭。

D6.en: As a boy, he played with black playmates Joby and Durwue, the cook's sons.
D6.zh: 他小时候和厨娘的儿子Joby、Durwue等黑人玩伴一起玩。

D2.en: In summer 1914, he began a four-year, personally tailored tutorial with Phil Stone.
D2.zh: 1914年夏天，他开始了与Phil Stone为期四年的个性化辅导。

D3.en: At age 7-10, teacher Miss Annie Chandler recognized his gifts and gave him books.
D3.zh: 7到10岁时，老师Annie Chandler小姐看出他的天赋，送他书。

D10.en: The death of his grandmother when he was nine struck him so forcefully that it precipitated The Sound and the Fury.
D10.zh: 他9岁时祖母去世，这件事对他打击极大，促使他创作了《喧哗与骚动》。

D5.en: At 18, he threw a chair at his adopted father's head and left him for dead.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他把椅子砸向养父的头，以为他死了。

D16.en: At 17, his English master asked if he had help on a composition; he denied it.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他的英语老师问他作文是否有人帮忙，他否认了。

D10.en: As a small child, he hated his own ears for their large size, deepening his shyness.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他讨厌自己的大耳朵，这加深了他的害羞。

D5.en: At 17, he alarmed someone by wanting to swim in a quarry pond near the Academy.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他想要在学院附近的采石场池塘游泳，吓到了别人。

D11.en: At 10, he included an advertisement for an invented poem, 'The Bloomer Girl'.
D11.zh: 10岁时，他为一首虚构的诗《The Bloomer Girl》做了广告。

M1.en: At 12-13, he received a Smith Academy report dated February 1, 1901.
M1.zh: 12到13岁时，他收到了一份1901年2月1日的史密斯学院报告。

D15.en: At 10-17, he was barred from playing football or strenuous games due to a hernia.
D15.zh: 10到17岁间，他因疝气被禁止踢足球或进行剧烈运动。

D9.en: At 16-17, he published 'The Man Who Was King' in Smith Academy Record.
D9.zh: 16到17岁时，他在《史密斯学院记录》上发表了《曾是国王的人》。

D7.en: At 14, he headed north to Quebec instead of staying at Eastern Point for the summer.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他前往魁北克北部，而没有留在东点过夏天。

D6.en: At 16, a fellow pupil at Forbes House was Scofield Thayer.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他在福布斯楼的同学是斯科菲尔德·塞耶。
