D2.en: From age 10, he was made to attend the dentist twice a week to straighten his teeth.
D2.zh: 从10岁起，他被迫每周看两次牙医来矫正牙齿。

D3.en: As a graduate student, he worked with Masaharu Anesaki on Japanese Buddhism and with Charles Lanman on Sanskrit.
D3.zh: 研究生期间，他与Masaharu Anesaki研究日本佛教，与Charles Lanman研究梵文。

D12.en: At 12-13, he was reported to have suffered brain fever from strain.
D12.zh: 12到13岁时，他因劳累被报道患了脑热病。

D13b.en: At 16, he stated he had passed subjects at Harvard's Elementary level and would sit Advanced exams.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他说自己通过了哈佛初级科目，即将参加高级考试。

D8.en: He took on a succession of schoolmates, many smaller than him.
D8.zh: 他挑战了一连串的同学，其中许多比他矮小。

M6.en: During his senior year in high school, he decided to become a writer.
M6.zh: 高中最后一年，他决定成为一名作家。

D17.en: He gave generous assistance to Italian soldiers more seriously wounded by the same explosion.
D17.zh: 他慷慨帮助了在同一爆炸中受伤更重的意大利士兵。

M4.en: He joined the Red Cross in France and was transferred to the Italian front in July 1918.
M4.zh: 他在法国加入红十字会，并于1918年7月被调往意大利前线。

D10.en: He could not say how scared he was in another country with surgeons who could not tell him in English if his leg was coming off.
D10.zh: 他无法说出自己有多害怕，在异国他乡，医生无法用英语告诉他腿是否要截掉。

D1.en: At 18, he was gravely wounded by shrapnel but helped other soldiers before himself.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他被弹片重伤，但先帮助其他士兵再照顾自己。

D13b.en: He had compulsory attendance at church and Sunday school from ages 7 to 14.
D13b.zh: 他7到14岁时必须去教堂和主日学校。

D13a.en: At 18, he rejected college despite his father's wish for him to attend Oberlin.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝了大学，尽管父亲希望他去欧柏林学院。

D18.en: At 18, he fished for pleasure but sold the fish to his landlady to reduce rent.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他钓鱼为乐，但把鱼卖给女房东以抵房租。

D5.en: At 18, he had shrapnel in his knees and many flesh wounds.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他膝盖里有弹片，还有许多皮肉伤。

D11.en: At about 5, he claimed to have stopped a runaway horse single-handedly.
D11.zh: 大约5岁时，他声称自己独自拦住了一匹脱缰的马。

D15.en: As a teenager, he worked potato fields enough to know he disliked manual labor.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他干过土豆田的活，深知自己不喜欢体力劳动。

D4.en: At 16-17, he kept fishing through an open window, adding trout to the basket.
D4.zh: 16到17岁时，他继续从开着的窗户钓鱼，往篮子里加鳟鱼。

D9.en: At 18, he served briefly with Ambulance Section Four but soon transferred to the Italian army.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他短暂服务于救护车第四组，但很快转入意大利军队。

D7.en: Clarence Hemingway refused to let his son volunteer for war at age 17-18.
D7.zh: Clarence Hemingway拒绝让17到18岁的儿子参军。

D6.en: On his 18th birthday, he dressed up, had gelati on the balcony, and played the Victrola.
D6.zh: 18岁生日那天，他打扮好，在阳台上吃冰淇淋，还放了留声机。

D2.en: After twelve-hour days of haying and farm work, he was ready to take it easy.
D2.zh: 在每天十二小时的割草和农活后，他准备放松一下。

D12.en: At age 7-8, his father forbade him from playing on Sundays.
D12.zh: 7到8岁时，他父亲禁止他在周日玩耍。

D16.en: He gave a speech at First Congregational Church on June 17, 1917.
D16.zh: 1917年6月17日，他在第一公理会教堂发表演讲。

D20.en: At 18, he was elected secretary of the Triangle Club for 1915-16.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被选为Triangle Club 1915-16年度的秘书。

D16.en: At 13, he became St. Paul Academy's star debater.
D16.zh: 13岁时，他成了圣保罗中学的明星辩手。

D10.en: At age 4, he cried so hard they took him out after one day.
D10.zh: 4岁时，他哭得太厉害，一天后就被带走了。

D1.en: At 18, he wrote: 'If I couldn't be perfect I wouldn't be anything.'
D1.zh: 18岁时，他写道：'如果我不能完美，我就什么都不是。'

D14.en: At age 7, he carried a cane when going with his father to have their shoes shined on Sundays.
D14.zh: 7岁时，他星期天和父亲去擦鞋时带着手杖。

D13b.en: He was brought up a Catholic, with habitual convictions.
D13b.zh: 他从小受天主教教育，养成了习惯性的信念。

D13a.en: At 16, he insisted on Princeton or nothing for college.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他坚持非普林斯顿大学不上。

D5.en: At 17, he had his first whiskey in March, and an April entry notes he was 'Tight at Susquehanna'.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他在三月喝了第一杯威士忌，四月的一条记录说他'在萨斯奎汉纳喝醉了'。

M1.en: At 17, he sent a telegram asking for football pads and shoes to be sent immediately.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他发电报要求立即寄来足球护具和鞋。

D15.en: At age 8, he turned the attic into a gymnasium and persuaded his family to give him a football outfit.
D15.zh: 8岁时，他把阁楼改造成健身房，并说服家人给他一套足球装备。

D4.en: At 8, he turned the attic into a gymnasium and got a football outfit with shinguards.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他把阁楼改造成健身房，并说服家人给他一套带护胫的足球装备。

D9.en: At 18, he leaped to the edge of the stage and quieted the audience with an improvised monologue.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他跳到舞台边缘，用即兴独白让观众安静下来。

D7.en: At 16-17, he disturbed the Christmas service at St. John's Episcopal Church.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他扰乱了圣公会圣约翰教堂的圣诞礼拜。

D6.en: At 14, he wrote in his ledger: 'Dancing school. Marie. Love.'
D6.zh: 14岁时，他在日记中写道：“舞蹈学校。玛丽。爱。”

D2.en: At 15, he scribbled on the blank pages of his textbooks throughout all his classes.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他在所有课堂的课本空白页上乱写乱画。

D3.en: At 16, he met Fay, the first important person who responded to and encouraged his aspirations.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他遇到了Fay，第一个回应并鼓励他抱负的重要人物。

D12.en: At 12, when the news came, he prayed silently that they might not have to go to the poorhouse.
D12.zh: 12岁时，听到消息后，他默默祈祷他们不必去济贫院。

D10.en: He passionately desired to write in Russian but felt he must not, finding the experience tortuous and tragic.
D10.zh: 他强烈渴望用俄语写作，但觉得自己不能写，觉得这种经历曲折而悲惨。

D14.en: He had five hundred copies of his work printed at his own expense.
D14.zh: 他自费印了五百本自己的作品。

D13b.en: He took his school-leaving exams a month before the formal time.
D13b.zh: 他在正式时间前一个月参加了毕业考试。

D18.en: With his new fortune, he treated his friends to Petrograd's finest restaurants.
D18.zh: 他用新得的财富请朋友去彼得格勒最好的餐厅。

D11.en: He wrote his first play, 'Vesnoy' ('In Spring'), a lyrical something in one act.
D11.zh: 他写了第一部剧本《春天》，一部抒情的一幕剧。

D15.en: At 17, he made save after save in goal during an interschool soccer game.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他在一场校际足球赛中扑出一个又一个球。

D4.en: At 18, he saw a hawkmoth pupa he had kept for seven years hatch in the warmth.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他养了七年的天蛾蛹在温暖中羽化了。

D9.en: As a teenager, he was among students who wrote an open manifesto to the school's trustees.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他参与起草了一份致学校董事的公开宣言。

D7.en: At 11, he would be dreadfully rude to them and then cry in the water closet.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他会对他们非常无礼，然后躲在厕所里大哭。

D6.en: As a small child, he enjoyed the company of his numerous cousins.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他喜欢和众多表亲待在一起。

D2.en: At 5, he was taught for a few hours each day by the village schoolmaster.
D2.zh: 5岁时，他每天由乡村教师教几个小时。

D12.en: In his youth, he contracted pneumonia twice.
D12.zh: 他年轻时得过两次肺炎。

D13a.en: He said, 'He has his own means. How can I cross his plans?'
D13a.zh: 他说：“他有自己的办法。我怎么能打乱他的计划？”

D19.en: He felt very proud to be the first to obey an order.
D19.zh: 他为自己第一个服从命令感到非常自豪。

M3.en: He became magnetized by the figure and work of Rubén Darío.
M3.zh: 他被鲁文·达里奥的形象和作品深深吸引。

M6.en: He knew he had to be a novelist and started writing La Casa.
M6.zh: 他知道自己必须成为小说家，开始写《房子》。

M4.en: He began to suspect the war with Peru was invented to steal his grandparents' rings.
M4.zh: 他开始怀疑与秘鲁的战争是编造的，目的是偷走祖父母的戒指。

D16.en: He learned to be funny and defend himself with his tongue.
D16.zh: 他学会了幽默和用言语自卫。

D10.en: He had terrible temper tantrums and something like schizophrenia.
D10.zh: 他有可怕的脾气爆发，类似精神分裂。

D14.en: He saw Rosa Elena crowned carnival queen of Aracataca twice.
D14.zh: 他看到罗莎·埃琳娜两次加冕为阿拉卡塔卡狂欢节女王。

D13a.en: At 17-18, he accepted finishing the baccalaureate and studying law.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁时，他接受了完成中学学业并学习法律。

D5.en: At 17-18, he disappeared to Majagual for ten days and lived it up in a whorehouse.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他消失到马哈瓜尔镇十天，在妓院里狂欢。

M1.en: At 13-15, he won prizes, honors, and decorations for excellent academic performance.
M1.zh: 13到15岁时，他因学业优秀获得奖项、荣誉和表彰。

D15.en: He was born with a herniated testicle.
D15.zh: 他天生患有睾丸疝气。

D9.en: At 17, he contributed to the first issue of the youth magazine 31 and took charge of the section 'Our Poets'.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他为青年杂志《31》创刊号供稿，并负责'我们的诗人'栏目。

D6.en: At 13, he was embraced by a vivacious young woman who announced herself as his sister Carmen Rosa.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他被一位活泼的年轻女子拥抱，她自称是他的妹妹卡门·罗莎。

D3.en: At 13-14, he sat reading in the shade during break while others played football.
D3.zh: 13到14岁时，他休息时坐在阴凉处读书，而其他人在踢足球。

D12.en: He stayed with his grandparents and was always more his grandfather's son than his father's.
D12.zh: 他和祖父母住，永远更像祖父的儿子而非父亲的。

D13b.en: He was taken away as one of the two older sons.
D13b.zh: 他被带走，作为两个大儿子之一。

D18.en: At 11-12, he got a job with a local printer, hawking samples on the streets.
D18.zh: 11到12岁时，他在当地一家印刷厂找到工作，沿街叫卖样品。

D4.en: At 13-15, he authored all the vignettes for the first six issues.
D4.zh: 13到15岁时，他创作了前六期的所有装饰画。

D7.en: At 8, he said 'Run, Margot, he’s going to hit us,' took her hand, and they ran.
D7.zh: 8岁时，他说'快跑，玛戈特，他要打我们了'，拉着她的手跑了。

D10.en: He announced flatly to his uncle, "I don't want to die."
D10.zh: 他直截了当地对叔叔说：“我不想死。”

D1.en: His first tuberculosis symptoms were discovered in December 1930 or early January 1931.
D1.zh: 他在1930年12月或1931年1月初首次发现肺结核症状。

D18.en: At 14-16, he answered a want ad and got a job in an ironmonger's shop, working eight hours a day for 150 francs per month.
D18.zh: 14到16岁时，他应征广告，在一家五金店找到工作，每天工作八小时，月薪150法郎。

D5.en: As a boy, he would steal rides on horse carts carrying barrels to the docks.
D5.zh: 小时候，他会偷搭运酒桶的马车去码头。

D11.en: He did his first non-school creative writing at age 18.
D11.zh: 他在18岁时进行了第一次非学校的创作写作。

M1.en: At 10, he was number one in December and again in January.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他12月得了第一，1月又得了第一。

D4.en: At 6-9, he helped his uncle in his work as a barrel maker on Thursdays.
D4.zh: 6到9岁时，他每周四帮叔叔做制桶工。

D2.en: At 15, he received a certificate of satisfaction for good behavior and work at school.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他因在校表现良好、功课优秀获得了一张满意证书。

D3.en: At 17, suddenly aware of his own mortality, he spent his time in bed reading Epictetus.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他突然意识到自己会死，躺在床上读爱比克泰德。

D7.en: He refused to take his mother's illness seriously until she died.
D7.zh: 他拒绝认真对待母亲的病情，直到她去世。

M3.en: At 17, he began hero-worshipping a series of writers.
M3.zh: 17岁时，他开始崇拜一系列作家。

M6.en: At 15-16, his determination to be a poet in his own language became more pronounced.
M6.zh: 15到16岁间，他更加坚定了用自己语言写诗的决心。

D16.en: At 5, he defied someone with a Spanish pun: 'lo hice con querer'.
D16.zh: 5岁时，他用西班牙语双关语表示反抗：'lo hice con querer'。

D1.en: At 15, he failed his first French exam; classmates petitioned successfully for leniency.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他第一次法语考试不及格；同学们请愿成功，他得到了宽大处理。

D14.en: At 10, he published his first work, a Spanish translation of Oscar Wilde's 'The Happy Prince'.
D14.zh: 10岁时，他出版了自己的第一部作品，奥斯卡·王尔德《快乐王子》的西班牙语译本。

D13a.en: He decided to follow his own conscience rather than seek the compadritos' approval.
D13a.zh: 他决定遵循自己的良心，而不是寻求那些混混的认可。

M1.en: At 14, he would not do well at school; his marks would be below average.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他在学校表现不佳，成绩低于平均水平。

D15.en: At 6, he learned to swim, perhaps the most important thing he learned in childhood after reading.
D15.zh: 6岁时，他学会了游泳，这可能是他童年除了阅读外最重要的事。

D4.en: At 5-6, he often lay on the floor drawing tigers.
D4.zh: 5到6岁时，他常趴在地上画老虎。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to teach himself German to read Schopenhauer in the original.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他决定自学德语，以便读叔本华原著。

D12.en: At 14, he departed Buenos Aires with his family aboard the Sierra Nevada.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他随家人乘Sierra Nevada号离开布宜诺斯艾利斯。

M4.en: At 18, he and his friends opposed Western capitalism and were drawn to revolutionary ideas.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他和朋友们反对西方资本主义，被革命思想吸引。

M2.en: At 9-10, he vowed to enter the Ecole Normale Supérieure to honor martyred Alsace.
M2.zh: 9到10岁时，他发誓要进入巴黎高师，以纪念受难的阿尔萨斯。

M4.en: He held anticolonialist views.
M4.zh: 他持有反殖民主义观点。

D10.en: At 12-15, he felt like a pitiful braggart stumbling through failures.
D10.zh: 12到15岁时，他觉得自己像个可怜的吹牛大王，在失败中挣扎。

D14.en: At 6-7, his mother ecstatically read, reread, and copied his texts for acquaintances.
D14.zh: 6到7岁时，他母亲欣喜地反复阅读、抄写他的文字给熟人看。

D13a.en: At 11, his mother requested a board meeting to solve his custody problem.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他母亲请求召开监护委员会会议解决他的监护权问题。

D15.en: As a small child, he was sickly, skinny, frail, often ill with flu, bronchitis, ear inflammations.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他体弱多病，瘦小，常患流感、支气管炎、耳炎。

D3.en: He realized he thinks with his eyes.
D3.zh: 他意识到自己用眼睛思考。

D8.en: As a small child, she always invented the games and was always the teacher.
D8.zh: 幼年时，她总是发明游戏，总是当老师。

D19.en: She liked order and discipline.
D19.zh: 她喜欢秩序和纪律。

M6.en: At 17, she felt she must have absolute commitment and lasting attachment.
M6.zh: 17岁时，她觉得必须有绝对的承诺和持久的依恋。

M4.en: At 17, she was determined to study philosophy.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她决心学习哲学。

D16.en: As a small child, she sometimes mortified her mother, while her aunts clucked disapproval.
D16.zh: 幼年时，她有时让母亲难堪，而她的姑妈们则啧啧表示不赞同。

D10.en: She mourned someone more emotionally than she mourned her parents.
D10.zh: 她哀悼某人时比哀悼父母时更动情。

D14.en: She performed with such aplomb that she was praised as a very well-mannered young lady.
D14.zh: 她表现得如此从容，总是被称赞为非常有礼貌的小姐。

D11.en: At 10, she sat for hours under her father's desk in her own private dream world.
D11.zh: 10岁时，她坐在父亲书桌下，沉浸在自己的幻想世界里。

M1.en: At 16, she received her premier baccalauréat with mention bien.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她以良好评语通过了中学毕业会考。

D7.en: At 17, she thwarted her parents' every suggestion because she was determined to study philosophy.
D7.zh: 17岁时，她反对父母的每个建议，因为她决心学哲学。

D3.en: From age 5 to 14, her ideas and role models came almost entirely from books.
D3.zh: 5到14岁间，她的想法和榜样几乎全部来自读的书。

D1.en: At 17, she barely passed philosophy but got respectable math grades.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她哲学勉强及格，但数学成绩还不错。

D13a.en: At 17, her parents refused to let her study philosophy; her father insisted on financial security.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，父母不让她学哲学；父亲坚持要她有经济保障。

D18.en: At 12-15, he was apprenticed to a family pharmaceutical business in Osaka.
D18.zh: 12到15岁时，他被送到大阪一家家族制药公司当学徒。

D6.en: At 12-15, he was apprenticed to a pharmaceutical business in Osaka.
D6.zh: 12到15岁时，他被送到大阪一家药材商行当学徒。

D3.en: At 16, he had his first magazine publication: Kuraki Sensei no Sōshiki, in Danran.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他首次在杂志上发表作品：《仓木先生之葬》，刊登于《团栾》。

D16.en: He resented being mocked but gave in out of politeness.
D16.zh: 他因被嘲笑而深感不满，但出于礼貌还是让步了。

D10.en: He said he could never insult her memory by labeling her love.
D10.zh: 他说他绝不能给她的爱贴上普通标签来侮辱她的记忆。

D14.en: He published poems pseudonymously in the family magazine Bharati.
D14.zh: 他用笔名在家族杂志《婆罗蒂》上发表诗歌。

D13a.en: He left school with family permission, but some were critical.
D13a.zh: 他经家人允许离开了学校，但有些人对此持批评态度。

D11.en: His first poem was published anonymously at age 12.
D11.zh: 他12岁时匿名发表了第一首诗。

D15.en: As a boy not even in his teens, he walked alone with only a staff in his hand.
D15.zh: 他不到十几岁时，独自一人，手里只拿着一根手杖走路。

D4.en: At 17, he wandered through rooms and imagined secrets in the stones.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他好奇地在房间间游荡，想象石头中藏着秘密。

D9.en: At 15-16, he formed a secret society in a Calcutta lane.
D9.zh: 15到16岁时，他在加尔各答一条小巷里组建了一个秘密社团。

D7.en: At 18, he tore off his offending facial hair and stuffed it in his pocket.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他扯掉惹事的胡子并塞进口袋。

D6.en: At 18, he quickly picked up and liked a string of English songs.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他很快学会并喜欢上一连串英文歌。

D2.en: At 12, he memorized Sanskrit declensions before dawn.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他在黎明前背诵梵语词形变化。

D3.en: At 18, he agreed to teach Bengali in exchange for English correction.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他同意教孟加拉语以换取英语纠错。

M3.en: She described Olive Schreiner as a sort of literary older sister.
M3.zh: 她把Olive Schreiner描述为一种文学上的姐姐。

D17.en: At 11, she cared for her scalded kitten after it jumped into hot water.
D17.zh: 11岁时，她照顾被热水烫伤的波斯猫。

D10.en: At 12-13, a comment from her mother made her explode with fury.
D10.zh: 12到13岁时，母亲的一句话让她暴怒。

D13a.en: She won her reprieve and was brought home from the convent.
D13a.zh: 她获得了缓刑，被从修道院带回家。

D18.en: As a teenager, she worked in Salisbury as a nurse-maid and telephone operator.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，她在索尔兹伯里做保姆和电话接线员。

D5.en: At around age 10-11, she touched a lighted match to a hut where dogs sheltered.
D5.zh: 10到11岁时，她用点燃的火柴碰了狗躲雨的小棚子。

D11.en: At 17, she left Salisbury to return to the farm to write a novel.
D11.zh: 17岁时，她离开索尔兹伯里回到农场写小说。

M1.en: At 12-13, her mother Maude was determined that she win a scholarship to a prestigious boarding school in England.
M1.zh: 12到13岁时，她母亲莫德决心让她赢得英国一所名校的奖学金。

D4.en: From childhood to young adulthood, she wandered among animals, rocks, streams, and bushes.
D4.zh: 从幼年到青年，她在动物、岩石、溪流和灌木丛中游荡。

D7.en: At around age 10-11, she insisted her brother come with her, cajoling and exhorting him.
D7.zh: 10到11岁时，她坚持要弟弟跟她走，连哄带劝。

D12.en: At 17-18, she often drove her parents to Salisbury for her father's medical treatment.
D12.zh: 17到18岁时，她常开车带父母去索尔兹伯里给父亲看病。

M4.en: As a child, he watched the 1919 revolution demonstrations from a rooftop, seeing soldiers fire.
M4.zh: 他小时候从屋顶看到1919年革命的示威，看到士兵开枪。

D10.en: He experienced true love for the first time in Abbasiyya, an abstract relationship due to age and class differences.
D10.zh: 他在阿巴西耶第一次体验了真正的爱情，由于年龄和阶级差异，这是一种抽象的关系。

D11.en: At age 8, he had his first experience of hearing bullets fired during a demonstration.
D11.zh: 他8岁时第一次听到示威中开枪的子弹声。

D7.en: He asked why we die, and when he insisted, he was flogged by the Shaykh.
D7.zh: 他问为什么我们会死，坚持追问时被谢赫鞭打。

D6.en: He made many friends during his adolescence.
D6.zh: 他在青春期交了很多朋友。

D2.en: As a small child, he began his education at the kuttab, learning religion and literacy before primary school.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他开始在kuttab学习宗教和读写，然后才上小学。

D3.en: He was born in al-Gamaliyya, in a house on Maydan Bayt al-Qadi.
D3.zh: 他出生在al-Gamaliyya，在Maydan Bayt al-Qadi的一所房子里。

D12.en: He was born on 11 December 1911 in al-Jamāliyya quarter, old Cairo.
D12.zh: 他于1911年12月11日出生在开罗老城的al-Jamāliyya区。

D20X.en: At 16, he ended his friendship with an older boy.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他终止了与一个年长男孩的友谊。

D8.en: At 15-16, he stubbornly tried to persuade someone to change the conversation.
D8.zh: 15到16岁时，他固执地试图说服某人改变话题。

D19.en: As a small child, he insisted on punctuality and was punctilious.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他坚持守时且一丝不苟。

M3.en: At 16-18, he saw Azuma as perhaps the first indelibly important figure in his literary life.
M3.zh: 16到18岁时，他可能视Azuma为他文学生涯中第一个不可磨灭的重要人物。

M4.en: He saw Azuma as a hero due to his devotion to art and early death.
M4.zh: 他认为Azuma因对艺术的奉献和早逝而成为英雄。

D10.en: Just before his fifth birthday, he vomited coffee-colored matter and went into a coma.
D10.zh: 他五岁生日前，吐了咖啡色的东西并陷入昏迷。

D1.en: At age 4, he was severely ill with auto-intoxication and remained weak until adulthood.
D1.zh: 他4岁时患自体中毒，病重垂危，直到成年才恢复健康。

D14.en: At age 15, he often included a recent poem at the end of his letters.
D14.zh: 他15岁时常在信末附上一首当天写的诗。

D13a.en: At age 14, he hid his stories so his father couldn't find them.
D13a.zh: 他14岁时把小说藏起来，不让父亲找到。

D18.en: As a child and young adult, he was a fan of manga.
D18.zh: 他幼年到青年时期是漫画迷。

D5.en: At 16, he wrote to a friend that he thought it unlikely he'd be drafted but wished for an uproarious war.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他写信给朋友说，他认为自己不太可能被征召入伍，但希望有一场轰轰烈烈的战争。

D11.en: At age 16, his story was serialized in Bungei-Bunka from September to December 1941.
D11.zh: 他16岁时，小说于1941年9月至12月在《文艺文化》连载。

M1.en: He was almost at the top of his class of sixty boys and excelled in all subjects.
M1.zh: 他在六十个男孩的班级里几乎名列前茅，各科成绩都很优秀。

D4.en: At 13, his grandmother took him to see Kanadehon chūshingura for the first time.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他祖母第一次带他去看了《假名手本忠臣藏》。

D9.en: At 15, he became the youngest editorial board member in the literary club's hundred-year history.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他成为文学社百年历史上最年轻的编辑委员。

D6.en: At 17, he associated with a group of writers who believed the war was holy.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他与一群认为战争是神圣的作家交往。

D2.en: At 12-16, he exchanged voluminous letters with someone nearly every day.
D2.zh: 12到16岁间，他几乎每天与某人交换大量信件。

D3.en: At 5, he learned to read and read every fairy story he could find.
D3.zh: 5岁时，他学会了阅读，读了他能找到的所有童话故事。

D8.en: At 17, he said, 'Why don’t you hit somebody your own size!'
D8.zh: 17岁时，他说：‘你怎么不打跟你一样大的人！’

D19.en: As a child aged 10-12, he arranged things with Spartan simplicity and order.
D19.zh: 10到12岁时，他以斯巴达式的简朴和秩序安排事物。

D10.en: At 10, he was so deeply moved by something that he spoke of it with fear and horror more than 60 years later.
D10.zh: 10岁时，他被某事深深触动，60多年后仍带着恐惧和惊骇谈起。

D1.en: At 12, he decided to walk the eight miles home.
D1.zh: 12岁时，他决定步行八英里回家。

D14.en: At 17, after a concert, he drove everyone mad by imitating birds with his voice.
D14.zh: 17岁时，音乐会后他模仿鸟叫，把大家都逼疯了。

D13b.en: At 17, he enrolled in the arts faculty to study modern languages.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他进入文学院学习现代语言。

D5.en: He deliberately poured sulphuric acid down a sink and was severely punished.
D5.zh: 他故意将硫酸倒进水槽，并因此受到严厉惩罚。

M1.en: At 14, he won a juvenile tennis tournament with S Roe.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他和S Roe赢得了青少年网球赛。

D15.en: He won a juvenile tennis tournament with S Roe in August 1920.
D15.zh: 他1920年8月与S Roe一起赢得了青少年网球锦标赛。

D4.en: He poured sulphuric acid from a beaker into a sink, claiming he thought it was water.
D4.zh: 他将烧杯中的硫酸倒进水槽，声称他以为是水。

D9.en: In his final year, he was captain of both the rugby team and the cricket eleven.
D9.zh: 在最后一年，他担任了橄榄球队和板球队的队长。

D7.en: He tormented Mr Breul to a pitch of rage, just as naughty as the others.
D7.zh: 他和其他人一样调皮，把Breul先生惹得暴怒。

D6.en: He toured England with Trinity College, Dublin, playing against Northants.
D6.zh: 他随都柏林三一学院巡游英格兰，与北安普敦郡队比赛。

D3.en: By age 18 or 19, he was already intrigued by the power of gesture.
D3.zh: 到十八九岁时，他已经对手势的力量产生了兴趣。

D11.en: At 8, he bought Diabelli's piano duet at Piggots and Co.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他在Piggots and Co.买了迪亚贝利的钢琴二重奏。

D16.en: At 13-15, he wrote love letters for a schoolfriend, like Cyrano.
D16.zh: 13到15岁时，他替同学写情书，像西哈诺一样。

D10.en: At 14, he wrote: 'I'm alone and sad, thinking about joy and pain, old age and youth.'
D10.zh: 14岁时，他写道：‘我独自悲伤，思考欢乐与痛苦，衰老与青春。’

D1.en: At 13, he urged people to follow their desires with perseverance, citing Columbus and Marconi.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他呼吁人们以毅力追随欲望，引用哥伦布和马可尼为例。

D14.en: At 13-15, he heard Mason recite poetry at public recitals in his home.
D14.zh: 13到15岁时，他听Mason在他家朗诵诗歌。

D13a.en: At 17, he moved back to Avenida Espana until sure his father had returned to Temuco.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他搬回西班牙大道，直到确定父亲已回特木科。

D5.en: At 16, he accepted a duel, learned fencing in two weeks, and won.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他接受决斗，两周学会击剑并获胜。

D11.en: At 14-15, he had 16 poems published in Corre-Vuela and others in student journals.
D11.zh: 14到15岁时，他在Corre-Vuela发表了16首诗，还在一些学生刊物上发表过其他作品。

M1.en: At 16, he won first prize in the Temuco Spring Games Competition for an early poem.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他凭一首早期诗作在特木科春季运动会比赛中获得一等奖。

D15.en: At 6, he fought with acorns in a large shed, noting how much they hurt.
D15.zh: 6岁时，他在大棚里用橡子打架，发现被打中很疼。

D4.en: At 10, he was stunned by the perfection of insects and marveled at finding partridge eggs.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他被昆虫的完美所震撼，并为找到鹧鸪蛋而惊叹。

D9.en: He called for people to follow their desires with enthusiasm and perseverance.
D9.zh: 他呼吁人们以热情和毅力追随自己的欲望。

D7.en: At 16, he decided to change his name to Pablo Neruda in October 1920.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他于1920年10月决定改名为巴勃罗·聂鲁达。

D6.en: At 17, he shared it with schoolfriend Alejandro Serani from Temuco.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他与来自特木科的同学Alejandro Serani分享了它。

D2.en: At 16-17, he put all his energy into reading and writing poetry.
D2.zh: 16到17岁时，他全力阅读和写诗。

D3.en: At 15, he avidly consumed reading material given by her: Tolstoy, Dostoyevsky, Chekhov.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他如饥似渴地阅读她给的读物：托尔斯泰、陀思妥耶夫斯基、契诃夫。

D12.en: His mother died two months after giving birth to him.
D12.zh: 他出生两个月后母亲去世。

M4.en: At 17, he was arrested by Peruvian police on suspicion of being a leftist.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他因被怀疑是左翼分子被秘鲁警方逮捕。

D10.en: She felt lonely, far from family and friends, and was always crying or silent.
D10.zh: 她感到孤独，远离家人和朋友，总是哭泣或沉默。

D13a.en: At 18, she decided to marry Nikolay Gumilyov, her friend of youth who had loved her for three years.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她决定嫁给青年时代的朋友尼古拉·古米廖夫，他爱了她三年。

D11.en: She was writing poetry at this turbulent time, but considered her early verses very poor.
D11.zh: 她在动荡时期写诗，但认为自己早期的诗很差。

D15.en: As a small child, she was a tomboy who could swim like a fish and climb like a cat.
D15.zh: 幼年时，她是个假小子，游泳像鱼，攀爬像猫。

D7.en: At 15-16, she secretly attended school with her mother's help against her father's will.
D7.zh: 15到16岁时，她瞒着父亲，在母亲帮助下偷偷去上学。

D6.en: At 17, she lived with her aunt and attended the Fundukleyevskaya Gymnasium.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她和姨妈住在一起，就读于Fundukleyevskaya中学。

D3.en: At 13, she was already infatuated with poetry, especially Alexander Blok's.
D3.zh: 13岁时，她已经痴迷于诗歌，尤其是亚历山大·勃洛克的诗。

D16.en: At 16, she announced to her parents her intention to become a writer.
D16.zh: 16岁时，她向父母宣布了自己要当作家的打算。

D10.en: At 14, she experienced paralysis and nervous giggling.
D10.zh: 14岁时，她感到瘫痪和紧张地傻笑。

D1.en: At 18, she aimed to write the Great Canadian Novel and kept her resolve.
D1.zh: 18岁时，她立志写出伟大的加拿大小说，并保持了决心。

D13a.en: At 11, she stopped babysitting for a family, leaving them to wonder why.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，她不再帮那家人照看孩子，让他们纳闷原因。

D18.en: At 12-13, she formed a partnership and performed hand puppet shows at birthday parties.
D18.zh: 12到13岁时，她与人合伙，在生日派对上表演手偶戏。

D5.en: As a teenager at summer camp, she and friends played pranks like gluing shoes to the floor and filling them with horse manure.
D5.zh: 十几岁时在夏令营，她和朋友搞恶作剧，把鞋粘在地板上，往鞋里塞马粪。

D11.en: As a small child, she drew things like flying rabbits in her pictures.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她在画里画了会飞的兔子之类的东西。

M1.en: In grade 13, she wrote ten exams and achieved impressively high scores.
M1.zh: 13年级时，她参加了十门考试并取得了极高的分数。

D15.en: In high school, sports weren't really her strong point.
D15.zh: 高中时，体育不是她的强项。

D4.en: As a child visiting cousins, she helped make a sailing boat to take out into the bay.
D4.zh: 小时候去表亲家，她帮忙做了一艘帆船，开到海湾里去。

D6.en: In high school, she participated in the model UN when her school represented Palestine.
D6.zh: 高中时，她参加了模拟联合国，那年她学校代表巴勒斯坦。

D2.en: As a child (age 8-12), she took piano lessons and went to Miss Pickering's Dance School for ballet and tap.
D2.zh: 小时候（8-12岁），她上钢琴课，还去皮克林小姐舞蹈学校学芭蕾和踢踏舞。

D3.en: At age 10-12, she read Moby Dick, skipping people parts and identifying with the whale.
D3.zh: 10到12岁时，她读了《白鲸》，跳过写人的部分，把自己当成鲸鱼。

D12.en: At age 11, she looked after a neighbor's young son.
D12.zh: 11岁时，她照看过邻居家的小儿子。

D20.en: As a teenager, he edited his house magazine and the school magazine under Charles Low.
D20.zh: 十几岁时，他在Charles Low指导下编辑了宿舍杂志和校刊。

D16.en: At 18, he questioned why one cannot study Religious Studies, Applied Mathematics and Gynaecology together.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他质疑为什么不能同时学习宗教研究、应用数学和妇科。

D10.en: He was amazed at the irrelevant thoughts passing through his mind during his greatest crisis.
D10.zh: 他惊讶于在人生最大危机时脑中闪过的无关念头。

D14.en: At 6-8, his participation in the school band showed his interest in music.
D14.zh: 6到8岁时，他参加学校乐队，显示了对音乐的兴趣。

D13b.en: As a teenager, his first punishment at college was for speaking Igbo to ask for soap.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他在学院第一次受罚是因为用伊博语向别人要肥皂。

D13a.en: At 13, his father told Clarke that he would go to Umuahia.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他父亲告诉Clarke他会去乌穆阿希亚。

D5.en: He wrote only as much as he thought necessary and handed in his exam paper early.
D5.zh: 他只写了认为必要的内容，并提前交卷。

D11.en: He edited ephemeral publications and contributed poems, essays, and short stories.
D11.zh: 他编辑了这些临时出版物，并贡献了诗歌、散文和短篇小说。

M1.en: He passed through Classes One and Two in 1944 and got a scholarship that same year.
M1.zh: 他在1944年通过了第一和第二班，并在同一年获得了奖学金。

D15.en: At 6-10, he accompanied his mates to fetch water at Isi-mili Iyienu Ogidi, the only spring nearby, on a rough, stony path.
D15.zh: 6到10岁时，他和同伴一起去伊西米利·伊耶努·奥吉迪打水，那是附近唯一的泉水，路很崎岖，布满石头。

D9.en: As a teenager, he was made a college prefect.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他被选为学院级长。

D6.en: He interacted with many people who later shaped his country's destiny.
D6.zh: 他与许多后来塑造国家命运的人互动。

D12.en: He helped his brother with cooking, a skill he learned at home.
D12.zh: 他帮哥哥做饭，这是他在家学到的技能。

D2.en: At 18, he matriculated with a scholarship to study medicine at University College, Ibadan.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他获得奖学金进入伊巴丹大学学院学医。

D17.en: At 6, he gave his own trousers to a shivering classmate.
D17.zh: 6岁时，他把自己的裤子给了一个冷得发抖的同学。

D2.en: At age 5, he was woken before dawn to study and recited the previous day's lesson to his father at night.
D2.zh: 5岁时，他每天天未亮就被父亲催起早读，晚上还要背课文给父亲听。

D3.en: At age 10, he preferred history books and could recite the Records of the Grand Historian and many classical essays.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他更喜欢历史书，能背诵《史记》全文及不少古文名篇。

D12.en: At 5, he studied in his father's private school after moving to Jinhua.
D12.zh: 5岁时，他随父母迁居金华后，在父亲的私塾读书。

M2.en: At 14, he passed the imperial exam and became a xiucai, bringing honor to his family.
M2.zh: 14岁时，他考中秀才，光宗耀祖。

D18.en: At 14, he gave away the silver dollars he received as a xiucai to jealous youths who accused him of cheating.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他把领到的银元全给了嫉妒他的小青年。

D11.en: At 8, he was carried to the yamen to compose poems, earning a reputation as a child prodigy.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他被背抱入衙答对作诗，被称为神童。

M1.en: At 13, he ranked first in the exam but was deliberately placed tenth to curb his pride.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他考取秀才成绩第一，但被降为第十名。

M3.en: At 10, he was shocked by a journalist's power to overturn injustice and decided to enter journalism.
M3.zh: 10岁时，他震惊于记者旋乾转坤之威力，决心投身报业。

M4.en: At 10, he resolved to become an upright journalist, using his pen for justice.
M4.zh: 10岁时，他立志做正派记者，用笔写出人间公理与不平。

D16.en: At 15, he became a field reporter for Anhui's Minyan Bao, starting his journalism career.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他担任安徽《民岩报》外勤记者，开始新闻事业。

D13a.en: At 13, he secretly joined a youth army student team but was stopped by his father before boarding.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他偷偷参加青年军学生队，登船前被父亲拦下。

D18.en: At 15, he was hired as a field reporter for Anhui's Minyan Bao.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他被安徽的《民岩报》聘为外勤记者。

D5.en: At 18, he returned to Anqing to start an anti-Yuan newspaper, was arrested, and released after 3 days.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他回安庆筹办反袁报纸，被捕关押3日后获释。

D9.en: At 18, despite his editor's orders, he arranged for friends' poems to be published in another newspaper.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他碍于总编指示，但仍牵线将友人诗文在《中华新报》发表。

D7.en: At 18, he drafted a notice opposing Liu Yazi's expulsion of a member, citing no rule allowing it.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他草拟公告反对柳亚子开除社员，认为社章无此规定。

D6.en: At 17, he formed a 'writing company' with friends, selling writings to magazines and newspapers.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他与友人组成“卖文公司”，向报刊投稿卖文为生。

D2.en: At 11, he entered a high primary class and was promoted to middle school after a month.
D2.zh: 11岁时，他考入高小班，一个月后升入中学班。

D3.en: At 10, he was shocked by the power of journalists and decided to enter journalism.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他震惊于新闻记者的力量，决定投身报业。

D12.en: At age 10, his father was framed and nearly dismissed; he accompanied his father seeking help from a journalist.
D12.zh: 10岁时父亲被诬陷，他随父求助记者方时荪。

D13b.en: At 18, he reluctantly married a plump woman chosen by his mother, but grew to depend on her.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他勉强接受了母亲为他选的胖媳妇，后来却离不开她了。

D18.en: At 13, he became an apprentice at a tea shop near Dongfugiao, introduced by an old neighbor.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他由邻居介绍到东浮桥口一家茶叶店当学徒。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote his first article for Yi Shih Pao supplement, signing as 'Juan Fei'.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他写了第一篇稿子投给《益世报》副刊，署名‘倦飞’。

D4.en: At 13-15, he used a pedicure knife to cut out favorite articles from the newspaper supplement and collected them.
D4.zh: 13到15岁时，他用修脚刀把副刊上喜爱的文章挖下来收藏。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to learn English and calculated that the correspondence school fees would be about 40 silver dollars.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他下定决心学英文，算下来函授学校费用约需四十元钱。

D3.en: At 15-17, he would take dry rations to the newspaper reading room every day, sometimes getting locked in and reading all night.
D3.zh: 15到17岁时，他每天带干粮去阅报室，有时被锁在里面彻夜读报。

D12.en: At 13, he became an apprentice at a tea shop near Dongfu Bridge.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他到东浮桥口一家茶叶店当学徒。

M8.en: As a small child, he was baptized by the nurse who looked after him.
M8.zh: 幼年时，他被照顾他的保姆施洗。

M7.en: At age 5-10, he maintained that rescuing all folk by a shining deed became an early serious concern.
M7.zh: 5到10岁时，他认为通过前所未有的光辉事迹拯救所有人成了他早期的重要关切。

M4.en: At 15, his reaction to events was spontaneous and untainted, arousing his father's envious approval.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他的反应自发且不受实际考虑影响，引起了他父亲羡慕的赞许。

D10.en: At age 4, according to his father, he was extremely timid with strangers.
D10.zh: 4岁时，据他父亲说，他对陌生人极其胆怯。

D13b.en: At 11, he entered second form after an extra year of private tuition.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他多上了一年私塾后直接进入二年级。

D13a.en: He enrolled as a law student but never seriously concerned himself with jurisprudence.
D13a.zh: 他1908年秋考入莫斯科大学法律系，但从未认真对待法学。

D5.en: He was whiplashed by a mounted Cossack during demonstrations.
D5.zh: 他在示威中被一名骑马的哥萨克鞭打。

D11.en: At 16, he and others parodied Dickens, calling it 'The Old Spicery Shop'.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他和别人模仿狄更斯，称之为'The Old Spicery Shop'。

M1.en: In his final year 1908 he was awarded a gold medal.
M1.zh: 1908年毕业时，他获得了金质奖章。

D15.en: He enjoyed walking busy streets and breathing the un-Muscovite atmosphere.
D15.zh: 他喜欢走在繁忙的街道上，呼吸非莫斯科的氛围。

D4.en: He composed 'Con moto' in 1906.
D4.zh: 他于1906年创作了《Con moto》。

D7.en: He was among student demonstrators and was whiplashed by a mounted Cossack.
D7.zh: 他参与学生示威，被一名骑马的哥萨克鞭打。

D6.en: He had a close pre-school friendship with Aleksandr Shtikh that survived into adulthood.
D6.zh: 他与亚历山大·什季赫的学前友谊持续到成年。

D2.en: At 13, he began serious study of musical composition under Iurii Engel and Reinhold Glière in Moscow.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他在莫斯科开始跟随尤里·恩格尔和莱因霍尔德·格里埃尔认真学习音乐作曲。

D3.en: He was drawn by his father while busily sketching in July 1898.
D3.zh: 1898年7月，他父亲画了他正在专注素描的样子。

D12.en: As a small child, he saw his mother shoulder the burden of organizing the family, household, and finances.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他看到母亲承担了组织家庭、家务和财务的重担。

D13b.en: He failed the imperial exam at 17, strengthening his resolve to avoid it.
D13b.zh: 他17岁时考秀才未中，这让他更坚决地无意科举。

D13a.en: He received a letter from elders urging him to study for the exams.
D13a.zh: 他收到族里长辈来信，让他用功备考以耀祖光宗。

D5.en: At 17, he suddenly decided to go to Shanghai to see the big city.
D5.zh: 他17岁时忽生念头，到上海去开开眼界。

M1.en: He finished first, handed in his paper excitedly, believing academics cannot be faked, and the exam couldn't beat him.
M1.zh: 他第一个做完，兴冲冲交了卷，觉得学术的东西不能作假，考试难不倒他。

D15.en: During New Year, he would jump over fireworks as they shot out.
D15.zh: 过年时，他会在焰火喷出时从上面跳过去。

D9.en: At 17, he was chosen by classmates to draft a telegram supporting the Nanyang Public School students.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他被同学推举执笔发电报声援南洋公学学生。

D6.en: At 18, he entered a garden, chose a tea house, and sat down to drink tea.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他进到园内，选了一家茶店坐下喝茶。

D2.en: As a child, he spent an hour each day learning English from a teacher named Huang Zijia.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他每天抽出一小时跟黄子甲先生学英语。

D3.en: He always went to the foreign language bookstore to buy books when bathing in town.
D3.zh: 他每次进城洗澡，总要到外文书店去买书。

D17.en: At 18, he bravely followed a stretcher in the dark to the riverbank and bid a tearful farewell to Mao Jun.
D17.zh: 18岁时，他勇敢地在黑夜中跟在担架后面步行至江边，与毛君垂泪永别。

D16.en: At 18, he published an essay under the pen name Charlie in the newspaper.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他以笔名查理在报纸上发表了一篇读李清照词的文章。

D10.en: As a teenager, he learned of his mother's and brother's deaths while away at school.
D10.zh: 十几岁时，他在外地得知母亲和弟弟病逝的消息。

D1.en: At 13, he fled with teachers and students, walking and boating through several towns after Hangzhou fell.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他随流亡师生乘船步行，经多地南行，因杭州沦陷。

D14.en: At 18, he wrote, directed, and starred in an English play, Rising of the Moon.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他自编自导自演英语话剧《月亮升起》。

D9.en: At 10, he co-edited a class magazine with his teacher.
D9.zh: 10岁时，他和老师一起编五年级级刊《喔喔啼》。

D7.en: At 17, he wrote a satirical article about the school's dean and posted it on the wall.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他写文章影射训导主任，贴在学校墙报上。

D6.en: At 9, he attended a poet's funeral on behalf of his family.
D6.zh: 9岁时，他代表全家去吊唁一位诗人。

D2.en: At 14-15, he studied diligently with other refugee students, reciting texts in the fields at dawn.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，他和流亡学生们一起在田野上朗读语文和英语。

D12.en: At 13-17, he lived on government stipends, wore straw sandals, and endured military training.
D12.zh: 13到17岁间，他靠公费度日，穿草鞋，受军训。

M4.en: At 17, he was expelled for comparing Chiang Kai-shek to Hitler.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他因把蒋介石比作希特勒而被学校开除。

M3.en: He was favored by a neighbor family who promised their daughter to him, so he got the best food.
M3.zh: 邻居福建人有意将女儿嫁他，所以他总先吃到好吃的。

D13a.en: At 5, hearing footsteps, he ran out and shouted to the nanny that he hadn't stolen a drink.
D13a.zh: 五岁时听到脚步声，他冲出来对奶妈大喊没偷喝。

D18.en: He made spinning tops from lychee pits to amuse other children, who then copied him.
D18.zh: 他用荔枝核做陀螺逗小孩玩，小孩抢着模仿。

D5.en: In junior high, he first tried coconut wine, drank a whole can, and collapsed.
D5.zh: 初中时他第一次喝椰酒，狂饮几公斤后倒地。

D15.en: He played a marble game called 'hungry marble' using both hands, with a favorite marble always in his pocket.
D15.zh: 他玩双手弹的“饿利”石弹，心爱的弹子总装在裤袋里。

D4.en: As a child, he carved a wooden toy with a knife and rubber bands.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他用小刀将木心切成齿型，绑上胶圈做成玩具。

M2.en: At 9, orphaned, he was forced to sell sugarcane and olives.
M2.zh: 九岁时父母双亡，他被迫去卖甘蔗、橄榄。

D1.en: At 9, orphaned, he sold sugarcane and olives, and memorized the whole 'Thousand Poems' by peeping into a school window.
D1.zh: 九岁时父母双亡，他卖甘蔗橄榄，偷窥私塾窗口强记整本《千家诗》。

M1.en: At 9, he memorized the entire 'Thousand Poets' by rote.
M1.zh: 9岁时，他整本《千家诗》强记起来。

D3.en: In his youth, he asked a student on a crowded train in Calcutta.
D3.zh: 年轻时，他在加尔各答挤火车时问身旁一个中学生。

D12.en: At 9, orphaned, he sold sugarcane, spied on school, and worked as a puppeteer.
D12.zh: 9岁时父母双亡，他卖甘蔗、偷窥师塾、演傀儡戏。

D16.en: At 14, he wrote an essay denouncing the filial piety story of Guo Ju as cruel and inhuman.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他写文章痛斥郭巨埋儿奉母的行为残忍无人心。

D1.en: At 13, he cried with his uncle and vowed to memorize English letters, practicing dozens of times.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他和叔父抱头痛哭，发誓记住字母，反复默写数十次。

D14.en: As a teenager, he saw his article published in a newspaper and felt greatly encouraged.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他看到自己的文章刊登在报纸上，受到很大鼓舞。

D13b.en: At 17, his father sent him to Nanyang Public School to study engineering.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他父亲送他进上海南洋公学读书。

D18.en: As a teenager, he kept submitting articles despite repeated failures.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他不断投稿，虽屡次失败仍不灰心。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote an essay overturning the traditional praise of Guo Ju's filial piety.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他写文章推翻旧案，痛斥郭巨行为荒谬。

D7.en: At 14, he boldly criticized the feudal filial piety story of Guo Ju.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他大胆批判封建孝道故事郭巨埋儿。

D6.en: At 16, he stood in a crowd and saw Sun Yat-sen riding a horse with a smile.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他站在人群中亲眼见到孙中山骑马含笑而过。

D2.en: As a teenager, he overcame learning difficulties with strong will and excelled in exams.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他以坚强的意志克服学习困难，每门课都深入钻研，考试名列前茅。

D12.en: At 18, he struggled financially and aimed for top grades to get a tuition waiver.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他经济困难，争取优行生资格以免学费。

D19.en: At 5-7, he obsessively lined up miniature cars and admitted them one by one into his garage.
D19.zh: 5到7岁时，他痴迷地把迷你车排成整齐的一列，一辆辆放进车库。

M3.en: At 16-17, he imitated an artist's style and way of painting.
M3.zh: 16到17岁时，他模仿了一位艺术家的风格和绘画方式。

M6.en: At 11, his hunger for belief turned into a shame he kept secret.
M6.zh: 11岁时，他对信仰的渴望变成了一种羞耻，他保守着这个秘密。

D20.en: At 5, he opened a thick book and copied out its lines.
D20.zh: 5岁时，他打开一本厚书，抄写里面的句子。

D16.en: He joked, told anecdotes, and imitated teachers to make classmates laugh.
D16.zh: 他开玩笑、讲故事、模仿老师，逗全班同学笑。

D10.en: At 8-12, he would lie on the carpet and cry like a child imagining his own funeral, then fall asleep.
D10.zh: 8到12岁时，他会躺在地毯上哭，像想象自己葬礼的孩子，然后睡着。

D1.en: At 6-7, his hand shot up to answer even when he didn't know the answer.
D1.zh: 6到7岁时，即使不知道答案，他的手也会不由自主地举起来。

D13b.en: At 13-16, he spent four years at Robert Academy.
D13b.zh: 13到16岁时，他在罗伯特学院读了四年。

D13a.en: At 8, he started skipping school in earnest.
D13a.zh: 8岁时，他开始认真逃学。

D11.en: During lycée, he could only exit the loop by writing or painting.
D11.zh: 高中时，他只有拿起笔写作或画画才能走出循环。

M1.en: At 16-17, he was not Utrillo but someone who tried to paint like him.
M1.zh: 16到17岁时，他不是郁特里罗，而是试图画得像郁特里罗的人。

D15.en: As a small child, his father took him to soccer matches.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他父亲带他去看足球赛。

D4.en: As a small child, he threw a nail or a firecracker he had skillfully attached to a string.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他扔出一颗钉子或一个他巧妙系在绳子上的鞭炮。

D9.en: He acknowledged veiled resentment of their American teacher with classmates.
D9.zh: 他和同学们承认了对美国老师的隐晦不满。

D7.en: During lycée, he found skipping school more fun, feeling guilt with every step in the city streets.
D7.zh: 高中时，他觉得逃课更有趣，走在城市街道上每步都感到内疚。

D6.en: During lycée, he joined a set of boys whose fathers were in industry.
D6.zh: 高中时，他加入了一群父亲从事纺织、矿业等行业的男孩。

D3.en: As a small child, he hated not being able to inspect counters or ice-cream freezers.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他讨厌不能查看柜台或冰淇淋冰柜内部。

D12.en: He was born in the middle of the night on June 7, 1952, in a small private hospital in Moda.
D12.zh: 他于1952年6月7日午夜出生在莫达的一家小私立医院。

D14.en: At 5, he mentioned in passing that the prime minister was his uncle.
D14.zh: 5岁时，他随口提到总理是他叔叔。

D5.en: At 5, he mentioned that the prime minister was his uncle.
D5.zh: 5岁时，他随口提到总理是他叔叔。

D10.en: At 13, he complained that their family's famous dish was egg drop soup, mocked by others.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他抬高嗓子说蛋花汤是家里的名菜，被人笑话。

D13a.en: At 13, he nodded repeatedly and said he understood.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他连连点头说晓得了。

D7.en: At 13, he looked displeased at the same dishes, pouted, and said he was tired of eating them.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他看到桌上又是老一套，不高兴地撅嘴说吃厌了冬瓜汤西瓜皮。

D12.en: At age 7, his mother died of puerperal fever 40 days after giving birth, and he moved to Sijing with his father.
D12.zh: 7岁时，母亲产后40天去世，他随父亲迁居泗泾镇。

M4.en: At 18, he said no one with national consciousness could swallow the humiliation.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他说凡稍有国家观念和民族意识者，谁也不能忍气吞声、茹辱含垢了。

D16.en: At 18, he gave impassioned speeches on the street.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他在街头慷慨激昂地发表演说。

D1.en: At age 6-9, he found school boring and wanted to go home.
D1.zh: 6到9岁时，他感到索然寡味，每每要吵着回家，横竖不想念这个书。

D13a.en: At 12, his mother beat him for playing truant and sent him back to school that night.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，母亲因他逃学勃然大怒，痛骂并打了他一顿，立命用书二哥连夜带他返塾。

D5.en: At 18, he was almost arrested by secret police while being monitored.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他几乎被军警密探逮捕而坐牢。

D9.en: At 18, he led street protests and organized large demonstrations, giving impassioned speeches.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他带头上街抗议，与同学们组织大规模的示威活动，在街头慷慨激昂地发表演说。

D7.en: At 12, he ignored everyone and shouted for help.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他不理所有人，只顾大喊救命。

D6.en: At 18, he became a core member of the school's movement against Japan's Twenty-One Demands.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他成了该校反对日本“二十一条”救国运动的中坚力量。

D3.en: At age 13-15, he ranked first in all exams, big or small.
D3.zh: 13到15岁时，他无论大考或小考，在校成绩皆为第一。

D12.en: At 12, his father suddenly fell ill, throwing the family into panic.
D12.zh: 12岁时，父亲雷锦贵猝然病倒，使这个家陷入一片恐慌之中。

D1.en: At 13-14, he secretly studied by lamplight under his blanket after lights-out.
D1.zh: 13到14岁时，他熄灯后在被窝里点油灯偷偷学习。

D13b.en: His mother worked extra sewing and sold noodles to pay for his schooling.
D13b.zh: 母亲靠做针线活和卖挂面挣钱供他读书。

D18.en: His mother worked extra sewing and sold noodles to earn money.
D18.zh: 他母亲额外做针线活和卖挂面挣钱。

D5.en: At 18, he was stranded, begged in tattered clothes, slept in a temple, and caught typhoid.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他被遣散，乞讨为生，睡破庙，患上伤寒。

M1.en: At 18, he sought to understand right and wrong and find China's revolutionary path.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他想通过读书弄清谁是谁非，寻找中国革命出路。

D9.en: At 18, he served as a squad leader.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他担任了一个班的班长。

D12.en: As a child, he wore clothes remade from adults' and never had new ones.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他穿大人衣服改小的，没穿过新衣服。

D16.en: At 17, she stood paralyzed with fear.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她吓得僵住了。

D10.en: At 10, she delighted in the family home with its honeysuckled veranda.
D10.zh: 10岁时，她喜欢家里有金银花藤门廊的大房子。

D14.en: At 18, five of her poems appeared anonymously in the Atlantic Monthly.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她的五首诗匿名发表在《大西洋月刊》上。

D13b.en: As a small child, she was enrolled, the only official notice of her birth.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她被登记入册，这是她出生唯一的官方记录。

D13a.en: At 15, she wore a martyr-like expression as part of being misunderstood.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，她像殉道者一样穿上它，当作被误解的代价。

D11.en: At 12, her earliest known letter is a breathless account of life at Pencraig.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她最早的信件是她在彭克雷格生活的激动描述。

D15.en: As a teenager, she enjoyed walking, riding, and archery contests.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，她喜欢散步、骑马和射箭比赛。

D7.en: As a young girl, she impertinently pointed out a smart canary-yellow brougham on Fifth Avenue.
D7.zh: 小时候，她无礼地指着第五大道上一辆漂亮的淡黄色四轮马车。

D6.en: At 5-6, she made friends with Margaret and Arthur Terry in Rome.
D6.zh: 5到6岁时，她在罗马与Margaret和Arthur Terry交了朋友。

D3.en: At 6-8, her parents discovered she was reading Fanny Lear, a play about a prostitute.
D3.zh: 6到8岁时，她父母发现她在读《Fanny Lear》，一部关于妓女的戏剧。

M4.en: He joined the Chinese Communist Party in Shanghai's Falan District in winter 1930.
M4.zh: 1930年冬天，他在上海法南区加入中国共产党。

D16.en: He won second place in a school current affairs speech contest.
D16.zh: 他在全校时事问题讲演中获得第二名。

D14.en: He participated in a school current affairs speech contest and got second place.
D14.zh: 他参加全校时事问题演讲比赛，获得第二名。

D5.en: He organized a strike among tram workers at the French Tram Company.
D5.zh: 他在法商电车公司组织了电车工人的罢工斗争。

D11.en: In 1930, he wrote a poem titled 'Book City' reflecting on his life.
D11.zh: 1930年，他写了一首题为《书城》的诗。

D15.en: He learned Shaolin boxing from a middle school PE teacher in the evenings.
D15.zh: 他晚上经常去学习少林拳术。

D4.en: He ran a night school for rickshaw pullers and laborers in Dapuqiao area.
D4.zh: 他在打浦桥一带办起工人夜校，帮助黄包车工人识字读书。

D9.en: He put up wall newspapers and ran worker night schools to spread Marxist ideas.
D9.zh: 他出壁报、办刊物，并举办工人夜校进行马克思主义普及教育。

D7.en: He secretly joined the Chinese Communist Party in Falan District introduced by Hao Lianru.
D7.zh: 他在法南区由郝连如介绍秘密加入中国共产党。

D6.en: At 14, he co-founded a small library called 'My Reading Club' with classmates, bringing books from home to exchange.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他和同学一起办起了“我的读书会”小图书馆，从家里拿书来交换阅读。

D2.en: As a small child, he was woken at dawn to sweep the yard and practice calligraphy, using a homemade brush to write on bricks.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他每天天亮前被叫起床，洒扫庭院后练字，用麻草笔蘸水在砖上写。

D3.en: At 16-17, he read practical books like Xu Guangqi's Complete Treatise on Agriculture and Song Yingxing's Tiangong Kaiwu.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，他课外认真阅读了徐光启的《农政全书》和宋应星的《天工开物》等经世致用的书。

D19.en: At 10, he began collecting Bilderbücher, stamps, coins, beer steins, and porcelain.
D19.zh: 10岁时，他开始收集图画书、邮票、硬币、啤酒杯和瓷器。

D13a.en: At 8, he pleaded with her for permission to walk home like the other boys.
D13a.zh: 8岁时，他恳求她允许他像其他男孩一样走路回家。

D18.en: At age 5-10, he asked his father for ice cream money and received a $20 gold piece, treating the whole neighborhood.
D18.zh: 5到10岁时，他向父亲要冰淇淋钱，却得到一枚20美元金币，请了整个街区吃糖果。

D5.en: At age 5, his mother booked passage for him on steamers from New York to California via Panama.
D5.zh: 5岁时，他母亲为他订了从纽约经巴拿马到加州的轮船票。

D11.en: At 14, he saw Booth's Hamlet and was so smitten by Adelaide Neilson's Juliet that he couldn't sleep for two nights.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他看了Booth的《哈姆雷特》，被Adelaide Neilson的朱丽叶迷住，两夜未眠。

D15.en: At age 4, he tried to turn somersaults and climb poles.
D15.zh: 4岁时，他试着翻跟头和爬杆子。

D9.en: At age 15, he and his friends built a theater in the stable and presented shows for their parents.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他和朋友在马厩里建了剧院，为父母表演歌舞剧。

D7.en: At age 5-10, he created panic at his mother's reception by releasing a mechanical mouse.
D7.zh: 5到10岁时，他在母亲的招待会上放出一只机械老鼠，引起恐慌。

D12.en: At age 4, his mother decided to rent out their San Francisco house and move to her parents' farm for the summer.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他母亲决定出租旧金山的房子，夏天搬到外公外婆的农场。

D17.en: At 10, he wanted to give away all his money and clothes to help others.
D17.zh: 10岁时，他想把自己所有的钱和衣服都送出去帮助别人。

D13b.en: As a small child, he was baptized in the Catholic church by the parish priest.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他在天主教堂由教区神父施洗。

D4.en: At age 10-11, in Venice, he bought glass objects.
D4.zh: 10到11岁时，他在威尼斯买了玻璃制品。

D2.en: At 10, he studied three hours daily and visited galleries, palaces, churches.
D2.zh: 10岁时，他每天学习三小时，并参观画廊、宫殿、教堂。

D3.en: At age 5-10, he learned Chinese from the house boy, counting to 100 and knowing many words.
D3.zh: 5到10岁时，他跟家里的佣人学中文，能数到100，还认识很多词。

D1.en: At 14, he collected his monthly salary, grabbed his bundle, and fled the food shop at night.
D1.zh: 14岁时，他领了当月薪水，提着包袱趁夜逃离了食品店。

D13a.en: At 14, he agreed with his father to study hard, and after effort, he returned to school.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他点头同意父亲要搞好学习，经过努力复了学。

D18.en: At 13, his father sent him to work as a shop assistant at a food store in Hankou.
D18.zh: 13岁时，父亲送他到汉口一家食品店当店员。

M1.en: At 17, he resolved to make a name in academia, especially in logic.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他暗下决心要在学术上，特别是逻辑方面干出名堂。

D9.en: At 15, he disagreed with his parents and insisted on going to Beijing.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他不同意父母意见，坚决要求去北京。

D7.en: At 13, he ran upstairs, locked the landlady and accountant in a room, then ran back down.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他冲上楼，将老板娘和账房先生锁在房里，然后跑下楼。

D6.en: At 15-17, he was already home when Du Dongsheng passed by, and Du would come in to chat with him.
D6.zh: 15到17岁时，他放学回到家后，杜东生路过就会进来和他大谈一通。

D2.en: At 15, he pleaded with his father to let him continue studying, saying his efforts would be wasted otherwise.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他反复对父亲说，如果不读书，过去的心血岂不白费，恳求让他去读书。

D3.en: As a teenager, he eagerly read every issue of Zhang Yinlin's articles on Russell and Western philosophy in the Ta Kung Pao.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他每期必读《大公报》上张荫麟介绍罗素和西方哲学的文章，读得废寝忘食。

D12.en: He stood at the counter for a while, then the boss's wife made him buy bread, the worst chore.
D12.zh: 他站了一段时间柜台后，老板娘让他去进面包，这是店里最苦的差事。

D4.en: At 17, after half a year, he finished translating the 400,000-word 'Logic Basics' and sighed with relief.
D4.zh: 17岁时，经过半年努力，他译完40万字的《逻辑基本》，松了一口气。

D18.en: At 16, he saw a recruitment ad on a utility pole and felt it was a chance to explore the outside world.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他看到电线杆上的招生广告，觉得是个闯荡的机会。

D5.en: At 16, he carefully walked into a cave along a rock wall, then turned back.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他扶着山壁踏着岩石走进山洞，片刻后转身走回。

D11.en: At age 11-12, while reading Water Margin, he imagined a fat monk jumping out of bed, and from then on could visualize characters.
D11.zh: 他十一、二歲時，讀《水滸傳》時想像出一個胖和尚，從此看小說就有這個功能。

M1.en: At 16, he felt a stir and saw a chance to venture outside.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他心里咯噔一下，觉得这是个去外埠闯荡的机会。

D2.en: As a child, he loved reading; his essays were often class models, and by junior high he had mastered 3000 Chinese characters.
D2.zh: 他从小爱读书，小学作文常是班里范文，初中毕业时已熟练掌握3000个常用汉字。

D3.en: At 11-12, he visualized stories as movies in his mind while reading.
D3.zh: 11到12岁时，他看书时在脑中把文字化为电影画面。

D12.en: At 15, his parents went to Hong Kong without him.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他父母去了香港，没带他。

M7.en: At 16, he marched into Inner Mongolia with vigor.
M7.zh: 16岁时，他意气风发地挺进内蒙古。

D9.en: At 16, he was in a 20-person work group transporting 200,000 jin of grain to Shuanggou.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他随20人工作组押运20万斤粮食挺进双沟。

D7.en: At 16, he sneaked out, jumped on a horse cart, and went to Tiger Hill to visit scenic spots.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他偷偷溜出跳上马车直奔虎丘游名胜古迹。

M6.en: From age 9 to 14, he felt like a chosen person.
M6.zh: 9到14岁时，他觉得自己是被选中的人。

M4.en: He found himself drawn to older men as mentors, pivotal to his intellectual, spiritual, and business development.
M4.zh: 他发现自己被年长男性吸引，视他们为导师，这对他的智力、精神和商业发展至关重要。

D10.en: At age 7, he felt forgotten and lonely, as if his heart was taken away.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他感觉被全世界遗忘，孤独空虚，心被掏空。

D1.en: He long sought his mother's praise before 1989, with no result.
D1.zh: 1989年前，他长期寻求母亲表扬，但无果。

D13a.en: He spent a year convincing his mother.
D13a.zh: 他花了一年时间说服母亲。

D11.en: At age 11-12, he wrote a poem expressing his yearning to see his father.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他写诗表达对父亲的思念。

D2.en: At 13-14, he predated his birth to November 1947 to work as a youngster in Hong Kong.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他把出生日期改到1947年11月，以便在香港做童工。

D18.en: He hustled from his early childhood days in China until age 12.
D18.zh: 他幼年时在中国就开始奔波谋生，直到12岁。

D5.en: At 12, he hid a coin and entered Macau without incident.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他藏了一枚硬币，顺利进入澳门。

D12.en: At 8, he became a porter at the Guangzhou train station.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他在广州火车站当搬运工。

D5.en: As a teenager, he often overtaxed himself and was bedridden after ambitious outings in cold and rain.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他经常过度劳累，在寒冷和雨中雄心勃勃地外出后卧床不起。

D11.en: At age 8, he declared he would start his own newspaper one day.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他宣称有一天要创办自己的报纸。

D15.en: As a teenager, he used cycling to build strength but often overtaxed himself and was bedridden.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他通过骑车锻炼体力，但常因过度劳累而卧病在床。

D4.en: At age 8, an editor gave him a bag of used type, possibly launching his Fleet Street career.
D4.zh: 8岁时，编辑给了他一袋旧铅字，或许由此开启了他的舰队街生涯。

D9.en: As a teenager, he led a local cycle club, with members rallying behind a bugler.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他领导当地自行车俱乐部，成员在号手带领下集合出游。

D7.en: At 16, he had made up his mind despite others' efforts to dissuade him.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他下定决心，别人怎么劝都没用。

D6.en: As a teenager, he joined the bicycling craze and co-owned a 48-inch Coventry high-wheel bicycle.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他加入自行车热潮，与人合买了一辆48英寸的考文垂高轮车。

D3.en: At 15-16, he hounded Milne until allowed to establish and edit the Henley House School Magazine.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，他缠着米尔恩，直到获准创办并编辑《亨利之家学校杂志》。

D12.en: As a child aged 10-14, as the eldest he helped maintain discipline among his siblings.
D12.zh: 10到14岁时，作为长子，他帮着维持弟妹们的纪律。

D10.en: At age 6-12, he burst into tears when his grandmother's aging touched his fragile nerves.
D10.zh: 他幼年时，姥姥的苍老触动了他脆弱的神经，他哇哇大哭。

D14.en: From elementary to high school, he was always the top student.
D14.zh: 他从小学到高中一直是班里最好的学生。

D13a.en: At 18, he grew his hair long as a sign of inner freedom.
D13a.zh: 18岁时他留起长发，感觉内心自由了。

D11.en: As a child, he cried reading about Huang Jiguang, but still played pranks.
D11.zh: 小时候读黄继光故事他流泪，但出门照样堵别人烟囱。

D9.en: In 1981, he entered Beijing Broadcasting Institute's journalism program.
D9.zh: 1981年他考入北京广播学院新闻系。

D12.en: His mother was a committee member and father a political commissar, both strict.
D12.zh: 他母亲是家属委员，父亲是部队政委，两人都很严肃。

D20X.en: At 17-18, he lost the class orator position to Brit.
D20X.zh: 17到18岁时，他失去了班级演说家的职位，输给了Brit。

M8.en: At 15-18, he said missionary life is the most honorable calling.
M8.zh: 15到18岁时，他说传教生活是世界上最光荣的使命。

D19.en: At 16-17, he kept meticulous notes on accepted works.
D19.zh: 16到17岁时，他详细记录被接受的作品。

M7.en: At 14, he learned from his father to see life as a mission for betterment.
M7.zh: 14岁时，他从父亲那里学到把人生看作改善世界的使命。

M6.en: At 15-18, he said his greatest ambition was to be of use in the Foreign Field.
M6.zh: 15到18岁时，他说他最大的抱负是在国外传教领域发挥作用。

M2.en: He felt lifelong tension between his secular ambitions and his father's sacrificial example.
M2.zh: 他一生都在自己的世俗抱负与父亲牺牲奉献的榜样之间感到矛盾。

D20.en: At 16, he scrambled for advancement in one organization after another.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他忙于在多个组织中争取晋升。

M4.en: At 16, he favored exporting armaments to the Allied powers.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他支持向协约国出口军火。

D16.en: At 17-18, he was named class poet and read an ode at Class Day.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他被选为班级诗人并在毕业日朗诵短诗。

D10.en: At 12, he felt life was a hanging torture and sympathized with prisoners wanting suicide.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他觉得生活像悬吊的折磨，同情想自杀的囚犯。

D14.en: As a small child, he declaimed before an imagined crowd of parishioners.
D14.zh: 幼年时，他对着想象中的教众演讲。

D13b.en: As a small child, he knelt in prayer before bed, a lifelong habit.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他睡前跪着祈祷，成为终身习惯。

D13a.en: At 6-7, he rebelled against governesses, so his mother took over his instruction.
D13a.zh: 6到7岁时，他反抗家庭女教师，母亲接管了他的教育。

D18.en: At 15-18, he learned to handle his own finances, travel, doctors, housing, vacations.
D18.zh: 15到18岁间，他学会处理自己的财务、旅行、医生、住房和假期。

D5.en: At 14, he boarded a ship in Shanghai to travel back to America alone.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他在上海登船，独自返回美国。

M1.en: At 17-18, he finished senior year second in his class, not first as usual.
M1.zh: 17到18岁间，他高中毕业时班级排名第二，而非往常的第一。

D4.en: At 14-15, he bought a bicycle, taught himself to ride, and cycled around St. Albans.
D4.zh: 14到15岁时，他买了一辆自行车，自学骑行，在圣奥尔本斯周边骑车。

D9.en: At 17, he took over business dealings for the Hotchkiss Lit.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他接手了霍奇基斯文学杂志的商务事务。

D7.en: At 6-7, he rebelled, thinking governesses were inappropriate for boys.
D7.zh: 6到7岁时，他反抗了，认为男孩不该有家庭女教师。

D6.en: At 6-11, he often played tennis with about a dozen boys his age.
D6.zh: 6到11岁时，他常和十来个同龄男孩打网球。

D2.en: At 14-15, he continued working on literature and music, seeking perfection.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，他继续在文学和音乐上努力，追求完美。

D3.en: At 17, he said: 'I learned a good deal of the mechanical part of publishing, and had a most interesting afternoon.'
D3.zh: 17岁时，他说：'我学到了不少出版的技术部分，度过了一个非常有趣的下午。'

D16.en: At 16, he began appearing in the school magazine and rarely missed a debate.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他的名字开始出现在校刊上，几乎每场辩论都参加。

D10.en: He often fled home in tears.
D10.zh: 他常常哭着跑出家门。

D1.en: At age 11, his headmaster said something about him.
D1.zh: 11岁时，他的校长对他有所评价。

D18.en: At 14, he often slipped off to the racetrack to bet on horses.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他常溜去赛马场赌马。

D5.en: At about 9, his mother threw him into a ship's pool's deep end and made him dog-paddle to the side alone.
D5.zh: 大约9岁时，他母亲把他扔进轮船游泳池的深水区，不让人救他，他只好自己狗刨到池边。

D4.en: At 4-9, he found the smell of ink, noise of presses, and charged atmosphere irresistible.
D4.zh: 4到9岁时，他觉得油墨味、印刷机噪音和紧张气氛无法抗拒。

D7.en: At 10-15, he disliked Darling and found it easier to defy his headmaster than his father.
D7.zh: 10到15岁时，他不喜欢Darling，觉得反抗校长比反抗父亲容易。

D6.en: As a small child, his closest friend was Helen; they were almost a self-sufficient unit.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他最好的朋友是Helen，他们几乎自给自足。

D3.en: At 4-9, he loved lying on his father's bed watching him mark good and bad bits in papers.
D3.zh: 4到9岁时，他喜欢躺在父亲床上看他标记报纸的好坏部分。

D12.en: He spent every night in a tree house in the garden as a child.
D12.zh: 他小时候每晚都睡在花园里的树屋里。

M4.en: He asserted he was a conscientious objector.
M4.zh: 他声称自己是出于良心拒服兵役者。

D16.en: At 14, he turned in a brilliant essay on Macaulay's indictment of Warren Hastings.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他交了一篇关于麦考利指控沃伦·黑斯廷斯的精彩文章。

D1.en: At 17-18, feeling misery and despair, he persisted with his idea of becoming a lawyer.
D1.zh: 17到18岁时，他感到痛苦和绝望，但仍坚持要当律师的想法。

D13b.en: At 16, he left school and started work in his cousin's merchant bank.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他离开学校，开始在堂兄的商业银行工作。

D11.en: At 16, he turned in an essay on Warren Hastings that astonished by its literary talent.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他交了一篇关于沃伦·黑斯廷斯的文章，其文学才华令人惊讶。

M1.en: At about 14, he showed mathematical ability and wrote a brilliant essay on Macaulay's indictment of Warren Hastings.
M1.zh: 大约14岁时，他展现了数学才能，并写了一篇关于麦考利指控沃伦·黑斯廷斯的精彩文章。

D3.en: He built a large library and read in his study until 2-3 AM.
D3.zh: 他建了一个大图书馆，在书房读书到凌晨两三点。

D14.en: At 10-15, he pumped the organ for 25 cents.
D14.zh: 10到15岁时，他为了25美分踩风琴。

D5.en: At 16, he borrowed money from a lumberman to buy his freedom.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他向木材商爱德华·辛克莱借钱赎回了自由。

D2.en: At 16, already identified as 'a man o' pairs', he moved on to the University of Edinburgh.
D2.zh: 16岁时，已被视为‘有天赋的人’，他进入了爱丁堡大学。

D10.en: At age 4-5, he froze and cried, then was whipped by Marie for his stubbornness.
D10.zh: 4到5岁时，他愣住哭了起来，玛丽因此打了他一顿。

D14.en: At 15-16, he was a clown in a small traveling circus.
D14.zh: 15到16岁时，他在一个小型巡回马戏团当小丑。

D13b.en: At 18, he enlisted in the Naval Reserve, hoping to see action.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他加入海军预备役，希望最终能上战场。

D13a.en: At 17-18, his parents insisted he finish high school, disappointing him.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁时，父母坚持要他读完高中，这让他很失望。

D5.en: At 15, he ran away from home, worked in sawmills, collected garbage, and was a coal miner.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他离家出走，在锯木厂干活、收垃圾、还当过煤矿工。

D11.en: At 13, he covered his bedroom walls with sketches of early race cars.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他在卧室墙上画满了早期赛车的素描。

D15.en: At 13, he attended a race and was enthralled by the crowd at the brick oval.
D15.zh: 13岁时，他去看了一场赛车比赛，被砖砌椭圆形赛道旁的人群迷住了。

D4.en: As a small child, he sat in the grass and started cutting off roses.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他坐在草地上开始剪玫瑰花。

D7.en: At 18, he was ashamed not to be in the Army at his commencement.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他因没参军而羞于参加毕业典礼。

D2.en: He damaged his eyes in early Army days by drawing for hours in poor light.
D2.zh: 他早期在军队里因光线差长时间画画，伤了眼睛。

D3.en: He drew pictures of cowboys and soldiers as a child, imagining his future self.
D3.zh: 他小时候画牛仔和士兵，想象自己长大后的样子。

D12.en: He went West on his own before age 16 and worked as a ranch hand.
D12.zh: 他不到16岁就独自去了西部，当牧场工人。

D8.en: At 12, he could not stand people he judged stupid or lazy, being bright and ambitious himself.
D8.zh: 12岁时，他无法忍受他认为愚蠢或懒惰的人，他自己聪明、勤奋、有抱负。

M3.en: In his early teens, he became a violent Kiplingmaniac, absorbing Kipling's subjects and imitating his cadences.
M3.zh: 他十几岁时成了狂热的吉卜林迷，吸收吉卜林的主题并模仿其韵律。

M2.en: As a small child, he was instilled with a sense of family honor.
M2.zh: 幼年时，他被灌输了对家族荣誉的信念。

D16.en: As a child, he showed early interest in newspapers.
D16.zh: 他小时候就对报纸感兴趣。

D13a.en: At 15-16, he went to work for Aug. Mencken & Bro.
D13a.zh: 15到16岁时，他去奥格·门肯兄弟公司工作。

D11.en: At 16, he tried his hand at a short story called 'Idyl'.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他尝试写了一篇短篇小说，名为《田园诗》。

D15.en: He kept one on the floor of his Hollins Street office to the end of his life.
D15.zh: 他把一个放在霍林斯街办公室的地板上，直到去世。

D4.en: At 7-8, he was given a printing press for Christmas.
D4.zh: 7到8岁时，他圣诞节得到了一台印刷机。

D9.en: At 18, he became a reporter on the Morning Herald in July 1899.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他于1899年7月成为《晨报》记者。

D2.en: As an adolescent, he spent so much time playing piano at parties that he never learned to dance.
D2.zh: 他十几岁时在派对上弹钢琴太多，以至于从未学会跳舞。

D3.en: At 12-13, he combined chemistry with photography.
D3.zh: 12到13岁时，他把化学和摄影结合起来。

D1.en: At 18, he came back the next day and every day for four straight weeks.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他第二天又回来了，并且连续四周每天都来。

M8.en: She was raised in the Ethical Culture movement with a strict moral code.
M8.zh: 她在伦理文化运动中长大，遵循严格的道德准则。

M3.en: She adored her father as her hero and genius.
M3.zh: 她崇拜父亲，视他为英雄和天才。

D17.en: As a child, she fed and tended two hundred caterpillars under glasses.
D17.zh: 小时候，她喂养和照料了两百只放在玻璃杯下的毛毛虫。

M2.en: She adopted her father's creed of the beauty of utility during childhood walks to factories.
M2.zh: 她童年时在去工厂的路上接受了父亲关于实用之美的信条。

D16.en: At 18, she said she wanted to be a news photographer-reporter and a good one.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她说她想成为一名新闻摄影记者，而且要成为优秀的。

D10.en: She was tired, dragging, nearly paralyzed with fatigue, and often unable to sleep.
D10.zh: 她疲惫不堪，几乎被疲劳拖垮，常常无法入睡。

D14.en: She wanted to give nature-study lectures to schoolchildren.
D14.zh: 她想给学童做自然研究讲座。

D13a.en: While still in college, she embarked on a disastrous first marriage.
D13a.zh: 还在大学时，她开始了一段灾难性的第一次婚姻。

D18.en: She was hired as the photography counselor at Camp Agaming the summer after freshman year.
D18.zh: 大一后的夏天，她被聘为Agaming营地的摄影辅导员。

D5.en: She never hesitated to hang over a cliff for the perfect vantage point.
D5.zh: 为了完美的拍摄位置，她毫不犹豫地悬在悬崖上。

D11.en: She wrote a poem in 1918 promising to bake pies and cakes.
D11.zh: 她在1918年写了一首诗，承诺会烤馅饼和蛋糕。

M1.en: She hoped a series would set her on the road to fame.
M1.zh: 她希望一个系列能让她走上成名之路。

D15.en: She walked to grade school along the narrow top edges of board fences.
D15.zh: 她沿着木板栅栏狭窄的顶部边缘走路上小学。

D4.en: As a small child, she built cages for turtles, snakes, and insects she brought home.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她为带回家的乌龟、蛇和昆虫制作笼子。

D9.en: At 18, she was elected house president.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她被选为宿舍主席。

D7.en: She walked to school with a puff adder twined around her arm.
D7.zh: 她手臂上缠着一条鼓腹毒蛇走去上学。

D6.en: At 18, she photographed for the yearbook throughout the school year.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她为年鉴拍照，持续了整个学年。

D2.en: At 13, she hoped others would think she was good.
D2.zh: 13岁时，她希望别人觉得她表现好。

D16.en: At 12, she went out for the debating team.
D16.zh: 12岁时，她参加了辩论队。

D14.en: At 16, she was voted the wittiest.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她被选为最风趣的人。

D6.en: At 18, she joined the campus paper, the Miscellany News.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她加入了校报《杂闻报》。

M7.en: At 17-18, he returned to Budapest 'to listen to a new music'.
M7.zh: 17到18岁时，他回到布达佩斯‘去听一种新的音乐’。

M4.en: At 17, he swung his pole at a mounted policeman, was wounded on the shoulder, and had a friend treat the wound to avoid expulsion.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他用棍子打一个骑警，肩膀被砍伤，让朋友处理伤口以免被开除。

D10.en: At 16-17, he laughed at his interrogators until they knocked him unconscious.
D10.zh: 16到17岁时，他嘲笑审讯他的人，直到被打晕。

D14.en: At 17, he spent any money he had on food and wine for friends, half generosity and half showing off.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他只要有钱就会花在给朋友买食物和酒上，一半是真心慷慨，一半是炫耀。

D13b.en: At nearly 6, he entered the distinguished Evangelisches School.
D13b.zh: 他快6岁时，进入了著名的福音学校。

D5.en: He risked grave consequences if caught by police or Nazis.
D5.zh: 他冒着被警察或纳粹抓住的严重后果。

D11.en: At 12, he daydreamed about saving girls from robbers to win the right to choose one to marry.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他幻想从强盗手中救出女孩，以此获得挑选一个结婚的权利。

M1.en: He finished school and passed his Abitur examinations.
M1.zh: 他完成了学业并通过了高中毕业考试。

D4.en: He came to Berlin to study at the Deutsche Hochschule für Politik.
D4.zh: 他来到柏林，在德国政治学院学习。

D9.en: He introduced himself to Guttmann and asked for a job.
D9.zh: 他向古特曼自我介绍并求职。

D7.en: He marched through working-class districts with banners, shouting slogans.
D7.zh: 他举着横幅在工人区游行，高喊口号。

D6.en: He occasionally sought refuge at his mother's cousin's apartment.
D6.zh: 他偶尔去他母亲的表亲家避难。

D2.en: He failed mathematics and geometrical drawing and was tutored by his uncle.
D2.zh: 他数学和几何作图不及格，由叔叔辅导。

D3.en: As a teenager, he accompanied a female photographer on expeditions through Budapest and the countryside.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他陪伴一位女摄影师在布达佩斯和乡村进行摄影探险。

D12.en: He was born on October 22, 1913, in Budapest.
D12.zh: 他于1913年10月22日出生在布达佩斯。

D16.en: At 8-9, he heard Theodore Roosevelt speak and was deeply moved.
D16.zh: 8到9岁时，他听了西奥多·罗斯福的演讲，激动得难以忘怀。

D14.en: At 17, his debate coach urged him to go to Columbia Law School after Harvard.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他的辩论教练建议他哈佛毕业后去哥伦比亚法学院。

D13b.en: At 14, he was confirmed in May 1904.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他在1904年5月接受了坚信礼。

D13a.en: As a small child to young adult, he insisted the Jewish issue did not touch him.
D13a.zh: 幼年到青年时期，他坚称犹太问题与他无关。

D11.en: At 16, he echoed his master in deploring the corruption of the modern age.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他附和老师，谴责现代社会的腐败。

D15.en: At 7, he made his first transatlantic crossing on R.M.S. Etruria.
D15.zh: 7岁时，他乘坐R.M.S. Etruria号首次横渡大西洋。

D7.en: At 18, he dropped out of the athletic circle and focused on Harvard's intellectual side.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他退出体育圈，专注于哈佛的学术方面。

D6.en: At 17, he took the trolley across the Charles River to the pink Italianate villa on the Fenway.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他乘电车穿过查尔斯河，来到芬威的粉色意大利式别墅。

D3.en: At 15-16, he was captivated by Ruskin's vision in The Stones of Venice and Seven Lamps of Architecture.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，他被拉斯金在《威尼斯之石》和《建筑的七盏明灯》中的视野所吸引。

D12.en: He was born in New York City.
D12.zh: 他出生在纽约市。

M1.en: At 14, he made Phi Beta Kappa.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他入选Phi Beta Kappa。

D18.en: At 17, he shocked wheat in Kansas with two classmates to earn money for a trip to California.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他和两个同学在堪萨斯州捆麦子挣钱去加州旅行。

D7.en: He kept his travel plans from his parents.
D7.zh: 他对父母隐瞒了自己的旅行计划。

D12.en: At 15, he had to sell his saxophone to pay for damage his father made him pay for.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他不得不卖掉心爱的萨克斯管来赔偿父亲让他支付的损失。

D11.en: At 12, she told her mother she intended to become a newspaperwoman.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她告诉母亲她打算成为一名女记者。

D2.en: As a child, she absorbed her parents' love of learning and value of education.
D2.zh: 小时候，她吸收了父母对学习的热爱和对教育的重视。

D3.en: As a young teen, she listened to her sister read Shakespeare aloud.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她听姐姐大声朗读莎士比亚。

M3.en: He clipped out stories about the Apollo program, treating them as sacred.
M3.zh: 他剪下阿波罗计划的报道，视若神圣。

D17.en: He shot a bird with a BB gun and gave up killing animals for life.
D17.zh: 他用BB枪打了一只鸟，从此不再杀生。

D14.en: He finagled getting a saxophone and clarinet for Christmas to learn Buddy Rogers songs.
D14.zh: 他设法在圣诞节得到萨克斯和单簧管，好学Buddy Rogers的歌。

D13a.en: He pleaded to stay in Missouri, but his pleas went nowhere.
D13a.zh: 他恳求留在密苏里，但没被理会。

D18.en: He delivered copies of the Post on a bicycle route to customers.
D18.zh: 他骑自行车沿路给顾客送《邮报》。

D5.en: At age 10-11, he dug a tunnel on a vacant lot using gunpowder from firecrackers and started a raging fire.
D5.zh: 10到11岁时，他在空地上用鞭炮的火药挖隧道，引发了一场大火。

D11.en: As a student, he was undistinguished but a voracious reader.
D11.zh: 他学生时代成绩平平，但酷爱阅读。

M1.en: At 18, he repeatedly begged his editor for an oceangoing vessel assignment.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他反复恳求编辑给他一个远洋船只的报道任务。

D15.en: He was involved in automobile racing at age 16.
D15.zh: 他16岁时参与赛车。

D9.en: At 17-18, he was named sports editor of the student paper, the Campus Cub.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他被任命为学生报纸《校园幼兽》的体育编辑。

D2.en: At 16-17, he sought out and voraciously read books on journalism suggested by his teacher.
D2.zh: 16到17岁时，他找来老师推荐的新闻学书籍并如饥似渴地阅读。

D19.en: As a small child, he was drilled in table manners and how to behave when adults entered.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他被训练餐桌礼仪和大人进屋时的举止。

D16.en: As a teenager, he was persuaded by Ruth Lawson to join the debate team.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他被Ruth Lawson说服加入了辩论队。

D10.en: Between ages 5 and 12, he had periodic outbursts his family called moody streaks, marking the onset of depression.
D10.zh: 5到12岁间，他周期性情绪爆发，家人称之为情绪化，标志着抑郁的开始。

D13a.en: At 18, he temporarily gave up smoking, drinking, and swearing and went home.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他暂时戒了烟、酒和脏话，然后回家了。

M1.en: In college, he was class president, top ROTC cadet, and campus politician.
M1.zh: 大学期间，他当了班长、顶尖ROTC学员和校园政治家。

D2.en: He took all her classes, and she became a major influence on him.
D2.zh: 他上了她所有的课，她对他产生了重大影响。

D12.en: At 5, he ate quickly, aware his parents were going hungry.
D12.zh: 5岁时，他快速吃饭，意识到父母在挨饿。

D17.en: At 16-17, he ordered flares set out, sent for a doctor, wrapped injured men in blankets, and comforted crying children.
D17.zh: 16到17岁时，他命令学生设置信号弹，派人打电话叫医生和救护车，用毯子包裹伤者，并安慰哭泣的孩子。

D13b.en: At 18, he chose Ohio Wesleyan over Cornell to be near his girlfriend.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他为了离女友近而选择俄亥俄卫斯理大学，放弃康奈尔。

D18.en: At 17, he decided to work in a logging camp to save money for college.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他决定在伐木营工作一年攒钱去东部。

D5.en: At 6, his family's move to Washington seemed a bold undertaking.
D5.zh: 6岁时，他家人搬到华盛顿似乎是个大胆的举动。

D15.en: At 16-17, he became his school's first bus driver.
D15.zh: 16到17岁时，他成了学校的第一位校车司机。

D4.en: At 17-18, he started as an axman at Bloedell-Donovan.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，他在Bloedell-Donovan从伐木工做起。

D9.en: At 17, he was elected senior class president and student body president.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他被选为高年级班长和学生会主席。

D6.en: At 18, he joined in songs celebrating Joe Hill and Wobbly heroes.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他跟着唱歌颂乔·希尔和世界产盟英雄的歌。

D3.en: At 16, he became the high school correspondent for the Cedar Rapids paper.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他成为锡达拉皮兹报纸的高中通讯员。

D14.en: At 16, she partied the night away with Dempster and hundreds of sybarites, hedonists, and celebrities.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她和Dempster以及数百名享乐主义者、名流彻夜狂欢。

D13a.en: At 17, with her father's help, she was accepted into a training program at Harrods, working in every department to become a buyer.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，在父亲帮助下，她进入Harrods的培训项目，在每个部门工作，目标是成为买手。

D15.en: At 10, she was amazingly fleet of foot.
D15.zh: 10岁时，她跑得飞快。

D6.en: At 11-12, she met cousins who arrived with their parents from Hollywood.
D6.zh: 11到12岁时，她见到了从好莱坞随父母来的表亲。

D2.en: At 15, she redid her room in elegant green, faintly striped wallpaper.
D2.zh: 15岁时，她重新装修了房间，用了优雅的绿色细条纹壁纸。

D3.en: At 14-16, she kept on top of restaurant, pub, and club ratings in trendy magazines and guides.
D3.zh: 14到16岁时，她紧跟时尚杂志和指南中的餐厅、酒吧和俱乐部评级。

D5.en: At 17-18, she almost set the classroom on fire during a science experiment.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，她差点在科学实验中把教室烧了。

D16.en: At 17-18, he won medals and ribbons in high school forensics club.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他在高中辩论俱乐部赢得了奖牌和绶带。

D10.en: At 12, he began to cry, feeling sorry for himself.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他哭了起来，为自己感到难过。

D1.en: At about 12-13, he resolved to prove he was better than their kids.
D1.zh: 12到13岁时，他下定决心要证明自己比他们的孩子强。

D13a.en: At 18, he scrambled off to bed, promising to call everyone and apologize as Dad insisted.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他匆忙上床，答应按父亲的要求第二天给所有人打电话道歉。

D5.en: At 15, he hitched a ride from Pickstown to Yankton, his new home.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他搭便车从Pickstown到了新家Yankton。

D11.en: At 3-5, he was shooting imaginary Japanese and German soldiers from a ditch.
D11.zh: 3到5岁时，他在沟里射击想象中的日本和德国士兵。

M1.en: In junior high, he qualified for the state track meet as a pole vaulter.
M1.zh: 初中时，他作为撑杆跳高选手获得了参加州田径赛的资格。

D15.en: At 12, he knew every Gopher player from reading the Tribune sports section weekly.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他通过每周阅读《论坛报》体育版认识了所有Gopher球员。

D4.en: As a small child, he got a wooden wagon, carved wooden cars, and a toy pistol from the metalworking shop.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他得到了一个木制马车、雕刻的木车和金属加工车间做的玩具手枪。

D9.en: At 15, he was elected homeroom president on his first day in class.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他在上课第一天被选为班级主席。

D7.en: As a teenager, he not only removed the snow but also emptied the basement of coal and drove off.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他不仅铲掉了薄雪，还把地下室新到的煤全运走了。

D6.en: As a teenager, he developed a taste for fish sticks thanks to Friday pregame meals with his Catholic teammates.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，因为周五赛前和天主教队友一起吃饭，他喜欢上了炸鱼条。

D2.en: At 15, he taught swimming, canoeing, and rowing before lifeguarding for the afternoon general swim session.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他教游泳、划独木舟和划船，然后在下午的普通游泳时段担任救生员。

D3.en: At 10-14, he joined a fossil and rock club organized for interested kids.
D3.zh: 10到14岁时，他加入了一个为感兴趣的孩子组织的化石和岩石俱乐部。

D14.en: At 12, he boldly introduced himself as a basketball player on the junior high team in Pickstown.
D14.zh: 12岁时，他大胆地自我介绍是皮克斯敦初中篮球队的球员。

D20X.en: At 17, he planned to later apply to Hunan Higher Industrial School.
D20X.zh: 17岁时，他准备以后再去投考湖南高等工业学校。

M5.en: At 17, he scored first overall in the entrance exam and became a member of the school's first cohort.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他以应试总成绩第一的高分成为该校成立后的第一班学员。

D8.en: At 17, he scored first overall in the entrance exam to become a member of the school's inaugural class.
D8.zh: 17岁时，他以应试总成绩第一的高分成为该校成立后的第一班学员。

M2.en: At 17, he considered his grandfather's words about family honor but did not give up his dream of studying industry.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他听了祖父关于家庭荣誉的话，但没有放弃学工业的理想。

M4.en: At 18, his passion for medicine burned forever in his heart.
M4.zh: 18岁时，为医学事业献身的热情永远在他心中燃烧着。

D1.en: At 18, he repeatedly weighed 'poverty' and 'illness' on the scales of his heart.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他反复把“贫”和“病”两个字投向心灵天平的两端，比较它们的分量。

D13a.en: At 17, he agreed to apply to medical school as a temporary measure, planning to later apply to Hunan Higher Industrial School.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他答应了去报考医科，但这只是权宜之计，他准备以后再去投考湖南高等工业学校。

D5.en: At 14, he secretly signed up for the student army against his family's wishes.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他瞒着祖父和父母报名参加了学生军。

M1.en: At 15-17, driven by industrial salvation, he studied harder and excelled in all subjects.
M1.zh: 15到17岁间，他满怀工业救贫的思想，更加奋发学习，各科成绩突出。

D15.en: At 18, he was an active participant and a natural member.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他是积极的参加者和当然的会员。

D7.en: At 13, he begged his grandfather to let him visit Yuelu Mountain.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他央求祖父允许他到岳麓山游玩一次。

D6.en: At 16, he joined the Furen Study Society founded by Yi Keyi and Zhou Changxian.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他参加了由易克嶷、周长宪等发起组织的“辅仁学社”。

D12.en: At 17, his father hoped he would become an apprentice to earn money for the family.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他父亲希望他中学毕业后到商店当学徒，挣钱养家。

M7.en: At 17, she couldn't believe workers only got 25 cents an hour.
M7.zh: 17岁时，她无法相信工人每小时只挣25美分。

M4.en: At 15, she joined an antiwar march in Washington, DC.
M4.zh: 15岁时她参加了华盛顿的反战游行。

D1.en: At 14-15, she got a lifesaving certificate and saved babysitting money.
D1.zh: 14到15岁时，她拿到了救生证，攒下了看孩子的钱。

D13a.en: At 16, she escaped.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她逃走了。

D18.en: At 16-17, she worked as a waitress one summer.
D18.zh: 16到17岁时，她有一个夏天当服务员。

D11.en: At 12, she started keeping a diary.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她开始写日记。

M1.en: At 16, she became a National Merit Scholarship finalist.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她成为国家优秀奖学金决赛选手。

D15.en: At 10, she crashed into an oak tree, breaking her nose and forehead.
D15.zh: 10岁时，她猛撞上一棵橡树，摔断了鼻子，磕破了额头。

D4.en: At 14, she took the tiller most of the day and sailed fast in the Sound.
D4.zh: 14岁时，她掌舵大半天，在长岛海峡快速航行。

D9.en: At 16, she decided to go abroad for a year on a cultural exchange with the American Field Service program.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她决定参加美国战地服务团的文化交流项目出国一年。

D7.en: At 14, she wore a mini skirt to church, which her parents disapproved of.
D7.zh: 14岁时，她穿迷你裙去教堂，父母不喜欢。

D6.en: At 16, she followed neighbors kissing each other because she couldn't talk to them.
D6.zh: 16岁时，她跟着邻居互相亲吻，因为她没法跟他们说话。

D2.en: At 17, she was fanatical about being in shape and obsessive about exercise.
D2.zh: 17岁时，她狂热地保持身材，痴迷于锻炼。

D3.en: At 16, she applied to go to South America, a decision that made news in Oyster Bay.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她申请去南美，这个决定在牡蛎湾成了新闻。

D14.en: At 16, she walked the catwalk as a debutante and danced the waltz with Oswaldo.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她作为社交名媛走秀，并与Oswaldo跳了华尔兹。

D14.en: She actively participated in performances, rarely missing one.
D14.zh: 她积极参加演出，几乎每次都不落下。

D11.en: At 9-10, she wished she had been born earlier to be a doctor and save her mother.
D11.zh: 9到10岁时，她天真地想：要是早生几年当医生，就能把妈妈留在世上。

M1.en: At 9, she couldn't stand scoring 99 when others got 100, and bounced a ball 1001 times.
M1.zh: 9岁时，别人考一百分，她不甘心考九十九；别人拍一千下皮球，她要拍一千零一下。

D15.en: At 9, she bounced a ball 1001 times when others did 1000.
D15.zh: 9岁时，别人拍一千下皮球，她要拍一千零一下。

D4.en: At 18 in 1920, she crocheted a black hairnet with fine thread, moving her hands skillfully.
D4.zh: 1920年18岁时，她用勾针勾织黑色发网，线细网小，但动作娴熟，花纹不断变幻。

D2.en: At 10-13, she often read greedily late into the night.
D2.zh: 10到13岁时，她常常在灯下贪婪地读到深夜。

D12.en: At 5 in 1906, her mother died of cervical cancer, and she lost maternal love too early.
D12.zh: 1906年5岁时，母亲患宫颈癌病故，她过早失去慈母之爱。

D13b.en: At age seven, she was baptized.
D13b.zh: 七岁那年，她参加了受洗礼。

D3.en: At 10-13, she often read late into the night, meeting characters like Romeo, Juliet, Carton, Jane Eyre.
D3.zh: 10到13岁时，她常在灯下读到深夜，罗密欧、朱丽叶、卡尔登、简·爱等人物闯进她的生活。

D18.en: As a child, he knew his family was not well off because his father was a teacher.
D18.zh: 他小时候知道家里不富裕，因为父亲是老师。

M1.en: He was pleased to be accepted and started his medical journey at age 17.
M1.zh: 他很高兴被录取，17岁开始了医学之路。

D3.en: Seeing this, he came to admire physicians and felt medicine was noble.
D3.zh: 看到这些，他非常敬佩医生，觉得医学是高尚的职业。

D12.en: He studied at Xiao He Yan medical university from 1927 to 1933 and graduated on June 30, 1933.
D12.zh: 他1927年至1933年在小河沿医科大学学习，并于1933年6月30日毕业。

D9.en: He was assigned to Robert Koch Hospital to take over the work.
D9.zh: 他被派到罗伯特·科赫医院接管工作。

D13a.en: He said his mother was determined to go back home, and what she decided, they did.
D13a.zh: 他说他母亲决心回家，母亲决定的事，全家都会照做。

D18.en: He came home triumphantly with a new dollar bill from lugging sticks.
D18.zh: 他得意地回到家，带着拖树枝赚来的一美元新钞。

D2.en: At 10-12, he went to the golf course after school to caddy and weeded greens on Saturday mornings.
D2.zh: 10到12岁时，他放学后去高尔夫球场当球童，周六早上除草赚两美元。

D12.en: As a boy, he brought home up to $15 some weeks to help his mother.
D12.zh: 他小时候有时一周能带回家15美元，帮补母亲的开销。

M1.en: At 17-18, he won the state high school golf championship, the Dayton District Men's tournament, and the Ohio Public Links title.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，他赢得了州高中高尔夫锦标赛、代顿地区男子赛和俄亥俄公共球场赛冠军。

D15.en: He played center forward on freshman and varsity teams for four years.
D15.zh: 他四年里在新生队和校队踢中锋。

M6.en: At 16, he decided to major in philosophy.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他决定主修哲学。

D16.en: At 18, he applied to the Philomathean Society and gave a talk on Robinson Jeffers.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他申请加入Philomathean Society，并做了关于Robinson Jeffers的演讲。

D13b.en: At 16, he gained a place at the University of Pennsylvania.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他进入了宾夕法尼亚大学。

D13a.en: At 12-14, he was told to watch customers but often read instead.
D13a.zh: 12到14岁时，他被要求盯着顾客，却常常只顾看书。

D18.en: At 14, he sold newspapers at the station every weekday morning that winter.
D18.zh: 14岁时，那年冬天每个工作日的早晨他都在车站卖报纸。

D11.en: At 17, he had a sonnet published in his high school yearbook.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他有一首十四行诗发表在高中年鉴上。

M1.en: At 16, he had to abandon his ambition to attend Harvard.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他不得不放弃上哈佛的志向。

D15.en: As a small child, he enjoyed fishing in Evans Pond.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他喜欢在Evans Pond钓鱼。

D9.en: He soon became its acknowledged intellectual star.
D9.zh: 他很快成为公认的学术明星。

D7.en: He convinced classmates to eat Limburger cheese or raw garlic, then closed windows to torment the teacher.
D7.zh: 他说服同学吃林堡奶酪或生大蒜，然后关上窗户折磨老师。

D6.en: At 18, he offered his dorm room to a friend.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他邀请朋友随时使用他的宿舍房间。

D3.en: At 14, he came upon back issues of the Nation and the New Republic.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他偶然发现了《国家》和《新共和》的过刊。

D12.en: He was born a peddler's son in the slums of Philadelphia.
D12.zh: 他出生在费城贫民窟，是个小贩的儿子。

M7.en: He chose to stay in Guangzhou out of patriotism.
M7.zh: 他出于爱国之心，选择留在广州。

D13b.en: He was admitted to the prestigious Affiliated High School of South China Normal University.
D13b.zh: 他考上了华南师范大学附属中学。

D5.en: He climbed out of the window and slid down a bamboo pole.
D5.zh: 他爬出窗台，顺着竹竿往下溜。

D11.en: He imitated martial arts heroes from comic books and challenged friends to a duel in the bamboo grove.
