D11.zh: 他学小人书里大侠的样子，约小伙伴到竹林里一决高低。

D15.en: At 11, he climbed out of a window, grabbed bamboo branches, and slid down a bamboo pole.
D15.zh: 11岁时，他爬出窗台，抓住竹枝，顺着竹竿往下溜。

D4.en: He loved to play with white mice in his father's study after school.
D4.zh: 他放学后总爱到父亲的书房里逗弄小白鼠。

D6.en: He was the Mandarin phonetics group leader in elementary school and class monitor in middle school.
D6.zh: 他在小学是国音组长，中学当上了班长。

D2.en: He became the family's mouse keeper, the most responsible little caretaker.
D2.zh: 他成了家里的白鼠饲养员，最负责的小饲养员。

D3.en: As a child, he would become quiet and immersed in books whenever he entered his father's study.
D3.zh: 小时候，他一进父亲的书房就安静下来，沉浸在书的世界里。

D12.en: At age 10-13, he became the family's white mouse caretaker.
D12.zh: 10到13岁时，他成了家里的白鼠饲养员。

D1.en: As the youngest student, he was bullied and ate only rice with lard.
D1.zh: 他年纪最小被欺负，只能吃猪油拌饭。

D18.en: As a child, he took a cigarette pack from a guest's pocket to collect cards.
D18.zh: 他小时候掏客人口袋拿香烟盒收集烟标。

D5.en: At 17, he fell into the Qiantang River while rafting and grabbed a pole to survive.
D5.zh: 他17岁时划竹排落水，抓住撑杆脱险。

M1.en: At 18, he was one of 200 admitted to Tongji University Medical School out of 2000 applicants.
M1.zh: 他18岁时从2000人中脱颖而出，考入同济大学医学院。

D15.en: As a teenager, he climbed to the 13th floor of Liuhe Pagoda to read daily.
D15.zh: 他十几岁时每天上六和塔十三层读书。

D4.en: As a small child, he raised a dog named Ringer, feeding and bathing it.
D4.zh: 他幼年时养了一条叫Ringer的狗，喂它洗澡陪睡。

D7.en: During nine years at school, he fought classmates who called his father a nickname.
D7.zh: 他在正则中学九年，因同学叫父亲外号而打架。

D2.en: His father made him memorize classical texts and knocked his head for mistakes.
D2.zh: 他父亲逼他背古文观止和唐诗，背不好就敲头。

D12.en: His mother sent him lard in a jar to mix with rice at school.
D12.zh: 他母亲用玻璃罐装猪油让他带到学校拌饭吃。

D6.en: He often played four-handed duets with his Polish friend Kolinski.
D6.zh: 他经常和波兰朋友Kolinski一起弹四手联弹。

D2.en: He was studious, had private math lessons, and took a mild interest in philosophy.
D2.zh: 他勤奋好学，上数学私教课，对哲学有轻微兴趣。

D12.en: He was born in Berlin in 1906 to a Russian emigre father and a German mother.
D12.zh: 他1906年出生于柏林，父亲是俄国移民，母亲是德国人。

M5.en: At 16, he thought he would feel foolish staying home while others went to war.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他认为自己如果待在家里而别人去参战会显得很傻。

M6.en: At 16-17, he had decided on a research career in science.
M6.zh: 16到17岁时，他决心从事科学研究事业。

M4.en: He said, 'I don't want to go, but I ought to, and that's the whole position.'
M4.zh: 他说：‘我不想去，但我应该去，这就是全部立场。’

D16.en: At 17, he spoke on this subject in a school debate.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他在学校辩论中就此主题发言。

D1.en: In spite of a second attack of pneumonia in 1914, he continued to excel at school.
D1.zh: 尽管1914年第二次患上肺炎，他仍在学校表现出色。

D14.en: He chose the moment of maximum exposure and then raised the curtain, which one must imagine was dramatic.
D14.zh: 他选择了曝光度最高的时刻，然后拉开帷幕，可以想象效果很戏剧化。

D13b.en: It was decided that he should enter the Medical School in Adelaide.
D13b.zh: 决定让他进入阿德莱德的医学院。

D13a.en: At 18, he was stopped from going to war by the government's decision to issue badges to medical students.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，政府决定给医学生发徽章，阻止了他去参战。

D5.en: At 18, he rode at terrifying speed out of the city and up a track into the Mount Lofty hills.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他以可怕的速度骑出城市，沿着一条小路进入Mount Lofty山丘。

D11.en: He had read a good deal of what had been published.
D11.zh: 他阅读了大量已发表的内容。

M1.en: At 13-16, he won prize after prize: five in chemistry, and seven in physics, mathematics, and history.
M1.zh: 13到16岁间，他赢得一个又一个奖项：化学五项，物理、数学和历史七项。

D9.en: As a teenager, he saw Coreega become a regular venue for gatherings and tennis parties.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他目睹Coreega成为聚会和网球派对的常去地点。

D7.en: At 18, when he approached his parents with his intention to enlist, they threw every argument in his way.
D7.zh: 18岁时，当他向父母提出入伍意向时，他们用各种理由阻拦他。

D2.en: At 13-18, he maintained this leading position in each of the five years he was at the college.
D2.zh: 13到18岁间，他在学院的五年里每年都保持领先地位。

D3.en: At 13, he acquired an enthusiasm for chemistry from an inspiring teacher, J. S. Thompson.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他从一位鼓舞人心的老师J. S. Thompson那里获得了对化学的热情。

D12.en: As a small child, he could see his father rattle home from the city in one of Adelaide's first cars.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他能看到父亲乘坐阿德莱德最早的汽车之一从城里颠簸回家。

D14.en: At 14, he stood at the back of the gallery to see Charley's Aunt.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他站在剧院楼厅后排看《查理的姑妈》。

D13b.en: At age five, he went to school.
D13b.zh: 五岁时，他去上学了。

D18.en: At age 14-15, he found employment in a shipping company.
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，他在一家航运公司找到了工作。

D5.en: At age 8-10, he broke his nose colliding with another boy.
D5.zh: 8到10岁时，他与另一个男孩相撞，撞断了鼻子。

D11.en: He was a great inventor of new games during long winter evenings.
D11.zh: 在漫长的冬夜里，他发明了许多新游戏。

D4.en: He was trained to do most things with rubber teats and capillary tubes, making his own gear.
D4.zh: 他接受训练，用橡胶乳头和毛细管做大部分事情，自己制作所需工具。

D9.en: At 14, he was the natural leader in expeditions with his companion Robert.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他是与同伴Robert探险中的自然领袖。

D7.en: At 16, he showed independence by wearing a bow-tie, which he continued for life.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他表现出独立性，戴上了领结，此后一生都如此。

D2.en: He arrived laden with diplomas and medals, a student already covered with glory.
D2.zh: 他带着许多文凭和奖章到来，是一个已经荣誉满身的学生。

D3.en: From childhood, he showed a lively interest in science and especially chemistry.
D3.zh: 从童年起，他就对科学，尤其是化学表现出浓厚兴趣。

D12.en: He was born in Scotland in 1881.
D12.zh: 他于1881年出生在苏格兰。

M3.en: As a child, he was inspired by reading about Moses and Lincoln to fulfill his duty of tikkun olam.
M3.zh: 幼年时，他受到阅读摩西和林肯故事的启发，立志完成修复世界的责任。

M1.en: At 14-17, he won first prize in a current events contest.
M1.zh: 14到17岁时，他在时事竞赛中获得一等奖。

D6.en: At 12, at Townsend Harris, he found himself among classmates just as smart or smarter than him.
D6.zh: 12岁时，在Townsend Harris，他发现自己周围都是和他一样聪明甚至更聪明的同学。

D2.en: He achieved a string of As through perseverance.
D2.zh: 他通过坚持不懈取得了一连串的A。

M3.en: He became haunted by the idea of continuing the legacy of the man he was named after.
M3.zh: 他总想着要延续他名字来源的那个人的事业。

D17.en: He sent his mother and sister on holiday to Switzerland.
D17.zh: 他送母亲和妹妹去瑞士度假。

D16.en: He became a prickly streak of aggressive argument, challenging every unexamined statement.
D16.zh: 他变得好斗，对每个未经审视的说法都要反驳。

D1.en: Between Christmas and Easter he moved from the bottom of the form to near the top.
D1.zh: 从圣诞节到复活节，他从班级倒数升到了前列。

D14.en: He turned like a mannequin to let fellow candidates appreciate the full effect.
D14.zh: 他像模特一样转身，让其他考生欣赏整体效果。

D13a.en: As a boy, he sat in his father's study envying his sister's facile flow.
D13a.zh: 小时候，他坐在父亲的书房里，羡慕姐姐流畅的表达。

D18.en: At 16-18, he earned pocket money by teaching mathematics, which was in demand.
D18.zh: 16到18岁时，他通过教数学赚零花钱，因为数学需求大。

D5.en: At around age 10, he boarded a boat under the imperious gaze of his companion.
D5.zh: 大约10岁时，他在同伴威严的目光下登上了船。

D11.en: At 9-10, he tried to express his feelings in poetry and sketches.
D11.zh: 9到10岁时，他尝试用诗歌和素描表达情感。

D15.en: As a boy, he was passionate and refused to wear an overcoat.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他热情冲动，不肯穿大衣。

D4.en: At 9, his legs finally grew long enough to reach the organ pedals.
D4.zh: 9岁时，他的腿终于长得够长，能够到管风琴的踏板了。

D7.en: At 9, he shooed birds away from his companion's catapult and fled home.
D7.zh: 9岁时，他把鸟赶离同伴的弹弓，然后跑回了家。

D6.en: At 18, he introduced himself to Münch and they discussed interpretation until late.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他自我介绍并与Münch讨论演奏诠释到深夜。

D2.en: At 9-10, he began to feel anxious about his passion for play and gradually gave up all games.
D2.zh: 9到10岁时，他开始为自己的玩心感到焦虑，逐渐放弃了所有游戏。

D3.en: At 9, he asked for a Bible and plunged into it with enthusiasm.
D3.zh: 9岁时，他要了一本圣经，并热情地投入阅读。

D10.en: At age 7, she fled the classroom in tears and ran home for comfort.
D10.zh: 7岁时，她含泪冲出教室，跑回家寻求安慰。

D1.en: At age 17, she persevered in school, earning a C+ in reading.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她坚持学业，阅读得了C+。

D15.en: As a child, she enjoyed skating, tennis, swimming, canoeing, and sailing.
D15.zh: 幼年时，她喜欢滑冰、打网球、游泳、划独木舟和帆船。

D4.en: As a child, she found lifelong pleasure in gardening.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她在园艺中找到了终生的乐趣。

D2.en: At 9, she studied flowers in the garden with unusual intensity, noticing details others missed.
D2.zh: 9岁时，她以异于常人的专注在花园里研究花朵，注意到别人忽略的细节。

D3.en: At age 9, she studied flowers until she understood fertilization.
D3.zh: 9岁时，她研究花朵直到理解受精过程。

D12.en: At age 11, her mother died, and she grew closer to her father.
D12.zh: 11岁时，母亲去世，她与父亲更加亲近。

M6.en: In his early twenties, he had a profound religious experience.
M6.zh: 他二十岁出头时经历了一次深刻的宗教体验。

D1.en: At 9, he was promoted to fifth grade, ranking 21st out of 24.
D1.zh: 9岁时，他升入五年级，在24人的班级中排名第21。

D14.en: At 8, he announced he was no longer to be called Henry, but Norman.
D14.zh: 8岁时，他宣布不再叫Henry，而要叫Norman。

D18.en: At 17, he worked as a busboy on passenger ships on the Owen Sound–Port Arthur route.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他在欧文桑德至阿瑟港航线的客轮上做服务员。

D5.en: At 17, he often quarrelled with the ship's crew while asserting his rights.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他经常与船员争吵，维护自己的权利。

M1.en: By age 8, his intention of being a surgeon was taken for granted.
M1.zh: 到8岁时，他当外科医生的打算被视为理所当然。

D15.en: At age 8-10, he led his younger brother up a steep cliff to catch a butterfly.
D15.zh: 8到10岁时，他带领弟弟爬上陡峭的悬崖去捉蝴蝶。

D4.en: At about age 8, he cut flesh from a cow's leg he had just boiled.
D4.zh: 大约8岁时，他切下刚煮过的牛腿上的肉。

D9.en: At 15, he was elected class representative on the student executive.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他被选为学生执行委员会的班级代表。

D7.en: At 14, he took to leaving home Friday nights to spend weekends with a friend.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他周五晚上离家去朋友家过周末。

D6.en: He shared his wealth with his older sister and younger brother as a small child.
D6.zh: 他幼年时与姐姐和弟弟分享他的财富。

D2.en: As a small child, he usually won the prize for properly pronouncing and defining a new word.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他通常因正确发音和定义新词而赢得奖金。

D10.en: At age 6-7, he tried not to cry on his birthday, went to the fair grounds alone, and started crying despite himself.
D10.zh: 6到7岁时，他生日那天努力不哭，独自去集市，还是忍不住哭了。

D1.en: At 17, he considered giving up, talked with his principal, and decided to stick with it.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他考虑放弃，和校长长谈后决定坚持。

D13a.en: At 12, he started off to school as usual the next morning.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，第二天早上他照常去上学。

D5.en: At 12, he took the back way through fields to the bridge, hid his boots, and went to school barefoot.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他走田间小路到桥下，藏好靴子，光脚去上学。

M1.en: As a teenager, he had unusually high aspirations, partly as a reaction to farm chores.
M1.zh: 十几岁时，他志向异常高远，部分是对农场杂活的反抗。

D15.en: He skated and played shinny on the Boyne River in winter, and swam in the summer.
D15.zh: 他冬天在博因河上滑冰打冰球，夏天游泳。

D4.en: He almost cut off his thumb trying to make a dog house for Kruger.
D4.zh: 他给克鲁格做狗屋时差点切掉拇指。

D9.en: He persuaded friends to join the militia and became a corporal.
D9.zh: 他说服朋友加入民兵，当上了下士。

D7.en: At 12, he hid his boots under a bridge and walked barefoot to school.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他把靴子藏在桥下，光脚去上学。

D6.en: He played tag at the church social.
D6.zh: 他在教堂社交会上玩捉人游戏。

D2.en: He never tried to take his watch apart; it worked for ten years until the mainspring broke.
D2.zh: 他从未试图拆开手表，它走了十年直到发条断了。

D3.en: He always kept an eye out for arrowheads and spearheads in the fields.
D3.zh: 他总在田里留意箭头和矛头。

D12.en: He grew up as a farm boy in a traditional rural culture.
D12.zh: 他是在传统农村文化中长大的农家男孩。

D18.en: At 18, he drifted back to Saskatoon, spent the rest of the summer stooking wheat and riding the binder on a farm.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他回到萨斯卡通，夏天剩下的时间在农场堆麦捆、开收割机。

D14.en: He gave a talk on how Bohr theory and quantum mechanics explained the periodic table.
D14.zh: 他做了一次关于玻尔理论和量子力学如何解释元素周期表的演讲。

M1.en: He won a scholarship from Northampton Grammar School to attend Mill Hill boarding school.
M1.zh: 他获得了北安普顿文法学校的奖学金，进入米尔希尔寄宿学校。

D15.en: He made the school tennis team.
D15.zh: 他入选了学校网球队。

D4.en: He learned glass blowing and did dangerous chemistry experiments in a garden shed.
D4.zh: 他学了吹玻璃，并在花园棚屋里做危险的化学实验。

D7.en: At about age 12, he suddenly refused to go to church, briefly upsetting his mother.
D7.zh: 大约12岁时，他突然拒绝去教堂，让母亲短暂不快。

D3.en: As a small boy, he feared everything would be discovered by the time he grew up, and was fascinated by scientific answers.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他害怕自己长大前一切都会被发现，并对科学答案着迷。

M3.en: As a teenager, he was fascinated by Professor Broom, who seemed young and enjoyed life.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，他被Broom教授迷住了，这位教授看起来年轻且享受生活。

M6.en: He declared he wanted to work with live human beings, not old bones.
M6.zh: 他说他想和活人打交道，而不是老骨头。

D20.en: He was the youngest among two brothers and two sisters.
D20.zh: 他是两个兄弟和两个姐妹中最小的。

D10.en: He turned away to hide his tears but could not stop them.
D10.zh: 他转过身不想让人看到眼泪，但忍不住。

D1.en: He practiced on 100-yard and 150-yard dashes to pick up speed.
D1.zh: 他练习100码和150码冲刺来提高速度。

D18.en: He expected to be paid well for his work.
D18.zh: 他期望自己的工作能得到丰厚报酬。

D11.en: He imagined her reaction to a romantic proposal with a split pomegranate.
D11.zh: 他想象了她对用裂开的石榴求婚的反应。

M1.en: He wanted to close the gap and maybe even beat him.
M1.zh: 他想要缩小差距，甚至可能超过他。

D15.en: He ran the mile for his school and was unbeaten in track meets.
D15.zh: 他代表学校跑一英里，在校际比赛中从未被击败。

D4.en: He was chosen to help the Professor with pick and shovel at a dig.
D4.zh: 他被选中用镐和铲帮助教授进行挖掘。

D9.en: His friends carried him off on their shoulders with shouts of triumph.
D9.zh: 他的朋友们欢呼着把他扛在肩上抬走。

D7.en: He went to the University Registrar and explained he needed help or could not stay.
D7.zh: 他去找大学注册官，解释自己需要帮助，否则无法留下。

D6.en: He went across the river with Fanie Bekker to fetch soup for the school.
D6.zh: 他和Fanie Bekker过河去为学校取汤。

D12.en: At 17, he decided to study medicine at the University of Cape Town.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他决定在开普敦大学学医。

D13b.en: At 17-18, I vowed never to fail at varsity like Barney did, and refused to be locked up for two years.
D13b.zh: 17到18岁时，我发誓绝不像Barney那样在大学挂科，并拒绝被关在家里两年。

D18.en: At age 14-15, he said 'We're going to pick citrus.'
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，他说“我们要去摘柑橘。”

D11.en: At age 9-10, he made charcoal drawings of reptiles and amphibians.
D11.zh: 9到10岁时，他画了爬行动物和两栖动物的炭笔画。

M1.en: At age 18, he was valedictorian.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他是致告别辞的学生代表。

D15.en: At age 14-18, he was quarterback and starting guard in basketball.
D15.zh: 14到18岁时，他是橄榄球四分卫和篮球首发后卫。

D9.en: At age 14-18, he was class president.
D9.zh: 14到18岁时，他是班长。

D6.en: In high school, he was popular with girls because he listened to them.
D6.zh: 高中时，他因为倾听女生说话而很受欢迎。

D2.en: At 9-10, he discoursed on animals' diets, reproduction, life spans, and announced each species by its Latin name.
D2.zh: 9到10岁时，他谈论动物的饮食、繁殖、寿命，并用拉丁名介绍每种物种。

D3.en: In fourth grade, he started a herpetology club; no classmates came, so his family became the club.
D3.zh: 四年级时，他创办了爬虫学俱乐部，没有同学来，于是家人成了俱乐部成员。

D12.en: He was born in North Adams in 1959, the second of six children.
D12.zh: 他1959年出生在北亚当斯，是六个孩子中的老二。

D1.en: He spent the summer between junior and senior years at a basketball camp in Riverhead, Long Island.
D1.zh: 他在高二和高三之间的暑假去了长岛Riverhead的一个篮球训练营。

D18.en: He delivered prescriptions on his Schwinn bicycle on weekends and during school breaks and summers.
D18.zh: 他周末和假期骑施文自行车送处方药。

M1.en: He made the cut but was ambivalent about accepting; after talking with his parents, he was convinced it was a unique opportunity.
M1.zh: 他通过了选拔但犹豫是否接受；和父母谈过后，他相信这是个难得的机会。

D15.en: His love of basketball and baseball began at age nine and intensified as a teenager.
D15.zh: 他对篮球和棒球的热爱始于九岁，并在青少年时期愈发强烈。

D9.en: He applied and received a full academic scholarship to Holy Cross in fall 1958.
D9.zh: 他申请并获得了Holy Cross的全额学术奖学金，于1958年秋季入学。

D7.en: He got into fistfights with guys from other neighborhoods when provoked.
D7.zh: 他被挑衅时会和其他街区的家伙打架。

D6.en: Moving to Dyker Heights introduced him to a new group of friends and organized sports.
D6.zh: 搬到Dyker Heights让他结识了一群新朋友，并接触了有组织的体育运动。

D2.en: He realized on his own how much he enjoyed school and learning even without outside pressure.
D2.zh: 他自己意识到即使没有外界压力，他也很喜欢上学和学习。

D3.en: As a child, his mother constantly bought or borrowed books for him to read.
D3.zh: 小时候，他妈妈经常给他买书或从图书馆借书给他读。

D14.en: At 12, he appeared on Quiz Kids radio but was eliminated on his third broadcast.
D14.zh: 12岁时，他参加Quiz Kids广播节目，但在第三次播出时被淘汰。

M1.en: At 12, he lasted three broadcasts on Quiz Kids but was tripped by Shakespeare and Old Testament questions.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他在Quiz Kids中坚持了三期，但被莎士比亚和旧约问题难倒。

D4.en: At 7, he received a book on bird migration from an uncle, leading to years of bird-watching with his father.
D4.zh: 7岁时，他收到叔叔送的鸟类迁徙书，此后多年与父亲一起观鸟。

D9.en: At about 15, he began attending the University of Chicago's Laboratory School, a progressive high school.
D9.zh: 15岁左右，他开始就读芝加哥大学实验学校，一所进步高中。

D7.en: At 12, he stopped going to Mass and went bird-watching with his father instead.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他不再去望弥撒，而是和父亲一起观鸟。

D2.en: At 15, he became one of the few students admitted to the University of Chicago after only two years of high school.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他成为少数仅读两年高中就被芝加哥大学录取的学生之一。

D3.en: At 17, he came across Schrödinger's What Is Life? in the university library.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他在大学图书馆偶然读到薛定谔的《生命是什么》。

D12.en: As a child, he was welcomed home from school by Nana and given supper when his mother was still at work.
D12.zh: 小时候，他放学回家由Nana迎接，并在母亲还没下班时吃上晚饭。

D10.en: At about 10-14, he screamed like a madman and the evening ended with slaps for Emma, who laughed, and tears for him.
D10.zh: 大约10到14岁时，他像疯子一样尖叫，那晚以艾玛挨打（她却在笑）和他哭泣告终。

D11.en: At about 6-7, he said he wanted to be a sailor to travel the world and join the navy on a destroyer that had visited.
D11.zh: 大约6到7岁时，他说长大后想当水手环游世界，要上个月来过的那艘驱逐舰上服役。

M1.en: At about 10, he came first in the final exam, to his parents' great joy.
M1.zh: 大约10岁时，他在期末考试中得了全校第一，父母非常高兴。

D15.en: He learned to swim instinctively in calm pools, becoming skilled enough to swim in open sea.
D15.zh: 他在平静的水潭里本能地学会了游泳，很快熟练到敢游到礁石外的开阔海域。

D4.en: He helped his father at construction sites, holding the end of a tape measure or leveling rod.
D4.zh: 他帮父亲在工地上干活，拿着卷尺或水准尺的一端。

D7.en: He felt something boil inside him hearing a teacher call the Lateran Pacts a betrayal.
D7.zh: 他听到老师说这个协议是背叛，内心感到沸腾。

D6.en: He wore the olive-gray uniform after his mother enrolled him in Catholic Boy Scouts.
D6.zh: 他穿上灰绿色制服，因为妈妈给他报了天主教童子军。

D2.en: He studied the rudiments of something intensely during a summer.
D2.zh: 他整个夏天刻苦学习某门学科的基础知识。

D3.en: He was curious to know what was being said outside Italy, understanding French and some German.
D3.zh: 他好奇意大利之外在说什么，因为他懂法语和一点德语。

D12.en: He was born in Catanzaro.
D12.zh: 他出生在卡坦扎罗。

D4.en: At 14, he was clever with his hands and good at carpentry and metalwork.
D4.zh: 14岁时，他手很巧，擅长木工和金工。

D3.en: At 17, he studied in the British Museum reading-room.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他在大英博物馆阅览室学习。

D12.en: He was born on 3 October 1844, the second eldest of nine children.
D12.zh: 他于1844年10月3日出生，是九个孩子中的次子。

D8.en: At 16-17, he used his skill at capping on people to make them shape up.
D8.zh: 16到17岁时，他用嘴皮子让人难堪，让他们老实了。

M3.en: At 15-16, he watched Sharper drill the ROTC unit and thought he was incredible.
M3.zh: 15到16岁时，他看着Sharper训练ROTC队伍，觉得他太厉害了。

M6.en: At 14, he felt his faith in God was intensely personal and important.
M6.zh: 14岁时，他觉得对上帝的信仰非常个人化且重要。

M2.en: At 10-11, his mother refused to let him settle for a lowly goal.
M2.zh: 10到11岁时，妈妈不愿让他满足于那么低的目标。

D10.en: At 18, despair washed over him as memories of fifth grade flashed through his mind.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他感到绝望，因为五年级的记忆闪过他的脑海。

D1.en: At 15-16, he determined to go as far as possible in ROTC before graduating.
D1.zh: 15到16岁时，他决心在毕业前在ROTC里走到最远。

D14.en: At 17-18, he marched at the head of the Memorial Day parade at the end of 12th grade.
D14.zh: 17到18岁时，他在12年级末的阵亡将士纪念日游行中走在最前面。

D13b.en: At 8, he followed church custom and went forward with his brother to accept Jesus.
D13b.zh: 8岁时，他按教会惯例和哥哥一起上前接受耶稣。

D13a.en: At 12-13, he and a friend quit practice without telling anyone and never went back.
D13a.zh: 12到13岁时，他和朋友没告诉任何人就退出了训练，再也没回去。

D18.en: At 17-18, he stared at a ten-dollar bill, knowing he had to make a choice.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他盯着面前的十美元钞票，知道自己必须做出选择。

D5.en: At 12-13, he was surrounded by angry White men in 7th grade.
D5.zh: 12到13岁时，他在七年级被一群愤怒的白人男子围住。

D11.en: At 8, he imagined being chased so vividly he felt it was real.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他想象自己被追赶，画面逼真到感觉像真的一样。

M1.en: At 11-12, he decided to become the smartest to know how it feels.
M1.zh: 11到12岁时，他决定成为最聪明的人，以体验那种感觉。

D4.en: At 8, he mixed chemicals and watched them fizzle, foam, or change color.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他混合化学品，看着它们嘶嘶作响、起泡或变色。

D9.en: At 16-17, he offered extra drill practice and the guys loved it.
D9.zh: 16到17岁时，他提供额外的操练练习，队友们很喜欢。

D7.en: At 12-13, he sat quietly while she railed at him.
D7.zh: 12到13岁时，他安静地坐着，任凭她责骂。

D6.en: At 14-15, he hung out with popular guys and went to parties.
D6.zh: 14到15岁时，他和受欢迎的朋友一起玩，参加派对和即兴演奏。

D2.en: At 14-18, he studied for hours to compete on College Bowl.
D2.zh: 14到18岁间，他花大量时间学习以参加College Bowl比赛。

D3.en: At 10-11, he chose library books on animals, nature, and science.
D3.zh: 10到11岁时，他选择关于动物、自然和科学的图书馆书籍。

D12.en: At 11, he learned they could get by without his father and saw his mother worry.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他意识到没有父亲他们也能过活，并看出母亲很担忧。

D20.en: She and Lilian organised 'the aunts' party'.
D20.zh: 她和Lilian组织了“阿姨们的聚会”。

D14.en: At 10-15, she danced so well that people stopped to watch.
D14.zh: 10到15岁时，她跳舞跳得好到人们停下来观看。

D9.en: She became head of her house at boarding school.
D9.zh: 她在寄宿学校成为宿舍长。

D7.en: She was furious at being treated arbitrarily.
D7.zh: 她因被武断对待而愤怒。

D6.en: At 14-15, she refused to take the school train and insisted on being driven.
D6.zh: 14到15岁时，她拒绝坐校车，坚持要开车送。

D2.en: She entered Oxford to study PPE.
D2.zh: 她进入牛津大学学习PPE。

M2.en: He was taken for granted to carry on the family tradition into its fourth generation.
M2.zh: 他被认为理所当然要继承家族传统到第四代。

D13a.en: He was expected to continue the family tradition into its fourth generation.
D13a.zh: 他被期望将家族传统延续到第四代。

M1.en: At 18, he suddenly moved to the top of his class at the University of Prague, remaining there until graduation in medicine.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他突然跃居布拉格大学班级前列，并保持到医学毕业。

D15.en: He excelled in gymnastics by training to overcome his fat, flabby body and eventually beat anyone in class.
D15.zh: 他通过锻炼在体操上表现出色，战胜了自己肥胖松弛的身体，最终打败了班上所有人。

D2.en: He decided to beat his soft body into shape until he could lick anybody in class, and eventually did.
D2.zh: 他决定把软弱的身体练强壮，直到能打败班上任何人，最终做到了。

D12.en: As the only son, he was expected to take over in time.
D12.zh: 作为独子，他被期望将来接班。

M5.en: At 15, she decided what to do with her life and never deviated.
M5.zh: 15岁时，她决定了人生方向并从未偏离。

D19.en: At 18, she boasted of small economies like buying second-hand items and choosing annual membership to save 6 shillings.
D19.zh: 18岁时，她炫耀自己的小节约，比如买二手货和选年度会员省6先令。

M6.en: As a child and young adult, she had a deep sense of vocation.
M6.zh: 幼年到青年时期，她有强烈的使命感。

D17.en: At 18, she willingly went to Buckinghamshire to assist, like her siblings.
D17.zh: 18岁时，她自愿去白金汉郡帮忙，像她的兄弟姐妹一样。

M4.en: At 17, she witnessed her parents' total unanimity in refugee relief work.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她目睹父母在难民救济工作中完全一致。

D10.en: At 9-10, she used self-control not to show emotion, pouring feelings into work and handiwork.
D10.zh: 9到10岁时，她用自控力不表露情绪，把感情投入到工作和手工中。

D1.en: At 10-11, by end of summer term 1931, she was gaining top place or near it almost every week.
D1.zh: 10到11岁时，到1931年夏季学期末，她几乎每周都获得第一名或接近第一名。

D13b.en: At 18, she entered Cambridge in 1938.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，她于1938年进入剑桥。

D18.en: As a small child, she was taught to care for small sums and pay attention to cost of anything bought.
D18.zh: 幼年时，她被教导要小心保管小额钱款，并注意所购物品的价格。

D11.en: As a teenager and young adult, she pursued various pastimes and interests.
D11.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，她从事各种爱好和兴趣。

M1.en: At 17, she went up to Cambridge to sit entrance exams in physics and chemistry.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她去剑桥参加物理和化学的入学考试。

D15.en: At 10-11, she was skilled in hockey, swimming and drawing.
D15.zh: 10到11岁时，她擅长曲棍球、游泳和绘画。

D9.en: At 18, she rang everyone she knew to get opinions on two colleges and chose Newnham.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她给所有认识的人打电话征求对两所学院的意见，然后选择了Newnham。

D7.en: At 18, she said, 'I cannot see why there has not been more criticism.'
D7.zh: 18岁时，她说：‘我不明白为什么没有更多的批评。’

D6.en: As a small child, she much enjoyed playing cricket and hockey with both boys and girls.
D6.zh: 幼年时，她非常喜欢和男孩女孩一起打板球和曲棍球。

D2.en: She determined to prove herself despite lack of encouragement.
D2.zh: 她决心在缺乏鼓励的情况下证明自己。

D3.en: At 9-10, she found a lecture on how a river begins very interesting.
D3.zh: 9到10岁时，她觉得一个关于河流如何开始的讲座非常有趣。

D12.en: She was born as the second child and first daughter of Ellis and Muriel Franklin.
D12.zh: 她出生时是Ellis和Muriel Franklin的第二个孩子和第一个女儿。

D5.en: At about 13, she was ready to go to a tournament but her mother gave away her ticket, so she couldn't go.
D5.zh: 13岁左右，她穿戴整齐准备去比赛，但母亲把她的票送人了，所以她没能去成。

D4.en: At 16, she roped together with five others and followed a guide over the Josterdal glacier.
D4.zh: 16岁时，她和另外五人用绳子系在一起，跟着向导穿越了Josterdal冰川。

M3.en: As a teenager, he admired four scientists: Rutherford, Mendel, Mendeleev, and Luke Howard.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，他崇拜四位科学家：Rutherford、Mendel、Mendeleev和Luke Howard。

D14.en: At 16, he played the organ at St Mary's Church.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在当地的St Mary's Church弹管风琴。

D13a.en: At 11, his mother insisted he accept a place at Manchester Central High School.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他母亲坚持让他接受曼彻斯特中央中学的录取。

M1.en: As a child, he and his brothers were competitive, determined to win or go down fighting.
M1.zh: 童年时，他和兄弟们有竞争精神，决心赢或至少战斗到底。

D2.en: At 16, he spent hours each week at the organ.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他每周花很多小时在管风琴上。

D12.en: He was the fifth and youngest son.
D12.zh: 他是第五个也是最小的儿子。

D19.en: He collected birds' eggs, insects, butterflies, coins, stamps, and postmarks in the attic.
D19.zh: 他在阁楼里收集鸟蛋、昆虫、蝴蝶、硬币、邮票和邮戳。

D20.en: He was president of his class during senior year.
D20.zh: 他在高年级时担任班长。

D10.en: At about age five, someone joked about his fiery tongue at a family party.
D10.zh: 大约五岁时，有人在家庭聚会上开玩笑说他嘴巴厉害。

D1.en: He felt discouraged about his place in the first division but was told he was improving.
D1.zh: 他对自己在甲班的位置感到沮丧，但被告知他正在进步。

D14.en: He and friends developed a tumbling routine; he was number two in the pyramid, performed in a charity show.
D14.zh: 他和朋友编排了翻筋斗表演，他在金字塔中排第二，并在慈善演出中表演。

D13a.en: At 18, he agreed not to play ball again in college.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他同意在大学不再打球。

D11.en: At age sixteen, he drew a colored pencil drawing of a lion's head, called the 'Cushing lion'.
D11.zh: 十六岁时，他用彩色铅笔画了一个狮子头，称为“库欣狮”。

M1.en: At 17, he stood eleventh in a class of eighty-three with a final average of 89.34.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他在83人的班级中排名第十一，四年平均分89.34。

D15.en: At 17, he walked two miles to the athletic field with Will Williams.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他和Will Williams步行两英里到运动场。

D4.en: At 17, he tried to make a life preserver out of cork.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他试图用软木做救生衣。

D9.en: At 16, he and four other boys each put in fifty cents to get Mr. Anderson a Christmas present.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他和四个男孩每人出50美分给安德森先生买圣诞礼物。

D7.en: As a small child, he got something from the pew, looking at his mother whose smile assured him.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他从长凳上拿了东西，看着母亲，她的微笑让他安心。

D6.en: At 17, he and his cousins went to Henry Thompson's, sat by the log fireplace, and sang to Will's guitar.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他和表亲们去了亨利·汤普森家，坐在木柴壁炉旁，伴着威尔的吉他唱歌。

D2.en: At 17, he had already completed 25 of them, not due until Tuesday.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他已经完成了25个，但要到周二才交。

D12.en: He was the tenth child of Henry Kirke Cushing and Betsey Maria Williams Cushing.
D12.zh: 他是亨利·柯克·库欣和贝齐·玛丽亚·威廉姆斯·库欣的第十个孩子。

D5.en: At 17, he secretly pawned some of his stepmother's jewelry to raise funds.
D5.zh: 他17岁时偷偷典当了继母的部分首饰来筹钱。

D2.en: At 18, he dropped out of school, unable to bear seeing his brother work hard to support his studies.
D2.zh: 他18岁时辍学，不忍心看兄长辛苦供自己读书。

D3.en: At 7, he could recite the next day what was taught the day before.
D3.zh: 他七岁时，前一天教的第二天早上就能背诵。

D4.en: He went on short excursions with his uncle to study nature.
D4.zh: 他和叔叔一起短途远足，研究自然。

D2.en: He was an industrious student with aptitude for math and natural sciences at age 8.
D2.zh: 他在8岁时是个勤奋的学生，对数学和自然科学有天赋。

D6.en: He became engaged to Emmy Fraatz at age 14-15.
D6.zh: 他在14到15岁时与Emmy Fraatz订婚。

M6.en: At 18, he joined the Wuchang Uprising of the Xinhai Revolution and witnessed the birth of the Republic.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他参加辛亥革命武昌起义，见证了民国的诞生。

M4.en: At 14-18, he was deeply influenced and cut off his queue with classmates to show support.
M4.zh: 14到18岁间，他深受影响，与同学们纷纷剪去长辫表示支持。

D1.en: At 9, he plotted with his brother and pushed a bully down stone steps, injuring him.
D1.zh: 9岁时，他与弟弟密谋，趁一个欺负人的同学下石阶时从背后推他，使其滚下受伤。

D5.en: At 18, he entrusted his gear to a classmate, slipped out of the west gate, and crossed mountains to rejoin the unit.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他把武器装备托付给同学，自己轻装从西门溜出，翻山越岭与大队会合。

D15.en: At 18, he marched long distances carrying a rifle, ammunition, and gear.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他肩上扛着步枪，腰间绑着子弹带，背着羊毡等物，负重长途行军。

D4.en: At 18, while on sentry duty in heavy snow, he quickly became a snowman.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他担任步哨，大雪纷飞，顷刻间他变成了一个雪人。

D9.en: At 18, he joined the Guangxi Student Daredevil Corps of 120 men and marched north with the army.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他参加广西学生军敢死队，共一百二十人随军北伐。

D7.en: At 9, he refused to submit to a bully who demanded 'tribute' from classmates.
D7.zh: 9岁时，他反抗一个强迫同学‘进贡’的恶霸。

D6.en: At 14, he was admitted to the Guilin Army Primary School.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他考入桂林陆军小学。

D3.en: As a child, he studied hard despite a difficult environment.
D3.zh: 他幼年在艰苦的环境中奋发勤学。

D12.en: At 10, he relied on his brother-in-law selling beef and sister sewing to fund his schooling after his father died.
D12.zh: 他10岁时父亲早死，靠姐夫卖牛肉和姐姐做针线供他读书。

D13b.en: At 14, he went to Japan to study.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他去日本留学。

M2.en: At 15, he dreamed of following his grandfather's path to military glory and family honor.
M2.zh: 15岁时，他梦想像祖父一样在军中建功立业，光大门楣。

D1.en: At 18, after losing multiple elders, he became tougher and returned to the Weihaiwei camp to rebuild his family.
D1.zh: 18岁时，长辈接连去世，他变得更加坚韧，安排好弟妹后重返威海卫军营。

D18.en: At 17, he relied on his uncle to get a position as a clerk in Weihai.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他投靠堂叔，补上了一名哨书。

D5.en: At about 16-17, he was short but sturdy, with broad shoulders, a round face, and dark skin.
D5.zh: 十六七岁时，他个子不高却很结实，宽肩圆脸，肤色黑红。

D12.en: Between ages 15 and 18, he lost his grandfather, father, and mother in succession.
D12.zh: 15到18岁间，他接连失去祖父、父亲和母亲。

D17.en: At 14, he cut flesh from his own body to heal his mother, saving her life.
D17.zh: 14岁时他割肉疗亲，救了母亲一命

D20.en: He and over ten others, including Wang Jiaju and Ao Jiaxiong, founded the Zhejiang Society in Hangzhou.
D20.zh: 他和王嘉榘、敖嘉熊等10余人在杭州成立“浙会”

M4.en: They were studying hard to find a way to save the nation.
M4.zh: 他们是在为探求救国之道而苦读

D16.en: His oratory was well trained in childhood when he told stories at teahouses.
D16.zh: 他的口才在童年上茶馆讲述平西征东时早已训练得很好

D1.en: With no way to raise funds, he decided to rely on his own ability and hard work, translating books and articles for fees.
D1.zh: 筹无处筹，借无处借，他决心靠自己的能力和勤劳解决费用问题，仿效译书汇编社成员的做法，翻译书籍和文章赚取稿费

D14.en: At age 6-7, he would go to the town teahouse and grandly perform storytelling for the patrons.
D14.zh: 六七岁的他常到镇上的茶馆里，大模大样地为喝茶的人“说书”

D5.en: At age 6-7, he accidentally hit a playmate with his command stick, and the parents came to confront him.
D5.zh: 他6到7岁时，指挥棍误伤了一个小伙伴，其父母赶来问罪。

D11.en: He wrote a poem mourning Tang Caichang and circulated it among classmates.
D11.zh: 他写了一首悼念唐才常的诗，在同学中间传阅。

M1.en: At 17, he took two Chinese exams and ranked first in both.
M1.zh: 他17岁时参加两次国文考试，均名列第一。

D4.en: At 16, he borrowed 10 silver dollars from relatives and went alone to study at an economics school.
D4.zh: 他16岁时从亲戚朋友处借了10块大洋，独自一人赴经济学堂求学。

D9.en: At age 6-7, he played war games with friends, often declaring himself general and dividing them into two teams.
D9.zh: 他6到7岁时喜欢与小伙伴们玩打仗游戏，常自封为大将军，将小伙伴们分成两队人马。

D7.en: In 1900, he copied the Qiushi Academy charter, deliberately writing every '是' as '字'.
D7.zh: 1900年，他抄写求是书院章程时，故意把每个“是”字写成“字”字。

D6.en: As a child, he often ran out of the temple to play and laugh with siblings at home.
D6.zh: 他幼年时常常从庙里跑出来，回到家中与兄弟姐妹们嬉笑玩耍。

D2.en: At 9-10, he finished reading the Four Books and then the Book of Songs and Book of Documents.
D2.zh: 9到10岁时，他读完了《四书》和《诗经》《尚书》。

D3.en: At age 14-15, he and a friend joyfully explored four cabinets of books in a small room, like finding treasure.
D3.zh: 他14到15岁时，与朋友像得了宝藏一样欣喜，在书院小房间里徜徉于四大橱书海中。

D12.en: In 1900, he decided to solve his expenses by translating books and articles for fees, following the Translation Society's example.
D12.zh: 1900年，他决心靠翻译书籍和文章赚取稿费来解决费用问题。

D18.en: At 12, he managed household shopping lists better than his uncle.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他开列购物清单比舅舅还高明。

D11.en: He loved playing with guns and sticks as a child.
D11.zh: 他从小就喜欢舞枪弄棒。

D3.en: At 16, he resolved to either learn English to study abroad or join the army to train elite troops.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他立志要么学好英语出洋，要么投笔从戎练精兵。

D12.en: At 12, he managed household accounts accurately and worked alongside laborers.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他管账准确无误，还和雇工一起干活。

M6.en: At 12-14, he changed his name to Xue Yue, vowing to save the nation like Yue Fei.
M6.zh: 12到14岁时，他改名薛岳，决心像岳飞那样救国救民。

D20.en: At 16-18, classmates made him their leader, and they swore brotherhood by a rock.
D20.zh: 16到18岁时，同学推他为小领袖，并在大石旁结拜为十兄弟。

M4.en: At 18, he discussed with sworn brothers and agreed to join the revolution.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他与盟兄弟商议，共约参加革命。

D18.en: As a small child, he did heavy farm work, cut wood, and cooked.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他下田耕作、上山砍柴、扫洒炊饪，无一不做。

D5.en: At 14, he begged his mother to let him study, then milled rice and cut wood in winter.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他恳求母亲允许求学，并在寒冬碾米砍柴。

M1.en: At 16, he ranked 32nd in the exam and entered the Whampoa Army Primary School.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他考了第32名，进入黄埔陆军小学。

D15.en: At 14, he worked day and night to mill all the autumn rice and cut firewood.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他夜以继日碾完稻谷，又上山砍柴备足木柴。

D4.en: At 16-18, during a night exercise, he led classmates to ambush the 'enemy' and won.
D4.zh: 16到18岁时，夜间演习他带领同学设伏，击败来袭之敌。

D9.en: At 16-18, classmates considered him bold and made him their leader.
D9.zh: 16到18岁时，同学因他胆识过人，推他为小领袖。

D7.en: At 18, he and nine others entered the principal's office to ask him to lead them in revolution.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他与九人径入校长室，要求校长率领全体同学参加革命。

D6.en: At 16-18, he was elected leader of ten sworn brothers by classmates including Zhang Fakui.
D6.zh: 16到18岁时，他被张发奎等同学推为小领袖，结拜为十兄弟。

D2.en: At 14, he worked day and night to husk all the autumn rice and chop enough firewood for the family.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他夜以继日碾完秋收稻谷，又上山砍柴备足木柴。

D3.en: At 12-14, he learned of Sun Yat-sen's revolutionary ideas and renamed himself Xue Yue, vowing to save the nation like Yue Fei.
D3.zh: 12到14岁时，他了解孙中山的革命思想，改名薛岳，决心像岳飞那样救国救民。

D12.en: As a small child, he did heavy farm work and household chores as the eldest son.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他作为长子承担繁重劳动，下田砍柴炊饪样样都做。

D20X.en: At 18, he rejected high office and went to Guangzhou after military training.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝了高官厚禄，学兵营毕业后去了广州。

M5.en: At 15, he studied hard to prove himself in military performance.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他珍惜学习机会，努力用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

D17.en: At 18, he fell from a horse and returned home to recover.
D17.zh: 18岁时，他骑马摔伤后回家养病。

M2.en: At 15, he studied hard to avoid disgracing his brother and classmates.
M2.zh: 15岁时，他努力用军事成绩不让大哥和同学看不起。

D10.en: At 16, he angrily scolded his brother for being deceived by a man with a big beard.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他怒骂大哥被张大胡子骗了。

D1.en: At 16, he went alone to Wuhu to join the Guangfu Army.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他一个人跑到芜湖投军。

D13b.en: At 17, he was admitted to a military training camp based on his combat experience.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他凭战斗资历被学兵营录取。

D13a.en: At 18, he married his first wife as ordered by his mother.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他遵照母命娶了首位夫人。

D18.en: As a child from a poor family, he feared he would be stuck in Weiyang Village forever if he didn't study.
D18.zh: 他小时候家里穷，如果不读书，恐怕一辈子都在卫杨村了。

D5.en: At 18, he spotted two spies noting his hotel room number, so he checked out and fled to the dock.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他发现两个特务在记他的门牌号，于是退房直奔码头。

M1.en: At 15, despite poor health, he studied hard to excel in military performance and impress his classmates.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他身体不好，但努力学习，要用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

D15.en: At 14-15, he played with stone rollers on the threshing ground to build strength, and few village children could match him.
D15.zh: 14到15岁时，他在打麦场上玩石磙练力气，村里小孩没几个比得过他。

D4.en: At 15, he disassembled and reassembled his rifle while on guard duty, soon beating veterans in speed and accuracy.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他站岗时拆装枪械，没多久就比老兵还快还准。

D9.en: At 16, he was appointed squad leader upon joining the Restoration Army because of his prior combat experience.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他因参加过战斗，一入光复军就被任命为班长。

D7.en: At 15, he cut off his queue and joined the revolutionary tide, following his brother's example.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他毅然剪掉辫子，投身革命洪流。

D2.en: At 9-11, he studied the classics from San Zi Jing to the Five Classics, working hard and refusing to fall behind the rich kids.
D2.zh: 9到11岁时，他从《三字经》学到“四书”“五经”，刻苦用功，不肯落在富家子弟后面。

D12.en: At 15, he left home, assigning farm and household chores to his younger siblings, then cut his queue and headed to He County.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他把农活家务交代给弟妹，剪辫辞母，背着包裹直奔和县。

D20X.en: He passed the exam for Baoding Military Academy but did not attend.
D20X.zh: 他考上了保定军官学校，但没去。

M5.en: His father promised him a battalion commander post if he graduated from the military academy.
M5.zh: 他父亲答应他，从讲武堂毕业就给他当营长。

D8.en: At 15, he scoffed at his father's money, saying he didn't care even for more.
D8.zh: 15岁时，他冲父亲笑说那点钱算什么，再多也没看在眼里。

D19.en: He boasted of his strong memory, still able to recite key lessons from his youth.
D19.zh: 他夸自己记忆力强，到现在还能背下功课要点。

M3.en: He became friends with Guo Songling, who also valued him.
M3.zh: 他和郭松龄结成了朋友，郭也看中了他。

D17.en: He opposed marrying his wife's niece while she was dying, to avoid hurting her.
D17.zh: 他反对在妻子病重时娶她侄女，怕催她死。

M2.en: At 18, he was asked if leaving would break his father's heart.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他被问：真要走了，不伤你父亲心吗？

D13b.en: At 16, he married a woman two years older.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他娶了一位比他大两岁的妻子。

D13a.en: At 18, he followed Chen Ying's advice to tell his father he wanted to study military, instead of running away.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他听了陈英的劝，跟父亲说想学军事，没离家出走。

D18.en: In his youth, he lived a dissolute life of drinking and dancing.
D18.zh: 他年轻时，有过酣歌醉舞的轻狂岁月。

D5.en: At 12, he climbed to the top of a two-story funeral canopy and made faces, ignoring people's screams.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他爬到两层楼高的灵棚顶上，扮鬼脸，不顾别人惊叫。

D11.en: As a child, he and his cousins caught flies and dyed them with colored chalk powder, pretending they were his soldiers.
D11.zh: 他小时候和表弟、弟弟抓苍蝇，用粉笔末染色，当自己的兵。

D15.en: At 18, he was told to train his body well as a soldier.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他被告知当军人要把身体锻炼好。

D2.en: At 6, he began studying Confucian classics at a family school under renowned scholars.
D2.zh: 6岁时，他被送进家塾，系统学习儒家经典，师从崔明耀、金梁、白永贞等名师。

D19.en: At 15-16, he insisted on finishing each day's homework before the next day.
D19.zh: 15到16岁时，他坚持当天的功课必须当天做完。

M7.en: At 16-17, he saw applying to the Army Primary School as a way to save the nation.
M7.zh: 16到17岁时，他把投考陆军小学当作救国的一条出路。

D10.en: At 12, he was deeply moved seeing his mother still watching over him and praying.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他见母亲仍在身旁守候祈求，深为感动。

D1.en: At 5-6, he repeatedly tried to ride a fierce black ox, getting thrown off and injured.
D1.zh: 5到6岁时，他一次次爬起再扑向黑牯牛，被摔得鼻青脸肿。

D13b.en: At 17-18, he was described as someone who absolutely obeyed orders and strictly followed school rules without daring to overstep.
D13b.zh: 17到18岁时，他被描述为绝对服从命令、严守堂规而不敢越雷池一步的人。

D18.en: At 4-5, he ran barefoot in the hills with brothers and friends, learning chores like gathering firewood.
D18.zh: 4到5岁时，他光脚满山跑，学拾柴扯猪草等家务。

D5.en: At 5-6, he knowingly jumped onto a fierce ox that always threw riders off.
D5.zh: 5到6岁时，他明知黑牯牛脾气暴，仍二话不说扑上去。

D15.en: At 15-16, he woke around 6 am daily to run and do calisthenics on the playground.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他每天早上六点左右起床到操场跑步做操。

D4.en: At 15-16, out of curiosity, he took down and fiddled with his father's treasured copper gun.
D4.zh: 15到16岁时，他出于好奇取下父亲心爱的铜炮枪摆弄。

D6.en: At 16-17, he was easygoing and diligent in public chores, gradually gaining favor from teachers and students.
D6.zh: 16到17岁时，他为人随和勤于公共杂务，逐渐得到师生好感。

D12.en: At age 6, he entered a family school as a study companion for his elder brothers Yingzhen and Yinglu.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他入家塾为大哥应桢、二哥应禄伴读。

D17.en: As a small child, he ran to dying birds, gathered them in his hands, and cried over them.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他跑到快死的鸟跟前，把它们捧在手里哭。

M2.en: At age 6, his parents decided to scrimp to educate him to 'support the family'.
M2.zh: 6岁时，他父母决心节衣缩食培养他读书来‘支撑门户’。

D10.en: At age 6, he cried silently, choking sobs when other boys tittered.
D10.zh: 6岁时，他默默流泪，听到别的男孩偷笑就忍住抽泣。

D18.en: At 12-13, he and his younger brother planted part of their new land with opium poppy.
D18.zh: 12到13岁时，他和弟弟在新地里种了部分鸦片。

D11.en: As a small child, he shook tree branches to make petals shower around him.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他摇晃树枝让花瓣像雨一样落在自己身上。

D15.en: At 4, he loved exercising, climbing mountains, swimming, and doing pull-ups, staying strong.
D15.zh: 4岁时，他喜欢锻炼身体，爱爬山、游泳，还翻杠架，所以身体一直很健壮。

D4.en: He grew up singing peasant songs and playing every musical instrument he could find.
D4.zh: 他从小唱民歌，玩所有能拿到的乐器。

D6.en: Throughout his childhood and adolescence, he heard such tales told and retold.
D6.zh: 整个童年和青少年时期，他反复听到这些故事。

D2.en: As a teenager, he walked this distance each morning and evening.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他每天早晚步行这段距离。

D12.en: At age 10-12, he became thin, humble, grave, and adult beyond his years.
D12.zh: 10到12岁时，他变得瘦弱、谦卑、严肃，比实际年龄成熟。

M7.en: He said being killed by foreigners is a glorious death for the country.
M7.zh: 他说被外国人打死是为国而死，荣幸之极。

M4.en: He fired several shots at the church's wooden plaque to vent his anger.
M4.zh: 他举起枪向着教堂的木匾连放数响，以泄一口气。

D16.en: He kept shouting while walking, ignoring others, earning the nickname 'Foreign点心'.
D16.zh: 他自走自喊，一概不管不理，被同伍兵士讥笑，送绰号'外国点心'。

D10.en: His dying mother wanted to eat pears and meat.
D10.zh: 他母亲弥留时，想吃梨和肉。

D13a.en: After his father scolded him, he quit alcohol for life.
D13a.zh: 他父亲严厉教责后，他终身戒酒。

D18.en: He sold leftover gunpowder strips and bought meat at the market.
D18.zh: 他把药条卖得十余枚，跑上东关去买些肉。

D5.en: He fought alone, knocked two people down, and beat them up.
D5.zh: 他只身与斗，仆其两人于地，痛打一顿。

M1.en: He practiced day and night, even shouting while walking alone on the street.
M1.zh: 他昼夜习之，甚至在街上独自行走时也一边走一边喊。

D4.en: At 13-14, he accompanied his father in the army and also worked as a laborer.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他随父亲在军中，也跟着做工。

D9.en: He urged his fellow soldiers to quit gambling, whoring, smoking, and drinking, helping them live a clean moral life.
D9.zh: 他时时劝导同营的弟兄们戒嫖赌、戒烟酒，帮助大家过道德上清洁的生活。

D7.en: At 14, he raised his gun and fired several shots at the church's wooden plaque.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他举起枪向着教堂的木匾连放数响。

D3.en: At 12-14, inspired by others, he took up reading and calligraphy, and learned abacus out of curiosity.
D3.zh: 12到14岁时，他受感动，走入好读书、勤练字的门径，又因求知欲激起而学会了打算盘。

D12.en: At 11, he went alone to the Liu Ye Temple to make a vow to save his mother, and kowtowed until his forehead was swollen.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他独自到刘爷庙许下救母大愿，常对天叩头，把额头磕到红肿。

M4.en: At 18, he joined the revolutionary Tongmenghui, signing a pledge and swearing blood oath, risking death.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他加入同盟会，填具志愿书，歃血为誓，不怕杀身之祸。

D10.en: At 13-14, he burst into loud crying.
D10.zh: 13到14岁时，他嚎啕大哭起来。

D1.en: At 13-14, he cried while struggling to carry a heavy load of firewood, until his mother came to help.
D1.zh: 13到14岁时，他一面哭一面勉强挑着柴担走，母亲来接才结束。

D13a.en: At 12-13, he was sent to get oil from his grandmother but hesitated and dared not enter.
D13a.zh: 12到13岁时，父亲叫他去外婆处取油，他徘徊不敢进去。

D18.en: At 16, he learned textile techniques from sizing to weaving over half a year.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他学了半年纺织技术，从浆纱到织布都学会了。

D5.en: At 18, he pricked his finger with a needle to draw blood and felt no pain.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他把针向手指上一戳，血出来了，并不觉得痛。

M1.en: At 13-14, he felt competitive and loaded a firewood bundle as heavy as adults.
M1.zh: 13到14岁时，他非常好胜，装了一大担柴，和成年人一样多。

D15.en: At 13-14, he loaded a firewood bundle as large as adults, driven by competitiveness.
D15.zh: 13到14岁时，他装了一大担柴，和成年人一样多，因为好胜。

D9.en: At 18, he joined a crowd shouting 'Not afraid' after someone led a yell.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他跟着队伍里有人带头大喊‘不怕’，也一起乱嚷。

D7.en: At 15, he was denied registration for being ten minutes late.
D7.zh: 15岁时，校方拒绝他报到，理由是他迟到了十来分钟。

D18.en: At 12, he worked for a wealthy farmer, herding cattle, cutting grass, and doing chores for 300 wen a month.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他给富农家看牛，每天割草、担水、推谷、舂米、插秧、扮禾，月得工钱三百文。

D4.en: At 11, he worked as a water-puller at a coal mine in Huangqiling.
D4.zh: 11岁时，他到黄碛岭土煤窑做车水工。

D7.en: At 18, he joined a wage protest and was expelled as a 'troublemaker'.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他参加堤工要求年关预发工资的斗争，被当局认为是“不安分子”而遭驱逐。

D3.en: At 12, he often reviewed old books at night and borrowed novels like 'Strange State of the World Witnessed Over 20 Years'.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他常在晚上温习旧书，并借阅了《二十年目睹之怪现状》等小说。

D12.en: At 13, he worked 12-13 hours a day at a coal mine, then carried extra coal on his back in low tunnels.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他到煤窑做车水工，每天劳动十二三小时，还加运一次煤，在低矮的煤洞里背煤爬行。

D19.en: He practiced calligraphy meticulously, using his finger as a pen to save paper.
D19.zh: 他一丝不苟地练毛笔字，用食指当笔在手心写。

M7.en: He felt his ideals conflicted with the system and that the Qing dynasty must be overthrown.
M7.zh: 他感到自己的理想与现存制度格格不入，认为不推翻清王朝就没有出路。

M4.en: He read works by Zou Rong and Huxley, broadening his view of the world.
M4.zh: 他读了邹容和赫胥黎的著作，开阔了眼界，认识到清廷的腐败。

D13b.en: He married Cheng Yizhi at his mother's insistence, though he was dissatisfied.
D13b.zh: 他奉母命与程宜芝结婚，虽心有不满但只得同意。

D18.en: He carried coal from a pit over ten li away to sell in the market.
D18.zh: 他到十多里外的煤场挑煤炭到赵家场街上叫卖。

D11.en: He told stories from Romance of the Three Kingdoms and Water Margin to other children.
D11.zh: 他给大家摆龙门阵，讲《三国演义》和《水浒传》的故事。

M1.en: He ranked first in every subject due to his diligence and intelligence.
M1.zh: 他勤奋好学，各科成绩都是第一名。

D15.en: He practiced throwing pebbles at a tree trunk every morning until he made a hole.
D15.zh: 他每天清早用鹅卵石投掷大树，直到树干打出洞穴。

D9.en: He led his friends to throw stones at children from a neighboring village.
D9.zh: 他带领小伙伴们跟邻村孩子互相投掷石子。

D7.en: At 15, he told a debt collector that he would repay his uncle in full.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他对逼债人说欠五伯父的钱一定如数奉还。

D6.en: As a child, he led a gang of cowherds to graze on rich men's land, using his height, courage, and martial arts to protect them.
D6.zh: 他小时候带着一群放牛娃，仗着个子高、胆子大、会武术，把牛赶到富人家的荒坡上放牧。

D2.en: At age 6-12, he practiced throwing 'flying bullets' at a tree target every morning until a hole formed in the trunk.
D2.zh: 6到12岁时，他每天清晨朝屋后大树投掷几十颗“飞弹”，久而久之树干上打出个洞。

D3.en: At 13-15, he read Zou Rong's 'Revolutionary Army' and Huxley's 'Evolution and Ethics', which deeply influenced his thinking.
D3.zh: 13到15岁时，他读了邹容的《革命军》和赫胥黎的《天演论》，这些书深刻影响了他的思想。

D12.en: At 15, he was forced to drop out of school and return to farming after his father's death caused financial hardship.
D12.zh: 15岁时，因父亲病故，家庭经济困难，他被迫辍学回乡务农。

D14.en: He played huqin, yangqin, piano, and trumpet, and was a skilled storyteller.
D14.zh: 他胡琴拉得好，还会打洋琴、弹钢琴、吹喇叭，讲古书也很吸引人。

D13a.en: He refused to return to school, writing that heroes often come from the grassroots.
D13a.zh: 他回信拒绝返校，说自古英雄多出自草莽，大丈夫何患乎文凭。

D4.en: He often went up the mountain to gather firewood with classmates after school.
D4.zh: 他经常利用课余时间，与同学结伴上山打柴。

D9.en: He was elected president of the student council and helped draft its charter.
D9.zh: 他被选为学生自治会会长，和师生磋商制定了《学生会章程》。

D7.en: He admired his teacher's courage and cut off his own queue like him.
D7.zh: 他佩服林老师的勇气，学着老师的样子剪掉了辫子。

D6.en: He led children in games renamed 'Beat the Foreign Devils' as a small leader.
D6.zh: 他带领小朋友玩‘打洋鬼子’游戏，通常是首领。

D2.en: He topped his class in exams and read many extracurricular books, especially poetry.
D2.zh: 他学习成绩拔尖，每次考试名列前茅，还借阅课外书，爱好写诗作文。

D3.en: He did homework by lamplight made from bamboo after helping with chores.
D3.zh: 他晚饭后帮妈妈收拾碗筷，用竹精子点燃照明做功课。

M4.en: At age 9, after being slapped, he resolved to study military and serve his country.
M4.zh: 9岁时挨了耳光，他决心学军事，献身国家。

D1.en: At 14, he left hospital to take exams but had to return due to infection, repeating a year at Tsinghua.
D1.zh: 14岁时他勉强出院考试，因伤口发炎未考完，再住院，多读一年清华。

D14.en: At age 2-7, he wrote large regular script with suspended wrist, earning praise from elders.
D14.zh: 他幼年时悬腕写大楷，秀丽圆润，博得长辈称赞。

D5.en: At 14, he fell from a seesaw when his partner got up, rupturing his ureter.
D5.zh: 他14岁时骑在跷板一端，同学起身离开，他摔下来砸破了输尿管。

M1.en: He taught his four children, all of whom excelled and strived for the top.
M1.zh: 他亲自教导四个儿女，个个品学优异，力争上游。

D4.en: At 14, he fell from a seesaw when his partner got up, causing injury.
D4.zh: 他14岁时骑在跷板一端，同学起身离开，他从高空摔下。

D6.en: At 18, he was elected captain of five sports teams in one year.
D6.zh: 他18岁时一年内被选为足球、篮球、棒球、排球、手球五项球队队长。

D2.en: He took out an English poetry book from his pocket and recited it with focus.
D2.zh: 他从口袋里掏出一本英文诗，专注地朗诵起来。

D3.en: As a small child, he was quiet and studious, memorizing texts after a few silent recitations.
D3.zh: 他幼年时生性沉静，默诵几遍就能熟记塾师教过的书文。

M1.en: At 18, he passed the exam for a government-funded work-study program in France.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他考取官费留法勤工俭学生。

D15.en: At 15-16, he was known as one of the 'Five Tigers' in Chengdu academia for his soccer skills as a forward.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他因踢得一脚好球，成为成都学界有名的‘五虎’之一。

D9.en: At 15-16, he began criticizing current affairs and leading student protests, becoming a prominent figure at his school.
D9.zh: 15到16岁时，他开始抨击时事，带领同学闹学潮，在甲工校成了头角峥嵘的人物。

D2.en: At 15, he was admitted to Chengdu A-Level Industrial School, majoring in dyeing and weaving.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他考入成都甲种工业学校，读染织专业。

D3.en: At 18, he bought and read many copies of New Youth and biographies of great figures.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他搜购了大量《新青年》和伟人传记阅读。

D7.en: At age 14-18, he and others secretly broke and removed Bodhisattva statues, leaving a note claiming a divine order to move.
D7.zh: 14到18岁间，他和同伴悄悄打碎搬走菩萨像，留字条称玉皇圣谕搬迁。

D3.en: As a teenager, he carved a motto on his desk and studied late into the night by an oil lamp.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他在课桌上刻下座右铭，每晚在小油灯下读书到深夜。

D20X.en: He joined twenty-five other children of Southern California gentry.
D20X.zh: 他加入了南加州上流社会的其他二十五个孩子。

M5.en: He was driven and obsessed with proving himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M5.zh: 他被证明自己配得上巴顿血统的念头驱使和困扰着。

M8.en: He was obsessed with proving himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M8.zh: 他受到驱使，痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿家族的血统。

D19.en: He developed a photographic mind that compensated for his dyslexia.
D19.zh: 他培养了照相式记忆，弥补了他的阅读障碍。

M3.en: He was inspired by ancestor-hero worship from his father and aunt.
M3.zh: 他受到父亲和姑姑点燃的祖先英雄崇拜的启发。

M6.en: At 18, he felt a firm conviction about his destiny, though perhaps wrongly.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他坚信自己的命运，尽管可能错了。

D20.en: At 11-12, he joined twenty-five other children of Southern California gentry.
D20.zh: 11到12岁时，他加入了南加州上流社会的其他25个孩子。

M4.en: He was driven and obsessed with proving himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M4.zh: 他执着于证明自己配得上巴顿家族的血统。

D10.en: At 18, he experienced severe mood swings, often within a single letter home.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他经历了严重的情绪波动，常常在一封家信中就出现。

D5.en: He was taunted by classmates for his spelling and reading mistakes.
D5.zh: 他因拼写和朗读错误被同学嘲笑。

D11.en: At 11-17, many of his essays dealt with ancient warriors he learned about from his father and aunt.
D11.zh: 11到17岁时，他的许多文章都涉及他从父亲和姑妈那里学到的古代战士。

M1.en: He scored first in the competitive examination.
M1.zh: 他在竞争性考试中得了第一名。

D15.en: As a small child, he and his sister rode horseback carefree across the vast estate.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他和妹妹无忧无虑地骑马穿越广阔的庄园。

D9.en: As first captain, he led the cadet corps in parade at the centennial celebrations.
D9.zh: 作为第一队长，他带领学员队在百年庆典上游行。

D7.en: He complained that the system of forbearance toward plebes will ruin the academy.
D7.zh: 他抱怨对新生宽容的制度会毁掉军校。

D6.en: At 16-17, he began a sporadic long-distance correspondence, often through an adult.
D6.zh: 16到17岁时，他开始与某人断断续续地长期通信，通常通过一位成年人传递。

D2.en: In 1903, at age 17, he studied intensely at Lake Vineyard and on Catalina Island.
D2.zh: 1903年，他17岁时，在Lake Vineyard和Catalina Island拼命学习。

D3.en: He absorbed Mosby's Civil War tales like a sponge.
D3.zh: 他像海绵一样吸收Mosby的内战故事。

D14.en: At 16, he was one of the water sprites in a play where Beatrice had the lead role of Ondine.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在一部剧中扮演水精灵之一，Beatrice饰演主角Ondine。

M3.en: He worshipped his father, calling him a saint and a friend.
M3.zh: 他崇拜他的父亲，称他为圣人和朋友。

D20.en: At 17, he became captain of the rugby and cricket teams.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他成为橄榄球队和板球队的队长。

D1.en: At 17, he worked hard after a shock report and passed into Sandhurst mid-list.
D1.zh: 17岁时，一份报告让他震惊，他努力用功，顺利考入桑赫斯特军校，成绩中游。

D13a.en: As a child, frequent beatings with a cane did not deter him.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，频繁的藤条抽打并未吓倒他。

M1.en: At 18, he passed into Sandhurst halfway up the list.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他考入桑赫斯特军校，成绩中游。

D15.en: At 14, he could swim like a fish and was strong and fit.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他游泳像鱼一样，强壮且健康。

D9.en: Between 13 and 18, he showed clear leadership in team sports.
D9.zh: 13到18岁间，他在团队运动中展现出明显的领导力。

D7.en: As a child, his early life was a series of fierce battles with his mother, who always won.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他的早期生活是与母亲的一系列激烈战斗，母亲总是获胜。

D2.en: He hurled himself into sport and became Captain of the Rugby XV and in the Cricket XI.
D2.zh: 他投身运动，三年多后成为橄榄球队队长和板球队队员。

D12.en: He learned early to stand or fall on his own.
D12.zh: 他早年学会靠自己成败。

D6.en: He was very happy at St. Paul's School.
D6.zh: 他在圣保罗学校非常快乐。

D17.en: At about 10, he sat by his sister, swatting flies, fanning her, and feeding her water or rice broth.
D17.zh: 大约10岁时，他坐在妹妹旁边，给她打苍蝇、扇扇子、喂水或米汤。

D1.en: At 13-14, he worked hard to catch up in arithmetic after a poor first year.
D1.zh: 13到14岁间，他第一年算术不好，第二年下功夫赶了上去。

D18.en: At 16, he earned 3 silver dollars from a year's labor, his first own earnings.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他劳动一年得了3块大洋，第一次凭自己劳动换来。

D5.en: At 10-13, he climbed a tree to break off pine cones to test if gods existed.
D5.zh: 10到13岁间，他爬树掰松盔想试试有没有神灵。

D11.en: At 16-18, he thought about learning martial arts to fight for the poor.
D11.zh: 16到18岁间，他曾想学武替穷苦百姓打不平。

D4.en: At 9, he learned to weave baskets and strainers.
D4.zh: 9岁时，他学会了编箩头、篮子、笊篱。

D9.en: At 10-13, he led peers in tree-climbing races, choosing harder trees for himself.
D9.zh: 10到13岁间，他拉着同伴比爬树，总选难爬的树。

D7.en: At 13, he was forced to leave school and returned to study the Four Books and Five Classics.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他被迫退学回村读四书五经。

D6.en: He liked climbing trees and often raced friends to see who was fastest.
D6.zh: 他喜欢爬树，经常拉着几个同伴比着上树，看谁快。

D2.en: His father taught him to read traditional primers and practice calligraphy.
D2.zh: 父亲教他读《百家姓》、《千字文》等书，并练习写字。

D3.en: He loved reading novels like Romance of the Three Kingdoms and Journey to the West.
D3.zh: 他喜欢看小说，比如《三国演义》、《西游记》等。

D12.en: He did many household chores, carrying water and charcoal over long distances.
D12.zh: 他经常做家务，挑水、背炭，要走很远的路。

D13a.en: At 18, he reluctantly applied to the Imperial Army on his father's insistence.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他在父亲坚持下不情愿地申请加入帝国陆军。

D5.en: At 18, reluctantly applied for Imperial Army entry on his father's insistence.
D5.zh: 18岁时，在父亲坚持下不情愿地申请加入帝国陆军。

D15.en: As a teenager, he enthusiastically took up skiing, bicycling, and exploration.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他热衷于滑雪、骑自行车和探险。

D2.en: In adolescence, he developed fast mathematically and applied himself to skiing, bicycling, and exploration.
D2.zh: 青春期时，他数学能力突飞猛进，并热衷于滑雪、骑自行车和探险。

D3.en: As a teenager, he discovered a taste for mathematics that stayed with him.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他发现自己喜欢数学，这种兴趣伴随他一生。

D12.en: At 18, he had a hernia and underwent an operation.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他得了疝气并接受了手术。

D13a.en: At 17, he had to follow his parents' orders and became a 'groom' according to tradition.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他只好按照父母之命当了新郎。

D11.en: At 13, he wrote an essay praising Yu the Great's noble character on the topic of his three passes without entering home.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他在作文中颂扬了大禹的高贵品德。

D4.en: At 8, he took out a sheet of paper and concentrated on folding a small boat.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他拿出一张毛边纸，专心致志地叠一只小船。

D7.en: At 12, he refused to participate in ancestral rites or ceremonies that his father wanted him to attend.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他拒绝跟父亲去参加接祖神、吃祭酒、分胙肉等活动。

D3.en: At 8, he became a bookworm, always reading whenever he had free time.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他变成了一个读书迷，一有空就捧着一本书。

D5.en: At 12, he sneaked into the Guandi Temple carrying a basket of dog and cow dung.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他背着盛有狗屎牛粪的粪筐悄悄溜进了关帝庙。

D8.en: At 10, he continually took on his older brother Edgar in frustration.
D8.zh: 10岁时，他不断沮丧地挑战哥哥Edgar。

M3.en: At 10-14, his first hero was Hannibal; later he admired George Washington.
M3.zh: 10到14岁时，他的第一个英雄是Hannibal，后来他崇拜George Washington。

M7.en: At 10, he resolved to rise above humble beginnings and amount to something.
M7.zh: 10岁时，他决心摆脱卑微出身，将来有所成就。

M6.en: At 10, he had a determination to rise above humble beginnings and amount to something.
M6.zh: 10岁时，他决心摆脱卑微的出身，将来有所作为。

D17.en: At 8, he was busily making a toy while his brother Earl played nearby.
D17.zh: 8岁时，他忙着做玩具，弟弟Earl在旁边玩。

D20.en: At 18, he wrote a constitution he hoped would ensure the association's survival.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他撰写了一部宪法，希望确保协会的生存。

M4.en: At age 5, his mother Ida converted to the Bible Student movement.
M4.zh: 5岁时，母亲Ida改信圣经学生运动。

D10.en: At 10, he cried into his pillow for an hour out of resentment and rage.
D10.zh: 10岁时，他因怨恨和愤怒趴在枕头上哭了一个小时。

D1.en: At 14-15, after a two-month convalescence, he recovered completely and repeated his freshman year.
D1.zh: 14到15岁时，经过两个月的康复，他完全恢复并重读了一年级。

D13a.en: At 10, he pounded an apple tree with his bare fists until they were raw and bleeding.
D13a.zh: 10岁时，他用赤手猛捶苹果树，直到拳头血肉模糊。

D18.en: At 8-10, he poured beer into a neighbor's hen and stripped a farm wagon to rebuild it on a barn roof.
D18.zh: 8到10岁时，他把啤酒灌进邻居家的母鸡嘴里，还拆了农场的马车在谷仓顶上重建。

D5.en: He played sports with abandon and utter disregard for his own safety.
D5.zh: 他运动时不顾一切，完全不顾自己的安全。

D11.en: At 8, he was busily making a toy.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他正忙着做玩具。

M1.en: He was more interested in impressing peers with his athleticism or fists.
M1.zh: 他更想用运动能力或拳头给同龄人留下印象。

D15.en: At 14-18, he enjoyed team sports, especially baseball and football.
D15.zh: 14到18岁时，他喜欢团队运动，尤其是棒球和橄榄球。

D4.en: Davis taught him fishing, trapping, poling a flatboat, duck shooting, and campfire cooking.
D4.zh: Davis教他钓鱼、设陷阱、撑平底船、打野鸭和篝火做饭。

D9.en: He organized the Abilene High School Athletic Association, independent of the school system.
D9.zh: 他组织了阿比林高中体育协会，独立于学校系统运作。

D7.en: He took his fair share of fighting as a child.
D7.zh: 他小时候没少打架。

D3.en: The Life of Hannibal ignited his great interest in Hannibal's exploits.
D3.zh: 《汉尼拔传》点燃了他对汉尼拔事迹的巨大兴趣。

D13b.en: He and Fritz determined to become army officers, endorsed by their father.
D13b.zh: 他和Fritz决定成为军官，得到父亲的支持。

D2.en: From age 13 to 15, he attended the Karlsruhe Cadet School in Baden.
D2.zh: 13到15岁时，他在巴登的卡尔斯鲁厄士官学校学习。

M7.en: At 15, he gave his pocket money to elderly and children begging by the roadside.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他把兜里的钱给了路边乞讨的老人和小孩。

M4.en: At 18, he joined the Chinese Revolutionary Party through Chen Tunan's introduction.
M4.zh: 18岁时，经陈图南介绍，他加入了中华革命党。

D18.en: At 14-15, he entered as a young muleteer, carrying grass and leading a mule, then unloaded and fed it.
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，他当小骡客，背草牵骡，卸驮喂骡。

D5.en: At 15-16, he punched Ran Taiheng in the back of the head with all his strength.
D5.zh: 15到16岁时，他一个箭步上前，抡起铁拳狠狠打了冉太横后脑勺一拳。

M1.en: At 10, he could fight his father to a draw.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他就能和贺士道打个平手。

D9.en: At 6, he always wanted to be the leader when playing with other children, especially fond of military formations.
D9.zh: 6岁时，和儿童们玩耍时他总要当首领，尤爱出兵摆阵。

D7.en: He angrily struck the gatekeeper, scared off the examiner, and disrupted the exam hall.
D7.zh: 他一怒之下打了守门官，吓跑了监考官，大闹了考场。

D6.en: At 12, he said many brothers and clan members came out to help him because the whole street was named He.
D6.zh: 12岁时，他说许多哥哥兄弟都出来帮助他，因为满街全是姓贺的。

D3.en: At 15, he went to Youyang with his brother-in-law, and while buying rice, he asked an old man if eating guanyin clay was harmful.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他随姐夫到酉阳，去买米时问一个老汉吃观音土有没有害。

D12.en: At 15, he was told to go to his brother-in-law's home in Dujiashan and join him in driving horses.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他被叫去杜家山姐夫家，跟他去赶马。

D16.en: At 9, he feared standing in front of the class and being laughed at.
D16.zh: 9岁时，他害怕站在全班面前被嘲笑。

D1.en: At 12-13, he tried to improve spelling to win a place beside her, but after missing a word he never tried again.
D1.zh: 12到13岁时，他努力提高拼写想赢得她身边的位置，但拼错一个词后他再也没试过。

D14.en: At 14-15, he photographed a prize tomato with a Brownie camera and sent the picture to a seed company.
D14.zh: 14到15岁时，他用布朗尼相机给一个获奖番茄拍照，并把照片寄给种子公司。

D13b.en: At 16, he suppressed a twinkle and was confirmed at St. Peter's.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他忍住笑意在圣彼得教堂接受了坚信礼。

D18.en: As a teenager, he picked the choicest fruit and sold it to a local grocer.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他挑选最好的水果卖给当地杂货店。

D5.en: At 16, he was ordered to squat over an unsheathed bayonet jammed into the floor.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他被命令蹲在一把插在地板上的出鞘刺刀上方。

D15.en: At 17-18, he was a lean and gawky cadet.
D15.zh: 17到18岁时，他是个瘦高笨拙的军校生。

D4.en: At 14-15, he rigged a greenhouse in a shed with his friend to start a nursery business.
D4.zh: 14到15岁时，他和朋友在棚子里搭了个温室，打算做苗木生意。

D9.en: At 18, he was appointed first sergeant for the following year.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他被任命为下一年的第一中士。

D7.en: At 16, he never reported his tormentors.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他从未举报欺负他的人。

D6.en: At 14-15, he rigged a greenhouse with his closest friend.
D6.zh: 14到15岁时，他和最好的朋友搭了个温室。

D3.en: He wrote the local congressman with a friend, requesting free seed packets from the government.
D3.zh: 他和一个朋友写信给当地议员，请求政府提供免费种子包。

D12.en: As a small child, he hosed horse droppings from the street in front of his house.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他用水管冲洗家门前的马粪。

D20.en: At 17, he won appointment to the Imperial Naval Academy at Eta Jima.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他考入了江田岛的帝国海军学校。

D16.en: At 17, he fielded taunts with grace and threw the rowdies out of his room.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他优雅地回应了挑衅，并把捣乱者赶出了房间。

D5.en: As a child, he chewed on a pencil without a word.
D5.zh: 小时候，他一声不吭地咬铅笔。

D4.en: As a child, he persisted until he got a match through a small hole in a coin.
D4.zh: 小时候，他坚持尝试，终于把火柴穿过十钱硬币的小孔。

D9.en: As a student, he was a leader from the first.
D9.zh: 作为学生，他从一开始就是领导者。

D7.en: At 17, he gracefully fielded taunts and threw the rowdies out of his room.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他从容应对嘲讽，把闹事者赶出房间。

D12.en: From age 10 to 17, he worked hard at home and school to help his family and win scholarships.
D12.zh: 10到17岁间，他努力在家帮忙、在校争取奖学金。

M1.en: At 17, he scored second from the top in the national entrance exam.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他在全国入学考试中排名第二。

D6.en: During his boyhood he attended the church of an American missionary called Newall in Nagaoka.
D6.zh: 少年时期，他在长冈参加了美国传教士Newall的教堂。

D3.en: Alone among the cadets, he had a Bible, which he sometimes read.
D3.zh: 他是学员中唯一有圣经的人，有时会读它。

D1.en: At 17, he was speechless when receiving his father's instruction, seeing an impossible mountain of work ahead.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他收到父亲的指示时哑口无言，看到面前一座似乎无法逾越的工作大山。

D9.en: At 17-18, he founded a literary society among half a dozen classmates in his new form.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他在新班级里和五六个同学创办了一个文学社。

D2.en: At 17-18, he crammed a subject daily after school with Gymnasium teachers while keeping up with normal homework.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他每天放学后到老师私人房间补习一门课，晚上还要完成正常作业。

D3.en: As a small child, he walked with his father and asked so many questions that his father wondered what was coming next.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他和父亲散步，问了很多问题，让父亲不知道接下来会问什么。

D12.en: At 3.5, he lost his mother.
D12.zh: 三岁半时，他失去了母亲。

D4.en: At 17-18, he continued his interest in geology and palaeontology, making excursions to collect rock samples and fossils.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，他继续对地质学和古生物学的兴趣，远足收集岩石样本和化石。

D13b.en: At 18, he entered active service as a Fähnrich in the 3rd Prussian Foot Guards, Berlin.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他以候补军官身份进入柏林第3普鲁士近卫步兵团服役。

D15.en: He undertook a busy regime of classes, gymnastics and sport.
D15.zh: 他忙于上课、体操和运动。

D4.en: He fought battles with his prized Heinrichsen tin soldiers on the dining-room table.
D4.zh: 他在餐桌上用他珍爱的海因里希森锡兵打仗。

D7.en: He resented the dumb authority of school.
D7.zh: 他憎恶学校里愚蠢的权威。

D13b.en: At 11, he was apprenticed to his uncle, a Moscow furrier.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他跟着舅舅当学徒，舅舅是莫斯科的皮货商。

D18.en: At 12, he was apprenticed to a furrier and sent to work in Moscow.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他给皮货商当学徒，被派到莫斯科干活。

D5.en: At 18, caught up in patriotic fervour, he was tempted to volunteer but decided to wait until his age cohort was called up.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他受爱国热情感染想志愿参军，但和同村皮货商商量后决定等轮到自己的年龄组再应征。

M1.en: At 10, he completed the local parochial school with praise.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他以优异成绩完成了当地教会学校学业。

D2.en: Between ages 7-10, he took the full three-year primary education course, unlike the two-year course most peasants chose.
D2.zh: 7到10岁间，他上了完整的三年制小学，而多数农民只上两年。

D3.en: Between ages 11-18, he studied Russian, maths, geography, popular science, and German with others.
D3.zh: 11到18岁间，他和别人一起学习俄语、数学、地理、科普读物和德语。

D12.en: At 7, he started school and successfully completed the three-year elementary course.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他入学并成功完成了三年制小学课程。

D13a.en: He refused an arranged marriage his father forced on him at 16.
D13a.zh: 他16岁时坚决反对父亲包办定下的亲事。

D18.en: He often helped shopkeepers in the street by wrapping sugar buns and counting money.
D18.zh: 他常到街上帮店铺包糖包子、算账、数铜板。

D5.en: At 17, he ran away from home to explore the outside world.
D5.zh: 他17岁时自行离家出走，决心到外面闯一闯。

D11.en: As a small child, he fantasized about becoming a chivalrous hero who eliminates bullies.
D11.zh: 他幼年时遐想长大后做一个为民除害的剑侠。

D4.en: He worked alongside bricklayers, accurately throwing bricks and catching tiles.
D4.zh: 他和砖匠一起干活，抛砖接瓦都很稳。

D9.en: He mobilized students to transfer from a missionary school, nearly causing its collapse.
D9.zh: 他动员峻德中学学生转学，使这所教会学校几乎瓦解。

D7.en: At 16, he joined a student strike against warlord troops and outsmarted thug soldiers with classmates.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他参加学生罢课抗议军阀部队，与同学智斗痞子兵。

D2.en: At 17, he ran 5000 meters every morning on the school track, rain or shine.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他每天早晨在校园操场坚持长跑5000米，风雨无阻。

D12.en: As a small child, his family moved to escape bandits, building a new house and falling into debt.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他一家为躲避匪患迁入城内，建新居欠了债，生活拮据。

D16.en: He was opinionated, obstinate, and quick to pick a fight.
D16.zh: 他固执己见，好斗，动不动就打架。

M1.en: At 15-18, he recognized that plain hard work would be necessary to keep up with or beat other students.
M1.zh: 15到18岁时，他认识到需要靠勤奋努力才能跟上或超过其他同学。

D15.en: As a schoolboy, he showed personal courage and tenacity in seeing a challenge through.
D15.zh: 他上学时表现出个人勇气和毅力，坚持完成挑战。

D7.en: He was quick to pick a fight.
D7.zh: 他动不动就打架。

D2.en: He believed plain hard work was necessary to keep up, and developed a driving intensity.
D2.zh: 他认为必须靠勤奋才能赶上，并形成了强烈的进取心。

D12.en: By the childhood deaths of two elder brothers, he became the eldest surviving son.
D12.zh: 由于两个哥哥幼年夭折，他成为家中长子。

M3.en: At 13, he was inspired by Arthur's example.
M3.zh: 13岁时，他被亚瑟的榜样所激励。

D20.en: At 17, he was chosen first sergeant of A Company, the highest rank for a day student.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他被选为A连第一中士，这是走读生能获得的最高军衔。

D10.en: As a small child, he asked, 'Do we have to live here?'
D10.zh: 幼年时，他问：“我们非得住这儿吗？”

D1.en: Despite discouraging handicaps, he achieved extraordinary self-education.
D1.zh: 尽管困难重重，他通过自学取得了非凡成就。

D14.en: At 17, he won a dramatic reading contest with 'The Fight of the Privateer General Armstrong'.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他凭朗诵《私掠船将军阿姆斯特朗之战》赢得比赛。

D13b.en: From his father he learned consecration to duty; from his mother, devotion.
D13b.zh: 他从父亲那里学会了献身于职责，从母亲那里学会了虔诚。

M1.en: At 18, he was ready to give up West Point to join the Army in wartime.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他准备放弃西点军校，在战时参军。

D15.en: At 16, his lips turned blue during scrimmages, but he got up and fought again.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他在混战中嘴唇发紫，但站起来继续打。

D4.en: As a small child, he played with a heliograph.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他玩日光反射信号器。

D9.en: At 17, he organized and led a prizewinning drill squad.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他组织并带领一支获奖的操练队。

D2.en: He was praised for performing every duty thoroughly and conscientiously.
D2.zh: 他因彻底认真地完成每一项任务而受到表扬。

D3.en: He acquired a remarkable vocabulary, mastery of Victorian prose, love of neo-Augustan rhetoric, and grasp of theory.
D3.zh: 他获得了非凡的词汇量、维多利亚散文的掌握、对新奥古斯都修辞的热爱以及对理论的掌握。

D17.en: At about 15, he fished out a paper, signed Quinn's name, and handed it in.
D17.zh: 大约15岁时，他捞出文件，签上Quinn的名字，然后交了上去。

D16.en: At 17, he won the Dunaway Debate Award in his senior year.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他在高年级赢得了Dunaway辩论奖。

D1.en: At 12, he insisted on a stern photo for the yearbook, saying, 'When I become a general, I want people to know that I am serious.'
D1.zh: 12岁时，他坚持在年鉴上使用一张严肃的照片，说：“当我成为将军时，我要让人们知道我是认真的。”

D5.en: At 3, he started walking around town by himself.
D5.zh: 3岁时，他开始独自在镇上走动。

D15.en: At 15-17, he was a brawny six-foot three-inch, 220-pound cadet who excelled in sports.
D15.zh: 15到17岁时，他是一个强壮、身高六英尺三英寸、体重220磅的学员，在体育方面表现出色。

D4.en: At 12, he was stirred by the great battles of Xerxes and Alexander the Great.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他被薛西斯和亚历山大大帝的伟大战役所震撼。

D6.en: As a small child, he developed an enduring intimate relationship with Sally.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他与Sally建立了持久的亲密关系。

D2.en: At 15-17, he followed a strictly disciplined schedule accounting for every hour from 6am to 9:30pm.
D2.zh: 15到17岁间，他遵循严格的时间表，从早上6点到晚上9点半的每一小时都安排妥当。

D3.en: As a small child, he showed great curiosity and independence.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他表现出极大的好奇心和独立性。

D12.en: At 10, he received an ancient Persian battle-ax from his father as a birthday present.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他收到了父亲送的一把古波斯战斧作为生日礼物。

D1.en: He quit the school after two weeks, finding the course inadequate.
D1.zh: 他两周后退学，认为课程不够好。

D13b.en: He signed a register listing his religion as a plebe.
D13b.zh: 作为新生，他签了一份登记表，上面列有他的宗教。

D13a.en: He ignored the dietary prohibitions he was raised with.
D13a.zh: 他无视了从小被教导的饮食禁忌。

D18.en: He worked in a fruit and vegetable store and drove deliveries.
D18.zh: 他在一家果蔬店工作，并赶马车送货。

D5.en: He fought another man and got the hell beat out of him.
D5.zh: 他和另一个人打架，被打得很惨。

D4.en: He made a reading stand, an ironing board, and bookcases in manual training class.
D4.zh: 他在手工课上做了一个阅读架、一个熨衣板和几个书架。

D9.en: As a midshipman, he studied more about battleships than other warships.
D9.zh: 他作为海军学员时，对战舰的学习多于其他军舰。

D2.en: At 7-13, he had after-school jobs and learned the value of money.
D2.zh: 7到13岁间，他做课后工作并学会金钱的价值。

M3.en: At 11, he wrote an essay praising Ritter von Epenstein.
M3.zh: 11岁时，他写了一篇赞美Ritter von Epenstein的文章。

D10.en: As a small child, he had fits of tears and tantrums, followed by gifts.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他动不动就哭闹发脾气，之后总会得到礼物和关爱。

D14.en: At 15, he presented the headmistress with a large bouquet of lilac, clicking his heels and kissing her hand.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他送给女校长一大束丁香花，立正碰脚跟并吻了她的手。

D13b.en: At 12, he was sent to the Cadet School at Karlsruhe.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他被送到卡尔斯鲁厄的军校。

D18.en: At 15, he sold his violin to a fellow pupil to pay his fare home.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他把小提琴卖给一个同学来付回家的路费。

D5.en: At 10, he scaled a sheer cliff to prove he could be trusted in the mountains.
D5.zh: 10岁时，他攀爬陡峭的悬崖，向长辈证明自己登山可靠。

D11.en: At 3-7, he was obsessed with medieval battles and knightly chivalry in his games.
D11.zh: 3到7岁时，他在游戏中痴迷于中世纪战斗和骑士精神。

D15.en: At 13-16, he dislocated his shoulder while climbing, reset it while dangling, and continued.
D15.zh: 13到16岁时，他在登山时肩膀脱臼，悬在空中自己复位，然后忍着剧痛继续攀登。

D4.en: At 11, he smashed his violin and cut the strings of other instruments before leaving school.
D4.zh: 11岁时，他砸碎了自己的小提琴，并剪断了学校乐队所有其他乐器的弦。

D7.en: As a small child, he was already proving to be a willful and difficult boy.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他已被证明是个任性难管的孩子。

D6.en: At age 7, he was wild and difficult to control, ordering his companions about in his eagerness to play at soldiers.
D6.zh: 7岁时，他野性难驯，急切地想玩打仗游戏，对同伴发号施令。

D3.en: As a small child, he saw his godfather as a shining hero to emulate in dress, manner, speech, and boldness.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他把教父视为光辉英雄，在衣着、举止、言谈和胆量上效仿他。

D12.en: As a small child, his mother left him in Germany after six weeks and did not see him for three years.
D12.zh: 幼年时，母亲六周后把他留在德国，三年未见他。

D17.en: At 14, he watched his grandfather care for his injured brother.
D17.zh: 14岁时，他看着祖父照顾受伤的弟弟。

D10.en: At 14, he carried his sobbing brother with a bloodied foot in his arms.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他抱着哭泣的弟弟，弟弟的脚在流血。

D14.en: As a teenager, he loved to sing, dance, and play the harmonica well.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢唱歌跳舞，手风琴也拉得不错。

D13a.en: At 8, relatives took him and his sister away from their mother.
D13a.zh: 8岁时，亲戚把他和妹妹从母亲身边带走。

D18.en: At 16-17, he worked as an assistant pastry chef and then an assistant dentist.
D18.zh: 16到17岁时，他先做糕点师助手，然后做牙医助手。

D5.en: At 18, he ran into an ambush, killed an enemy cavalryman, and warned his unit.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他冲入埋伏，杀死一名敌骑兵，并警告了部队。

D15.en: As a child, he learned to ride horses well.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他学会了骑马，骑得很好。

D4.en: As a teenager, he engraved his initials into the tombstones he made.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他在自己做的墓碑上刻下姓名缩写。

D9.en: As a child, he drew family and friends into his orbit.
D9.zh: 他小时候把家人和朋友吸引到自己身边。

D7.en: At 16-17, he argued with his boss.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他和老板吵了一架。

D6.en: At 14, he rushed to slash a nettle with a parade sword with neighborhood guys.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他和邻居伙伴们用阅兵剑砍荨麻。

D2.en: At 7-8, he was raised with work and regimen.
D2.zh: 7到8岁时，他在工作和纪律中长大。

D3.en: From age 6-15, he was interested in military history books about Alexander the Great and Roman generals.
D3.zh: 6到15岁时，他对关于亚历山大大帝和罗马将军的军事史书感兴趣。

D12.en: At 14-15, he was forced to drop out of upper secondary school.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，他被迫从高中辍学。

M4.en: At 18, he was arrested for hiding a red banner during a 1912 demonstration.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他因在1912年示威中藏匿红旗被捕。

D8.en: He picked out someone not too big to fight, but made errors in judgment.
D8.zh: 他挑了一个不太大的人打架，但判断有误。

M3.en: He cherished the romantic image of the first flying machine he saw at age five.
M3.zh: 他珍藏着五岁时第一次看到飞行器的浪漫印象。

D18.en: He worked nine hours a day, six days a week for $35 a week.
D18.zh: 他每周工作六天，每天九小时，挣35美元。

M1.en: He aimed to earn a civil engineering degree and an ROTC commission.
M1.zh: 他目标是拿到土木工程学位和ROTC委任。

D4.en: At 13, he bought a Model T with a friend, then bought out his partner and rebuilt the car.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他和朋友合买了一辆福特T型车，然后买下朋友的份额，自己把车拆开重装修好。

D7.en: He disagreed with a teacher, tried to leave through a locked door, then an open window.
D7.zh: 他反对老师的观点，想从锁着的门出去，又试图爬开着的窗户。

D2.en: He set up a drawing board in the living room and studied dedicatedly, knowing what he wanted.
D2.zh: 他在客厅里架起画板，专心致志地学习，知道自己想要什么。

D3.en: At age four or five, he saw a flying machine and wanted to catch it.
D3.zh: 四五岁时，他看到一架飞行器，想抓住它。

M5.en: At age 10-12, he challenged anyone who made him lose face to a fight.
M5.zh: 10到12岁时，他挑战任何让他丢脸的人打架。

D8.en: At age 7, he waited for Dov to be put down, then beat him up.
D8.zh: 7岁时，他等Dov被放下后，就揍了他一顿。

D17.en: As an infant, his mother traveled by cart seeking medical treatment for him.
D17.zh: 他幼时，母亲赶着马车四处求医为他治病。

D20.en: At age 14, as ringleader, he apologized and promised to pay for damage.
D20.zh: 14岁时，他作为头目去道歉并承诺赔偿损失。

M4.en: At age 12, he believed Jews and Arabs were not inherently different.
M4.zh: 12岁时，他相信犹太人和阿拉伯人没有本质区别。

D14.en: At age 7-8, tone-deaf, he failed choir and played triangle poorly.
D14.zh: 7到8岁时，他五音不全没进合唱团，打三角铁也总打不准。

D18.en: At age 10-12, he built a grafting shed and apple nursery; his seedlings were in demand.
D18.zh: 10到12岁时，他建了嫁接棚和苹果苗圃，他的树苗很抢手。

D5.en: At 17, he visited the Arab village of Zippori, headquarters of those who attacked his home.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他参观了阿拉伯村庄Zippori，那里是袭击他家的阿拉伯人的总部。

D11.en: At age 18, he published an article defending the spirit of Nahalal.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他发表文章捍卫Nahalal的精神。

M1.en: He said, 'I want to be among the most diligent pupils.'
M1.zh: 他说：“我想成为最勤奋的学生之一。”

D15.en: He played soccer against Arab village youngsters at age 8.
D15.zh: 他8岁时和阿拉伯村的孩子踢足球。

D4.en: He helped build the first forty permanent houses at Nahalal at age 18.
D4.zh: 他18岁时帮助在Nahalal建造了第一批四十栋永久房屋。

D9.en: He and his friends resisted the overture at age 18.
D9.zh: 他18岁时和朋友一起抵制了那个提议。

D7.en: At age 6, he proposed picking a fight with older Arabs to show taunting did not pay.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他提议和年长的阿拉伯人打架，以证明挑衅不会得逞。

D6.en: His friends flocked to his home after hearing about his acquisitions at age 16.
D6.zh: 他16岁时，朋友们听说他得到的东西后涌到他家。

D3.en: He and another youngster uncovered important relics at Bet She'arim.
D3.zh: 他和另一个年轻人一起在贝特沙瑞姆发现了重要文物。

D12.en: He lay motionless, pale, with eyes shut as a small child.
D12.zh: 他幼年时一动不动，脸色苍白，闭着眼睛。

D10.en: At age 18, he gave her an ultimatum: marry or sleep together.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他给她下最后通牒：要么结婚，要么上床。

M4.en: He was drawn to the Communist Party's organization and discipline.
M4.zh: 他被共产党的组织和纪律所吸引。

D16.en: He signed his articles under aliases Van Dinh and Hai Thanh.
D16.zh: 他用笔名文定和海青签署文章。

M1.en: At 14, he finished second in the examination.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他在考试中得了第二名。

D15.en: As a small child, he played da kien, kicking a coin shuttlecock.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他玩踢毽子游戏，用脚踢硬币毽子。

D4.en: As a small child, he herded ducks, watched buffalo, pounded rice, and sang work songs.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他放鸭、看水牛、舂米，并唱劳动号子。

D9.en: At 14, he defied school administration and joined a student strike.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他违抗学校管理，参加了学生罢课运动。

D7.en: At 18, he participated in strikes protesting the Yen Bay execution.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他参加了抗议安沛起义者被处决的罢工。

D6.en: At 16, he joined public ceremonies mourning patriot Phan Chu Trinh.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他参加了悼念爱国者潘周桢的公众仪式。

D2.en: At 15-18, he wrote articles with meticulous care, seeking precise words.
D2.zh: 15到18岁间，他精心撰写文章，寻找准确的词语表达意思。

D3.en: At 15-18, he read everything from world commentaries to social analysis.
D3.zh: 15到18岁间，他阅读一切，从世界局势评论到社会分析。

M2.en: He believed bringing shame upon the family was the worst sin.
M2.zh: 他认为给家庭带来耻辱是最不可饶恕的罪过。

D11.en: As a small child, he tracked WWII troop movements on maps with neighborhood boys.
D11.zh: 幼年时他和邻居男孩在地图上追踪二战部队动向。

D2.en: He was at the drill hall most Saturdays, practicing routines, polishing shoes and brass.
D2.zh: 他大多数周六都在训练厅练习队列、擦鞋和擦铜器。

D3.en: He was mesmerized by battlefields half a world away.
D3.zh: 他被半个地球外的战场迷住了。

D13b.en: At 16-17, his father Arie decreed that he would study engineering.
D13b.zh: 16到17岁时，他父亲Arie决定让他学工程。

D13b.en: At 18, he enlisted for a two-year hitch.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他入伍服役两年。

D18.en: At 6-8, he sold blackberries for ten cents a gallon with Roy.
D18.zh: 6到8岁时，他和Roy卖黑莓，每加仑十美分。

D5.en: He was in a bind, too low to eject, cockpit filling with black smoke.
D5.zh: 他陷入困境，高度太低无法弹射，座舱充满黑烟。

D7.en: He was brassy and pushed his way up the chain of command at group headquarters, arguing his case.
D7.zh: 他厚着脸皮，在指挥部一级级往上推，为自己争辩。

D6.en: He and Mack had stuck together all the way, from Nevada on.
D6.zh: 他和Mack从内华达起就一直待在一起。

D2.en: He played trombone in his high-school marching band, and would've been a damned good trombone player if only he practiced.
D2.zh: 他在高中军乐队吹长号，要是练的话本可以吹得特别好。

D12.en: He helped out on Saturday afternoons, giving shaves to the old codgers.
D12.zh: 他周六下午帮忙给老头们刮胡子。

D20.en: At 16, he signed a deed of partnership with Pratt to buy and sell stamps for profit.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他和Pratt签署了合伙协议，买卖邮票以盈利。

D18.en: From his mid-20s, he wrote and published short stories and articles, mainly for money.
D18.zh: 二十几岁起，他写作并发表短篇小说和文章，主要为了赚钱。

D9.en: At 17, he joined the Officer Training Corps and was promoted to Lance-Corporal.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他加入军官训练团，被提升为代理下士。

D7.en: At about 15, he defied the headmaster's punishment by attending the school play anyway.
D7.zh: 15岁左右，他违抗校长的惩罚，还是去看了学校的戏剧。

D6.en: As a teenager, he walked away with a private, amused smile when insulted, winning over a boy who had disapproved of him.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他被骂后带着觉得好笑的微笑走开，赢得了曾不认同他的男孩的好感。

D2.en: At 16-18, he worked as a pupil-teacher and uncertificated teacher in Birmingham's poorest schools.
D2.zh: 16到18岁间，他在伯明翰最差的学校当实习教师和未认证教师。

D3.en: At 5-6, he pored over pictures of soldiers and began to daydream of being one.
D3.zh: 5到6岁时，他仔细看士兵的图片，开始幻想自己成为士兵。

D13b.en: At 12, he was enrolled at St Philip's, a Catholic grammar school in Edgbaston.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他被送进伯明翰埃德巴斯顿的圣菲利普天主教文法学校。

D10.en: As a child, he had violent temper outbursts and fought often, once taking on two brothers.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他脾气暴躁，经常打架，有一次同时与两兄弟打架。

D1.en: At 16, he caught pneumonia, spent a month in hospital, and fell behind in studies, requiring a mighty effort to catch up.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他得了肺炎，住院一个月，学习落后，需要付出巨大努力才能赶上。

M1.en: At 16, he won the local Naval Academy examination in April 1901.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他在1901年4月的当地海军学院考试中胜出。

D9.en: He was tutored by Susan Moore and coached by John Graves Toland as a community project to help him achieve his goals.
D9.zh: 他接受了Susan Moore的辅导和John Graves Toland的指导，这是社区帮助他实现目标的一部分。

D7.en: He and his friends defied regulations by drinking strong beverages.
D7.zh: 他和朋友们违反规定，喝了烈性饮料。
