D9.en: At 17, she joined the Seagull Drama Society organized in Qingdao.
D9.zh: 17岁时，她参加了在青岛组织的海鸥剧社。

D7.en: At 11-12, she was expelled after conflicts with a teacher who had caned her.
D7.zh: 11到12岁时，她因与老师冲突被开除，老师曾用戒尺打她。

D6.en: At 11, she gathered neighborhood children to play a drama game.
D6.zh: 11岁时，她召集左邻右舍的孩子们一起玩戏段。

D2.en: At 15-16, she lacked patience in basic drama training and was scolded and beaten many times.
D2.zh: 15到16岁时，她练基本功不认真，挨了不少训斥和板子。

D3.en: As a teenager, she heard new ideas for the first time and found them fresh.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她第一次听到这些道理，觉得十分新鲜。

D13a.en: At 16, her marriage ended.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她的婚姻结束了。

D19.en: At 18, he wrote his diary in shorthand.
D19.zh: 他18岁时用速记写日记。

M3.en: At 16, he read everything by and about Gladstone and hung his portrait over his desk.
M3.zh: 他16岁时读了所有格拉德斯通的著作和关于他的书，并把他的肖像挂在书桌上。

M6.en: At 16, he applied for membership in the First Presbyterian Church, a turning point.
M6.zh: 他16岁时申请加入第一长老会教堂，这成为他的人生转折点。

D20.en: He lived in Princeton at ages 1-4.
D20.zh: 他1到4岁时住在普林斯顿。

D16.en: At 9-14, he developed a strong ear for language from his father's pulpit cadences.
D16.zh: 他9到14岁时从父亲讲道的韵律中培养了对语言的敏锐听觉。

D10.en: At 16-17, he suffered a terrible cold and extreme depression, feeling unworthy and homesick.
D10.zh: 他16到17岁时得了重感冒和极度忧郁，感到不配和想家。

D1.en: At 17-18, he gained marked skill through painstaking and inexhaustible practice.
D1.zh: 他17到18岁时通过刻苦不懈的练习获得了显著技巧。

D5.en: At age 10-12, he played hooky to follow a circus elephant, padding his pants with cotton for the expected whipping.
D5.zh: 10到12岁时，他逃学跟着马戏团的大象走，在裤子里垫棉花准备挨打。

D11.en: At 17, he composed a constitution for his mythical flotilla.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他为他虚构的舰队编写了一部宪法。

M1.en: As a youth, he applied himself to mastering unfamiliar techniques.
M1.zh: 年轻时，他努力掌握不熟悉的技术。

D4.en: At 8-12, he led a bespectacled youth through mills and warehouses, explaining their workings.
D4.zh: 8到12岁间，他带领戴眼镜的年轻人参观工厂仓库并讲解运作。

D9.en: At 16-17, his fellow Eumaneans appointed him to lead their devotional exercises.
D9.zh: 16到17岁时，他被尤曼尼安会的同伴任命带领他们的灵修活动。

D2.en: At 9-14, his father drilled him on what they had seen and challenged his expression.
D2.zh: 9到14岁时，他父亲就所见之事训练他，并挑战他的表达方式。

D3.en: At about 8-12, he became enamored of James Fenimore Cooper's Leatherstocking Tales.
D3.zh: 大约8到12岁时，他迷上了詹姆斯·费尼莫尔·库柏的《皮袜子故事集》。

M5.en: At 16, he felt he could never win the approval his parents gave his elder brother.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他觉得自己永远无法赢得父母给予哥哥的那种认可。

D8.en: At 14, after a squabble, he divided the room into two parts with his possessions on one side.
D8.zh: 14岁时，大吵一架后，他把房间分成两半，自己的东西放在一边。

D20.en: At 17, Billings and Horton deferred to him as the leader.
D20.zh: 17岁时，比林斯和霍顿都听从他的领导。

D16.en: At 18, he adopted a mock-heroic, self-deflating, witty style.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他采用了一种戏谑英雄、自嘲、机智的风格。

D1.en: As a child, he was often on the losing side of fights but never submitted to his brother.
D1.zh: 幼年时，他常在打斗中落败，但从不向哥哥屈服。

D14.en: He established a reputation for wit and clownishness.
D14.zh: 他建立了机智和滑稽的名声。

D13b.en: At 7, his parents decided to enroll him in a private school.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他父母决定送他上私立学校。

M1.en: He decided to make a go of his LSE course.
M1.zh: 他决定努力完成伦敦政治经济学院的课程。

D15.en: He raced in Joe's boat from Edgartown to Nantucket and back to Hyannis.
D15.zh: 他乘坐乔的船从埃德加敦到楠塔基特再返回海恩尼斯比赛。

D9.en: He began his second year at Choate in East Cottage under Mr. Eugene Musser.
D9.zh: 他在乔特中学开始第二年，住在东小屋，由尤金·穆瑟先生负责。

D2.en: At 14, he slogged away at Latin all summer in the Belmore Tutoring Studio, calling it purgatory.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他整个夏天在Belmore补习班苦学拉丁语，称之为炼狱。

D10.en: At 18, he called it the most harrowing experience of his storm-tossed career.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他称这是自己风雨飘摇的生涯中最痛苦的经历。

D5.en: He arrived at Princeton on Thursday.
D5.zh: 他星期四到达普林斯顿。

D7.en: At 18, he threw himself into persuading his father and transferring there.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他全力说服父亲并转学过去。

D12.en: At 18, he withdrew from university for medical examinations and treatment.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他因病退学接受检查和治疗。

D19.en: At age 10-11, he alone kept methodically at his record, never missing a day during the entire year.
D19.zh: 10到11岁时，他独自坚持记录，全年一天不落。

M3.en: At 11, he realized he must strive to be like the heroes he worshipped.
M3.zh: 11岁时，他意识到必须努力像他崇拜的英雄一样。

D17.en: As a child, he looked after his younger siblings.
D17.zh: 他小时候照顾弟弟妹妹。

D16.en: Classmates soon learned to goad him into an argument for the sheer fun of hearing him.
D16.zh: 同学们很快学会故意激他争论，只为听他说话的乐趣。

D10.en: He observed that his sister Conie's sickness decreased when Mama left the room and she couldn't be petted.
D10.zh: 他注意到妹妹Conie的病在妈妈离开房间、没人哄她时会减轻。

D1.en: During his asthma attacks, they held him, walked him around, and tried to comfort him.
D1.zh: 他哮喘发作时，他们抱着他走动并试图安慰他。

D14.en: At 9, he jumped with delight upon hearing about a mockingbird and asked for its feathers.
D14.zh: 9岁时，他听说有嘲鸫后高兴得跳起来，并想要它的羽毛。

D13b.en: At 18, he was told to take care of his morals first, health next, and finally studies.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他被告知先注意道德，再注意健康，最后才是学习。

D5.en: At 13, he was given a gun and a large pair of spectacles in the summer of 1872.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他在1872年夏天得到了一支枪和一副大眼镜。

D11.en: At 10, he wrote a letter to Edith Carow during the family's first tour abroad.
D11.zh: 10岁时，他在家庭首次出国旅行期间给Edith Carow写了一封信。

M1.en: At 15-17, he accomplished in two years what normally took three and did well on entrance exams.
M1.zh: 15到17岁间，他两年内完成了通常需要三年的学业，并在入学考试中表现出色。

D15.en: At 10, he walked most of the way with his father and had a splendid day.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他和父亲走了大部分路，度过了愉快的一天。

D6.en: As a child, he listened to his mother tell stories of her early life and ancestors.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他听母亲讲述她早年生活和祖先的故事。

D2.en: At 18, he said he did not find it nearly as hard as expected not to drink and smoke.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他说不喝酒不抽烟并没有他想象的那么难。

D3.en: At 9, he wrote a letter describing his collection of mice to his sister.
D3.zh: 9岁时，他写信给姐姐描述他的老鼠收藏。

D12.en: At 11, putting himself to bed that night, he found he had too little breath even to blow out his candle.
D12.zh: 11岁时，那天晚上他上床睡觉，发现他连吹灭蜡烛的气都不够。

D20.en: He joined a dining-club composed almost exclusively of Boston Brahmins.
D20.zh: 他加入了一个几乎全由波士顿名流组成的餐饮俱乐部。

D13a.en: At 10, he escorted his tearful siblings aboard a steamer bound for Liverpool.
D13a.zh: 10岁时，他护送哭泣的弟妹登上开往利物浦的汽船。

D18.en: At 13, he said he hadn't had a respectable suit or a white shirt since leaving home.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他说自己离家后就没穿过体面的西装或白衬衫。

D16.en: As a teenager, he would stand clear of his desk and square his shoulders before speaking in class.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他会在课堂上发言前离开书桌，挺直肩膀。

D1.en: West Point rejected him due to his eyesight, and college was impossible because of his father's financial troubles.
D1.zh: 西点军校因视力问题拒绝了他，而父亲的经济困难让他无法上大学。

D13b.en: At his father's urging, he took the job.
D13b.zh: 在父亲的催促下，他接受了那份工作。

D13a.en: His father told him to quit the job and spend more time studying.
D13a.zh: 他父亲让他辞掉工作，多花时间学习。

D18.en: At 18, he worked for Clinton from 7 a.m. until schooltime and from 4 to 10 p.m.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他每天从早上7点工作到上学时间，再从下午4点工作到晚上10点。

D5.en: They traveled like hobos, grabbing rides on freight cars.
D5.zh: 他们像流浪汉一样旅行，扒货运火车。

D11.en: They read Shakespeare aloud, taking parts; after Hamlet, Ethel began calling him Horatio.
D11.zh: 他们分角色朗读莎士比亚；演完《哈姆雷特》后，埃塞尔开始叫他霍雷肖。

M1.en: At 16, he decided to try for West Point and prepared with extra history lessons.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他决定报考西点军校，并额外补习历史课。

D4.en: He had a bald goat with a black face.
D4.zh: 他有一只秃头黑脸的山羊。

D9.en: At 16, he and others launched the school's first yearbook.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他和同学创办了学校第一本年鉴。

D7.en: At 6, he led an expedition to a water hole and was punished as the ringleader.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他带领探险队去水坑玩泥巴，被当作头目单独惩罚。

D2.en: At 14, his family bought an upright piano from the Kimball Piano Company.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他家从Kimball钢琴公司买了一架立式钢琴。

D3.en: At 16, he copied down lines from Tennyson's poem 'Locksley Hall'.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他抄写了丁尼生诗歌《洛克斯利大厅》的句子。

D12.en: At 5-6, he attended to his baby sister, fixing her hair and rocking her to sleep.
D12.zh: 5到6岁时，他照顾妹妹，给她梳头并摇她入睡。

D19.en: He meticulously notched a tree branch by the river during summers at age 15-18.
D19.zh: 他15到18岁间的夏天，在河边树枝上仔细刻痕。

M6.en: He described an evening as an epiphany that revealed his career.
M6.zh: 他描述一个晚上是揭示他职业生涯的顿悟。

D10.en: At 17, ecstatic, he heard an ovation loud enough to warm his blood.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他欣喜若狂，听到的欢呼声足以让他热血沸腾。

D14.en: At 17, he was accepted after reading character sketches from scripts.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他朗读剧本中的角色片段后被录取。

D18.en: As a teenager, his paycheck helped cover family bills.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他的工资帮助支付家庭账单。

D11.en: At 16, he drew silhouette pictures of himself as an authority figure on cards.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他在卡片上画了自己作为权威人物的剪影画。

D15.en: At 17, he graduated from Dixon High School, active in athletics and dramatics.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他从迪克森高中毕业，活跃于体育和戏剧活动。

D4.en: At 15, he cleared sites for a contractor, swinging a pick at blue clay for ten hours a day.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他为承包商清理场地，每天挥镐挖蓝色黏土十小时。

D6.en: At 10, his group's exploits were chronicled in a local newspaper.
D6.zh: 10岁时，他所在团体的冒险事迹被当地报纸报道。

D2.en: He worked as a lifeguard at Lowell Park every vacation and washed dishes or taught swimming during school.
D2.zh: 他每个假期都在Lowell Park当救生员，上学期间洗碗或教游泳来挣食宿。

D3.en: At 5, he read aloud a newspaper account of a bombing to his parents.
D3.zh: 5岁时，他给父母朗读了一篇关于一起爆炸案的报纸报道。

D13a.en: At 11, he came back to her and said, 'I want to declare my faith and be baptized.'
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他回来对她说：“我想宣告我的信仰并受洗。”

D8.en: At 18, he used his longer reach to take the biggest pork chop and the last biscuit.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他利用自己更长的胳膊抢走了最大的猪排和最后一块饼干。

M3.en: At 10, he said, 'I want to wind up just like my Daddy, gettin’ pensions for old people.'
M3.zh: 10岁时，他说：'我想像我爸爸一样，为老年人争取养老金。'

M6.en: Cox said of him, 'He was a Bunton!'
M6.zh: 考克斯说他：‘他是个邦顿家的人！’

M2.en: As a small child, he heard stories from parents and grandparents about famous, influential ancestors.
M2.zh: 幼年时，他从父母和祖父母那里听到关于著名、有影响力的祖先的故事。

D16.en: At 18, he practiced debating and political oratory while pushing a broom.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他一边推着扫帚一边练习辩论和政治演说。

D10.en: At 5, he came down with whooping cough and spent the rest of the school year at home.
D10.zh: 5岁时，他得了百日咳，学年剩下的时间都在家度过。

D14.en: At 18, he wrote articles about club meetings for the campus newspaper, the College Star.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他为校报《学院之星》撰写俱乐部会议的文章。

D18.en: At 18, he said he couldn't make enough money to cover expenses and might have to leave school unless he got a loan.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他说自己赚不到足够的钱支付开销，除非能贷款，否则将被迫退学。

D5.en: At 16, he decided without asking or telling anyone to go with them and jumped into the car.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他没问也没告诉任何人就决定跟他们走，跳上了车。

D11.en: At age 1, he performed amusing capers and was called an orator by his father.
D11.zh: 1岁时，他表演滑稽动作，被父亲称为演说家。

M1.en: At 16, he wanted to get ahead in the world so badly that he was aggressive.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他非常想出人头地，以至于变得咄咄逼人。

D15.en: At 17, he described the labor as back-breaking for a boy of seventeen.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他说这种劳动对一个十七岁的男孩来说是累断腰的。

D9.en: He wrote editorials for the College Star in 1927.
D9.zh: 他于1927年为《College Star》撰写社论。

D7.en: He ate with loud, slurping noises, cramming food into his mouth.
D7.zh: 他吃饭时发出响亮的啧啧声，猛地把大勺食物塞进嘴里。

D3.en: He hid under the dining table to listen to adult talk.
D3.zh: 他躲在餐桌底下听大人们谈话。

D8.en: As a child, he insisted on setting the rules when playing games with other children.
D8.zh: 小时候和其他孩子玩游戏时，他坚持要制定规则。

M2.en: At age 12, he was determined to do even better to make up the loss to his parents.
M2.zh: 12岁时，他决心做得更好来弥补父母的损失。

D20.en: He was the perfect founding president of the group.
D20.zh: 他是该团体完美的创始主席。

D16.en: He learned and practiced speaking and debating techniques in high school.
D16.zh: 他在高中学到了演讲和辩论技巧并加以练习。

D10.en: Before a debate, he would get so tense he could not eat.
D10.zh: 辩论前，他会紧张到吃不下饭。

D1.en: He was not talented, but gave it all he could and never shrank from the pounding.
D1.zh: 他没有天赋，但全力以赴，从不畏惧打击。

D18.en: At 15-16, he worked various jobs including janitor and plucking chickens.
D18.zh: 15到16岁时，他做过各种工作，包括清洁工和拔鸡毛。

D5.en: At 15-16, he drove into Los Angeles to attend a burlesque show, daring for a Quaker boy.
D5.zh: 15到16岁时，他开车去洛杉矶看脱衣舞表演，这对一个贵格会男孩来说很大胆。

D15.en: At age 3, he was thrown from a horse-drawn wagon and struck by its metal wheel.
D15.zh: 3岁时，他从马车摔下并被金属轮子撞到。

D4.en: As a small child, he played with defused artillery shells.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他玩拆掉引信的炮弹。

D9.en: In college, he was elected to student office and chosen to lead the junior Kiwanis club.
D9.zh: 大学时，他被选为学生干部，并被选为少年基瓦尼斯俱乐部负责人。

D6.en: At 17, thanks to Ola, he concluded high school in a blaze of glory.
D6.zh: 17岁时，多亏奥拉，他辉煌地结束了高中生涯。

D3.en: At age 5, he especially liked James W. Riley's poems and asked to have them read repeatedly.
D3.zh: 5岁时，他特别喜欢詹姆斯·W·赖利的诗，要求反复读给他听。

M4.en: At 13, he went through a public conversion and spiritual reawakening.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他经历了一次公开的转变和精神觉醒。

D14.en: As a small child, he was encouraged to stand up and speak out before the congregation.
D14.zh: 幼年时，他被鼓励在会众面前站起来发言。

D13b.en: His mother forced him to practice, against his wishes, every afternoon.
D13b.zh: 他母亲强迫他每天下午练习，违背了他的意愿。

D13a.en: His mother prevailed upon a family friend to give him a desk in a law office, and back he slunk.
D13a.zh: 他母亲说服一位家族朋友在律师事务所给他一张桌子，他灰溜溜地回去了。

M2.en: At 18, she felt her father tried to realize his ambitions in her.
M2.zh: 18岁时，她觉得父亲试图在她身上实现他的抱负。

D16.en: At 10, she began elocution lessons that punctuated her career.
D16.zh: 10岁时，她开始上演讲课，这些课贯穿了她的职业生涯。

D10.en: As a child, she kicked against having less than others.
D10.zh: 小时候，她反抗比别人拥有的少。

D1.en: At 9, when told she was lucky, she insisted she deserved it.
D1.zh: 9岁时，别人说她幸运，她坚持说自己应得。

D14.en: At age 12, she won a prize for a piano duet at the eisteddfod.
D14.zh: 12岁时，她在艺术节上赢得钢琴二重奏奖。

D13b.en: As a small child, all pleasure was banned on Sundays, even a newspaper.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，星期天所有娱乐都被禁止，连报纸也不行。

D11.en: At age 13, she wrote: 'Smile a while and when you smile another smiles and then there’s miles and miles of smiles.'
D11.zh: 13岁时，她写道：'笑一笑，当你笑时别人也笑，然后就有数英里的微笑。'

M1.en: At age 10, she won a scholarship to Kesteven and Grantham Girls' School.
M1.zh: 10岁时，她获得奖学金进入Kesteven and Grantham女子学校。

D4.en: As a child, she learned sewing and adapting materials from her mother.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她跟母亲学习缝纫和改造旧物。

D9.en: At age 18, she joined OUCA immediately upon arriving at Oxford.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她一进牛津就加入了OUCA。

D7.en: At age 12-14, she was told: 'Never do things just because others do; make up your own mind.'
D7.zh: 12到14岁时，她被告知：'不要因为别人做就做；自己决定并说服别人。'

D6.en: At age 18, she decided to join the Conservative Association immediately upon arriving at Oxford.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她决定一到牛津就加入保守党协会。

D2.en: She stuck at it conscientiously as her duty and was more than competent.
D2.zh: 她认真坚持做这件事，认为这是她的责任，而且做得很好。

D3.en: She became fascinated with the law, but could not pursue it.
D3.zh: 她对法律产生了兴趣，但无法继续学习。

D12.en: Her father left school at 13 to earn a living.
D12.zh: 她父亲13岁辍学谋生。

M6.en: At 10-12, she wanted to be an Indian civil servant to serve the empire.
M6.zh: 10到12岁时，她想成为印度公务员，为帝国服务。

D5.en: At age 18, she cleaned floorboards with bleach and acid, nearly overcome by chlorine gas.
D5.zh: 18岁时，她用漂白粉和盐酸擦洗地板，差点被氯气熏倒。

D19.en: As a young man, he played the flute well.
D19.zh: 他年轻时笛子吹得很好。

M3.en: He looked up to his elder brother as a hero figure.
M3.zh: 他非常崇拜他的哥哥，视其为英雄人物。

D20.en: His ancestors were solid middle-class citizens, cultured and sensible.
D20.zh: 他的祖先是坚实的中产阶级，有文化、有头脑。

D16.en: He said the wall was rotten and only needed a push to collapse.
D16.zh: 他说这堵墙是腐朽的，只需一推就会倒塌。

D10.en: He wrote the letter with intense concentration, obviously relishing every moment of it.
D10.zh: 他全神贯注地写那封信，显然很享受每一刻。

D1.en: At 15, he threw himself more fiercely into his work to deaden his brief.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他更加拼命地投入工作来消磨时间。

D13a.en: He gave up smoking and never resumed it.
D13a.zh: 他戒了烟，并且再也没有抽过。

D5.en: He refused to take the steamer and persuaded a boatman to ferry them over.
D5.zh: 他拒绝坐轮船，说服了一个船夫渡他们过去。

M1.en: He focused solely on passing the examination with honors.
M1.zh: 他全神贯注于以优异成绩通过考试。

D15.en: At age ten or eleven, he could swim across to the other bank.
D15.zh: 他在十岁或十一岁时就能游到对岸。

D7.en: At 12-13, he made clear he would sacrifice anything for his rights.
D7.zh: 12到13岁时，他明确表示愿为争取权利做出任何牺牲。

D6.en: At 17, he established contact with revolutionaries and joined a student circle in Kazan.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他在喀山与革命者建立联系并加入学生小组。

D2.en: At 15, he did his lessons so well he stayed top of the class.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他功课极好，保持全班第一。

D3.en: As a teenager, he enjoyed Pushkin and developed affection for Turgenev's novels.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢普希金并逐渐爱上屠格涅夫的小说。

D8.en: He was difficult to intimidate, unbullyable, and proud of it, with a streak of the devil in him.
D8.zh: 他很难被吓倒，不受欺负，并为此自豪，还有一点顽劣。

D13b.en: At 17, he entered Moscow University.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他进入莫斯科大学。

D13a.en: As a child, he studied at home up to seventh grade, protected from brutal reality.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他在家学习到七年级，被保护免受残酷现实。

D4.en: At 12-14, he built a crystal radio based on his father's design.
D4.zh: 12到14岁时，他根据父亲的设计制作了一台矿石收音机。

D6.en: At 17, he was one of 20-25 members of a club that formed.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他成为一个自发形成的20到25人俱乐部的成员。

D3.en: At 14-15, he attended math group in 8th-9th grade but declined to continue in 10th.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，他参加了八年级和九年级的数学小组，但拒绝了十年级的邀请。

D8.en: At 13, he snubbed Seely, turning his back on a popular boy.
D8.zh: 13岁时，他冷落了Seely，背弃了一个受欢迎的同学。

M7.en: At 13-17, he wondered intensely how to do something glorious for his country.
M7.zh: 13到17岁间，他强烈地思考如何为国家做光荣的事。

M6.en: He had a strong sense of duty.
M6.zh: 他有强烈的责任感。

D17.en: At 14-18, he showed his nanny Mrs Everest all over Harrow, to her delight.
D17.zh: 14到18岁间，他带保姆Everest夫人游览了整个哈罗公学，她非常开心。

D16.en: At 16, he said, 'You cannot imagine what vials of wrath you have uncorded. All the papers simply rave.'
D16.zh: 16岁时，他说：‘你无法想象你引发了多大的愤怒。所有报纸都在咆哮。’

D10.en: At 17, he wrote to his mother: 'I am so wretched. Even now I weep.'
D10.zh: 17岁时，他写信给母亲：‘我很难过。现在还在哭。’

D1.en: At 18, he failed again, worsening in Latin, French, and English composition.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他再次失败，拉丁文、法文和英文作文更差了。

D14.en: At 17, he traveled to Dieppe and Paris, mixing French and English in his account.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他去了迪耶普和巴黎，叙述中夹杂法语和英语。

D13b.en: At 13-14, he entered Harrow with the lowest marks in school.
D13b.zh: 13到14岁间，他进入哈罗公学，成绩全校最差。

D18.en: At 7-9, he asked someone for autographs to sell to classmates.
D18.zh: 7到9岁时，他找人要签名卖给同学。

D5.en: At 10, he was stabbed in the chest with a penknife by a boy whose ear he was yanking.
D5.zh: 10岁时，他被一个他正拽着耳朵的男孩用铅笔刀刺中胸口。

D11.en: As a teenager, he wrote a lugubrious poem inspired by a flu epidemic.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他写了一首阴郁的诗，灵感来自流感疫情。

M1.en: At 13-16, he imagined his father's pride if he were elected to Parliament.
M1.zh: 13到16岁时，他想象如果自己当选议员，父亲会多么自豪。

D4.en: At 17, he wrote to Jack about buying toy soldiers in France.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他写信给Jack说在法国买了玩具士兵。

D9.en: At 16, he built a fort and a catapult that threw green apples at a cow.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他建了一座堡垒和一个弹弓，向附近的牛扔青苹果。

D7.en: At 17, he reminded his mother of her promise for an extra week at home.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他提醒母亲她承诺的多一周假期。

D2.en: At 7-13, he developed a lifelong capacity for memorizing prose and verse.
D2.zh: 7到13岁间，他培养了终生背诵散文和诗歌的能力。

D3.en: At 9-13, he read voraciously, especially epic tales of adventure.
D3.zh: 9到13岁间，他大量阅读，尤其是史诗冒险故事。

D12.en: At 13, he wrote that his holidays were disrupted but might have been worse if Woomany had died.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他写道假期被打乱，但如果Woomany死了可能会更糟。

D6.en: At 12, he joined the Brighton branch of the organization.
D6.zh: 12岁时，他加入了该组织的布莱顿分会。

M3.en: At 16, he imagined himself as a future spy, Major Belov cum Johann Weiss.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他想象自己成为未来的间谍，马约尔·别洛夫兼约翰·韦斯。

D10.en: At 16, he furiously said, 'I'm not going to be a cop!'
D10.zh: 16岁时，他愤怒地说：“我不当警察！”

D1.en: At 13, the discipline of sambo and judo intrigued him like nothing else.
D1.zh: 13岁时，桑搏和柔道的纪律让他前所未有地着迷。

D13b.en: At 7, he began attending School No. 193 in Leningrad.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他开始在列宁格勒第193学校上学。

D13a.en: At 16, he decided to study law at university against his parents' wishes.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他决定上大学学法律，违背了父母的意愿。

D5.en: At around age 8-10, he and friends rode a commuter train to an unknown part of the city in search of adventure.
D5.zh: 大约8到10岁时，他和朋友们坐通勤火车去城市未知区域探险。

D11.en: At 16, he told a classmate he would be a spy.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他告诉同学他要当间谍。

D15.en: As a child and teenager, he trained in sambo and then judo.
D15.zh: 幼年到十几岁时，他先练桑搏，后练柔道。

D4.en: At around age 6-8, he and his friends would chase with sticks.
D4.zh: 大约6到8岁时，他和朋友们拿着棍子追逐。

D7.en: At around age 8-10, he was caught carrying a knife at school and rebuked by a neighborhood party committee.
D7.zh: 大约8到10岁时，他在学校被抓住带刀，并被街道党委批评。

D6.en: As a teenager aged 16-18, he spent time with a core group of friends outside the communal apartment.
D6.zh: 16到18岁时，他和一群核心朋友在公共公寓外度过时光。

D2.en: At age 12, his studies improved after he took up martial arts.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他练习武术后学习成绩提高了。

D3.en: At 16-18, he pursued humanities, literature, history, and continued German lessons.
D3.zh: 16到18岁时，他学习人文学科、文学、历史，并继续上德语课。

D12.en: As a small child, he lived in a kommunalka with his family sharing one room and facilities.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他住在公共公寓里，全家共用一间房和厨房厕所。

M4.en: At 16, he walked into KGB headquarters and volunteered his service.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他走进克格勃总部，自愿提供服务。

D8.en: He was domineering with classmates and led immature pranks.
D8.zh: 他对同学很霸道，带头搞幼稚的恶作剧。

M4.en: He imbibed the Schönerer creed in nationalist Linz.
M4.zh: 他在民族主义的林茨吸收了舍内雷尔信条。

D10.en: He showed the first signs of tantrums if he did not get his way.
D10.zh: 他表现出最初的发怒迹象，如果他不顺心的话。

D1.en: He took a re-sit in geometry.
D1.zh: 他补考了几何。

D13b.en: He had to accept a transfer to another school at Steyr, where he finished his education at sixteen.
D13b.zh: 他不得不接受转学到施泰尔的一所学校，并在十六岁完成了学业。

D13a.en: At 14-16, he defied his father's wish for him to become a civil servant.
D13a.zh: 14到16岁时，他违抗了父亲让他当公务员的意愿。

D5.en: At 18, he told his guardian he was going back to Vienna.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他告诉监护人他要回维也纳。

D11.en: At 16-18, he spent his days drawing, painting, reading, or writing poetry.
D11.zh: 16到18岁间，他整天画画、阅读或写诗。

M1.en: At 18, he took the admission exam for the Academy of Fine Arts in Vienna.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他参加了维也纳美术学院的入学考试。

D2.en: At 12-13, he improved after being scolded at home during his repeated year.
D2.zh: 12到13岁时，他在留级那年被家里骂了一顿后有所进步。

D8.en: As a teenager, he got used to lording it over people and always wanted to develop himself.
D8.zh: 十几岁时，他习惯了支配别人，总想发展自己。

D19.en: He was an avid reader who tried to learn English and German on his own.
D19.zh: 他酷爱读书，自学英语和德语。

M7.en: As a teenager, he became a Komsomol activist.
M7.zh: 十几岁时，他成为了一名共青团积极分子。

D20.en: At 17-18, he was appointed to the Komsomol committee for the whole district.
D20.zh: 17到18岁时，他被任命为全区共青团委员会委员。

D16.en: At 17-18, he reprimanded a woman in front of everyone.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他当众斥责了一名女性。

D10.en: At 7, he sat with his relatives while his father wept and recounted what had been done to him.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他和亲戚们坐在一起，父亲哭着讲述了自己的遭遇。

D1.en: At 18, he won the Red Labor Banner for helping his father break harvesting records.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他因帮助父亲打破收割纪录而获得劳动红旗勋章。

D13b.en: At 14, he was required to join the Komsomol, like all Soviet youngsters.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他像所有苏联青少年一样被要求加入共青团。

D4.en: He spent five summers helping his father operate a combine harvester.
D4.zh: 他连续五个夏天帮父亲操作联合收割机。

D9.en: He organized sports and social activities and led morning gym class.
D9.zh: 他组织体育和社交活动，并带领晨练。

D7.en: He shouted 'Stop! We're not afraid of them!'
D7.zh: 他喊道：“站住！我们不怕他们！”

D6.en: He was sought out to resolve disputes and referee fights.
D6.zh: 他被找来调解争端和裁判打架。

D2.en: His parents trained him to work hard and well.
D2.zh: 他的父母训练他努力并出色地工作。

D3.en: He knew not only short poems but long ones by heart.
D3.zh: 他不仅熟记短诗，还能背诵长诗。

D12.en: He had to leave childhood behind when barely into his teens.
D12.zh: 他刚进入十几岁就不得不告别童年。

M7.en: Three students organized the rally, but he was not among them.
M7.zh: 三名学生组织了集会，但他不在其中。

M6.en: At 18, he felt greatness was expected of him and wanted to be prepared.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他觉得自己注定要成就伟大，并为此做准备。

D20.en: He appeared in the most famous public photo of the Choom Gang at age 17-18.
D20.zh: 他17到18岁时出现在Choom Gang最著名的公开照片中。

D16.en: He typed a letter that was published in the Star-Bulletin four days later.
D16.zh: 他打了一封信，四天后发表在《星报》上。

D1.en: He was rejected by Swarthmore College in Pennsylvania.
D1.zh: 他被宾夕法尼亚州的斯沃斯莫尔学院拒绝了。

D18.en: At age 17 in 1978, he applied for a job at Baskin-Robbins with someone else.
D18.zh: 他17岁时在1978年和别人一起去Baskin-Robbins申请工作。

D5.en: He was offered beer, pot, and cocaine by Oxy and Punahou.
D5.zh: Oxy和Punahou向他提供了啤酒、大麻和可卡因。

D11.en: In December 1978 at age 17, his poem 'The Old Man' was published in the student newspaper.
D11.zh: 他17岁时在1978年12月，学生报纸发表了他写的诗《老人》。

M1.en: He performed well on the ETS entrance exam for fifth-graders at age 10.
M1.zh: 他10岁时在ETS五年级入学考试中表现出色。

D9.en: He was part of Group Y with other high-powered students at age 18.
D9.zh: 他18岁时是Y组的成员，和其他优秀学生一起。

D6.en: As a Choom Gang member, he started pot-smoking trends at age 14-15.
D6.zh: 作为Choom Gang成员，他14到15岁时引领了抽大麻的风潮。

D14.en: He performed as John Travolta in Saturday Night Fever.
D14.zh: 他扮演了《周末夜狂热》中的约翰·特拉沃尔塔。

M3.en: As a child, she read biographies of great Europeans and found her role model in Marie Curie.
M3.zh: 小时候，她读欧洲伟人的传记，找到了榜样居里夫人。

D17.en: She was always willing to help less gifted classmates.
D17.zh: 她总是愿意帮助不太有天赋的同学。

M4.en: At 17-18, she declared solidarity with the Vietnamese people and FRELIMO.
M4.zh: 17到18岁时，她表示声援越南人民和FRELIMO。

D10.en: She seemed to have a strong inner life all her own.
D10.zh: 她似乎有着自己丰富的内心世界。

D14.en: At 14, she began telling the story about the stamp-collecting boy in Czechoslovakia.
D14.zh: 14岁时，她开始讲述捷克斯洛伐克集邮男孩的故事。

D13b.en: At 15, she was confirmed and reaffirmed her belief in God.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，她接受了坚信礼，重申了对上帝的信仰。

D13a.en: At 14-17, she joined the FDJ, excelled in Russian, and was a model Socialist pupil.
D13a.zh: 14到17岁时，她加入FDJ，俄语成绩优异，是模范社会主义学生。

D18.en: She was the first in her school to wear blue jeans, received as contraband from Hamburg relatives.
D18.zh: 她是学校里第一个穿蓝色牛仔裤的人，那是汉堡亲戚送的走私品。

D5.en: At 17-18, she faced the risk of losing her university place.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，她面临失去大学名额的风险。

M1.en: She was the best in her class but did not win Pupil of the Year.
M1.zh: 她是班上最好的学生，但没有获得年度学生奖。

D15.en: At 7, she had an ambition to become a figure skater.
D15.zh: 7岁时，她梦想成为花样滑冰运动员。

D9.en: She was a classmate of Peter Bliss, the FDJ chairman in her school.
D9.zh: 她是学校自由德国青年团主席Peter Bliss的同班同学。

D7.en: She chose Lutheran confirmation over a state-sponsored Youth Dedication ceremony.
D7.zh: 她选择了路德宗坚信礼，而不是国家支持的青年奉献仪式。

D6.en: She joined the Young Pioneers, a pre-Communist Party organization.
D6.zh: 她加入了少先队，一个共产主义预备组织。

D2.en: She was the best in Russian, the most time-intensive subject.
D2.zh: 她在俄语上是最好的，这门课课时最多。

D3.en: She practiced English by reading the Morning Star, a UK communist newspaper.
D3.zh: 她通过阅读英国共产党报纸《晨星报》练习英语。

D12.en: She was late learning to walk and prone to falls and breaks as a small child.
D12.zh: 她学走路晚，幼年时容易摔倒和骨折。

M8.en: He participated in family prayers and Sunday mass as a child.
M8.zh: 他幼年时参加家庭早晚祈祷和周日弥撒。

M2.en: As a child, he practiced filial obedience to his parents.
M2.zh: 他幼年时对父母尽孝。

D10.en: At 6, his face was as pale as death.
D10.zh: 6岁时，他的脸像死人一样苍白。

D1.en: At 6, his father gave him tasks so hard even adults couldn't solve them by that time.
D1.zh: 6岁时，他父亲给他布置了当时连大人都解不出的难题。

D11.en: Between ages 9 and 14, he avidly read adventure stories.
D11.zh: 9到14岁时，他痴迷于阅读冒险故事。

M1.en: At 18, he answered one of three questions for his Abitur exam.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他回答了Abitur考试中的三个问题之一。

D15.en: At age 6-12, he lay in plaster for six weeks and later wore steel braces up to his hips.
D15.zh: 6到12岁时，他打了六周石膏，后来还戴着到臀部的钢支架。

D4.en: At age 10-15, he helped with hay-making and harvest and discovered an eagle-owl nesting in the church loft.
D4.zh: 10到15岁时，他帮忙割草和收割，并在教堂阁楼发现了一只雕鸮筑巢。

D9.en: At 18, he proposed that each examinee make several errors in a pre-arranged translation sample to match their knowledge level.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他提议每个考生在预先准备好的翻译样本中故意犯几个错误，错误的性质和数量应大致对应其知识水平。

D7.en: At 18, he called the event pure fraud but noted it went like clockwork.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他说这完全是骗局，但重要的是一切进行得井井有条。

D2.en: At age 5, his father taught him reading, writing and arithmetic.
D2.zh: 5岁时，他父亲教他读、写和算术。

D3.en: At 18, he earned a 'good' in physics, praised for enthusiastic participation.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他物理得了‘良好’，评语称他积极参与课堂。

D13a.en: At 18, he took for granted his father's offer to work at a bank.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他理所当然地接受了父亲让他去银行工作的提议。

D8.en: At 13, he assumed the authority of a husband.
D8.zh: 13岁时，他承担起丈夫的权威。

M4.en: At 15, he chewed cooked goat's flesh with Sheik Mehtab by the river.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他和Sheik Mehtab在河边嚼了煮熟的羊肉。

D13b.en: At 13, he married while in high school.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他作为高中二年级学生结婚了。

D5.en: At 18, he took his first journey by that mode of transport.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他第一次乘坐那种交通工具旅行。

M1.en: At 18, he averaged about 45% in English and Gujarati.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他英语和古吉拉特语平均约45分。

D15.en: As a small child, he was restless as mercury.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他像水银一样好动。

D9.en: He enrolled for a BA degree at Samaldas College in Bhavnagar.
D9.zh: 他在巴夫纳加尔的萨马拉斯学院注册攻读学士学位。

D6.en: He rented a room in a Vaishnavite locality and cooked his own food.
D6.zh: 他在毗湿奴派地区租了一个房间，自己做饭。

D2.en: He performed creditably in his fifth grade exams, averaging 55.75% and coming third in his class.
D2.zh: 他在五年级考试中表现不错，平均分55.75%，班级第三。

D3.en: He read 'The Truth about Women' and found it interesting but disagreed with the conclusion.
D3.zh: 他读了《女性的真相》这本书，觉得有趣但不同意作者的结论。

D12.en: At 17, his father's death in 1886 may have led him to leave the peninsula.
D12.zh: 17岁时父亲去世可能促使他离开半岛。

D17.en: At 16, he volunteered as a stretcher-bearer in Lourdes.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他自愿在卢尔德担任担架员。

M2.en: At 18, he told his father he owed his success in the Saint-Cyr exam to him.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他告诉父亲，他考入圣西尔军校的成功归功于父亲。

M4.en: As a child, he believed he would one day render France conspicuous service.
M4.zh: 幼年时，他相信自己终有一天会为法国做出显著贡献。

D13b.en: As a small child, he was raised with northern Catholic discipline.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他受到北方天主教纪律的严格教育。

D5.en: He wrote: 'Happy are those who die for the earthly land, provided it is in a just war.'
D5.zh: 他写道：'为尘世土地而死的人是幸福的，只要是在正义的战争中。'

D11.en: At 15, he saw himself at the head of the armies of France.
D11.zh: 15岁时，他想象自己站在法国军队的前列。

D6.en: As a child, he often spent family holidays in a rented villa in Wimereux near Calais.
D6.zh: 他幼年时常在加来附近的维姆勒租别墅度家庭假。

D2.en: At 17, he ranked 12th in math and 2nd in physics/chemistry, reading history and German.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他数学考了第12名，物理化学第2名，还读历史和德语。

D3.en: He read a great deal of history, natural history, and German.
D3.zh: 他大量阅读历史、自然史和德语书籍。

D12.en: He was born in his grandparents' house in Lille on November 22, 1890.
D12.zh: 他于1890年11月22日在里尔的外祖父母家中出生。

D15.en: At 16-17, he distinguished himself as an aggressive full-back in Antoing.
D15.zh: 16到17岁时，他在安托万队中作为激进的后卫表现出色。

M5.en: At 18, he pompously said beauty is skin-deep and without other qualities would be harmful.
M5.zh: 18岁时，他自负地说美貌只是肤浅的，没有其他品质反而有害。

D8.en: At 6-10, he tried to dominate over his sister.
D8.zh: 6到10岁时，他试图支配他的妹妹。

M2.en: At 18, his father promised him a 'real gem' of a girl from a large field of selection.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他父亲承诺从大量候选人中为他找一个'真正的宝石'般的女孩。

D1.en: At 17, he thought he would get a prize and that would be the end of it.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他以为会得个奖，事情就结束了。

D14.en: At 15, he was fortunate enough to get nothing to sing though he nearly got one.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他幸运地没被安排唱歌，尽管差点被安排。

D13b.en: At 18, he had to go to London to apply for admission to the Inner Temple on December 1, 1908.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他于1908年12月1日去伦敦申请进入内殿律师学院。

D13a.en: As a small child, he was caught peeping and told to apologize to his father.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他偷看被抓，被要求去向父亲道歉。

D11.en: As a small child, he was dressed as an Indian prince for photos, then given a Lord Fauntleroy outfit for his tricycle.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他被打扮成印度王子拍照，然后穿上Lord Fauntleroy衣服骑三轮车。

M1.en: At 15, he was top of his form and told a prize would be given to him.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他是年级第一名，并被告知会获得奖品。

D15.en: At 15, he had to prepare footbaths for older students after football.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他得在足球赛后给高年级学生准备洗脚水。

D4.en: At 17, Nehru said he would have dearly liked to have been there.
D4.zh: 17岁时尼赫鲁说他非常想去那里。

D6.en: At 15, he joined the Chess Club.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他加入了国际象棋俱乐部。

D2.en: At 15, he said he had his work for tomorrow morning to do.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他说他明天早上有工作要做。

D3.en: At 16, he came across Meredith Townsend's Asia and Europe, and his mind was receptive.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他读到Meredith Townsend的《亚洲与欧洲》，思想很受启发。

D17.en: He saw her bring home local children who needed support with school fees.
D17.zh: 他看到她带回家需要学费资助的当地孩子。

M2.en: He was inspired by his father's resistance and tales of black opposition handed down by his mother and elders.
M2.zh: 他被父亲的抵抗以及母亲和长辈传述的黑人反抗故事所激励。

D16.en: He successfully shouted, 'I am an adult man!'
D16.zh: 他成功喊道：“我是一个成年男人！”

D5.en: He was tense, hoping not to cry out or flinch at the cuts.
D5.zh: 他紧张地希望自己不会在割礼时叫喊或退缩。

D15.en: He underwent Thembu initiation rituals to prove his courage.
D15.zh: 他参加了坦布族成人礼以证明自己的勇气。

D4.en: He helped fix a young white man's motorbike and got paid.
D4.zh: 他帮一个年轻白人修摩托车，并得到了报酬。

D9.en: He arranged a welcome party for his family after finishing elementary school.
D9.zh: 他为家人安排了一场欢迎会，庆祝他小学毕业。

D7.en: He rushed at a girl who made a remark, wanting to choke her.
D7.zh: 他冲向一个说了话的女孩，想掐死她。

D6.en: He delighted in playing traditional games and sports with others.
D6.zh: 他喜欢和别人一起玩传统游戏和运动。

D2.en: At 15, he completed primary education and followed someone to a new school.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他完成小学教育，跟着某人去了新学校。

D3.en: He first heard about the African National Congress at Healdtown.
D3.zh: 他在希尔德镇第一次听说非洲人国民大会。

M7.en: At 12, she told someone they were committed after reading something.
M7.zh: 12岁时，她告诉别人既然读了，就要承诺。

D17.en: At 18, she traveled with her mother to Bhowali with nurses and a doctor.
D17.zh: 18岁时，她随母亲和医护人员前往Bhowali。

M2.en: At 16, she became acutely aware that her father had never earned money and they lived on her grandfather's money.
M2.zh: 16岁时，她强烈意识到父亲从未赚钱，他们靠祖父留下的钱生活。

D20.en: At 14-16, the school's mock parliament chose her as prime minister.
D20.zh: 14到16岁时，学校的模拟议会选她当总理。

M4.en: At 12, she was the first to volunteer but was told she was too young.
M4.zh: 12岁时，她第一个报名，但被笑说太小。

D16.en: At 17, she wrote a cheerful letter to her father describing Dr Atal's hapless attempts at learning German.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她给父亲写了一封愉快的信，描述Dr Atal学德语的狼狈。

D10.en: At 18, she woke up with a queer singing feeling in her heart and wrote to her father about it.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她醒来时心中有一种奇妙的歌唱感，并写信告诉了父亲。

D1.en: At 12, she begged to be promoted to 4th class by her own power.
D1.zh: 12岁时，她恳求凭自己的实力升入四年级。

D14.en: At 16-17, her great passion was Indian classical dance of the Manipuri School.
D14.zh: 16到17岁时，她热衷于曼尼普尔派的印度古典舞。

D13b.en: At 14, she was suggested by Gandhiji to join the People’s Own School in Poona.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，甘地建议她加入浦那的人民学校。

D13a.en: At 12, restrained from accompanying her mother, she went to Nainital with her uncle in protest.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，因被阻止陪母亲政治活动，她与叔叔去奈尼塔尔以示抗议。

D11.en: As a small child, she loved to fantasize, create plays, design costumes, and dress up.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她喜欢幻想、编戏、设计服装和打扮。

D4.en: At 16-17, she joined Kala Bhavan and came under the influence of painter Nandlal Bose.
D4.zh: 16到17岁时，她加入卡拉巴万，受画家南德拉尔·博斯影响。

D9.en: At age 5, she organized a Bal Charkha Sangh for children to learn spinning and weaving.
D9.zh: 5岁时，她组织了一个儿童纺车协会，教小孩子纺纱织布。

D7.en: At age 6, she ignored others while watching birds settle or fly away.
D7.zh: 6岁时，她不理会别人，只顾看鸟儿停在枝头或飞走。

D3.en: At age 8, she spent hours collecting all the greens in nature from her surroundings.
D3.zh: 8岁时，她花了好几个小时收集周围自然中所有的绿色。

D15.en: At 9, she skied and sledged at Chesieres and skied with her father.
D15.zh: 9岁时，她在谢西耶滑雪和坐雪橇，还和父亲一起滑雪。

D2.en: At age 18, she had to return to Badminton, cram Latin she loathed, and retake the exam in March.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她不得不回到巴德明顿，死记硬背她讨厌的拉丁文，并在三月份重考。

D8.en: As a teenager, he was often granted sexual favors by women for free.
D8.zh: 十几岁时，他经常得到女性无偿的性恩惠。

M4.en: At 15-16, he was moved by the patriotism in a work he read.
M4.zh: 15到16岁时，他被读到的作品中的爱国主义所打动。

D16.en: He began to display a forcible capacity for convincing his audience in his orations.
D16.zh: 他在演讲中开始展现出说服听众的强力能力。

D14.en: He asked the boys to jump over him while he stood up straight.
D14.zh: 他让其他男孩从他身上跳过去，而他站得笔直。

D13b.en: In his youth, he attended Bektashi gatherings in Salonika.
D13b.zh: 他年轻时参加过萨洛尼卡的贝克塔什集会。

D13a.en: His military rank meant everything to him since he first contrived to enter the Military School in Salonika as a boy.
D13a.zh: 从他小时候设法进入萨洛尼卡军事学校起，军衔对他来说就意味一切。

D5.en: He and a friend tried to join the fighting forces but were discovered and returned to school.
D5.zh: 他和一个朋友试图加入战斗部队，但被发现并送回了学校。

M1.en: He graduated second in his class from the military high school in Manastır at age 17.
M1.zh: 他17岁时以全班第二名的成绩从马纳斯提尔军事高中毕业。

D15.en: As a small child, he rolled hoops through the streets of Salonika with others.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他和别人一起在萨洛尼卡街头滚铁环。

D9.en: At 14, he threw himself into the swirling world of Monastir and thrived on it.
D9.zh: 他14岁时投身于莫纳斯提尔的纷繁世界，并从中茁壮成长。

D7.en: At 13-14, refusing to accept loss of status, he left home and lodged with a relative.
D7.zh: 他13到14岁时，因拒绝接受地位下降，离家寄宿在亲戚家。

D6.en: At 18, he made new friends, most importantly a tall cadet named Ali Fuat (Cebesoy).
D6.zh: 他18岁时结交新朋友，最重要的是一个高个子学员Ali Fuat (Cebesoy)。

D2.en: At age 12-13, he entered the military preparatory school in Salonica and acquired the name Kemal.
D2.zh: 他12到13岁时进入萨洛尼卡军事预备学校，并获得了Kemal这个名字。

D3.en: At age 10-12, he urgently wanted to learn but his education was being neglected.
D3.zh: 10到12岁时，他迫切想学习，但教育被忽视。

M3.en: As a boy, he admired Lincoln and saw him as a model.
M3.zh: 他从小就崇拜林肯，视其为榜样。

M6.en: At 16-17, he loved Cicero's orations and saw oratory as his path.
M6.zh: 16到17岁时，他喜欢西塞罗的演说，并看到自己可能用演讲。

D14.en: At 17, he was selected to write and deliver the farewell speech.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他被选中撰写并发表告别演讲。

D13b.en: At 15, in 1887, he was registered at Black River Academy.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，1887年，他注册进入黑河学院。

D18.en: As a child, he sold apples and popcorn at meetings, following his father's example.
D18.zh: 他小时候在会议上卖苹果和爆米花，学他父亲的样子。

D2.en: As a boy and young man, he worked long hours to impress his father.
D2.zh: 他从小努力长时间工作，想给父亲留下好印象。

D3.en: He was an avid reader and used classical references in speeches.
D3.zh: 他酷爱读书，演讲中常引用古典典故。

D12.en: At 18, he went to Amherst College to sit the entrance exam.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他去阿默斯特学院参加入学考试。

D10.en: At 17, he said: 'It is lonesome here without Abbie.'
D10.zh: 17岁时，他说：“没有阿比这里很寂寞。”

D4.en: At age 10, he stitched a quilt top of Tumbling Blocks pattern.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他缝制了一个万花筒图案的被子面。

M3.en: At 16, he saw Leber as a father figure.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他把Leber视为父亲般的人物。

D14.en: At 16, he was a contributing editor of the Volksbote.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他担任《人民信使报》的撰稿编辑。

D5.en: At 18, he was invited to stand as a candidate despite being underage.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他被邀请作为候选人，尽管未到最低年龄。

D9.en: At 17, he and half the Socialist Youth joined enthusiastically.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他和约一半社会主义青年组织成员热情加入。

D6.en: As a small child, he joined children's groups of the workers' sport association.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他加入工人体育协会的儿童团体。

D2.en: At 18, he would cycle 60 km to Hamburg to get speakers to come to Lübeck.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他常骑车60公里去汉堡请知名演讲者来吕贝克。

D3.en: At 14, he wrote frequent contributions to the paper and decided to become a journalist.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他频繁为报纸撰稿，并决定成为记者。

D12.en: As a small child, he was raised exclusively by women up to age five.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他五岁前完全由女性抚养。

D15.en: He won a 5000 m race only because he was the only contestant.
D15.zh: 他赢了一场5000米比赛，只是因为他是唯一的参赛者。

D20.en: At 16, he established himself as the group's unrivalled convenor.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他成为小组无可争议的组织者。

M4.en: At 16-17, he brought an ethical system called 'optimalism' to the group.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，他带着一套名为‘最优主义’的伦理体系加入小组。

D16.en: At 16-17, he denied charges, insisting man is a thinking creature.
D16.zh: 16到17岁时，他否认指控，坚称人是会思考的生物。

D14.en: At 10, dressed in a navy suit, he recited a poem about seasons and politics before his class.
D14.zh: 10岁时，他穿着深蓝色西装，在班上朗诵了一首关于季节和政治的诗。

D5.en: He made a squiffy sarcastic remark about a man understanding the Great October Revolution.
D5.zh: 他醉醺醺地讽刺说，有人理解十月革命的意义。

D11.en: At 17, he was already writing poems, one dedicated to her.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他已经在写诗，其中一首献给了她。

D9.en: At 17, he wrote a 28-page assessment of the group's performance.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他写了一篇28页的小组工作评估。

D2.en: He was told to apply himself to learn a language.
D2.zh: 他被告知要努力学习一门语言。

D3.en: At 16, he was beginning to fancy himself an intellectual.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他开始自认为是知识分子。

D13b.en: In the first week of September 1942 at age 6, he began attending school.
D13b.zh: 1942年9月第一周，他6岁时开始上学。

D13a.en: At 13-14, he was expelled from school after two and a half years.
D13a.zh: 13到14岁时，他在读了两年半后被学校开除。

D12.en: At age 6, his mother decided to move him to a country school.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他母亲决定送他去乡村学校。

D8.en: At 17, he ensured everybody worked purposefully on the play.
D8.zh: 17岁时，他确保每个人都专注地排练话剧。

D17.en: His mother Hazel nursed his father Leo back to health over three years.
D17.zh: 他母亲Hazel照顾他父亲Leo康复三年。

M2.en: He felt he couldn't let his father down after his father's illness.
M2.zh: 父亲生病后，他觉得自己不能让他失望。

D16.en: At 15, he had a fifteen-minute argument about changing something at school.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他花了十五分钟争论学校应该立即改变某件事。

D1.en: At 11, his father's stroke was the most important event in his first 18 years.
D1.zh: 11岁时，父亲中风是他前18年中最重要的事件。

D14.en: At 17, he was cast as Stanhope as the most experienced senior actor.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他作为最有经验的高年级演员被选为Stanhope一角。

M1.en: At 18, he achieved AAC grades in English, History and French.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在英语、历史和法语中取得了AAC的成绩。

D9.en: At 17, he volunteered to help run a summer camp for a boys' club.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他自愿帮助为一个男孩俱乐部举办夏令营。

D7.en: As a teenager, he knocked on Eric Anderson's door asking why he had to obey school rules.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他敲开Eric Anderson的门，问他为什么必须遵守校规。

D6.en: At 12, he was the Conservative candidate in a school mock election.
D6.zh: 12岁时，他在学校模拟选举中担任保守党候选人。

D2.en: He attended St. John's College, one of the richest colleges at Oxford.
D2.zh: 他上了牛津大学最富有的学院之一圣约翰学院。

D12.en: At 11, his father Leo was deprived of speech for three years by a stroke.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他父亲Leo因中风失语三年。

D18.en: At 18, he hatched a plan to sell a recipe to Beecham.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他策划了一个向Beecham出售配方的计划。

D11.en: At age 12, he published a poem titled 'A Story of Romance'.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他发表了一首题为《A Story of Romance》的诗。

D15.en: At 15-16, a serious rugby injury left him sightless in one eye, delaying his start by one term.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，一次严重的橄榄球受伤使他一只眼失明，入学推迟了一个学期。

D4.en: At 18, he bought a decrepit blue Ford Thames van for £50 to ferry bands and equipment.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他花50英镑买了一辆破旧的蓝色福特Thames面包车，用来运送乐队和设备。

M7.en: She was inspired by the activism of the Jews of Pinsk.
M7.zh: 她被平斯克犹太人的行动主义所激励。

M2.en: She decided to live with Sheyna so she could study.
M2.zh: 她决定和Sheyna住在一起，以便能上学。

M4.en: At 15-16, she was intrigued by discussions about Zionism and socialism.
M4.zh: 15到16岁时，她对关于犹太复国主义和社会主义的讨论很感兴趣。

D13a.en: She placated her mother by disinfecting teacups after visitors left.
D13a.zh: 她通过客人走后给茶杯消毒来安抚母亲。

D18.en: At 16, she found a job in a department store taking measurements for skirt linings.
D18.zh: 16岁时，她在百货公司找到一份工作，为定制裙衬量尺寸。

D15.en: At 8, she stayed healthy on deck during a rough crossing, showing strong constitution.
D15.zh: 8岁时，她在颠簸的航程中待在甲板上保持健康，显示出强健的体质。

D9.en: She hosted a meeting at home where her mother returned to find young conspirators.
D9.zh: 她在家里主持了一场会议，母亲回来时发现满屋都是年轻密谋者。

D6.en: She made friends in the group and joined them at lectures, concerts, and picnics.
D6.zh: 她在团体中交到朋友，并和他们一起参加讲座、音乐会和野餐。

D12.en: She dropped out of school on June 5, 1914, and went to work at a laundry.
D12.zh: 她于1914年6月5日辍学，去一家洗衣店工作。

D7.en: Defying her parents, she enrolled in North Division High School on September 3, 1912.
D7.zh: 她违抗父母，于1912年9月3日入读北区高中。

D19.en: As a small child, he charted every expenditure in a miniature account book.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他用一本小账本记录每一笔开销。

M6.en: He believed money and success were necessary to control one's destiny.
M6.zh: 他相信金钱和成功是掌控命运所必需的。

D1.en: At 17, after failing Swain's exams, he agreed to go to Palo Alto three months early to prepare with a tutor.
D1.zh: 17岁时，在斯温考试失败后，他同意提前三个月去帕洛阿尔托，跟家教准备新一轮考试。

D13b.en: At 10, he joined the Band of Hope, a prohibitionist children's group.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他加入了Band of Hope，一个禁酒儿童团体。

D18.en: He tried to make money by repairing and selling abandoned sewing machines in Salem.
D18.zh: 他尝试在塞勒姆修理和出售废弃的缝纫机来赚钱。

D5.en: He slipped to the back of the building and shut off the gas, leaving the businessmen in total darkness.
D5.zh: 他溜到楼后关掉煤气，让愤怒的商人们陷入一片漆黑。

D15.en: He built forts and tunnels along the creek bank and crawled through storm sewers with other boys.
D15.zh: 他和其他男孩在河岸边建堡垒和隧道，并爬过主街下的雨水管。

D4.en: He caught sunfish with a willow pole and trapped rabbits with a crackerbox.
D4.zh: 他用柳枝钓翻车鱼，用饼干盒捉兔子。

D9.en: Unwilling to perform onstage, he ran a lucrative lecture and concert series.
D9.zh: 他不愿上台表演，于是经营了一个赚钱的讲座和音乐会系列。

D7.en: Despite Quaker nonviolence, he started a fistfight with a boy who played pranks on him.
D7.zh: 尽管贵格会主张非暴力，他还是和一个捉弄他的男孩打了一架。

D6.en: As a Stanford student, he led an antifraternity element against campus swells.
D6.zh: 在斯坦福大学时，他领导反兄弟会势力对抗校园精英。

D2.en: He wrote a letter to Nell May Hill on August 30, 1892.
D2.zh: 他于1892年8月30日给Nell May Hill写了一封信。

D3.en: At age five, he tossed a burning stick into hot tar to see what would happen.
D3.zh: 五岁时，他把一根燃烧的木棍扔进热焦油里，想看看会发生什么。

D12.en: His lot was menial labor: cleaning the barn, cutting thistle, picking potato bugs.
D12.zh: 他干的是粗活：打扫谷仓、割蓟草、捉马铃薯虫。

D10.en: He felt some anger inside him.
D10.zh: 他内心感到一些愤怒。

D13b.en: He followed the rule to respect elders even if undeserving.
D13b.zh: 他遵守规则，尊重长辈，即使他们不完全值得。

D13a.en: Until about age 10, he called his stepfather 'dad'.
D13a.zh: 直到大约十岁，他叫继父‘爸爸’。

D15.en: He preferred being a goalkeeper because his short legs couldn't keep up with other boys.
D15.zh: 他更喜欢当守门员，因为他的短腿跟不上其他男孩。

D7.en: He was constantly reprimanded for smoking, wearing a cap in his pocket, and impertinence.
D7.zh: 他因抽烟、把帽子放在口袋里以及无礼而不断受到训斥。

D6.en: He was a mediocre student in an average class, decent at math but poor at Polish.
D6.zh: 他在一个普通班级里是个平庸的学生，数学还行，但波兰语很差。

D3.en: At 13, he began to listen more carefully to the words around him.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他开始更仔细地听周围的话语。

D12.en: After school, he worked on the farm, cutting straw and tending livestock.
D12.zh: 放学后，他在农场干活，切稻草和照料牲畜。

M2.en: As a child, he visited shrines and mausoleums, deepening his sense of ancestral importance.
M2.zh: 他小时候参拜神社和皇陵，加深了对祖先重要性的认识。

D20.en: At 11, he became crown prince and was given ranks of second lieutenant in the army and ensign in the navy.
D20.zh: 11岁时，他成为皇太子，被授予陆军少尉和海军少尉军衔。

D10.en: At 11, he was overcome with emotion upon hearing of Nogi's death, tears welling up.
D10.zh: 11岁时，他听到乃木的死讯后情绪激动，泪流满面。

D13b.en: At 7, he entered the elementary course at the Peers' School.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他进入学习院初等科。

D13a.en: At 6, he and his brothers readily complied with Nogi's order to salute and address him as 'Excellency'.
D13a.zh: 6岁时，他和弟弟们顺从地按照乃木的要求敬礼并称呼他为“阁下”。

D15.en: At 18, he celebrated his coming of age in spring 1919.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他于1919年春天举行了成人礼。

D4.en: At 5, he was given a special tour of the warship Katori.
D4.zh: 5岁时，他获准特别参观战舰香取号。

D7.en: At 4-7, he played tag strictly according to the rules, never using tricks.
D7.zh: 4到7岁时，他玩捉人游戏时严格遵守规则，从不耍花招。

D6.en: At 3-4, he and his brothers enacted the Russo-Japanese War in play.
D6.zh: 3到4岁时，他和弟弟们在游戏中扮演日俄战争。

D2.en: He began playing war games as early as the fourth or fifth year of elementary school.
D2.zh: 他早在小学四五年级时就开始玩战争游戏。

D3.en: At 18, he made his first scientific discovery, of a new species of prawn.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他做出了第一个科学发现，一种新虾类。

D12.en: He was removed from the court and placed in the care of the Kawamura family.
D12.zh: 他被带离宫廷，交由川村家抚养。

M7.en: He felt disillusioned as fellow Asiatics.
M7.zh: 他作为亚洲同胞感到幻灭。

D20.en: He was asked to make the opening speech about complaints against Ungku Aziz.
D20.zh: 他被要求做开场发言，陈述对Ungku Aziz的不满。

D16.en: He made a case in measured tones, firmly but not aggressively.
D16.zh: 他用平和的语气陈述理由，坚定但不咄咄逼人。

D10.en: He said spontaneously, "That's the end of the British Empire!"
D10.zh: 他脱口而出：“大英帝国完了！”

D14.en: At 4, he served as a page boy at his aunt's wedding, dressed in traditional costume.
D14.zh: 4岁时，他在阿姨的婚礼上当小傧相，穿着当时的传统服装。

D13b.en: He was given a double promotion from primary 1 to standard I.
D13b.zh: 他从小学一年级跳级到标准一年级。

D13a.en: He was sent to an English school to prepare for an English university.
D13a.zh: 他被送到英语学校，为上英国大学做准备。

D5.en: He refused weapons and comforted a friend, saying no battle is lost until it's over.
D5.zh: 他拒绝武器，安慰朋友说战斗没结束就不算输。

M1.en: At 12, he came first in school and won a place in Raffles Institution.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他考了全校第一，进入了只收顶尖学生的莱佛士书院。

D15.en: He had a broken nose from boxing in his younger days.
D15.zh: 他年轻时打拳击导致鼻子骨折。

D7.en: At 14-15, he was caught in the laboratory drawing the back of his teacher's bald head.
D7.zh: 14到15岁时，他在实验室画老师后脑勺的秃斑被抓到。

D6.en: At 13-16, he enjoyed Raffles Institution, active in Scouts, cricket, tennis, swimming, and debates.
D6.zh: 13到16岁时，他在莱佛士书院过得很开心，活跃于童子军、板球、网球、游泳和辩论。

D2.en: At 7, he had to sit with his father for an hour three evenings a week before dinner.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他每周有三个晚上要在晚饭前和父亲坐一小时。

D3.en: As a teenager, he used Raffles Library, borrowing books and reading eclectically, preferring westerns.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他使用莱佛士图书馆借书，广泛阅读，偏爱西部小说。

D12.en: He was born in Singapore on 16 September 1923.
D12.zh: 他于1923年9月16日出生在新加坡。

D9.en: At 18, he volunteered for the MAS and cycled daily to his post three miles away.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他志愿加入MAS，每天从家骑车三英里到岗。

M2.en: As a child, his mother corrected his table posture, citing Confucius: 'Do not eat if the table is not straight.'
M2.zh: 他幼年时，母亲因桌不正而纠正他，引用孔子的话。

D14.en: At 5-10, he imitated monks doing Buddhist services, draping a sheet as a kasaya, directing other children in his performances.
D14.zh: 他摹仿和尚做佛事，身披被单为袈裟，导演调度邻里儿童，表演欲得到满足。

D13a.en: After becoming a monk, he thought of himself as dead and returned gifts to avoid emotional attachment.
D13a.zh: 出家后，他把自己当死人看，把收到的礼物退回去，免得动心挂念。

D11.en: At 6-7, he ignored someone and played with his cat.
D11.zh: 他六岁时没睬人，顾自逗猫玩去了。

M1.en: At 18, his essay and fu were ranked first, and his calligraphy showed Han-Wei style, praised by Teacher Zhang.
M1.zh: 他十八岁时论文和赋均列首选，书法富于汉魏气息，得张师佳评。

D4.en: At age 6-7, he found a copy of Jieziyuan Huapu, was overjoyed, and traced pictures on the ground with paper.
D4.zh: 他六七岁时找到一本《芥子园画谱》，喜不自禁，趴在地上用纸印描起来。

D9.en: At 17, he joined a literary society late but his works won deep respect from members.
D9.zh: 他17岁入社最迟，但所作诗文篆刻书法深得社友器重。

D7.en: At age 6-7, he was so angry he wanted to jump up, but was held back by Lady Wang.
D7.zh: 他六七岁时气得要跳起来，被王夫人一把拉住。

D2.en: At 17, he asked a friend to buy dozens of seals and iron pens, and many calligraphy books, regardless of price.
D2.zh: 他17岁时托朋友买数十块图章、数支铁笔和数十部篆隶帖，价昂无碍。

D3.en: At 17, he planned to invite teachers to study arithmetic and foreign languages after the Dragon Boat Festival.
D3.zh: 他17岁时打算过五月节后请人教算术、学洋文。

D12.en: As a small child, his parents gave him a branch as a keepsake, which he kept by his side.
D12.zh: 他幼年时父母送他树枝作纪念品，他长留在身旁。

D14.en: At 9, he insisted on accompanying his grandmother on a pilgrimage to Mount Jiuhua, marking his first long journey.
D14.zh: 9岁时，他强要随外祖母去九华山进香，开始了远游。

D13b.en: At 14, he returned to Chongde to sweep ancestral graves but failed to gain inheritance rights, then hurried back to Haining.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他回崇德祭扫祖坟，但未能取得祖产继承权，便赶回海宁。

D18.en: At 15, he became an apprentice at a department store in Chang'an but was too weak for chores and longed for Buddhist freedom.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他在长安百货店当学徒，但因体弱不堪家务，向往佛门自在。

D5.en: At 16, he left Chang'an on a pretext, intending to go to Mount Putuo to become a monk.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他藉故离开长安，想去普陀山出家。

D9.en: In his later years, he founded the weekly "Awakening the Masses" in Shanghai and wrote short commentaries under a pen name.
D9.zh: 晚年他在上海创办《觉群》周报，写短评，用笔名。

D6.en: At 18, on the Buddhist Ullambana day, he swore brotherhood with Yuanying at the Tianning Monastery library.
D6.zh: 18岁时，佛教自恣日，他在天童御书楼与圆暎结为兄弟。

D2.en: At 14, he quit due to recurring malaria, stayed with his grandmother in a nunnery, rested and read, and began to learn writing.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他因疟疾复发退业，寄居外祖母处养息温读，开始学写文章。

D3.en: At 8, listening to his uncle's stories by lamplight, his understanding grew and he could compose couplets.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他在灯下听舅父讲解，理解力渐增，能对三五字联。

D12.en: At 13, he was sent to work as an apprentice in a general store in Chang'an town, but left due to illness after about a year.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他被送到长安镇的一家百货店当学徒，一年左右因病退业。

D12.en: At 5, she carried a baby into an air-raid shelter whenever sirens sounded.
D12.zh: 5岁时，每当警报响起，她就背着小娃娃跑进防空洞。

D19.en: At 17, he won the prize for best-kept tent.
D19.zh: 17岁时，他赢得了最佳帐篷奖。

D16.en: As a teenager, he was argumentative and loved to argue about politics.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他很好辩，最喜欢争论政治。

D14.en: He excelled at charades and was a good mimic of politicians.
D14.zh: 他擅长猜字游戏，并且很会模仿当时的政治家。

D18.en: At 17, he earned his first income as a superintendent at a Rifle Corps summer camp.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他担任步枪队夏令营主管，赚了第一笔收入。

D15.en: At 9-13, he played rugby passionately and loved cricket.
D15.zh: 9到13岁时，他热衷橄榄球和板球。

D6.en: At 13-17, he was a keen member of the Volunteer Rifle Corps.
D6.zh: 13到17岁时，他是志愿步枪队的活跃成员。

D3.en: At 16, he condemned his headmaster for being too pro-Boer.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他谴责校长过于亲布尔人。

D13a.en: At 17, his father celebrated his marriage to the two girls against his will, placing him under house arrest.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他父亲不顾他的意愿为他举办了婚礼，并把他软禁起来。

D7.en: At 14, his father discovered he wanted to join the Sangha and hired a Daoist to teach him at home.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他父亲发现他想出家，便请了一位道士来家里教他道术。

D2.en: From age 5 to 17, he disliked meat and Confucian classics, preferring Buddhist sutras.
D2.zh: 5到17岁时，他不喜欢吃肉，也不喜欢儒家经典，偏爱佛经。

D14.en: She learned to play the piano with enthusiasm and aptitude.
D14.zh: 她热情又有天赋地学习弹钢琴。

D13b.en: At age 5-10, her mother made her go to a room and pray every night.
D13b.zh: 她5到10岁时，母亲每晚让她去房间祈祷。

D15.en: She took riding lessons at the Presidential Bodyguard's exercise grounds.
D15.zh: 她在总统卫队的训练场地上马术课。

D9.en: She set up a scheme for bright young Burmese to study abroad.
D9.zh: 她设立了一个计划，让聪明的缅甸年轻人出国留学。

D6.en: At age 11, she transferred to the more prestigious Methodist English High School in Signal Road.
D6.zh: 11岁时，她转学到信号路上更著名的卫理公会英语高中。

D2.en: By 15, through steely application, she was regularly coming first in her class in every subject.
D2.zh: 到15岁时，通过刻苦努力，她经常在每门课上都考全班第一。

D17.en: Her brother handed her a gun and told her to look after it.
D17.zh: 她弟弟把枪递给她，让她保管。

D5.en: She was terrified but trusted him and made it step by step.
D5.zh: 她很害怕但信任他，一步步地成功了。

D3.en: As a teenager, she examined her countrymen from her new perspective and saw how right May Oung had been.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她从新角度审视同胞，发现May Oung说得多么正确。

D12.en: At 15, she and her mother boarded a plane for Delhi, beginning her years of exile.
D12.zh: 15岁时，她和母亲登上飞往德里的飞机，开始了流亡岁月。

M3.en: At 18, he enjoyed attending Su Xuelin's lectures.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他喜欢听苏雪林的课。

M2.en: He was born into a scholarly family.
M2.zh: 他出生于书香门第。

D16.en: At 7, he said, 'Guanyin Pavilion's Buddha blesses the people.'
D16.zh: 7岁时，他说：“观音阁观音大佛保佑黎民平安。”

D11.en: At about 9, he recited a poem: 'Mountain thin, stream rises; snow clears, sky colder.'
D11.zh: 约9岁时，他吟诗：“山瘦溪水涨，雪后天更寒。”

D4.en: At 7, he used a bamboo pole to free a dragonfly from a spider web.
D4.zh: 7岁时，他找来竹竿挑开蜘蛛网救出蜻蜓。

D9.en: At 18, he continued as class monitor.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他继续当班长。

D7.en: At age 3, he was pulled down by an elder for bumping into ancestral tablets.
D7.zh: 3岁时因冲撞祖宗牌位被长者揪耳朵扯下

D2.en: At 5, he studied under private tutors Cai Shaoshan and Cai Gongheng, hired by his father.
D2.zh: 5岁时，他父亲赵炜如请来蔡少珊和蔡拱恒兄弟做他的私塾先生。

D3.en: At 7, he picked up a thin book 'The Hundred Parables Sutra' to read.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他拿起一本薄薄的《百喻经》看。

D12.en: At 4, he moved with his parents from Anqing to Siqianhe.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他随父母从安庆搬到寺前河。

D3.en: At 18, he read Confucius, Mencius, and the classics, but still remembered Zhuangzi.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他阅读孔孟及《书》《诗》《易》，但仍不忘《庄子》。

D12.en: He was born at seven months, fell ill on day 11, and was frail and pale as a child.
D12.zh: 他七个月出生，第十一天大病，从小瘦弱面白。

M6.en: At 17, he pledged himself to serve God wholly and utterly, and never went back on his word.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他立誓要完全彻底地侍奉神，并且从未食言。

M4.en: At 17, he made a vow to God to serve Him for life and never break it.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他在一次青年奉献呼召中向神许愿要终身侍奉祂，决不反悔。

D13a.en: At 17, he went to the principal, confessed his deeds, and explained why he confessed.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他去见校长，供认自己所做的事，并说明认罪的原因。

D5.en: At 17, he faced expulsion that would destroy his hope of a scholarship and study abroad.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他面临开除，这会毁掉他获得奖学金和出国留学的希望。

D6.en: At 10, he listened with a boy's inquisitive ears to political talk.
D6.zh: 10岁时，他好奇地听着政治谈话。

M3.en: He read the life of Abraham Lincoln and was encouraged that a humble background could achieve greatness.
M3.zh: 他读了林肯传记，受到鼓舞，认识到出身卑微也能成就伟大。

D1.en: At 16-17, he realized confessing his sin might end his college hopes, but he knew he must confess at any cost.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他意识到认罪可能会毁掉上大学的前途，但他知道必须不惜代价认罪。

D13b.en: As a baby, his parents dedicated him to God's service.
D13b.zh: 他幼年时，父母将他奉献给神。

D11.en: As a small child, he was full of ideas for games to play.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他满脑子都是玩游戏的点子。

M1.en: At 9-12, he kept himself at the top of his class and first in examinations.
M1.zh: 9到12岁时，他保持全班第一，考试也第一。

D9.en: He arranged prayer meetings, Bible studies, and a social service group.
D9.zh: 他安排了祷告会、查经班和一个志愿服务团体。

D7.en: At 16, resentful over an unjust beating, he at first refused to go with the rest of the family.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他因一次不公正的责打而愤愤不平，起初拒绝跟家人一起走。

D2.en: At 14, he was taught to pray, study the Bible, and keep a spiritual diary.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他被人教导祷告、读经和记灵修日记。

D3.en: At 12-15, he received a solid grounding in the Christian life at Hongdong and showed unusual promise.
D3.zh: 12到15岁时，他在洪洞的基督教生活方面打下了坚实基础，并展现出非凡潜力。

D12.en: He felt obligated to stop his education to help his mother at home.
D12.zh: 他感到有责任辍学，在家帮助母亲。

M7.en: At 6-12, he wanted to be a performer to make people laugh and delight the crowd.
M7.zh: 6到12岁时，他想当表演者，让人们笑、取悦观众。

M6.en: At 14, he hungered to be a monk for the enormous freedom he envisioned.
M6.zh: 14岁时，他渴望成为僧人，因为他想象到巨大的自由。

D17.en: At 10-12, his mother took in girls like a mother hen; he observed.
D17.zh: 10到12岁时，他母亲像母鸡护小鸡一样收留了这些女孩，他看在眼里。

D10.en: At about 9, he kept waking up in the middle of the night in terror.
D10.zh: 大约9岁时，他经常半夜惊醒，感到恐惧。

D14.en: At 6-12, he loved the strange goings-on and thought it wonderful to be a monk helping the deceased.
D14.zh: 6到12岁时，他喜欢这些奇怪的事，觉得当僧人帮助死者从这个世界到下一个世界很美妙。

D13b.en: At 12-14, he built fortifications and caves for bombs and fences around soldiers' billets because the Japanese insisted.
D13b.zh: 12到14岁时，他修建了防御工事和防空洞，以及士兵营房周围的围栏，因为日本人坚持。

D13a.en: At 13 in 1943, he was grateful his mother wasn't going to force him to marry her.
D13a.zh: 1943年他13岁时，他感激母亲没有强迫他娶她。

D18.en: At 13, he served cold roasted-wheat tea from a big ceramic pot.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他从大陶罐里倒出冷的大麦茶招待客人。

D11.en: At 14, he was very excited, weaving a beautiful dream of his future life on Wolf Mountain.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他非常兴奋，编织着在狼山未来生活的美梦。

M1.en: At 11-12, he felt he must study to have a future.
M1.zh: 11到12岁时，他觉得不读书就没有前途。

D15.en: At 10, he fell from a tree and fractured his tailbone.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他从树上摔下来，尾骨骨折。

D4.en: At about 15, he cultivated crops at Dasheng, including rice.
D4.zh: 大约15岁时，他在大圣种庄稼，包括水稻。

D9.en: At 12-13, he whispered answers through a window to help a student pass an exam.
D9.zh: 12到13岁时，他透过窗户小声告诉答案，帮一个学生通过了考试。

D7.en: At 10, he bumped into his mother when he was supposed to be in school.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他本该在学校，却意外撞见了母亲。

D2.en: At 6-12, he grew to love chanting and would often chant on his own while doing chores or walking.
D2.zh: 6到12岁时，他爱上了唱诵，经常在做家务或走路时自己唱诵。

D20X.en: At 12, he became a novice monk.
D20X.zh: 12岁时，他出家当了沙弥。

M5.en: He accepted the words and thought success is not urgent.
M5.zh: 他当下承担了那句话，心想有没有出息不急于一时。

M8.en: He worshipped Guanyin Bodhisattva daily.
M8.zh: 他每日礼拜观音菩萨。

M6.en: At 17, he vowed to propagate Buddhism and repay his teacher's kindness.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他默默发愿将来要弘法利生以报师恩。

D20.en: His mother gave birth to four children: Guohua, Suhua, Hsing Yun, and Guomin.
D20.zh: 他母亲生了四个孩子：国华、素华、他和国民。

D10.en: He recalled his mother being most anxious and nervous.
D10.zh: 他回忆母亲最着急紧张的一次。

D1.en: At 17, ill with malaria, he received half a bowl of pickled vegetables from his master and vowed to dedicate his life to Buddhism.
D1.zh: 十七岁染疟疾，师父送来半碗咸菜，他发愿以身心奉献佛教。

D13b.en: Due to the war, he became a monk at Qixia Mountain.
D13b.zh: 由于战争，他在栖霞山剃发出家。

D13a.en: At 12, his mother said no to his becoming a monk, but he insisted he had already promised.
D13a.zh: 十二岁时母亲反对他出家，他说已承诺无法反悔。

D18.en: At 5-6, he cleaned the house and picked up cow dung to sell for coins to help his parents.
D18.zh: 五六岁时打扫环境、捡牛粪换铜钱减轻父母负担。

D5.en: At 16-17, he played ball so intently that he was almost expelled when caught by a teacher.
D5.zh: 十六七岁时打球太入神被老师逮到，差点被开除。

D11.en: At 18, while at Jiaoshan Buddhist College, he created his own magazine titled 'My Garden', published monthly.
D11.zh: 十八岁在焦山佛学院编了一份专属杂志《我的园地》，每月出版。

M1.en: At 12-15, he thought time would prove his worth, not needing immediate recognition.
M1.zh: 十二到十五岁时心想时间会证明出息，不急于一时。

D15.en: At 9, heartbroken over a lost pet pigeon, he attempted suicide by drowning but floated to the other shore.
D15.zh: 九岁时因小白鸽飞失投河自杀，却顺水浮到彼岸。

D7.en: At 10, he asked if his mother, grandmother, and sister could join him in becoming a monk; when told no, he hesitated and refused.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他问妈妈、外婆、姐姐能否一起去当和尚，被告知不能后，他犹豫耍赖说不去了。

D3.en: At 15-17, he managed the library and read books that teachers and seniors had borrowed.
D3.zh: 15到17岁时，他管理图书馆，专看老师学长借过的书。

D13a.en: He supported land reform and praised the Party for building schools, hospitals, and roads.
D13a.zh: 他支持土地改革，赞扬党修建学校、医院和道路。

D9.en: At age 7, he was brought to worship Buddha before being confirmed as the new Panchen, attended by over 100,000 people.
D9.zh: 7岁时，他被带去拜佛，随后被确认为新的班禅，有超过10万人参加仪式。

D7.en: He was advised to throw in his lot with the Communist party.
D7.zh: 他被建议与共产党共命运。

D2.en: He taught himself Chinese using a small, worn dictionary.
D2.zh: 他靠一本破旧的小字典自学了中文。

D12.en: He was born to the village headman's wife, Sonam Dolma.
D12.zh: 他出生在村长妻子索南卓玛家中。

D15.en: He shared his enthusiasm for riding, fencing, and target practice.
D15.zh: 他分享了对骑马、击剑和打靶的热情。

D3.en: At 13-14, he demonstrated precocious learning and was enthroned as abbot the next year.
D3.zh: 13到14岁时，他表现出早慧的学识，次年升任住持。

M6.en: At 18, she was convinced her calling was to be a missionary.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她确信自己的使命是成为传教士。

D17.en: As a child, she helped her mother wash a sore-covered alcoholic woman and visited a dying mother of six.
D17.zh: 幼年时，她帮母亲清洗一个满身疮痍的酗酒女人，并探望一位临终的六个孩子的母亲。

D14.en: On Christmas 1928, she sang carols around a paper crib under a starry canopy.
D14.zh: 1928年圣诞节，她在星光下围着纸做的马槽唱圣诞颂歌。

D13b.en: At 18, she left for Ireland in September 1928, leaving a photo with her aunt.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，她于1928年9月前往爱尔兰，把这张照片留给了姨妈。

D5.en: After Christmas 1928, her ship landed in Colombo en route to Bengal.
D5.zh: 1928年圣诞节后，她的船在科伦坡靠岸，前往孟加拉的洛雷托传教区。

D11.en: Before 18, she composed poems and had two articles published in the local newspaper.
D11.zh: 18岁前，她创作诗歌，并在当地报纸上发表了两篇文章。

D4.en: At 18, she prayed that her weight was light enough not to cause a man to suffer.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她祈祷自己的体重足够轻，以免让那个男人太痛苦。

D9.en: At 18, she was a born organizer and something of a driving force in her activities.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她天生是个组织者，在参与的活动中有一定的推动力。

D6.en: As a small child, she attended elementary school within the Sacred Heart Church.
D6.zh: 幼年时，她在圣心教堂内的小学上学。

D2.en: At 18, she and another postulant were asked never to speak to each other in their own language, a directive they faithfully kept.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她和另一位初学生被要求永远不要用母语交谈，她们忠实地遵守了这一指示。

D3.en: At 15-18, she was fond of reading, supplied with books by a library initiated by Father Jambrekovic.
D3.zh: 15到18岁间，她喜欢读书，由Jambrekovic神父创办的图书馆为她提供书籍。

D20X.en: At 11-12, his school closed and his teacher left the village.
D20X.zh: 11到12岁时，他的学校关闭了，老师离开了村子。

M5.en: At 11-12, he was irritated that most local classical texts were about Chinese, not Vietnamese history.
M5.zh: 11到12岁时，他恼火地发现当地大部分经典文本讲的是中国历史而非越南历史。

D8.en: At 11-12, an older student tied a string to him to keep an eye on him.
D8.zh: 11到12岁时，一个年长学生在他身上系了根绳子以便看管他。

D19.en: At 11-12, he tried to memorize key passages.
D19.zh: 11到12岁时，他尝试背诵关键段落。

M4.en: At 11-12, he memorized passages to quote to friends back in Kim Lien.
M4.zh: 11到12岁时，他背诵段落以便向金莲的老朋友们引用。

D16.en: At 11-12, he memorized passages to quote to friends.
D16.zh: 11到12岁时，他背诵段落以便向朋友们引用。

D10.en: At 10-11, he joined others in weeping bitterly over their sacrifice.
D10.zh: 10到11岁时，他和其他人一起为他们的牺牲痛哭。

D1.en: At 18, he arrived and took his seat just as the bell rang for class.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他在上课铃响时赶到并坐到了自己的座位上。

D14.en: At 18, he cut classes and gave haircuts to passersby with friends.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他逃课，和朋友一起给路人理发，有时未经请求。

D13b.en: At 17, his father enrolled him in the National Academy in Hué.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他父亲把他送进了顺化的国学院。

D13a.en: At 11, his father gave him the new name Nguyen Tat Thanh.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他父亲给他起了新名字阮必成。

D18.en: At 11-12, he walked to Vinh to buy books.
D18.zh: 11到12岁时，他步行到荣市买书。

M1.en: At 17, he finished a two-year course in one year.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他一年内完成了两年课程。

D15.en: At 10-11, he climbed hills, especially Mount Chung.
D15.zh: 10到11岁时，他爬山，最难忘的是钟山。

D4.en: At 11-12, he was taught to use the forge and taken bird hunting.
D4.zh: 11到12岁时，他学用铁炉，还被带去猎鸟。

D9.en: At 11-12, he helped serve guests who came to lecture.
D9.zh: 11到12岁时，他帮忙招待来演讲的客人。

D7.en: At 18, he turned his hat upside down as a sign to overthrow the status quo.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他把帽子倒戴，表示需要推翻现状。

D6.en: At 15-16, he had his first political involvement.
D6.zh: 15到16岁时，他第一次参与政治。

D3.en: At 11-12, he preferred reading Chinese classics like Romance of the Three Kingdoms.
D3.zh: 11到12岁时，他更喜欢读《三国演义》这样的中国经典。

D19.en: He began his journal at age 14 and kept it for life.
D19.zh: 他14岁时开始写日记，并坚持了一辈子。

M6.en: His aim in life is to become a holy priest.
M6.zh: 他的人生目标是成为一名圣洁的神父。

D17.en: He felt orphaned by the death, writing 'I am left an orphan to my immense loss'.
D17.zh: 他因死亡感到成了孤儿，写道：‘我成了孤儿，损失巨大。’

D10.en: At 9, he felt his heart endangered and left quickly to hide his emotion.
D10.zh: 9岁时，他感到心脏危险，赶紧离开以掩饰内心情感。

D1.en: He had to translate Caesar's Gallic War and was slapped or knelt as punishment for errors.
D1.zh: 他必须翻译凯撒的《高卢战记》，出错会被打耳光或罚跪。

D14.en: At age 5 or 6, he learned his first poem by heart.
D14.zh: 他五六岁时背下了第一首诗。

D13b.en: At 11, he arrived as a boarder at the seminary.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他作为寄宿生进入神学院。

D5.en: He crept up a creaking staircase to see a dead old woman.
D5.zh: 他蹑手蹑脚地爬上吱吱作响的楼梯去看一位死去的老年妇女。

D4.en: At 8, he crept up a creaking staircase to see a dead old woman.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他爬上吱吱作响的楼梯去看一个死去的老年妇女。

D7.en: At 17, he was rebuked for behaving arrogantly during vacation and had to humble himself.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他因假期中行为傲慢受到训斥，不得不谦卑自己。

D2.en: At 17, he prayed for his will to be in perfect harmony with God's will.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他祈祷自己的意志与天主的意志完全一致。

D3.en: At 10, he showed a taste and aptitude for study that previous teachers had failed to awaken.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他表现出对学习的兴趣和才能，这是之前的老师未能激发的。

D12.en: At 7, he was already serving early morning Mass daily.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他已经每天清晨做弥撒服务。

D20X.en: He had no special friendships until his student years.
D20X.zh: 他在学生时代之前没有特别的朋友。

M5.en: He saw theology as offering the prospect of achieving something special of his own.
M5.zh: 他认为神学提供了取得自己独特成就的前景。

D8.en: He fended off unreasonable demands and won particular respect.
D8.zh: 他挡开不合理的要求，赢得了特别的尊重。

D19.en: As a small child, he followed a disciplined daily pattern.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他遵循有规律的日常作息。

D17.en: He chivalrously helped and protected the two little girls.
D17.zh: 他骑士般地帮助和保护了两个小女孩。

M4.en: At 17, he believed he was acting in the service of a state of which he approved.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他相信自己是在为一个他认可的国家服务。

D16.en: At 16, he asked what would become of Germany if its best leaders were killed.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他问如果德国最优秀的领袖被杀，德国会变成什么样。

D1.en: At around 6, he wanted to do everything the elder children could.
D1.zh: 大约6岁时，他想做所有大孩子能做的事。

D14.en: At 15, he went to the first evangelistic meeting of his life.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他参加了人生中第一次布道会。

D13b.en: At 17, he followed family tradition by beginning university with a year in Tübingen.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他遵循家族传统，大学第一年在图宾根度过。

D5.en: He said he would go for the first few days but would come back if his parents definitely objected.
D5.zh: 他说他会先去几天，但如果父母坚决反对就回来。

M1.en: He wanted to do everything the elder children could.
M1.zh: 他想做所有大孩子能做的事。

D15.en: He got used to being the strongest and most agile of the three youngest children.
D15.zh: 他习惯了成为三个最小的孩子中最强壮、最敏捷的一个。

D4.en: He said they were building a subterranean cave and passageway from the arbor to the cave.
D4.zh: 他说他们在建一个地下洞穴和通道，从凉亭通到洞穴。

D9.en: He noted that all the Hedgehogs up to the seventh term were going, and it would cause difficulties if one fewer showed up.
D9.zh: 他指出所有到第七学期的Hedgehogs都去，少一个人会出现麻烦。

D7.en: He described someone as very narrow-mindedly right-wing, and noted the only thing he had forgotten was his swastika.
D7.zh: 他形容某人非常狭隘地右翼，并指出那人唯一忘了的是他的万字符。

D6.en: He changed schools as a sixth-grader around Easter 1919 to the Grunewald Gymnasium.
D6.zh: 他在1919年复活节前后作为六年级学生转学到Grunewald文理中学。

D2.en: He spent almost the entire day sitting in the theological college.
D2.zh: 他几乎整天都坐在神学院里。

D3.en: At 18, he spun through Rome like a cyclone, absorbing its culture.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他旋风般游历罗马，尽情吸收其文化。

M6.en: At 16, on the eve of his confirmation, he resolved to become a theologian to understand the creed.
M6.zh: 16岁时，在坚信礼前夕，他决心成为神学家以理解信条。

D10.en: At 13, he was still sore about a punishment, but not so much.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他仍对惩罚感到难过，但没那么严重。

D14.en: At 13, in solemn silence, he shouted, shrieked, and barked loudly, drawing everyone's attention.
D14.zh: 13岁时，在肃静中，他大喊、尖叫并大声吠叫，引起所有人注意。

D13a.en: At 18, he began studying in Berne with his father's kind but earnest guidance.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他在父亲善意而认真的指导下开始在伯尔尼学习。

D5.en: As a schoolboy, he joined street fights between rival groups.
D5.zh: 他上学时加入了街头斗殴，在敌对团体之间打架。

D11.en: At 15, he wrote his longest play, Leonardo of Montenuova, or Freedom and Love.
D11.zh: 15岁时，他写了最长的剧本《蒙特努奥瓦的莱昂纳多，或自由与爱情》。

D15.en: At 18, he climbed the Titlis and the Urirotstock on a school trip.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他在学校旅行中攀登了Titlis和Urirotstock。

D4.en: As a child and young adult, he pursued interests from music to military history.
D4.zh: 幼年到青年时期，他追求从音乐到军事史的各种兴趣。

D9.en: At 16, he produced offerings for 'Patria' and sometimes appeared as president.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他为《Patria》供稿，有时还担任主席。

D7.en: At 13, his teacher pulled out two of his hairs, which he put in his museum.
D7.zh: 13岁时，老师拔了他两根头发，他把它们放进了自己的博物馆。

D6.en: At 18, he and his companions proudly walked and pranced in their caps around the city.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他和同伴们骄傲地戴着帽子在城里走来走去，蹦蹦跳跳。

D3.en: At 15-16, during classes, he decided to study theology.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，他在上课期间决定学习神学。

D12.en: At 15-16, he underwent confirmation instruction under Robert Aeschbacher.
D12.zh: 15到16岁时，他接受了Robert Aeschbacher的坚信礼指导。

M6.en: At 17, he had a confession experience that unlocked his vocation.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他在告解中经历了一次启发圣召的体验。

D14.en: As a teenager, he loved to dance and was good at the tango.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢跳舞，探戈跳得很好。

D13b.en: As a small child, he hated being inside the classroom and always wanted to be outside.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他讨厌待在教室里，总想在外面。

D13a.en: At 13, the family priest hastily found places for him and his two siblings at Salesian boarding schools.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，家庭神父匆忙为他和他两个兄弟姐妹在慈幼会寄宿学校找到了位置。

D18.en: At 15-16, he got a job in a food laboratory.
D18.zh: 15到16岁时，他在一家食品实验室找到工作。

D11.en: At 13, he gave catechesis on the sacrament.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他讲授圣事要理。

D15.en: At age 6-12, he liked to kick around with friends but wasn't much good because he had flat feet.
D15.zh: 6到12岁时，他喜欢和朋友踢球，但踢得不太好，因为他是扁平足。

D4.en: At 13-14, he started a six-year vocational course at Escuela Nacional de Educación Técnica, earning a chemical technician diploma.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他进入国立技术教育学校开始六年制职业课程，获得化学技术员文凭。

D6.en: As a small child, he played with many playmates in the local square.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他在当地广场和许多玩伴一起玩耍。

D2.en: At 6-12, he rose hours before classmates to serve Mass for a Salesian priest.
D2.zh: 6到12岁间，他比同学早起床几小时，为一位慈幼会神父辅祭。

D3.en: At 16-17, he recognized his vocation during Confession on 21 September.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，他在9月21日的告解中认出了自己的圣召。

D12.en: At 13, he was shocked when his father said it was time for him to start work.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他听到父亲说该开始工作了，感到震惊。

D16.en: He was lured into performing in an anti-Semitic play as a teenager.
D16.zh: 他十几岁时被诱骗参演了一部反犹戏剧。

D10.en: At age 12, he said 'Such was God's will'.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他说‘这是上帝的旨意’。

D1.en: At age 13, he obtained sponsorship to found a chapter of the Marian Sodality.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他获得赞助成立了圣母会的分会。

D14.en: In June 1933 at age 13, he had a nonspeaking part in a summer pageant.
D14.zh: 1933年6月13岁时，他在夏季庆典中扮演了一个无台词角色。

D13a.en: He entered a seminary, believing it was his late mother's wish.
D13a.zh: 他进入神学院，认为这是已故母亲的愿望。

D5.en: At age 6-7, he and friends pulled a sword from its scabbard, fell, and tripped over a policeman.
D5.zh: 6到7岁时，他和朋友拔出剑，失去平衡摔倒，绊倒了警察。

M1.en: He graduated with mostly As, though he stumbled in chemistry, physics, and history.
M1.zh: 他毕业时成绩大多是A，但在化学、物理和历史上有过失误。

D15.en: He did his national service on a road construction job in Zubrzyca Górna.
D15.zh: 他在Zubrzyca Górna做修路工，完成了国民服役。

D4.en: At age 12, he helped build sets and absorbed the rehearsal atmosphere.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他帮忙搭建布景，沉浸在排练氛围中。

D9.en: At 16, Kotlarczyk offered him his first professional adult acting job.
D9.zh: 16岁时，Kotlarczyk给了他第一个专业成人演员工作。

D6.en: He joined the School Drama Circle, directed by the Polish language teacher.
D6.zh: 他加入了学校戏剧社，由波兰语老师指导。

D2.en: As a child, he served as altar boy for multiple services daily.
D2.zh: 他小时候每天多次担任辅祭，超出常规。

D3.en: At 12, he was dazzled by an evening at the theater.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他被一场戏剧之夜迷住了。

D12.en: He was born on May 18, 1920, in Wadowice.
D12.zh: 他于1920年5月18日出生在瓦多维采。

M8.en: At 18, he prayed at Santa Sabina and linked it to the morning after his crisis in the room.
M8.zh: 18岁时，他在Santa Sabina祈祷，并将此与他在房间危机后的早晨联系起来。

D19.en: At 16-17, he wrote Audenesque lines in the poem 'St. John Baptist' and throughout the Portsmouth Road section.
D19.zh: 16到17岁时，他在诗'St. John Baptist'和Portsmouth Road部分写了奥登风格的诗句。

D17.en: At 11, they fattened him up with butter and milk and gave him love and care.
D17.zh: 11岁时，他们用黄油和牛奶把他养胖，并给了他关爱。

M2.en: He mentions some shuffling of investments in the trust after 1930.
M2.zh: 他提到1930年后信托基金的投资有所调整。

M4.en: At 14-15, he went out on maneuvers with a book in his pocket.
M4.zh: 14到15岁时，他外出演习时口袋里揣着一本书。

D16.en: At 15, he defended the right of Indians to rule their own country in a debate against the rugby captain and head prefect.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他在辩论中反对橄榄球队长和级长，捍卫印度人统治自己国家的权利。

D10.en: At 12, he was taken to task for his own bad language when staying with the Privats.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他在Privats家做客时因说脏话被责备。

D1.en: At 15, he was in the fifth form three months after arriving at Oakham.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他到达Oakham三个月后就升入了五年级。

D14.en: Before he could talk, he chanted 'Dah-hou!' meant to be church bells of Prades.
D14.zh: 还不会说话时，他就哼唱'Dah-hou!'模仿Prades的教堂钟声。

D13b.en: As a small child, he was constantly punished for his inability to grasp multiplication and division.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他因完全学不会乘除法而不断受罚。

D13a.en: From age 8 to 16, his godfather and guardian was Dr. Bennett.
D13a.zh: 8到16岁间，他的教父兼监护人是Bennett博士。

D11.en: At 1-2, he answered his own question with 'Oo-oo-oo' supposed to be the wind.
D11.zh: 1到2岁时，他自问自答，用'Oo-oo-oo'模仿风声。

M1.en: He kept the really important schoolboy rules, appearing to have an uncanny knack of coming first and winning prizes.
M1.zh: 他遵守了真正重要的校规，似乎有一种不可思议的本领，总能得第一并获奖。

D15.en: At 14, he remembered with pride having made 32 not out for the school's Second XI.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他自豪地记得在校二队打出32分未出局。

D4.en: At 15-16, he learned to ride a horse, seeing it as an analogy to continuing conversion.
D4.zh: 15到16岁时，他学习骑马，将其视为持续皈依的类比。

D9.en: At 18, he argued Archibald MacLeish's pacifist ideas and decided he might be a communist.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他争论Archibald MacLeish的和平主义观点，并决定自己可能是个共产主义者。

D7.en: At 18, his grandfather took him aside to accuse him of being sarcastic and hurting feelings.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他祖父把他拉到一边，指责他挖苦人、伤害感情。

D6.en: At 17, he saw an attractive brown-haired girl he remembered from the other end of the British Isles.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他看到一个迷人的棕发女孩，记得她来自不列颠群岛的另一端。

D2.en: At 17, he spoke of taking more than one journal with him when he left Oakham in December 1932.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他说1932年12月离开奥克姆时带走了不止一本日记。

D3.en: As a small child, he knew the wind and rain, held out his hands to feel them, and talked to 'Monsieur Wind'.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他认识风和雨，伸出手去感受它们，并和‘风先生’说话。

D12.en: At 15, he visited his father, who was sitting up in bed drawing subjects he couldn't recognize.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他去看望父亲，父亲正坐在床上画他认不出的东西。

M5.en: At 17, he published the slogan 'Wählt Hitler' in a school magazine.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他在校刊上发表了口号'Wählt Hitler'。

D8.en: At 17, he wrote 'Wählt Hitler' in The Oakhamian.
D8.zh: 17岁时，他在The Oakhamian上写了'Wählt Hitler'。

M3.en: As a preteen, he was fascinated by his father, thrilled by his sermons, and awestruck by visiting synod officials.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，他对父亲着迷，为父亲的布道激动，对来访的教会官员感到敬畏。

M6.en: In his preteen years, he made up his mind to become a minister.
M6.zh: 十几岁时，他下定决心要成为一名牧师。

D16.en: He lost a school debate but was praised by a judge who thought he was superior.
D16.zh: 他在学校辩论中输了，但一位评委认为他表现更优并称赞了他。

D10.en: At 7, he reached for cheese with his left hand, was hit by his father, and ran upstairs enraged.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他用左手拿奶酪，被父亲打了一下，愤怒地跑上楼。

D14.en: Between 15 and 18, he took part in a Shakespearean drama.
D14.zh: 15到18岁间，他参加了一部莎士比亚戏剧。

D13b.en: At 14-15, he left Lincoln High after ninth grade to enter a synod boarding school for ministers.
D13b.zh: 14到15岁时，他九年级后离开林肯高中，进入教会寄宿学校准备当牧师。

M1.en: He scored in the 90s, highest in Latin, with averages of 95 and 97.
M1.zh: 他考了90多分，拉丁语最高，平均分95和97。

D15.en: Between 16 and 18, he had a run-in with a bull at a relative's farm.
D15.zh: 16到18岁间，他在亲戚的农场与一头公牛发生了冲突。

D9.en: He requested admission to the Evangelical Pro-Seminar, promising to obey all instructions.
D9.zh: 他请求进入福音预科班，并承诺遵守所有指示和要求。

D6.en: He organized the Lincoln Lyceum, a campus literary society that published Keryx.
D6.zh: 他组织了林肯学社，一个出版Keryx的校园文学社团。

D2.en: He had private confirmation instruction three times, twice with siblings and once alone.
D2.zh: 他接受了三次私人坚信礼指导，两次与兄弟姐妹一起，最后一次独自一人。

D3.en: His study of Greek progressed from Homer's Odyssey and Plato's Apology to New Testament Greek.
D3.zh: 他的希腊语学习从荷马《奥德赛》和柏拉图《申辩篇》进展到新约希腊语。

D7.en: He had no interest in following his father's path into the ministry.
D7.zh: 他对跟随父亲进入牧师行业没有兴趣。

M3.en: At 17-18, he wrote home telling his mother how much he longed to be like this one or that one.
M3.zh: 17到18岁时，他写信回家告诉母亲他多么渴望像这个或那个人。

M7.en: At 16, he said, 'I'm a changed boy.'
M7.zh: 16岁时，他说：“我变成了一个不一样的男孩。”

M4.en: At 16, he made his decision for Christ.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他做出了信基督的决定。

D16.en: He was introduced as a new convert who wanted to tell what Jesus had done for him.
D16.zh: 他被介绍为一个新信徒，想要讲述耶稣为他做的事。

D1.en: At 18, he lost all his first week's earnings but refused to ask home for help.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他第一周挣的钱全丢了，但拒绝向家里求助。

D14.en: At 18, he preached his first four real sermons.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他讲了最初的四场真正的布道。

D13b.en: At 10, he was required to memorize the Westminster Shorter Catechism.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他需要背诵《威斯敏斯特小要理问答》。
