D13a.en: At 18, his deep reluctance to challenge authority made it hard to act on his inclinations.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他极度不愿挑战权威，这让他很难按自己的意愿行事。

D18.en: At 18, he spent the summer selling Fuller Brush products in the Carolinas.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他整个夏天都在卡罗来纳州推销Fuller Brush产品。

D5.en: At 16, he drove a car right up onto the sidewalk in downtown Charlotte.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他在夏洛特市中心把车开上了人行道。

D15.en: At 13-15, he played first base on his high school team and some innings with a semi-pro team.
D15.zh: 13到15岁时，他在高中棒球队打一垒，还在一支半职业队打过几局。

D4.en: At 18, he worked as a bellhop, waiter, caddy, and high-speed dishwasher.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他做过门童、服务员、球童和高速洗碗工。

D7.en: At 15, he dismissed the revivalists as fanatics.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他称那些复兴布道者为狂热分子。

D6.en: At 18, he spent evenings in Bible study and prayer with friends.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他晚上与朋友们一起研经祷告。

D3.en: At 17-18, he studied others' strengths and weaknesses to find his own service.
D3.zh: 17到18岁间，他研究他人的优缺点，以找到自己服侍的方式。

D17.en: He dipped his bread in soup, ate it, and gave the crumbs to ants around the building.
D17.zh: 他把面包蘸汤吃，然后把面包屑喂给楼周围的蚂蚁。

D16.en: He answered all of Shaikh Emin's difficult questions correctly and without hesitation.
D16.zh: 他正确且毫不犹豫地回答了谢赫·艾敏的所有难题。

D1.en: After about a year, he set off again to continue his studies full-time.
D1.zh: 大约一年后，他再次出发，全职继续学业。

D5.en: He showed proverbial courage and boldness from youth to maturity.
D5.zh: 他从青年到成年都表现出众所周知的勇气和大胆。

D11.en: At 13-14, he decided to sit by the Sirat bridge to meet the Prophet and kiss his hand.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，他决定坐在绥拉特桥旁，等待先知经过时亲吻他的手。

M1.en: At 14, he received his diploma and started teaching.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他获得了文凭并开始教书。

D15.en: He and his students hiked, wrestled, and played sports.
D15.zh: 他和学生们一起远足、摔跤和做运动。

D7.en: He challenged older students and corrected scholars.
D7.zh: 他挑战年长的学生并纠正学者。

D3.en: He grew up and was educated in districts peripheral to the Ottoman Empire.
D3.zh: 他在奥斯曼帝国边缘的地区长大并接受教育。

D6.en: High school boys started visiting him, including Abdullah Yegin.
D6.zh: 一些高中男生开始拜访他，其中包括Abdullah Yegin。

M3.en: At 16, he became a disciple of Nietzsche after reading a book.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他读了一本书后成为尼采的信徒。

D10.en: As a small child, he wanted to see his mother and felt infinite deprivation.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他想见母亲，感到无尽的失落。

D14.en: At 18, he published four essays on Vienna's avant-garde poets in a Warsaw journal.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他在华沙文学杂志上发表了四篇关于维也纳先锋诗人的文章。

D13a.en: As a child living with his grandparents, he was well rooted in Judaism externally.
D13a.zh: 幼年时与祖父母同住，他外在扎根于犹太教。

D11.en: At 16, he came across Nietzsche's The Birth of Tragedy and became a sick disciple.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他读到尼采的《悲剧的诞生》，成为病态的信徒。

D15.en: He wandered through mountain and dale, punctuated by woods and lakes.
D15.zh: 他漫步于山间和溪谷，点缀着树林和湖泊。

D7.en: He felt a raging hatred for the official morality and education.
D7.zh: 他对官方道德和教育感到极度憎恶。

D2.en: He received private lessons in English, French, German, and Jewish subjects.
D2.zh: 他接受了英语、法语、德语以及犹太科目的私人课程。

D3.en: He maintained a lifelong interest in Chinese philosophy.
D3.zh: 他终生对中国哲学保持兴趣。

D12.en: At age 3, he was sent to live with his paternal grandparents in Lemberg.
D12.zh: 他3岁时被送到伦贝格的祖父母家生活。

D13b.en: He studied in a yeshiva in Yekaterinoslav from 1909 to 1915.
D13b.zh: 他于1909年至1915年在叶卡捷琳诺斯拉夫的一所犹太学校学习。

D5.en: He coordinated a complex transatlantic banking arrangement to move funds from America to help Jews in Stalinist Russia.
D5.zh: 他协调了一项复杂的跨大西洋银行安排，将资金从美国转移以帮助斯大林主义俄国的犹太人。

D11.en: As a small child, the vision of future Redemption began to take form in his imagination.
D11.zh: 幼年时，未来救赎的景象开始在他的想象中成形。

D3.en: He had an early fascination with astronomy, with a map of the universe in his bedroom.
D3.zh: 他早年对天文学着迷，卧室里挂着宇宙地图。

M7.en: As a child, he stood by the road to wave goodbye to convoys going to war.
M7.zh: 小时候，他站在路边向开赴战场的车队挥手告别。

D17.en: At 14-15, he gave away one of his two sweaters to a boy with none and swore his sister to silence.
D17.zh: 14到15岁时，他把自己两件毛衣中的一件送给了一个没有毛衣的男孩，并让姐姐保密。

D16.en: At 18, he joined the debating team and won the school's prize for best speaker in 1949.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他加入了辩论队，并在1949年获得了学校的最佳演讲者奖。

D10.en: He said it was one of the worst nights of his life.
D10.zh: 他说那是他一生中最糟糕的夜晚之一。

D1.en: As a small child, he became very ill and later it was found he had polio.
D1.zh: 幼年时，他病得很重，后来发现他得了小儿麻痹症。

D14.en: At about 14, he was fascinated by movies, especially Stormy Weather with African-American stars.
D14.zh: 大约14岁时，他对电影着迷，尤其是由非裔美国人主演的《暴风雨天气》。

D13b.en: At 12, he renewed his baptismal vows and was confirmed in his faith.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他重申了洗礼誓言并接受了坚信礼。

D18.en: He ran errands for his parents, buying meat or the newspaper.
D18.zh: 他为父母跑腿，买肉或报纸。

D5.en: As a small child, his flannel pajamas caught fire while warming himself at a fire.
D5.zh: 幼年时，他在火边取暖时法兰绒睡衣着火了。

M1.en: He was among a handful promoted to Standard Six.
M1.zh: 他是少数升入六年级的学生之一。

D15.en: A friend took him jogging and taught him to box.
D15.zh: 一个朋友带他慢跑并教他拳击。

D4.en: He caddied for white golfers at the Killarney golf course.
D4.zh: 他在基拉尼高尔夫球场为白人高尔夫球手当球童。

D7.en: He stubbornly refused to move away from the brazier despite being told not to.
D7.zh: 他固执地不肯离开火盆，尽管被劝阻。

D6.en: At about age seven, he became a server at St. Francis Anglican Church in Tshing.
D6.zh: 大约七岁时，他在Tshing的圣公会圣弗朗西斯教堂当侍者。

D2.en: In the half-yearly exams, he came top of all 250 Form I pupils.
D2.zh: 在半年考试中，他在250名一年级学生中排名第一。

D3.en: Encouraged by his father, he developed a love of reading.
D3.zh: 在父亲的鼓励下，他养成了阅读的爱好。

D12.en: His mother Matse had only primary education and domestic science training at Tiger Kloof.
D12.zh: 他母亲Matse只受过小学教育，在Tiger Kloof接受家政培训。

D13a.en: His father encouraged him to read instead.
D13a.zh: 他父亲鼓励他读书而不是做别的。

D9.en: At age 17, he helped organize the Khilafat movement in Jubalpur and brought Muslims into the Congress.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他帮助在朱巴尔普尔组织哈里发运动，并让当地穆斯林加入国大党。

D12.en: At age 15, he was forced to earn a living.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他被迫谋生。

D11.en: At age 11, he translated Qasim Amin's Al-Mir'ah al-jadidah from Arabic into Urdu.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他把卡西姆·阿明的《新女性》从阿拉伯语翻译成乌尔都语。

D13b.en: At age 7, he was sent to a local maktab run by Akhund Mollah Abolqassem.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他被送到当地一所由阿洪德·毛拉·阿博尔加塞姆开办的私塾。

D11.en: From his teens, he knew how to versify and wrote his own poetry as a pastime.
D11.zh: 从十几岁起，他就懂得作诗，并常以写诗为消遣。

D15.en: He could beat other boys in wrestling, and was the local champion at leapfrog.
D15.zh: 他摔跤能赢过其他男孩，跳背游戏是当地冠军。

D9.en: After much consultation with his brother Morteza, he decided to go to Isfahan.
D9.zh: 他与哥哥Morteza商量后，决定去伊斯法罕。

D2.en: At fifteen, he started learning Arabic grammar seriously with Morteza.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他开始认真跟Morteza学习阿拉伯语语法。

D3.en: He plunged into his father's library, devouring works of literature, poetry, and philosophy.
D3.zh: 他埋头于父亲的图书馆，大量阅读文学、诗歌和哲学作品。

D19.en: As a child aged 6-12, he kept his books in a special tin box to protect them.
D19.zh: 6到12岁时，他把书放在特制的铁盒里以防虫防尘。

M3.en: In the 1920s, he found a mentor he aspired to be like.
M3.zh: 1920年代，他找到了一位他渴望成为的导师。

M7.en: At 16, he held anti-imperialist, anti-colonial, and anti-British ideas.
M7.zh: 16岁时，他持有反帝国主义、反殖民主义和反英思想。

M6.en: At 15, he acted with a sense of destiny and mission.
M6.zh: 15岁时，他带着使命感和宿命感行事。

M2.en: At 13, his father died.
M2.zh: 13岁时，他的父亲去世了。

D20.en: He was elected president of the school's literary society.
D20.zh: 他被选为学校文学社的主席。

D16.en: At 13, he delivered patriotic speeches.
D16.zh: 13岁时，他发表爱国演讲。

D10.en: He felt relief when his mother vetoed a medical treatment for his injury.
D10.zh: 他因母亲否决了对他伤势的医疗处理而感到宽慰。

D1.en: At 6, he rejected the kuttab and returned to public school due to squalid conditions.
D1.zh: 6岁时，他拒绝了经学堂，因条件肮脏而返回公立学校。

D14.en: At 15, he began publishing poems in local literary magazines.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他开始在当地文学杂志上发表诗歌。

D13b.en: His parents gave him an amulet of Prophet Solomon to wear against Iblis.
D13b.zh: 他父母给了他一个所罗门先知护身符，用来抵御易卜劣厮。

D13a.en: At 6, his parents debated whether to send him to government school or religious kuttab.
D13a.zh: 6岁时，他父母争论送他去公立学校还是宗教经学堂。

D15.en: He and his friends playfully jumped into water from their school rooftop.
D15.zh: 他和玩伴们从学校屋顶跳进水里玩耍。

D7.en: At age 6, he rejected a choice and attended the kuttab instead.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他拒绝了某个选择，转而去了库塔布学校。

M4.en: At 16, he joined the Khilafat movement.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他加入了哈里发运动。

M3.en: He idolized his grandfather and made it his life's object to serve Him.
M3.zh: 他崇拜他的祖父，并将服务他作为人生的目标。

M6.en: He aimed his entire course of studies at fitting himself to serve the Master.
M6.zh: 他整个学习生涯的目标就是让自己能够服务 Master。

D16.en: He rebuked someone for not writing, saying he expected numerous letters full of glad-tidings.
D16.zh: 他责备某人不写信，说他期待收到许多充满喜讯的信件。

D10.en: At about twelve, he said he went away in the dark and cried for her.
D10.zh: 大约十二岁时，他说他走到暗处为她哭泣。

D1.en: His father showed a letter to 'Abdu'l-Bahá, Who then arranged for him to enter the Syrian Protestant College.
D1.zh: 他父亲把一封信给阿博都巴哈看，阿博都巴哈随后安排他进入叙利亚新教学院。

D14.en: At age five, he memorized prayers and chanted them loudly so the whole neighborhood could hear.
D14.zh: 五岁时，他背下祈祷文并大声吟唱，让整个街区都能听到。

D13a.en: 'Abdu'l-Bahá decided to send him to Beirut to attend a Catholic school as a boarder.
D13a.zh: 阿博都巴哈决定送他去贝鲁特，在一所天主教学校寄宿。

M1.en: In February 1915, he won first prize in the Freshman-Sophomore Prize Contest.
M1.zh: 1915年2月，他在新生-大二学生奖赛中获得一等奖。

D15.en: At age ten, he wore short pants, long dark stockings, a fez, a jacket, and a huge sailor collar.
D15.zh: 十岁时，他穿着短裤、深色长袜、戴菲斯帽、穿夹克，肩上搭着大水手领。

D4.en: He anxiously awaited photographs he had sent, asking for reassurance by postcard.
D4.zh: 他焦急地等待自己寄出的照片，要求用明信片回复确认。

D9.en: He played a dominant role in the activities of Bahá'í students in Beirut.
D9.zh: 他在贝鲁特的巴哈伊学生活动中发挥了主导作用。

D6.en: He sat disconsolate and forlorn on the steps of the Master's house.
D6.zh: 他坐在大师家的台阶上，沮丧而孤独。

D2.en: He wrote to a horticulturist, calling himself a lover of flowers and gardens.
D2.zh: 他写信给园艺师，自称是花卉和花园爱好者。

D3.en: He showed keen interest in Travel magazines sent from America.
D3.zh: 他对从美国寄来的《旅行》杂志表现出浓厚兴趣。

D12.en: He lived in the prison-city of Akka in the Turkish province of Syria.
D12.zh: 他住在土耳其叙利亚省的监狱城市阿卡。

D5.en: At age fifteen, Italian health inspectors declared his eyes diseased and ordered him to return to the Middle East.
D5.zh: 十五岁时，意大利卫生检查官宣布他的眼睛有病，命令他返回中东。

M6.en: At 17, he felt he had to solve the problem himself, there and then.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他感到必须自己当场解决这个问题。

D10.en: At 17, a sudden violent fear of death overtook him.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他突然被一种对死亡的强烈恐惧所笼罩。

D14.en: At 16, he hastened straight to the great Temple, and all the gates and doors stood open in mute welcome.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他直奔大庙，所有大门和内门都敞开着，无声地欢迎他。

D13b.en: At 17, he went with the Swami to Gurumurtam.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他跟随斯瓦米去了古鲁穆尔塔姆。

D13a.en: At 16, he was given an exercise to copy out three times for not learning it; it was forenoon.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他因没学会而被罚抄写三遍，时间是上午。

D18.en: At 16, he pawned his earrings to raise money for a train journey.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他典当耳环换钱坐火车。

D11.en: At 17, he dramatized death: he lay stiff as a corpse and imitated rigor mortis.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他戏剧化地表演死亡：僵硬地躺着，模仿尸僵。

D4.en: He single-handedly built a path from dawn to dusk, day after day.
D4.zh: 他独自一人从早到晚，日复一日地修路。

D9.en: He lied about needing to attend a special class to get permission to leave.
D9.zh: 他谎称要上电学特课，以获准离家。

D7.en: He threw away his remaining money and never handled money again.
D7.zh: 他扔掉剩下的钱，从此再没碰过钱。

D6.en: He once ran away to Benares and returned after a day.
D6.zh: 他有一次跑到贝拿勒斯，待了一天就回来了。

D2.en: He used pranayama (breath control) as an aid to concentration.
D2.zh: 他使用调息法来帮助专注。

D12.en: He ran away alone, unknown to his parents, in a mood of enthusiasm.
D12.zh: 他一时兴起，独自跑走，父母不知情。

M6.en: At 13, he felt a desire to become a priest, suggested by the town's mayor.
M6.zh: 13岁时，他在镇长的建议下产生了成为神父的愿望。

D13b.en: At 13, he left Ciudad Barrios for the minor seminary in San Miguel.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他离开Ciudad Barrios前往圣米格尔的小修院。

D13a.en: At 12-13, he was apprenticed to a local carpenter by his father.
D13a.zh: 12到13岁时，他被父亲送去跟当地木匠当学徒。

D18.en: At 12-13, he was apprenticed as a letter carrier and telegraph operator.
D18.zh: 12到13岁时，他当学徒做信差和电报员。

D15.en: At 13, he traveled seven hours on horseback from Ciudad Barrios.
D15.zh: 13岁时，他骑马七小时从Ciudad Barrios出发。

D4.en: At 12-13, he learned to make doors and tables.
D4.zh: 12到13岁时，他学会了做门和桌子。

D6.en: At 13, he spoke with the mayor about his desire to go to the seminary.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他和镇长谈了自己想进修院的愿望。

D2.en: At 12-13, he ran from the carpentry shop straight to church for prayers.
D2.zh: 12到13岁时，他从木工店跑出来直奔教堂祈祷。

D3.en: As a child, he learned to send and receive telegrams.
D3.zh: 他小时候学会了收发电报。

D12.en: He was born into poverty in a small, insignificant country.
D12.zh: 他出生在一个贫穷的小国。

M6.en: At 18, he had a mental realization of the Self.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他内心觉悟了真我。

M4.en: At 18, he resolved to work for Indian freedom.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他决心为印度自由而奋斗。

D1.en: At 16-17, a challenge spurred him to improve in every subject.
D1.zh: 16到17岁间，一个挑战激励他每门课都进步了。

D11.en: At 10-11, he wrote a poem 'Light' and published it in a magazine.
D11.zh: 10到11岁时，他写了诗《光》并发表在杂志上。

D2.en: At 16-17, he carried an unusually heavy load hoping to score a double jackpot: ICS selection and an Oxbridge scholarship.
D2.zh: 16到17岁时，他承担了异常沉重的课业，希望一举两得：通过ICS选拔并获得牛津剑桥奖学金。

M8.en: At 17, she felt Jesus' pardoning blood flowing over her.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她感到耶稣的宝血流淌在她身上。

D8.en: At 7, she grabbed a prize, leaving other children with barked shins and bloody noses.
D8.zh: 7岁时，她抢到了奖品，其他孩子小腿擦伤、鼻子流血。

D17.en: As a small child, she befriended animals and hovered over her guinea pigs to protect them.
D17.zh: 幼年时，她与动物为友，紧张地守护她的豚鼠。

M4.en: At 17, she converted, received Spirit baptism, and spoke in tongues.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她归信、受圣灵洗并说方言。

D16.en: At 15-16, she wrote a letter to the editor that was published in a major newspaper.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，她写信给编辑，发表在蒙特利尔最大的报纸上。

D10.en: At 18, she hobbled in excruciating pain to the train back to Chicago.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她忍受剧痛一瘸一拐地登上返回芝加哥的火车。

D1.en: At 17, she prayed from Monday to Friday in the snow to be emptied of self and filled with power.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她从周一到周五在雪中祷告，求倒空自己、被天上的能力充满。

D14.en: At 17, she won a newspaper subscription contest, with her picture in the Woodstock Sentinel Review.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她赢得报纸订阅比赛，照片登在Woodstock Sentinel Review上。

D13a.en: At 17, she announced she wouldn't join Christmas entertainments and burned her ragtime music, novels, and dancing slippers.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，她宣布不参加圣诞娱乐活动，把拉格泰姆乐谱、小说和舞鞋扔进炉子烧了。

D18.en: At 17, she sold newspaper subscriptions with great skill.
D18.zh: 17岁时，她卖报纸订阅，手法娴熟得让Horace Greeley都自愧不如。

D11.en: As a small child, she was drawn to a well by voices in its depths that sounded like her own.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她被井底像自己的声音吸引到井边。

M1.en: At 11-12, she won a gold medal in a public speaking contest sponsored by the Woman's Christian Temperance Union.
M1.zh: 11到12岁时，她在基督教妇女禁酒联合会举办的演讲比赛中赢得金牌。

D15.en: At 18, she tumbled down stairs at the mission in Findlay and broke her ankle.
D15.zh: 18岁时，她在芬德利布道所滚下楼梯，摔断了脚踝。

D7.en: At 17, she brought home an invitation to a dance; her mother refused, then relented.
D7.zh: 17岁时，她带回家一张舞会请柬；母亲先是拒绝，后来让步了。

D6.en: At 17, she went to costume skating carnivals, play rehearsals, and college balls.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她参加了化装滑冰嘉年华、戏剧排练和大学舞会。

D2.en: At 17, she spent more hours praying, ate and slept less, blurring waking and sleeping.
D2.zh: 17岁时，她花更多时间祷告，吃得少睡得少，醒着和睡着几乎没有区别。

D3.en: At 17, she argued with a preacher about evolution and made a fool of him.
D3.zh: 17岁时，她与一位牧师争论进化论，并让他出了丑。

D12.en: At 13, she and her younger brother led services designed for youngsters.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她和弟弟主持了专为年轻人设计的礼拜。

D13b.en: At 17, she married Robert Semple in Salford.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，她在索尔福德嫁给了Robert Semple。

M8.en: He experienced inexpressible ecstasy from a unique pantomime.
M8.zh: 他从一个独特的哑剧中体验到了难以言表的狂喜。

D8.en: He pinched Jitendra as resoundingly as Jitendra had pinched him on the train.
D8.zh: 他狠狠地掐了Jitendra一下，就像Jitendra在火车上掐他那样。

M7.en: He hoped to find the master from his visions amidst Himalayan snows.
M7.zh: 他希望在喜马拉雅山的雪中找到他常在幻象中见到的上师。

M6.en: He sought direct perception of God, unsatisfied with affiliation or creed.
M6.zh: 他寻求对上帝的直接感知，不满足于归属或信条。

M2.en: At 16, he took his first flight.
M2.zh: 16岁时，他第一次坐飞机。

M4.en: He was conscious of his dependence on God.
M4.zh: 他意识到自己对上帝的依赖。

D10.en: At 16-17, he wept for two hours and felt transformed by an alchemical cleanser.
D10.zh: 16到17岁时，他哭了两个小时，感觉像被某种炼金清洁剂改变了。

D1.en: At 16-17, he nearly collapsed from hunger, his stomach protesting.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他几乎饿垮，胃在抗议。

D14.en: At 8, he was healed through a photograph of Lahiri Mahasaya.
D14.zh: 8岁时，他通过Lahiri Mahasaya的照片被治愈。

D13a.en: At about 17, he said he would not proceed with the test if money was taken as final security.
D13a.zh: 大约17岁时，他说如果拿走钱作为最终担保，他就不会进行测试。

D5.en: At 11, he fled toward Naini Tal in the Himalayan foothills.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他逃往喜马拉雅山麓的奈尼塔尔。

D11.en: At 11, he had a clear picture of himself roaming India as a monk.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他清晰看到自己作为僧侣漫游印度。

M1.en: At about 17-18, he graduated from Hindu High School in Calcutta.
M1.zh: 大约17到18岁时，他从加尔各答的印度教高中毕业。

D4.en: He removed the leather cover from his Bhagavad Gita and straps from his sola topee.
D4.zh: 他取下《薄伽梵歌》的皮套和太阳帽的皮带。

D9.en: He accepted the challenge.
D9.zh: 他接受了挑战。

D7.en: He and others chose the next day for their flight, needing precautions against his brother Ananta.
D7.zh: 他和别人选择第二天逃跑，需要防范他哥哥Ananta。

D2.en: He kindly outlined solutions to problems likely set by instructors.
D2.zh: 他友好地概述了老师可能出的问题的解法。

D3.en: At 11, he felt intense longing for God and was drawn to the Himalayas.
D3.zh: 11岁时，他强烈渴望上帝，被喜马拉雅吸引。

D12.en: After Mother's death, he grew especially tender toward his younger brothers Sananda and Bishnu.
D12.zh: 母亲去世后，他对弟弟Sananda和Bishnu格外温柔。

D20X.en: At 18, he thought all would go well if Raja were relieved of his duties.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，他认为如果Raja被解除职务，一切都会顺利。

D8.en: At 18, he said it was time to take his affairs into his own hands.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他说是时候把自己的事情掌握在自己手中了。

M3.en: As a small child, he was deeply affected by Abanendranath Tagore's painting 'Buddha' without knowing what Buddhism was.
M3.zh: 幼年时，他被Abanendranath Tagore的画作《佛陀》深深打动，尽管不知道佛教是什么。

M7.en: At 18, he felt for Mrs Besant that her efforts seemed a failure.
M7.zh: 18岁时，他为Besant夫人感到难过，觉得她的努力似乎失败了。

D17.en: At 7, he often gave his school supplies to poorer boys.
D17.zh: 7岁时，他经常把铅笔、石板和书本送给更穷的男孩。

M2.en: At 18, he wrote to Annie Besant expressing his devotion and love for her as his dearest Mother.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他写信给Annie Besant，表达对她的忠诚和爱，称她为最亲爱的母亲。

D10.en: At 18, he felt misunderstood about a letter to C.W.L. and stressed he was not ungrateful.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他觉得自己写给C.W.L.的信被误解了，并强调自己并非忘恩负义。

D1.en: He studied for days but left exam papers blank.
D1.zh: 他学习数日，但考试时交白卷。

D13b.en: At age 15, his father Narianiah signed a document without apparent unwillingness.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，他父亲Narianiah签署了一份文件，似乎并无不愿。

D13a.en: He declared he would not return to his father.
D13a.zh: 他宣称绝不回到父亲身边。

D18.en: He collected money for Mrs. Besant's work and pledged 2s 6d weekly.
D18.zh: 他为贝赞特夫人的工作募捐，并承诺每周捐出2先令6便士。

D5.en: He was determined to show he knew what he was doing.
D5.zh: 他决心表明自己清楚在做什么。

D15.en: He rode 31½ miles and planned to ride more.
D15.zh: 他骑了31.5英里，还打算再骑一些。

D4.en: He took his father's clock apart and refused to eat until he reassembled it.
D4.zh: 他拆了父亲的钟，不装好就不吃饭。

D9.en: At 18, he successfully conducted a conference of Star workers in London because George was indisposed.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他成功主持了伦敦的星界工人会议，因为乔治身体不适。

D7.en: At 18, he showed a spirit of independence in a letter to Leadbeater from Genoa.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他在从热那亚写给Leadbeater的信中表现出独立精神。

D2.en: At 15, he wrote to Mrs. Besant in a beautiful script, possibly from intense copybook practice.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他用漂亮的字体给贝赞特夫人写信，可能是大量练字的结果。

D12.en: At 11-13, he would take food from the servants and carry it himself.
D12.zh: 11到13岁时，他会从仆人那里拿食物并自己端走。

D3.en: At 16, he sent in a sample of his own blood, believing absolutely in the machine.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他寄出了自己的血样，完全相信那台机器。

M6.en: At 16, he gave his first speech at the Round Table.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他在圆桌会议上发表了第一次演讲。

M4.en: As a teenager, he was obsessed with the desire to make China strong against its oppressors.
M4.zh: 十几岁时，他痴迷于让中国强大以对抗压迫者的愿望。

D16.en: At 17, Liang was forced by Chen Yuan-hsi into a pen battle.
D16.zh: 17岁时，梁漱溟被陈元熙逼入笔战。

D1.en: At age 7, he had to drop out of school because of the Boxer Rebellion and the Eight-Nation Alliance.
D1.zh: 7岁时，闹义和团，八国联军进了城，学校被迫关门，他也因此休学。

D13a.en: At 17-19, his father did not interfere in his disagreements, fostering his independent thinking.
D13a.zh: 十七到十九岁时，父亲对他相左之事不加干涉，他逐渐养成独立思考的习惯。

D18.en: At 8-9, he saved a string of copper coins and often played with them.
D18.zh: 八九岁时，他自己积蓄得一小串铜钱，常挂在身边玩。

D5.en: At 18, he took his first trip outside Peking to Changping on secret revolutionary business.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他第一次出北京城到昌平，从事秘密革命活动。

M1.en: At 18, he held to his constitutionalist stand until the Hsin-hai year (1911).
M1.zh: 18岁时，他坚持立宪派立场直到辛亥年（1911年）。

D15.en: In elementary school, he fell from a pole and fainted, never trying again.
D15.zh: 小学时，他爬杆子摔下来昏倒，以后不敢再试。

D4.en: At age 8, he furtively burned all his English textbooks.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他偷偷烧掉了所有英语课本。

D9.en: At 18, he was chosen to run a coal shop as a front for revolutionaries.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他被选去经营一家煤铺作为革命党人的掩护。

D7.en: At 18, he joined the Beijing-Tianjin Alliance and participated in revolutionary activities.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他加入京津同盟会，参加辛亥年革命党的活动。

D6.en: At 14, he was close friends with three classmates: Liao Fushen, Wang Yufen, and Yao Wanli.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他和廖福申、王毓芬、姚万里三个人彼此很要好。

D2.en: As a teenager, he developed the habit of solitary meditation for long periods.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他养成了长时间独自冥想的习惯。

D3.en: At 9-11, he used the Xinmin Congbao and New Novel as his earliest self-study textbooks.
D3.zh: 9到11岁时，他把《新民丛报》和《新小说》作为最早的自学课本。

D12.en: At 6, he felt the impact of the West very directly.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他非常直接地感受到西方的影响。

D14.en: At 10-14, he composed spiritual bouquets of prayers on cards.
D14.zh: 10到14岁时，他制作了祈祷的灵性花束卡片。

D13b.en: At 18, he enrolled in a philosophy course at Rome's Gregorian University.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他在罗马的格里高利大学注册了哲学课程。

D15.en: In his youth, he was a broad-shouldered skier and mountain walker.
D15.zh: 他年轻时是宽肩的滑雪者和山地徒步者。

D7.en: At 18, he informed his parents that he wanted to become a priest.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他告诉父母他想成为神父。

D2.en: Before age 18, he knew the entire book by heart.
D2.zh: 18岁前，他背下了整本书。

D3.en: He admired Jacques-Bénigne Bossuet's eloquence as a favorite religious author.
D3.zh: 他欣赏Jacques-Bénigne Bossuet的雄辩，将其作为喜爱的宗教作家。

D12.en: He was born in Rome in 1876 into a family of Church lawyers.
D12.zh: 他1876年生于罗马，出身教会律师家庭。

D4.en: In his youth, he was more used to physical action than to scholarship.
D4.zh: 他年轻时更习惯体力活动而非学术。

M5.en: At 16, he considered himself the top of the Four Talents of Haining but failed the imperial exam.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他自恃海宁四才子之首，却名落孙山。

D17.en: At 16, he helped with his sister's wedding, running errands and greeting guests, as he was close to her.
D17.zh: 16岁时，姐姐出阁，他鞍前马后，出力奔走，陪客行礼。

M2.en: At 16, he felt it was unlikely to fulfill the hope of bringing glory to the family as the eldest son.
M2.zh: 16岁时，他觉得作为长房长子光宗耀祖的愿望很难实现。

D10.en: At 16, he lost interest in the imperial exam and became enthusiastic about studying the Book of Han.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他对科举冷淡下来，对研究《汉书》却热情起来。

D13a.en: He observed his second brother's delayed arrival at the wedding as an act of defiance against their father.
D13a.zh: 他目睹二哥在婚礼前夜迟迟未到，以此反抗父命。

M1.en: At 16, he passed the county-level exam but failed the prefectural exam in Hangzhou.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他考中秀才，但去杭州应府试没考取。

D7.en: At 16, his father scolded him in his diary for neglecting eight-legged essays and writing poetry carelessly.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他父亲在日记里骂他不爱八股文，写诗也草率。

D6.en: At 15, he was ranked first among the Four Talents by locals, often visiting friends to discuss classical learning.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他被当地人推为四才子之首，常与朋友来往讨论旧学。

D12.en: At 4, his mother died, and he was raised by his great-aunt and step-grandmother.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他母亲病逝，由祖姑母和叔祖母抚养。

D19.en: At 14, he wrote his first diary entry on April 15, 1906.
D19.zh: 14岁时，他1906年4月15日写了第一篇日记。

M4.en: At 14, he and friends secretly hoped for revolution against the Qing dynasty.
M4.zh: 14岁时，他和朋友们私下盼着革命推翻清朝。

D10.en: As a child aged 10-12, he felt embarrassed about missing front teeth and became introverted.
D10.zh: 10到12岁时，他因没了门牙难为情，性格变得内向。

D1.en: At 18, he passed the Boxer Indemnity exam, placed 2nd, and went to the US in 1910.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他考取庚款留美第二名，1910年去了美国。

D13a.en: At 14, he was sad about an arranged marriage he couldn't control.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他为无法做主的包办婚姻伤心。

D18.en: As a child aged 5-9, he collected ancient coins with other kids.
D18.zh: 5到9岁时，他和小孩们攒古钱玩儿。

D11.en: At age 7-8, he wrote a poem starting with 'The hall is full of chrysanthemum fragrance'.
D11.zh: 七八岁时，他作诗开头写“满堂菊花香”。

D15.en: As a child aged 4-10, he learned to avoid bumps by swaying with a cart.
D15.zh: 4到10岁时，他顺着车摇晃避免碰头。

D4.en: As a small child, he got a magnifying glass and figured out several ways to play with it on his own.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他得到了一个三寸大的放大镜，自己琢磨出了好几种玩法。

D9.en: At 14, he helped organize a youth club and built a library.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他参与组织了一个青年集益社并建了一个图书馆。

D7.en: At 16, when ordered to wail at the imperial funeral, he and others burst out laughing instead.
D7.zh: 16岁时，学堂让他趴地哭灵，赞礼人喊“举哀”，他和大家都大笑起来。

D6.en: At 14, he organized a Youth Benefit Society.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他组织了一个青年集益社。

D2.en: At 14, he attributed his lack of illness that year to daily cold baths in the morning.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他觉得自己那年没生病是因为天天晨起冷水浴。

D3.en: At 16, he noted in his diary that he was rewriting 'The World and Its People' into paragraphs.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在日记里说改写《The World and Its People》为一派拉克拉夫（段落）。

D12.en: At 11-12, after his mother fell ill, he idled outside for about two years with little schooling.
D12.zh: 11到12岁时，母亲病了，他差不多有两年在外面闲逛，念书三心二意。

M6.en: At 16-18, he decided to join the world state and become a global citizen.
M6.zh: 16到18岁时，他决定加入世界国家，做一个世界公民。

M4.en: He urged his disciples to endure and persist in spiritual life for great rewards.
M4.zh: 他劝诫弟子们要忍耐坚持，因为胜利的奖赏很大。

D10.en: At 16-18, he had a deep fear of ghosts and avoided walking alone through forests at night.
D10.zh: 16到18岁时，他非常怕鬼，不敢独自走夜路穿过森林。

D1.en: At 13-16, he completed the first level of the Nak Tam Dhamma Exams.
D1.zh: 13到16岁时，他通过了Nak Tam佛法考试的第一级。

D13b.en: At age 9, he received permission from his parents to enter a monastery.
D13b.zh: 9岁时，他得到父母允许进入寺院。

D13a.en: At age 9, his parents took him to the local village monastery.
D13a.zh: 9岁时，父母带他去了当地的乡村寺院。

D15.en: At 16-18, he and friends walked up to 15 km to a monastery fair and back late at night.
D15.zh: 16到18岁时，他和朋友步行多达15公里去寺院庙会，深夜再走回来。

D9.en: As a small child, he was acknowledged as the natural leader of his group of friends.
D9.zh: 幼年时，他被公认为朋友中的自然领袖。

D2.en: At 13-16, he spent time everyday walking up and down in the shade, memorizing Pali chants.
D2.zh: 13到16岁时，他每天在阴凉处走来走去，背诵巴利文经文。

D12.en: At 7, he was given the onerous task of caring for his bed-ridden father.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他被赋予照顾卧床父亲的繁重任务。

D6.en: At 18, he fell in love with Phut's step-sister Jai and spent more time courting her at Phut's house.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他爱上了Phut的继妹Jai，开始花更多时间在Phut家追求她。

M6.en: At 11, he decided to become an unusual priest who could tell people the truth.
M6.zh: 11岁时，他决定成为一名不寻常的僧侣，能告诉人们真理。

D10.en: At 13, he was so embarrassed by his appearance that he wore his coat during PE.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他因外貌难为情，体育课也穿着外套。

D1.en: At 15, he ran into the master's room to make one last attempt to answer his koan.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他跑进师父房间，最后一次尝试回答公案。

D11.en: As a small child, he told his family he planned to build a zoo with a train.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他告诉家人他计划建一个带火车的动物园。

M1.en: At 14, he failed the entrance exam, a great embarrassment.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他入学考试没通过，非常丢脸。

D15.en: At 17, he walked about forty miles, arriving that night.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他走了大约四十英里，当晚到达。

D4.en: As a small child, he helped his father move stones in the rock garden.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他帮父亲在岩石花园里搬石头。

D9.en: At 13-15, he and a friend turned the tokonoma into an altar with candles and incense.
D9.zh: 13到15岁间，他和朋友用蜡烛、香把壁龛改成了佛坛。

D7.en: At 8, he ran away when his father chased him with a stick.
D7.zh: 8岁时，他父亲拿着棍子追他，他飞快逃走了。

D2.en: At 16, he learned to wake up without an alarm clock, a revelation.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他学会了不用闹钟准时起床，这让他恍然大悟。

D14.en: At 12, he dressed in borrowed formal attire and acted as a nominal matchmaker at a relative's wedding.
D14.zh: 12岁时，他借来小礼服，穿戴翎顶，坐轿到男家做名义上的媒人。

D13a.en: At 7-8, he bought candies with a maid, but his grandmother threw them onto the roof, so he couldn't eat any.
D13a.zh: 七八岁时，他和女佣买了广东糖食，祖母却把糖食扔到屋瓦上，他一点也没吃到。

D18.en: From age 11, he frequented bookstores and bought hundreds of books yearly, often asking his mother for money.
D18.zh: 11岁以后，他天天出入书肆，一年买五六百册书，常向母亲要钱。

M1.en: At 11-12, he read the Imperial School Regulations and learned about university degrees equivalent to jinshi.
M1.zh: 十一二岁时，他读到《钦定学堂章程》，知道大学毕业可取得进士资格。

D4.en: As a teenager, he compiled a list of Qing scholars' works, noting their status and criticisms, to understand their methods.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他汇集清代学者著作名目，记录成书未成、已刻未刻及批评，以认识其工作实况。

D7.en: At 3, he wet the bed, his mother threw him onto the floor, and he clung to his grandmother's neck.
D7.zh: 3岁时，他尿床被母亲扔到地上，冻得大哭，抱住祖母的脖子不放。

D2.en: At 15-16, he spent a year compiling 'Qing Scholarship Records' with 300 authors from bibliographies and catalogs.
D2.zh: 15到16岁时，他用一年时间根据书目和广告编成《清代著述考》，约三百家。

D3.en: At 11-12, he loved reading Liang Qichao's essays, memorizing 'The Young China' and others.
D3.zh: 十一二岁时，他爱读梁启超的文章，把《少年中国说》等背得烂熟。

D12.en: At 14, his grandmother cared for him while his father worried he might die, saying he couldn't live without him.
D12.zh: 14岁时，祖母全力护持他，父亲从杭州赶回，担心他死去。

D11.en: As a child, he loved reading novels, even the most vulgar ones.
D11.zh: 他少年时喜欢读小说，即使很粗俗的也看。

D2.en: At 17, he transferred to Fudan Public School in Shanghai.
D2.zh: 他17岁时考入上海吴淞复旦公学，作为插班生。

D1.en: As a teenager, he ignored others' disdain and silently studied hard despite being placed in humiliating seats.
D1.zh: 十几岁时，他不管别人冷眼，虽常被安排在屈辱的座位，始终不气馁，默默用功苦读。

D13b.en: At 16, he married Qin Zuying from the same county as arranged by his parents.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他遵父母之命与同县龙树堂村的秦祖瑛结婚。

D13a.en: At 16, he married a strange rural girl under family arrangement.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他在家庭包办下与一个陌生农村姑娘结婚。

D11.en: At 14, he thought up his own name Wang Li and courtesy name Liaoyi.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他想了想，自己起了个名字叫王力，字了一。

D9.en: At 17, he set up a private school at home to teach his younger brother.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他因教育幼弟，在家自办私塾。

D7.en: At 11, upon hearing about the Wuchang Uprising, he joyfully cut off his own queue.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他听到武昌起义，乐得手舞足蹈，主动剪掉小辫子。

D2.en: At 17, he persisted in self-study, helping with chores occasionally but mostly reading and writing in his study.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他坚持自学，有时帮干家务，其余时间待在书房读书习文不辍。

D3.en: At 14, he stayed home reading and writing, self-studying with a solid foundation from primary school.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他每天呆在家里自己看书习文，因高小基础好，自学日见长进。

M6.en: At 17-18, he resolved to save the country through industry.
M6.zh: 17到18岁时，他立下了实业救国的志向。

D14.en: At 17-18, inspired by a chemistry teacher and a factory visit, he resolved to develop modern industry to strengthen China.
D14.zh: 17到18岁时，受化学老师和参观化工厂的影响，他立下了实业救国的志向。

D11.en: At 17, he began writing ci poetry and has continued for over 50 years.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他开始填词，至今50多年从未间断。

D3.en: In the 1970s, he was no longer satisfied with Lange's theory and decided to explore a new path for socialist economics.
D3.zh: 1970年代，他不再满足于兰格理论，决心自己去探寻一条研究社会主义经济的新路。

M1.en: At 18, he graduated with honors from high school and was recommended for admission to Jinling University.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他以优异成绩从金陵大学附中毕业，并被保送进入金陵大学。

M7.en: He hoped to find the root of modern China's ills and gain deep understanding of past leaders to prepare for serving the nation.
M7.zh: 他希望從歷史中找出現代中國的病根，深入了解過去政治領袖的得失，以備將來為國家做大事。

M4.en: At 18, his patriotic consciousness, budding since age 12-13, suddenly found a direction when he heard Yin Haiguang's teaching.
M4.zh: 18歲時，他從十二、三歲開始萌芽的愛國意識，在聽到殷海光教誨的那一刻，突然看到了落實愛國情懷的基本大方向。

D5.en: At 14-17, during a school soccer match, his team was short a player, so the teacher made him play; he felt miserable and clumsy.
D5.zh: 14到17歲時，學校足球賽班隊缺人，老師只好讓他上場，他在場中痛苦不堪，笨手笨腳。

D9.en: From elementary school on, he was often assigned or recommended to be class president or student council chair.
D9.zh: 從小學開始，他經常被老師指派或同學推薦擔任班長或學生會主席。

D7.en: At age 14-17, he was very dissatisfied with the school's tracking system that divided students into superior and inferior classes.
D7.zh: 14到17岁时，他对学校将学生分为优等班和次等班的做法非常不满。

D3.en: At 12-13, he remembered reading books including Zhu Guangqian's 'Twelve Letters to Youth', Lu Xun's works, and 'Hu Shi Anthology'.
D3.zh: 12到13歲時，他記得看到的書包括朱光潛的《給青年的十二封信》、魯迅的作品與《胡適文存》。

D13b.en: He applied to and was admitted into the Foreign Languages Department of National Taiwan University at age 18.
D13b.zh: 他18岁时报考台大外语系并被录取。

D11.en: He started reading martial arts novels at age 8, from barely literate to nearly fluent.
D11.zh: 他8岁开始读武侠小说，从识字不多到差不多认识。

D9.en: He participated in refugee relief visits with his school at age 18, reconnecting with real life.
D9.zh: 他18岁时随学校参与难民救济访问，被拉回真实人生。

D7.en: At 16-17, he was drawn into political debates among classmates during the civil war.
D7.zh: 他16到17岁时被卷入同学间的政治辩论。

D2.en: For three years (ages 13-15), he immersed himself in reading, which eased his depression and shaped his moral standards.
D2.zh: 他13到15岁三年沉浸于读物，抑郁得到调节，构建了做人标准。

D12.en: As a small child during the war (ages 7-15), he could not attend school due to a disability that made walking difficult.
D12.zh: 他幼年时因残疾不良于行，抗战期间未能入学。

D7.en: At 17, he said, 'I don't want to; I want to study in a big city!'
D7.zh: 17岁时他说：'我不愿意，我要到大城市去读书！'

D12.en: As a young man, he attempted suicide by drowning to resist feudal family rules and pursue education.
D12.zh: 他年轻时曾投河自尽，以反抗封建礼教、争取读书自由。

D13a.en: At 17, he obeyed his mother's plan to let her go first so he could stay in the city for work.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他听从母亲安排让她先下乡，这样自己可以留城工作。

D11.en: Around age 10-13, he read new novels and poems, imitating Ai Qing's poetry and Ai Wu's fiction.
D11.zh: 10到13岁时，他看新小说、新诗，模仿艾青的诗和艾芜的小说。

M1.en: He applied to Wuhan University and Peking University philosophy departments as his first choice and was admitted to both.
M1.zh: 他以第一志愿报考武大哲学系和北大哲学系，都被录取了。

D4.en: At 16-18, he borrowed Ren Jiyu's library card, each time borrowing 30 thread-bound books (4 volumes per case).
D4.zh: 16到18岁时，他借任继愈老师的借书证用，每次借30函线装书，一函4本。

D9.en: He says he has administrative and organizational talent.
D9.zh: 他说他有行政和组织才能。

D2.en: At 7, he could play table tennis but never learned to smash.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他会打乒乓球，但始终不会抽球。

D3.en: He read Hume's book in college in 1953.
D3.zh: 他1953年读大学时读了休谟的书。

D12.en: His father died when he was 12.
D12.zh: 他12岁时父亲就死了。

D20.en: At 15-16, he and two classmates started an English wall newspaper.
D20.zh: 15到16岁时，他和同学沈逢吉、张若衡一起办了一张英文壁报。

D16.en: At 8, he challenged an adult's view on finding swift horses, arguing that the solution didn't say where to find the horse expert.
D16.zh: 8岁时，他对关于千里马的文章提出质疑，认为文章没说去哪儿找伯乐。

D1.en: At 15, he took the entrance exam for Nankai twice, finally being admitted as a transfer student in his last year of high school.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他为了进南开考了两次，第二次在高中最后一年被录取为插班生。

D13b.en: At 11, he left home in Liuzhou to board at Hanmin Middle School in Guilin.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他离开柳州的家到桂林的汉民中学住校上学。

D5.en: At 14-15, he experienced several close Japanese bombings, with deafening explosions like a call from death.
D5.zh: 14到15岁时，他几次遇到日机投弹，距离很近，震耳欲聋的爆炸声像是死亡之门的召唤。

M1.en: At 15, he took the entrance exam for Nankai twice and was admitted as a transfer student in his final year of high school.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他考了两次南开，最后一年被录取为插班生。

D9.en: He and his classmates Shen Fengji and Zhang Ruoheng ran an English wall newspaper, publishing four or five issues.
D9.zh: 他和同学沈逢吉、张若衡一起办了一张英文壁报，一共出了四五期。

D7.en: He stomped on the principal's foot with his little foot to express anger.
D7.zh: 他伸出自己的小脚用力在校长大人的脚上踩了一脚表示愤慨。

D6.en: A classmate took him home every Sunday, where his mother cooked two dishes for them to feast on.
D6.zh: 同班同学每星期日带他回家，他母亲总是炒两个菜供他们大吃一顿。

D2.en: His grandmother used a reward system to motivate him to practice calligraphy; he worked hard all winter and summer breaks.
D2.zh: 祖母用奖励制度激励他练字，他练字十分卖力，整个寒暑假都在练习。

D12.en: As a child, he experienced the hardships of the War of Resistance and the misfortune of national poverty in a displaced life.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他经历了抗战的苦难，在颠沛流离中体会到国家贫弱的不幸。

D16.en: He spent a lot of time arguing about everything as a child.
D16.zh: 他小时候花很多时间争论一切。

D1.en: At 11, he was moved from sixth to seventh grade, becoming two years younger than peers.
D1.zh: 11岁时，他从六年级跳到七年级，比同学小两岁。

D9.en: He encouraged the organization of a Jewish troop at about age 12.
D9.zh: 他大约12岁时鼓励组织了一个犹太童子军队伍。

D7.en: At about 12, he ran home to avoid having to eat nonkosher food.
D7.zh: 大约12岁时，他跑回家以避免吃非犹太洁食。

D3.en: At 18, he decided to become an economist.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他决定成为一名经济学家。

D12.en: At 11-13, he experienced his father's death.
D12.zh: 11到13岁间，他经历了父亲的去世。

D10.en: He said he is afraid to mention the past, as sorrow outweighs joy and happy memories only bring loneliness.
D10.zh: 他说他很怕提起过去的事，因为悲多乐少，欢乐的事回味起来会引起孤独的惆怅。

D14.en: He started submitting articles to the magazine 'Youth'.
D14.zh: 他开始向《少年》杂志投稿。

D13b.en: His mother brought him to Wang Jiyu, and he became one of the few male students at Zhenhua Girls' School.
D13b.zh: 他母亲杨纫兰把他领到王季玉面前，他成了振华女校的少数男学生之一。

D13a.en: His grandmother taught him to respect paper with writing by burning it in the stove.
D13a.zh: 他祖母教导他要敬惜字纸，把有字的纸聚在炉子里焚烧。

D11.en: He published his third work, encouraging a friend to fight through difficulties by telling a story about persistence.
D11.zh: 他发表了第三篇作品《读陈龄君通讯感》，通过讲述哥哥的朋友坚持上学的故事，鼓励陈龄在阻力中猛斗。

D9.en: He was involved in assisting his brother Fei Qing in running a school for workers and peasants.
D9.zh: 他协助哥哥费青办起了面向工农大众的平成义务普及学校。

D2.en: He studied hard, got good grades, and told friends he had to rely on himself.
D2.zh: 他很用功，分数考得好，对朋友说自己没有资本，只能靠自己。

D3.en: He respected his elementary school principal, who patted his head and asked about his health.
D3.zh: 他敬重小学校长沈天民，校长会拍拍他的小脑袋，问他身体可好些了。

D12.en: He was helped by his sister Fei Dasheng to finish middle school and enter university.
D12.zh: 他由姐姐费达生帮助完成中学学业并进入大学。

M8.en: At 16-17, the revolutionary novel 'What Is to Be Done?' first influenced his worldview.
M8.zh: 16到17岁时，小说《怎么办？》第一次影响了他的世界观。

D19.en: At 17, he recorded it word by word and distributed copies among acquaintances.
D19.zh: 17岁时，他逐字记录并复写多份在熟人里传播。

M3.en: At 16-17, the protagonist Rakhmetov became his idol.
M3.zh: 16到17岁时，主角拉赫美托夫成了他的偶像。

M6.en: At 16-17, he thought his mother's constitutional ideal was illusory and weak; only revolution could save China.
M6.zh: 16到17岁时，他认为母亲的宪政理想虚幻软弱，必须通过革命才能拯救中国。

M4.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an radio in bed every day, then Guan Zaihan published it under a foreign news agency name.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社名义发表。

D16.en: At 16, he witnessed a tragic incident in Chongqing.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他在重庆亲历了一场惨案。

D13a.en: At 18, he obediently chose the College of Liberal Arts.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他乖乖地选了文学院。

D18.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an radio while lying in bed.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播。

D5.en: He secretly listened to Yan'an broadcasts in bed every day, then had Guan Zaihan publish them under a foreign news agency name.
D5.zh: 他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D15.en: At 11-12, he promised to run 800 meters every evening.
D15.zh: 11到12岁时，他向学校保证每天晚上跑800米。

D7.en: In October 1948, he fled with his mother to Hong Kong, waiting for a ship to the liberated area.
D7.zh: 1948年10月，随母逃亡香港，待船去解放区。

D6.en: In 1941, he entered Chongqing Nankai Middle School.
D6.zh: 1941年，进重庆南开中学。

D2.en: He secretly listened to Yan'an broadcasts in bed every day.
D2.zh: 他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播。

D12.en: At age 3, his mother Deng Jixing married Chen Mingde.
D12.zh: 3岁时，母亲邓季惺与陈铭德结婚

D5.en: He entered the army in March 1917, two months before turning 18, and was sent to the Italian front after training.
D5.zh: 1917年3月，他差两个月满18岁参军，训练七个月后被派往意大利前线。

M1.en: Despite lack of natural aptitude, he emerged among the top five or six of seventy to eighty cadets.
M1.zh: 尽管缺乏天赋，他在七八十名学员中名列前五六名。

D9.en: As a student, he attempted to foster contact between LSE and Cambridge.
D9.zh: 他作为学生时，试图促进伦敦政治经济学院和剑桥大学之间的联系。

D3.en: At 14-15, he developed a lasting interest in evolution.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，他对进化产生了持久的兴趣。

D7.en: At 14, he failed Latin, Greek, and math, repeated a grade, and changed Gymnasium twice due to teacher conflicts.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他拉丁语、希腊语和数学不及格，留级，并因与老师冲突两次转学。

D16.en: At 15-16, he overheard a poem revision and learned how to polish verses.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，他听到改诗的过程，深受启发，懂得了诗句要这样推敲。

D14.en: At 17-18, he enjoyed listening to free Peking opera at night.
D14.zh: 17到18岁时，他夜晚听免费京剧，觉得是一大乐事。

D11.en: At 11, he learned the four tones from his teacher, starting with 'tian zi sheng zhe'.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他跟着老师从平上去入四声学起，先背了「天子圣哲」四个字。

D2.en: At 7-16, he read selected passages from classical texts like Records of the Grand Historian, not full recitation.
D2.zh: 7到16岁时，他选读《史记》《战国策》等，并非从头到尾背诵，也读过《四书》但不完整。

D3.en: At 17-18, he asked his teacher if truth itself changes, and the teacher admitted he couldn't answer.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他问老师：这条真理本身变不变？老师坦率说理论修养不够，无法回答。

D12.en: At 14, he witnessed a tragic death and saw the body still in place.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他清晨赶去看望，尸体还在原处，这是他早年亲历的悲伤惨事。

D6.en: In 1948, at age 18, he led me and another young relative on a three-day hiking trip to the Summer Palace and other sites.
D6.zh: 1948年，他18岁时，带着我和另一个年轻亲戚进行了三天三夜的远足，去了颐和园等地。

D20X.en: He was seen on equal footing in high-ranking social circles.
D20X.zh: 他被看到与上流社会平等交往。

D19.en: He gave himself grades as if addressing a report card to his mother.
D19.zh: 他给自己打分，仿佛在给母亲交成绩单。

D16.en: He demolished others' ideas with facts and logic.
D16.zh: 他用事实和逻辑摧毁别人的观点。

D11.en: At age 10-18, he probably kept a diary and may have recorded pessimism and depression.
D11.zh: 10到18岁时，他可能记过日记，也许记录了一些悲观和抑郁。

M1.en: At age 18, he graduated from Theresianum with high honours.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他以优异成绩从特蕾西亚学院毕业。

D15.en: At age 10-18, he was seen on terms of equality in houses above the smart set.
D15.zh: 10到18岁时，他被发现在上流社会的家中平等交往。

D9.en: He quickly mastered the manners of his aristocratic classmates.
D9.zh: 他很快掌握了贵族同学的举止。

D2.en: He took books home and worked late into the night.
D2.zh: 他把书带回家，工作到深夜。

D3.en: He set out to discover the laws governing society and the economy.
D3.zh: 他着手发现支配社会和经济的规律。

D12.en: He and his mother were left to rely on each other for love and support.
D12.zh: 他和母亲只能互相依靠，给予爱与支持。

D13b.en: As a small child, he trudged the Stations of the Cross with his mother.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他和母亲一起走苦路。

D4.en: He copied windows, arches, and doors repeatedly to draw them by heart.
D4.zh: 他反复临摹窗户、拱门和门，直到能凭记忆画出它们。

M7.en: At 17, he was appointed drill instructor for the militia by his elder brother.
M7.zh: 17岁时，他受命为教官，教诸伯叔父兵操。

M4.en: Since childhood, he held nationalist and revolutionary views, inspired by his teacher.
M4.zh: 他自幼即抱民族观念，同情革命民主，由伯圭师启发。

D16.en: At 8, he performed as Zhuge Liang and Zhang Zhao in a recitation.
D16.zh: 8岁时，他以背诵兼表演，扮诸葛亮和张昭。

D10.en: At 11, he angrily demanded the supervisor tell him how to fill a form.
D10.zh: 11岁时，他一时愤慨，大声请监督告以当何等式样填写。

D1.en: At 17, after illness, he returned to school and stayed to join the revolutionaries.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他病后返校，同学四散，他留校等待革命军进城投效。

D13a.en: At 16, his elder brother arranged his engagement to the Shen family.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他兄长替他定婚沈家。

D18.en: At 10, he won 'The Unofficial History of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom' as a school prize.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他读小学时奖得了《太平天国野史》上下两册。

D5.en: At 17, he took the last train from Nanjing to Shanghai as the school was evacuated.
D5.zh: 17岁时，学校下令驱逐所有人，他乘南京开出的最后一班车赴上海。

D11.en: At 16, he read Tan Sitong's 'Ren Xue' by moonlight and cut off his queue the next day.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他读谭嗣同的《仁学》忘了时间，在院子里借着月光读，第二天就剪了辫子。

D15.en: At 17, after three months of illness, he ate meat for the first time and prepared to return to school.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他病三个月，一日开始吃荤，就想第二天回学校，哥哥为他整理行装。

D4.en: As a small child, he used a dilapidated room as a study to read diligently.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他用几间破房作书房，读书很用功。

D9.en: At 11, he was elected as one of five representatives to propose curriculum changes.
D9.zh: 11岁时，他被公推为代表之一，提议修改课程。

D7.en: At age 7-8, he asked his father about the teacher's words and learned about 'Manchu-Han' signs.
D7.zh: 七八岁时，他回家问父亲老师的话，父亲告诉他店铺招牌写'满汉'的由来。

D6.en: At about 12, he and seven other students were sent by boat to Changzhou Middle School.
D6.zh: 约12岁时，他和另外七名学生被用船送到常州中学。

D2.en: At 13, he entered Changzhou Middle School and, encouraged by senior classmates, took and passed the exam for a higher grade.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他考入常州府中学堂，在同学鼓励下报考二年级并录取。

D19.en: At 18, he found that when appointed to a committee, he was inevitably made to do all the work.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他发现一旦被任命为委员会成员，就不可避免地要做所有工作。

D20.en: At 18, he was elected to the Committee of Management of the School Stores.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被选入学校商店管理委员会。

D16.en: At 17, he said a piece was readable but not more than readable.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他说某作品可读，但仅此而已。

D10.en: At 7, he said that was by a great effort he had prepared for days.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他说那是经过多日准备的巨大努力。

D1.en: At 15, he wrote home that the winner of the Junior Mathematical Prize should be locked up as a dangerous lunatic.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他写信回家说初级数学奖得主应该被当作危险疯子关起来。

D14.en: At 18, he was enacting the part of the melancholy Dane.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他在扮演忧郁的丹麦王子。

D5.en: At 12, he took a trip to Gibraltar, through Egypt to the Cape.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他去了直布罗陀，经埃及到开普敦旅行。

M1.en: At 14, he came out top of the division in the fortnightly order, to his great surprise.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他出乎意料地在双周排名中名列全班第一。

D15.en: At 16, he enjoyed rowing immensely and went out on the river every day that week.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他非常喜欢划船，那一周每天都去河上划船。

D4.en: At 17, he and Young had Moss sew art muslin together, nailed it to a goal post, and launched it from a top tower window.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他和Young让Moss把艺术薄纱缝在一起，钉在球门柱上，从塔顶窗户放飞。

D9.en: At 17, he put up a motion in Private Business in College Pop but was defeated by one vote.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他在College Pop的Private Business中提出动议，但以一票之差被否决。

D7.en: At 17, he said the Church is the refuge for those who cannot preach.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他说教会是不能布道者的避难所。

D6.en: At 18, he attended the first meeting of the Literary Society (technically called the Essay Society).
D6.zh: 18岁时，他参加了文学社（正式名称为论文社）的第一次会议。

D2.en: At 14, he read Homer with a tutor as private work.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他私下跟老师一起读荷马史诗。

D3.en: At 17, he made investigations about the comparative lengths of some long poems.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他调查了一些长诗的长度比较。

D13b.en: As a teenager, he spent three to four years at Leamington College.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他在Leamington College待了三四年。

D19.en: At 15-17, he read widely and took dense notes, filling his notebooks.
D19.zh: 15到17岁时，他看得多记得勤，笔记本上记得密密麻麻。

D13b.en: At 12, he had to stop schooling because his family could no longer afford tuition.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他因家庭无力承担学费而不得不停止学业。

D5.en: He asked to be taken in as an orderly but was refused.
D5.zh: 他要求他们收留当勤务兵，但被谢绝。

D15.en: At 12-13, he went on long hikes every Saturday afternoon from the firm to Labor University.
D15.zh: 12到13岁时，他每周六下午从事务所长途远足到劳动大学。

D4.en: At 14, he engraved copper plates at night at Pan Xulun's request.
D4.zh: 14岁时，他受潘序伦之托，夜间刻铜版。

D9.en: He became head of Lixin Accounting Correspondence School in 1930, later taught and became school department head.
D9.zh: 他1930年出任立信会计函授学校负责人，后担任夜校教师及学校部主任。

D6.en: He hiked to Labor University every Saturday and stayed overnight in Gan Jirui's dormitory.
D6.zh: 他每周六远足到劳动大学，周六夜晚在甘绩瑞的宿舍留下。

D2.en: From age 12, he engraved 3000 characters nightly, studied English, printed handouts, graded papers, and taught at night school.
D2.zh: 从12岁开始，他每晚刻3000字，自学英文，印讲义、改课卷，到夜校讲课。

D3.en: He immersed himself in Chinese and Western civilizations through extensive reading and thinking in his spare time.
D3.zh: 他通过业余时间的大量阅读与思索，使自己浸润于中西文明之中。

D12.en: He entered Pan Xulun's Lixin Accounting Firm as an apprentice after junior high graduation in 1927.
D12.zh: 他1927年初中毕业后，进入立信会计师事务所当练习生。

D17.en: At 10-13, he took on the heavy responsibility of supporting his family.
D17.zh: 10到13岁时，他承担起赡养家庭的重任。

D1.en: At 16-17, he was driven off the podium by students within a week, but a year later he taught another class and succeeded.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他上课不到一周便被学生轰下台；一年后他再担任老师，这回站住了脚。

D7.en: He enthusiastically supported the uprising and told colleagues, 'I will also raise the red flag.'
D7.zh: 他表示热烈赞成暴动，还对同事们说：“我也要托起红旗来。”

D1.en: At 13, he won first place in the 100-meter dash in fall 1932, then quit sports to prepare for Tsinghua.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他1932年秋获乙组百米第一，之后挂钉鞋专心准备投考清华。

D13b.en: At 11.5, he entered Nankai Middle School in summer-autumn 1928.
D13b.zh: 1928年夏秋之际，他考进南开中学，时年11岁半。

D13a.en: At 16, he had to forge a transfer certificate in Beijing in early 1933.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他1933年初在北平不得不假造一张转学证书。

M1.en: At 17, he was admitted to Tsinghua University in late summer 1934.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他1934年夏末考取清华。

D7.en: As a child, his father scolded him harshly, telling him to prove himself in the bigger world.
D7.zh: 幼年时，父亲用粗豪语气教训他：“狗洞里做天王算得了什么，有本事到外边大的世界去做天王。”

D2.en: At 13-14, he impulsively stayed home, turned on all lights, and recited Lincoln's Gettysburg Address dozens of times.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他临时冲动决定不去，开了所有电灯，踱来踱去，几十遍地朗诵林肯的盖茨堡演说。

D3.en: At 18, he bought Feng Youlan's History of Chinese Philosophy translated by Derk Bodde and carried it through wartime displacement.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他花15元高价买了卜德英译的冯友兰《中国哲学史》上册，奔波流徙中始终随身携带。

D12.en: At 15, he was expelled from Nankai High School for participating in a student protest.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他因学潮被南开高中开除。

D11.en: At 11-12, he taught himself singing, percussion, and huqin, and performed with a clear, slightly husky voice.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他自拉自唱，嗓音清亮略带沙哑，谭派腔调十足。

D15.en: As a teenager, he practiced basic skills that later enabled him to perform Zhang Fei in 'Reed Flats' in his late 40s.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他自练基本功，后来在40年代末与工会合演《芦花荡》扮演张飞。

D8.en: From 1912 to 1914 he debated the classification of deep-water Limnaea from Lake Geneva.
D8.zh: 1912到1914年间，他参与了关于日内瓦湖深水椎实螺分类的辩论。

D19.en: At 16, he completed his master's catalog of mollusks and took over other projects.
D19.zh: 16岁时，他完成了软体动物主目录并接手了其他项目。

M6.en: At 11, he decided to consecrate his life to the biological explanation of knowledge.
M6.zh: 11岁时，他决定将一生献给知识的生物学解释。

D20.en: At 16, he and another composed the new Committee and in 1914 published their researches jointly.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他和另一个人组成了新委员会，并于1914年联合发表了他们的研究。

D16.en: At 15, his first professional article opened with an anecdote publicizing his relationship with Godet.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他的第一篇专业文章以一则轶事开头，公开了他与Godet的关系。

D14.en: At 13, tradition demanded he give a talk; he began by quoting Paul Godet.
D14.zh: 13岁时，传统要求他做一次演讲；他以引用Paul Godet开场。

D13b.en: At 16, he belonged to the parish of Neuchâtel's Collegiate church, followed religious instruction, and was confirmed in May 1912.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他属于纳沙泰尔学院教堂的教区，参加了宗教指导，并于1912年5月接受了坚信礼。

D11.en: At 11, he published a religious prose-poem about war, The Mission of the Idea.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他发表了一篇关于战争的宗教散文诗《思想的使命》。

M1.en: At 16, his scientific precocity was accompanied by ambition and determination.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他的科学早熟伴随着野心和决心。

D15.en: From 14 to 17, he gathered mollusks at different altitudes from lake shores to mountain heights.
D15.zh: 14到17岁间，他从湖岸到山巅不同海拔采集软体动物。

D4.en: As a schoolboy, he was curator of molluscs at the Natural History Museum in Neuchâtel.
D4.zh: 他学生时代担任纳沙泰尔自然历史博物馆的软体动物管理员。

D9.en: At 16-18, he rapidly built a large network of malacologists.
D9.zh: 16到18岁间，他迅速建立了包括多位知名软体动物学家的大型网络。

D6.en: At 14, he belonged to the Club of the Friends of Nature from June 1910 to September 1915.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他从1910年6月到1915年9月加入了自然之友俱乐部。

D3.en: At 16, he read at least the first long chapter of Creative Evolution before September.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在9月前至少读了《创造进化论》的第一长章。

D12.en: At around 10, he started to forgo play for serious work, partly to imitate his father.
D12.zh: 10岁左右，他开始放弃玩耍去做正经事，部分是为了模仿父亲。

D13b.en: He graduated from the Jewish gymnasium of A. E. Ratner in Gomel in 1913.
D13b.zh: 他1913年在戈梅利的A. E. Ratner犹太中学毕业。

D13a.en: Within months of admission, he transferred to the Faculty of Jurisprudence.
D13a.zh: 入学后几个月内，他转到了法律系。

M1.en: He graduated with excellent grades and a gymnasium gold medal.
M1.zh: 他以优异成绩毕业并获得中学金质奖章。

D9.en: At 15-17, he led a kruzhok of younger, mostly female, Jewish peers in Gomel.
D9.zh: 15到17岁时，他在戈梅利领导了一个由年轻（多为女性）犹太同龄人组成的小组。

D6.en: He led a group enthusiastic about national history and culture.
D6.zh: 他领导了一个对民族历史和文化充满热情的小组。

D2.en: He passed through the sixth, seventh, and eighth years of studies and graduated with a gold medal.
D2.zh: 他通过了六、七、八年级的学习，并以金质奖章毕业。

D3.en: As early as his gymnasium days, he was fascinated by Hegel's dialectical philosophy.
D3.zh: 早在中学时代，他就对黑格尔的辩证法哲学着迷。

D8.en: He frowned on his sister reading Balzac and Dumas as too risqué.
D8.zh: 他不赞成妹妹读巴尔扎克和大仲马，认为太露骨。

D16.en: At 17, his professor told him he had an 'idiotic style,' which he was impressed by and spread the news.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他的教授告诉他，他有一种‘白痴风格’，他对此印象深刻并传播了这个消息。

D10.en: He experienced intervals of depression, irrational hostility, and thoughts of suicide.
D10.zh: 他经历了抑郁、非理性敌意甚至自杀念头的间歇期。

D1.en: He resisted his mother and sought to seduce his beloved nurse.
D1.zh: 他反抗母亲，并试图引诱他心爱的保姆。

D14.en: He excelled in classical literature and history at school.
D14.zh: 他在学校古典文学和历史方面表现出色。

D13b.en: He underwent treatment after treatment and consulted expensive specialists to no avail.
D13b.zh: 他接受了一次又一次的治疗，咨询了昂贵的专家，但毫无效果。

D11.en: As a schoolboy, he was a fascinating writer drawing from inner loneliness.
D11.zh: 他上学时是个迷人的作家，从内心孤独中汲取灵感。

M1.en: People imagined a glorious career for the studious schoolboy.
M1.zh: 人们为这个好学的男孩想象了辉煌的职业生涯。

D15.en: He took up tennis, swimming, and later mountain climbing, his favorite sport.
D15.zh: 他打网球、游泳，后来还登山，这是他最爱的运动。

D4.en: He cruelly tortured butterflies.
D4.zh: 他残忍地折磨蝴蝶。

D9.en: He helped his siblings with lessons and lectured them about the world.
D9.zh: 他帮弟弟妹妹做功课，还给他们讲世界知识。

D7.en: At age 10-12, his father began taking him on walks.
D7.zh: 他10到12岁时，父亲开始带他散步。

D6.en: At 10-18, he considered becoming a lawyer or a doctor.
D6.zh: 10到18岁时，他考虑过当律师或医生。

D3.en: At age 3, his sister initiated him into sexual games.
D3.zh: 3岁时，他姐姐引导他玩性游戏。

D13b.en: His father changed his name to Frank Samuelson upon arriving at Ellis Island.
D13b.zh: 他父亲在埃利斯岛入境时改名为Frank Samuelson。

D18.en: His parents were Jewish immigrants from Suwalki.
D18.zh: 他的父母是从Suwalki来的犹太移民。

D11.en: As a teenager, he wrote a short story that appeared in The Scroll.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他写了一篇短篇小说，发表在《卷轴》上。

M1.en: At 17, he won a prize for a political science essay.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他因一篇政治学论文获奖。

D15.en: As a teenager, he participated in track, basketball, and fencing.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他参加了田径、篮球和击剑。

D9.en: At 18, he took Labor Problems with Aaron Director, moving toward economics.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他选了Aaron Director的劳工问题课，转向经济学。

D7.en: At around 5-10, he lost control of a handpump while trying to hold it, and it sank into a well, requiring professional recovery.
D7.zh: 他大约5到10岁时，在操作手压泵时没抓住，泵头掉进井里，得找专业人士捞上来。

D6.en: He was born on May 15, 1915, in the office above his parents' drugstore.
D6.zh: 他于1915年5月15日出生在父母药店楼上的办公室。

D3.en: At 17-18, he followed up on mathematical elements in his lectures.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他跟进讲座中的数学元素。

D12.en: His parents were Jewish immigrants from Suwalki.
D12.zh: 他的父母是从苏瓦乌基来的犹太移民。

D20X.en: At 9, he hid a box on a beam in the attic, feeling safe because no one went there.
D20X.zh: 9岁时，他把盒子藏在阁楼的横梁上，觉得安全，因为没人去那里。

M5.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and placed it in a pencil box.
M5.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，放进铅笔盒里。

D8.en: At 12-13, he vowed never to let anyone best or pity him again.
D8.zh: 12到13岁时，他发誓再也不让任何人胜过或可怜他。

D19.en: He kept meticulous diaries about his health, recording coughing, fever, nausea, indigestion, and depression.
D19.zh: 他详细记录了自己的健康状况，包括咳嗽、发烧、恶心、消化不良和抑郁。

D16.en: At 11-12, he avidly learned and eagerly showed off what he knew.
D16.zh: 11到12岁时，他热切学习并急于炫耀自己知道的东西。

D1.en: At 9-10, when unhappy or threatened, he thought of his bedded-down manikin with its stone and felt comforted.
D1.zh: 9到10岁时，不开心或受威胁时，他会想起自己安放好的小木人和石头，感到安慰。

D14.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler, sawed it off, and bedded it in a pencil box.
D14.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小木人，锯下来，放在铅笔盒里安顿好。

D13a.en: At 16-18, after confirmation, he decided to forgo communion, and his father accepted without a word.
D13a.zh: 16到18岁时，坚信礼后他决定放弃圣餐，父亲默许了。

D5.en: At 3, he sat with one leg dangling beneath a rickety railing, mesmerized by the water below.
D5.zh: 3岁时，他坐在摇摇欲坠的栏杆上，一条腿悬在下面，被下面的水迷住了。

D15.en: At 14, his father took him to Lucerne, where he finally approached the mountains.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他父亲带他去卢塞恩，他终于接近了群山。

D4.en: As a child and teenager, he searched unceasingly for fossils, minerals, bones, and the like.
D4.zh: 他小时候和十几岁时，不停地寻找化石、矿物、骨头之类的东西。

D9.en: At 11-12, he organized a solemn duel between two schoolfellows in the Vicarage garden.
D9.zh: 11到12岁时，他在牧师花园里组织了两个同学之间的庄严决斗。

D7.en: At 9, he rejected the stork myth his parents told him.
D7.zh: 9岁时，他拒绝了父母告诉他的鹳鸟神话。

D2.en: As a child and young adult, he became consumed with learning to deflect the dreariness of daily life.
D2.zh: 他小时候和年轻时，埋头学习以逃避日常生活的沉闷。

D12.en: He was the fourth-born and first-surviving child of a poor country parson and his unhappy wife.
D12.zh: 他是一个贫穷乡村牧师和他不快乐妻子的第四个孩子，也是第一个存活下来的。

D14.en: He was active from the outset, contributing to debates, intervening from the floor, and making speeches.
D14.zh: 他从一开始就很活跃，参与辩论，从会场发言并发表演讲。

D18.en: For a time the whole house became involved in printing fabrics; he carved linoleum blocks and printed designs on velvet.
D18.zh: 有一段时间全家都参与印染布料，他雕刻油毡版并在天鹅绒上印制图案。

D5.en: As a teenager, he jumped on buses and explored outer suburbs with friends.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他跳上公交车，和朋友一起探索远郊。

M1.en: His first published work was a laudatory essay on Gracchus Babeuf, written as a dissertation and published in L’Églantine.
M1.zh: 他的第一部出版作品是一篇赞扬格拉古·巴贝夫的文章，最初是作为论文写的，发表在《野蔷薇》上。

D9.en: He began participating in the Groupe socialiste interkhâgnal, holding weekly meetings for talks and seminars on social issues.
D9.zh: 他开始参加跨年级社会主义小组，每周举行会议，讨论工厂工作条件、工人合作社、工会和殖民主义等话题。

D6.en: At 16, he was introduced to socialist thinkers by Wauters, who mentored him like an older brother.
D6.zh: 16岁时，Wauters像哥哥一样引导他，向他介绍社会主义思想。

D3.en: At 16, he read Das Kapital while studying philosophy at the lycée.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在读高中时读了《资本论》。

D11.en: He has always dreamed since childhood about being a composer or at least a conductor.
D11.zh: 他从小就一直梦想成为作曲家，或者至少是指挥家。

D4.en: As an adolescent, he took photos, invented film scenarios, composed trios, drew costumes, and painted sets.
D4.zh: 青少年时，他拍照、编电影剧本、创作三重奏、画戏服和布景。

D2.en: As a teenager, he took violin lessons at the Opéra.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他在歌剧院上小提琴课。

M3.en: At 15-16, he felt he had to dare to do what he wanted, like Ulrich von Hutten.
M3.zh: 15到16岁时，他觉得自己必须敢于做想做的事，就像Ulrich von Hutten一样。

M6.en: At 15-16, he wanted to stay in science and devote his life to botany.
M6.zh: 15到16岁时，他想留在科学领域，献身于植物学。

M1.en: He felt he could not rise to distinction.
M1.zh: 他觉得自己无法出人头地。

D4.en: At 3-4, he kept flower beds that he had to sow, water, and care for.
D4.zh: 3到4岁时，他打理花坛，必须播种、浇水、照料。

D9.en: At 15-16, he explored Gothic church architecture and monuments with friends.
D9.zh: 15到16岁时，他和朋友探索了上城哥特式教堂的建筑和纪念碑。

D7.en: At 16-17, he decided to take up medicine without inclination, as it was closest to his interests, though he had no real desire.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他决定学医，虽无兴趣，但这是最接近他兴趣的学科，不过他并不真正想学医。

D2.en: He started learning piano early and enjoyed Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven, and Bach more as he grew.
D2.zh: 他从小开始学钢琴，随着年龄增长越来越喜欢海顿、莫扎特、贝多芬和巴赫。

D3.en: At 9-10, he said he wanted to prepare duck, goose, and pigeon heads to compare them.
D3.zh: 9到10岁时，他说他想准备鸭头、鹅头和鸽子头来比较它们。

D17.en: At 5, she played cards with her brother all summer and wouldn't let anyone scold him.
D17.zh: 5岁时，她整个夏天陪弟弟打牌，不许任何人说他一句重话。

D10.en: At 7, she watched the funeral procession from the window and felt proud so many followed her grandpa.
D10.zh: 7岁时，她从窗户看着送葬队伍，为有这么多人送外公而自豪。

D14.en: At 4, she sang a lot with real passion, but completely off-key.
D14.zh: 4岁时，她唱歌很多，充满激情，但完全跑调。

D5.en: At 14, she left home through her bedroom window and took the streetcar to Stolp alone.
D5.zh: 14岁时，她从卧室窗户离家，独自乘电车去Stolp。

D11.en: At 18, she wrote and sent Heidegger a self-portrait called 'The Shadows'.
D11.zh: 18岁时，她写了一篇自画像《影子》并寄给海德格尔。

M1.en: At 17, she passed the exam in spring 1924, a year ahead of her class.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她在1924年春天通过了考试，比同班同学早一年。

D15.en: As a small child, Hannah Arendt had childhood illnesses.
D15.zh: 幼年时，Hannah Arendt 患有儿童疾病。

D4.en: At 14, she threw a pebble against Anne's window to wake her up.
D4.zh: 14岁时，她向Anne的窗户扔石子把她叫醒。

D9.en: At 15, she led her classmates in a boycott of the teacher's classes.
D9.zh: 15岁时，她带领同学抵制老师的课。

D7.en: At 18, she defiantly announced her right to indifference toward such judgment and argument.
D7.zh: 18岁时，她挑衅地宣称自己有权“对这样的判断和争论漠不关心”。

D6.en: At 14, she started a long and close friendship with Anne Mendelssohn.
D6.zh: 14岁时，她与Anne Mendelssohn开始了长久而亲密的友谊。

D2.en: At 17-18, she prepared for the Abitur with an intense six months of all-day study.
D2.zh: 17到18岁间，她通过六个月的全天高强度学习准备高中毕业考试。

D3.en: At 16, she decided to make theology her major field of study at university.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她决定上大学后主修神学。

D12.en: As a small child, she was extremely lively, moved by sad stories, and loved everything cheerful.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她非常活泼，被悲伤的故事打动，喜欢一切快乐的事物。

D13b.en: As a small child, she was sent to synagogue with her Arendt grandparents.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她被送去和Arendt祖父母一起上犹太会堂。

D17.en: At age 9-10, he gave a cigarette tin full of rice to beggars, with his grandmother's permission.
D17.zh: 9到10岁时，他在祖母允许下给乞丐一烟罐米。

M4.en: At age 10, the Bengal famine inspired his determination to prevent famines.
M4.zh: 10岁时，孟加拉饥荒激发了他防止饥荒的决心。

D16.en: At 18, he went to Baharampur for an all-Bengal debating competition and had some success.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他去巴哈拉姆普尔参加全孟加拉辩论赛并取得一些成功。

D10.en: At 18, he felt terribly dejected but also triumphant.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他感到极度沮丧但也得意洋洋。

D14.en: As a small child, his drawings showed extraordinary artistic talent.
D14.zh: 幼年时，他的画作显示出非凡的艺术天赋。

D13a.en: At age 9-10, he refused to retreat from his new school because he loved it.
D13a.zh: 9到10岁时，他拒绝离开新学校，因为他喜欢它。

D11.en: He contributed to school magazines between ages 8 and 14.
D11.zh: 他8到14岁时为校刊投稿。

M1.en: At 18, he applied only to Trinity College, so certain was his choice.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他仅申请了三一学院，对自己的选择如此确定。

D9.en: At 17-18, he registered to study economics with mathematics at Presidency College.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他在总统学院注册学习经济学和数学。

D2.en: At 16-18, he studied 'Intermediate Science' at Santiniketan.
D2.zh: 16到18岁时，他在Santiniketan学习‘中级科学’。

D3.en: At 18, he used the University Library to look up books on cancer to quieten his mind.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他利用大学图书馆查阅关于癌症的书籍以平复心情。

D17.en: He asked his parents for a few coppers to give to the poor.
D17.zh: 他向父母要几个铜板施舍给穷人。

M4.en: At 16-17, he converted to communism.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，他皈依了共产主义。

D10.en: At 13, psychologically depressed, he fell ill with rheumatic fever.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他心理抑郁，患上了风湿热。

D1.en: At 16-17, he grew unhappy with the communists and broke with the party after the jail attack.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他对共产主义者感到不满，监狱袭击后与党决裂。

D18.en: At 16-17, he worked as an office boy at communist headquarters.
D18.zh: 16到17岁时，他在共产主义总部当勤杂工。

D5.en: At 16-17, he was with a crowd that attacked a police station; police fired, killing 12.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，他跟着人群袭击了警察局，警察开枪打死12人。

D4.en: At 16, he went around Vienna in an old military uniform, eager to join the revolution.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他穿着旧军装在维也纳四处走动，渴望参与革命。

D9.en: At 16, he and his friends, in military uniforms, were in charge of an emergency evacuation during floods.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他和朋友们穿着军装，负责洪水期间的紧急疏散。

D7.en: At 15-16, he challenged a teacher who vowed eternal hatred to the Allies.
D7.zh: 15到16岁时，他挑战了一位发誓永远仇恨协约国的老师。

D6.en: As a teenager, he was a Marxist, then became an enthusiastic Social Democrat.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他先是马克思主义者，后来成为热心的社会民主党人。

D2.en: At 17-18, he earned income by guiding American students around the university and city.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他通过带领美国学生参观大学和城市赚取收入。

D12.en: At 17-18, he left home to live with an eccentric group of political outcasts and wayward students.
D12.zh: 17到18岁时，他离家与一群古怪的政治流亡者和任性学生同住。

D13a.en: At 15-16, he rebelled against his parents and left school.
D13a.zh: 15到16岁时，他反抗父母并离开了学校。

D1.en: He learned with some joy that he could overcome his stammer when speaking in rhyme.
D1.zh: 他有些高兴地发现，自己在用韵文说话时能克服口吃。

D4.en: He owned an experimental chemistry set and produced smells and explosions, preferably after Sunday school.
D4.zh: 他有一套化学实验器材，并在教父提供的额外化学品帮助下，制造各种气味和爆炸，最好是在主日学之后。

D9.en: He was a success at Kent: high marks, senior prefect, football and wrestling teams, advertising manager on the yearbook board.
D9.zh: 他在Kent很成功：高分、学长、足球队和摔跤队、年鉴广告经理。

D6.en: At age 6-12, he noticed racial segregation and visited a black friend's home despite his mother's disapproval.
D6.zh: 6到12岁时，他注意到种族隔离，并不顾母亲反对去黑人朋友家玩。

D3.en: At 18, moved by the imminent war, he studied World War I in the university library.
D3.zh: 18岁时，受迫近的战争触动，他在大学图书馆研究一战。

D12.en: At 8, he recovered from diphtheria, then caught pneumonia while his brother died.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他得了白喉后康复，接着又得了肺炎，而他的弟弟去世了。

D15.en: He was accepted onto the freshmen football team.
D15.zh: 他被新生足球队录取了。

D17.en: At 15, he dearly loved his youngest siblings and wrote his mother letters about them.
D17.zh: 15岁时，他深爱年幼的弟妹，写信给母亲讲述他们的活动。

M4.en: At 6, the outbreak of the War of 1870 made a lasting impression on him.
M4.zh: 6岁时，1870年战争的爆发给他留下了深刻印象。

D16.en: At 18, he used occasional invitations to professors' homes as material for his gift of mimicking people and events into anecdotes.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他利用偶尔受邀去教授家的机会，用他模仿人物和事件的天赋将其编成趣闻。

D10.en: As a small child, he suffered nervous anxieties as an aftereffect of disease.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他因疾病后遗症而遭受神经焦虑。

D13b.en: At 18, he passed his Abitur and went to study in Heidelberg, then switched to Berlin.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他通过高中毕业考试，去海德堡学习，后转到柏林。

D13a.en: At 14-15, he endured it with apparent indifference.
D13a.zh: 14到15岁时，他忍受了这一切，表面漠不关心。

D5.en: At 18, he helped his friends cheat their way through.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他帮助朋友作弊过关。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote to his mother from Erfurt to give her news.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他从埃尔福特写信给母亲，告诉她一些消息。

D4.en: As a small child, playing with trains occupied him for a long time.
D4.zh: 幼年时，玩火车玩具占据了他很长时间。

D7.en: As a child and young adult, he was inclined by temperament to be aggressively self-opinionated, whether in relation to his father.
D7.zh: 幼年到青年时期，他天性倾向于咄咄逼人地固执己见，即使是对父亲也是如此。

D3.en: From childhood to age 14, he knew inside out the whole list of liberal complaints about Bismarck.
D3.zh: 从幼年到14岁，他对自由派对俾斯麦的所有抱怨了如指掌。

D12.en: At age 13, his mother fell into depression after his sister Helene died.
D12.zh: 13岁时，妹妹Helene去世，母亲陷入抑郁。

D1.en: He failed to enter the École and entered Lycée Louis-le-Grand for preparatory studies.
D1.zh: 他未能考入巴黎高师，进入路易大帝中学继续预科学习。

D13a.en: He decided as a schoolboy not to follow the family tradition.
D13a.zh: 他还在上学时就决定不遵循家族传统。

D2.en: He was an outstanding pupil at the Collège d'Épinal, skipping two classes and easily earning his baccalauréats.
D2.zh: 他在埃皮纳勒中学是杰出学生，跳了两级并轻松获得业士学位。

D3.en: He experienced a brief crisis of mysticism under the influence of an old Catholic school-mistress.
D3.zh: 他在一位老派天主教女教师的影响下经历了一次短暂的神秘主义危机。

D12.en: He grew up in a close-knit, orthodox Jewish family.
D12.zh: 他在一个紧密、正统的犹太家庭中长大。

M4.en: At 12-13, he moved more towards anarchism and away from Marxism.
M4.zh: 12到13岁时，他越来越倾向于无政府主义，远离马克思主义。

D5.en: At 13, he began traveling alone by train to New York City, visiting relatives and bookstores.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他开始独自乘火车去纽约市，探访亲戚并逛二手书店。

D7.en: At 16-17, he did not celebrate the end of WWII and could not identify with the jubilant reactions.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他没有庆祝二战结束，也无法认同周围人的欢庆反应。

D6.en: As a teenager, he belonged to several clubs and was well liked by his peers.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他参加了几个俱乐部，很受同龄人喜欢。

D3.en: At 8-12, he was reading anarchist material by Diego Abad de Santillán.
D3.zh: 8到12岁时，他在阅读Diego Abad de Santillán的无政府主义材料。

D11.en: At 10, he published his first article, an editorial on the fall of Barcelona.
D11.zh: 10岁时，他发表了第一篇文章，一篇关于巴塞罗那陷落的社论。

M8.en: At 11, she raced with her atheist parents to the baptism she insisted on.
M8.zh: 11岁时，她拉着无神论父母赶去参加她坚持要做的洗礼。

M7.en: At 16, she danced for joy in an uncharacteristic outburst.
M7.zh: 16岁时，她一反常态地跳起舞来，表达喜悦。

M6.en: At 10, she wrote: 'Today has been one of the happiest days of my life. I was Baptized this afternoon.'
M6.zh: 10岁时，她写道：'今天是我一生中最快乐的日子之一。我今天下午受洗了。'

D14.en: At 11, she posed as a model for her painting teacher in exchange for free art lessons.
D14.zh: 11岁时，她为绘画老师当模特以换取免费美术课。

D5.en: At 16, she was stricken with acute appendicitis.
D5.zh: 16岁时，她患上急性阑尾炎。

D11.en: At 13-14, her imagination was fed by the school stories she enjoyed.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，她喜欢的校园故事滋养了她的想象力。

M1.en: Between ages 11-14, she and Margery secured scholarships to St. Margaret's School.
M1.zh: 11到14岁间，她和Margery获得奖学金进入圣玛格丽特学校。

D15.en: By age 11, she had developed breasts and begun to menstruate.
D15.zh: 11岁时，她乳房发育并开始月经。

D9.en: As a teenager, she organized her schoolmates and was a natural leader.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，她组织同学，是个天生的领导者。

D6.en: As a teenager, she fell sentimentally in love with older, good-looking girls.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，她情感上爱上了年长、漂亮的女孩。

D3.en: As a small child, she devoured everything she could read.
D3.zh: 幼年时，她如饥似渴地阅读一切能拿到手的东西。

D7.en: At 17, she prevailed upon Luther to train for the Episcopalian priesthood.
D7.zh: 17岁时，她说服Luther去接受圣公会牧师培训。

D10.en: At 15, he got up, dropped his bicycle, and ran into the house.
D10.zh: 15岁时，他起身丢下自行车跑进屋里。

D13a.en: At 17, his father arranged for him to repeat his senior year elsewhere.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他父亲安排他去别处重读高三。

D18.en: At 18, he enrolled in the Ontario Agricultural College.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他进入安大略农学院。

D7.en: He found the atmosphere under a tyrannical principal repellent.
D7.zh: 他觉得在专横的校长管理下的氛围令人反感。

D6.en: The two-and-a-half-hour train ride with two other local boys marked the start of a fresh life.
D6.zh: 他和另外两个当地男孩一起坐了两个半小时的火车，这标志着他新生活的开始。

D12.en: He was born into the hamlet of Iona Station, Ontario, Canada.
D12.zh: 他出生在加拿大安大略省的一个小村庄Iona Station。

D20X.en: At 16, she decided to spend 30-60 minutes daily watching technique videos after training.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，她决定每天训练后花30到60分钟看技术录像。

M5.en: At 17, she worried about letting down the Chinese team.
M5.zh: 17岁时，她担心会不会丢中国队的脸。

M8.en: At 17, she told herself the trees would have sprouted by the time she returned.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她对自己说：回来时这些树就该发芽了。

D19.en: At 16, she made detailed plans for the 1986 World Championships, including warm-up and approach timing.
D19.zh: 16岁时，她为1986年世锦赛准备了详细计划：赛前准备活动、模仿时机、走向跳板等。

M3.en: At 14, she first saw American diver Louganis on TV and he became her idol.
M3.zh: 14岁时，她第一次从电视上看到美国运动员洛加尼斯，他成了她的偶像。

M7.en: At 17, she was told not to let down the Chinese team.
M7.zh: 17岁时，她被要求不要丢中国队的脸。

M6.en: At 14, she resolved to compete in the Olympics, with targets to beat along the way.
M6.zh: 14岁时，她决心一定要参加奥运会，路上有要赢的目标和人。

M4.en: She resolved to overcome all obstacles to become a champion by focusing all her strength on every detail.
M4.zh: 她决心克服所有阻碍冠军的因素，在每一个细节上集中自己所有力量。

D16.en: At 15-16, she was scolded by Coach Xu for drinking 'butter swim' and never dared again.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，她被徐教练痛骂喝“黄油游泳”，从此再也没敢喝。

D10.en: She bit her lip and tried to hold back tears while looking away from her father.
D10.zh: 她把目光从爸爸脸上转开，咬着牙拼命睁大眼睛咽下泪水。

D1.en: She climbed the 10m platform, terrified, and told herself to jump quickly.
D1.zh: 她重新爬上10米跳台，特别害怕，对自己说快跳。

D14.en: She won her international 3m springboard championship with a high score of 540.
D14.zh: 她以540分的高分赢得女子3米板国际比赛冠军。

D13a.en: At 15, Coach Liu ordered me off the team, but I survived this bloody lesson and obstacle.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，刘指导让我退队，但我挺过了这次血的教训和障碍。

D18.en: At age 4, she started walking to kindergarten by herself every day.
D18.zh: 4岁时，她开始每天自己上幼儿园。

D5.en: A loud bang exploded in the air just after she took off for a dive.
D5.zh: 她起跳后一刹那，空中突然炸开一声巨响。

D11.en: She decided to treat training as competition, imagining scenarios to pressure herself.
D11.zh: 她决定把训练场变成赛场，假设情境给自己施加压力。

M1.en: At 16, she said there is only one champion, and you have to fight to get to that sole position.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她说冠军只有一个，要想杀到那个唯一的位置。

D15.en: At 9, she trained by running, stretching joints, and building strength.
D15.zh: 9岁时，她跑步、压关节、练力量。

D7.en: At 15, she nodded and climbed back onto the 10-meter platform, very scared, legs trembling.
D7.zh: 15岁时，她点点头又爬上了10米台，但非常害怕，两条腿不停地发抖。

D6.en: At 9, she did school homework with swimming and diving teammates.
D6.zh: 9岁时，她和游泳队、跳水队的队员一起做学校布置的作业。

D3.en: At 16-17, she analyzed how to dive based on energy and weight changes.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，她像找到猎物一样，研究体力好时怎么跳、体力不好时怎么跳，体重增减时又怎么跳。

D4.en: At 16, she did something unprecedented: she asked a water polo player for a water polo cap.
D4.zh: 16岁时，她干了一件前无古人的事：跟水球队的队员要了一顶水球帽。

D15.en: At 14-15, she walked 50 km over mountains alone to visit her grandfather in the mountains of Liaoyang.
D15.zh: 14到15岁时，她一人步行五十公里翻山越岭回辽阳大山里去看望祖父。

D2.en: From age 6 to 12, she wrapped a small bundle with textbooks and flatbread around her waist and walked a long way to school daily.
D2.zh: 6到12岁时，她每天在腰里缠一个长形小包袱，里头是课本和饼子，走很远的路去学校。

D3.en: From age 6 to 14, she relied entirely on herbal medicine and believed in it since childhood.
D3.zh: 6到14岁时，她全靠草药，从小就信这个。

D12.en: As a small child, her family of over ten people lived alone on a mountain ridge, a nearly primitive self-sufficient life.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她全家老少十几口人独门独户住在大山梁子上，过着近乎原始的、自给自足的山林生活。

D20X.en: He and Zhang Wuchang parted ways because Zhang entered high school.
D20X.zh: 他和张五常因张升高中而分手。

M5.en: At 15, he was observed as thin, with slightly bowed legs and a stylish haircut, eyes sparkling.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他被人打量，身体瘦削，罗圈腿，花旗装小分头，眼神闪闪。

M8.en: At 17-18, he was deeply absorbed in music, as if possessed by its spirit.
M8.zh: 17到18岁时，他听音乐听得入迷，好像被乐魂附了身。

M7.en: At 16-17, he often led teams to compete in East and West Ring areas, winning every match.
M7.zh: 16到17岁间，他常领队出征东环、西环比赛，每战必胜。

M6.en: At 16-17, he dreamed of becoming a Hong Kong star player.
M6.zh: 16到17岁间，他梦想自己将来会成为香港球星。

D17.en: At 16-17, he helped market vendors move goods, clean, and run errands.
D17.zh: 16到17岁间，他帮市场摊档搬菜、搬猪肉、拣鱼、挑水、打扫垃圾、跑腿打杂。

M2.en: At 16-17, he gave money to his mother and helped with household chores.
M2.zh: 16到17岁间，他把钱交到母亲手里，又帮家里挑水煮饭。

D16.en: At 17-18, he could hum Beethoven's Fifth Symphony after hearing it once.
D16.zh: 17到18岁间，他只听一遍贝多芬第五交响曲就能悠悠哼出来。

D10.en: At 15-16, he was angry and wanted to talk back but held back.
D10.zh: 15到16岁时，他很生气，想顶撞但又忍住了。

D13b.en: At 10, he had to transfer to a lower-quality school.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他只好转到一所师资较差的同济小学就读。

D13a.en: At 18, he decided not to bow for a meager salary, resigned with his father, and retrieved his luggage.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他决心不为五斗米折腰，跟随父亲去东买提出辞职并取回行李。

D5.en: At 18, he gritted his teeth and decided to risk it all, fighting with all his might.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他咬紧牙关，决心豁出去，拼了。

D11.en: At 16-17, he sat with legs crossed, hands behind head, gazing at stars and moon, full of beautiful fantasies.
D11.zh: 16到17岁时，他跷起二郎腿，双手后枕，仰望着天上的星星和月亮，充满许多美丽的幻想。

D4.en: At about 6, he set up a makeshift table tennis table with a bed board and bamboo pole, playing with his father.
D4.zh: 大约6岁时，他搬出床板架在长凳上当球桌，用竹竿做网，和父亲对打乒乓球。

D9.en: He was tasked to select players and recruited four people for the team.
D9.zh: 他被安排物色人选，找了四个人组队。

D6.en: At 18, he met Yu Junming at a table tennis match and became close friends, through whom he met progressive workers.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他在一次乒乓球赛中认识了余骏明，两人过从甚密，并通过他结交了进步工人。

D2.en: At age 6-8, he practiced table tennis during breaks and after school, rushing back to school to practice even after meals.
D2.zh: 6到8岁时，他下课打球，放学后扒几口饭就插着球拍回学校练球。

D3.en: At 4-5, he explored a new environment, flipping through sports magazines and watching adults play chess and table tennis.
D3.zh: 4到5岁时，他来到新地方，翻阅体育画报，看大人下棋和打乒乓球。

D7.en: At age 4, he defiantly stuck out his tongue and taunted, 'Yacha, yacha, nose-pick with salty shrimp.'
D7.zh: 4岁时，他攥着拳头吐舌头，不服气地讥讽道：“亚差、亚差，鼻屎捞咸虾。”

M4.en: At 18, he angrily refused to write a confession, shouting 'Patriotism is not a crime.'
M4.zh: 18岁时，他愤怒拒绝写悔改书，大喊‘爱国无罪’。

D14.en: At 18, his performances drew full houses, and clubs competed to hire him as a coach.
D14.zh: 18岁时，凡是他出场表演，几乎全场满座，多家俱乐部争相请他当教练。

D1.en: At 15-16, he failed and then passed the entrance exam.
D1.zh: 15到16岁时，他先没考上入学考试，后来又考上了。

D13a.en: His mother enrolled him in a religious secondary school.
D13a.zh: 他母亲把他送进了一所宗教中学。

D5.en: He slashed his chest with a razor and lay on the ground.
D5.zh: 他用剃刀割伤自己的胸口，躺在地上。

D11.en: He passionately devoured Bainville's history of France and was fascinated by Charlemagne.
D11.zh: 他如饥似渴地读了巴恩维尔的法国史，对查理曼大帝特别着迷。

M1.en: At 17, he passed his baccalauréat with better than average results.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他以高于平均水平的成绩通过了中学毕业会考。

D15.en: He was a good cyclist and regularly rode to visit his grandmother as a teenager.
D15.zh: 他骑车骑得很好，十几岁时经常骑车去探望祖母。

D9.en: At age 12, he gave history lessons to his brother and sister.
D9.zh: 12岁时，他给弟弟妹妹上历史课。

D7.en: At 17, he had difficult, even violent discussions with his father.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他和父亲的讨论很困难，甚至很激烈。

D6.en: As a child aged 7-12, he took his first communion and was a choirboy.
D6.zh: 7到12岁时，他第一次领圣体，并当过一段时间的唱诗班男孩。

D2.en: As a small child aged 4-6, he had homework in reading and spelling exercises.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他就有阅读和拼写练习的作业。

D3.en: At 16, he bicycled to Ligugé and talked about Plato, Descartes, Pascal, Bergson.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他骑自行车去Ligugé，谈论柏拉图、笛卡尔、帕斯卡、柏格森。

M3.en: At 16, she met Jackie Robinson and felt thrilled.
M3.zh: 16岁时，她见到了Jackie Robinson，感到非常激动。

M7.en: At 15, her mother told her to be the best and never give up.
M7.zh: 15岁时，她母亲对她说：你要做到最好，永不放弃。

D16.en: At 16, she said making a speech scared her more than running in the Olympics.
D16.zh: 16岁时，她说演讲比参加奥运会跑步更让她害怕。

D10.en: At 15-16, she would often quit the team but return the next day.
D10.zh: 15到16岁时，她经常退出队伍，但第二天又回来训练。

D1.en: At 16, she thought: four years from now, I'll bring home gold for America.
D1.zh: 16岁时，她想：四年后，我要为美国拿回金牌。

D14.en: At 11, she took off her brace, walked into church, and sat in the front pew.
D14.zh: 11岁时，她取下支架，走进教堂，坐在前排长椅上。

D13a.en: As a child, her parents told her to accept racial inequities and hold her tongue.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，父母告诉她不要在意种族不平等，要管住嘴。

D18.en: At 6, she and her siblings watched white people enter the county fair.
D18.zh: 6岁时，她和兄弟姐妹看着白人进入县集市。

D15.en: At 11, with family support, she walked without a brace.
D15.zh: 11岁时，在家人的支持下，她不用支架就能走路了。

D4.en: At 12, she started playing basketball in the yard with her brothers.
D4.zh: 12岁时，她开始在院子里和兄弟们打篮球。

D9.en: As a teenager, she was first a basketball star, then track became her home.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，她先是篮球明星，然后田径成了她的家。

D6.en: At 15-16, she and Robert liked each other and were getting closer.
D6.zh: 15到16岁时，她和Robert互相喜欢，关系更近了。

D2.en: At 14-18, she was coached by Marion Armstrong-Perkins at David T. Howard High School.
D2.zh: 14到18岁时，她在David T. Howard高中接受Marion Armstrong-Perkins的指导。

D3.en: At 16, she learned in earth science that winter isn't everywhere at the same time.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她在地球科学课上学到冬天不是同时在全球出现。

D7.en: At 9, she took off her brace and walked into church, surprising parishioners.
D7.zh: 9岁时，她取下支架走进教堂，让教友们惊讶。

M3.en: As a child, he idolized Peter Gade, the 'Golden Boy' of Denmark.
M3.zh: 他年少时的偶像是丹麦金童皮特·盖德。

M2.en: He set becoming a world champion in badminton as his ultimate goal from childhood.
M2.zh: 他从小就把成为羽毛球世界冠军作为终极目标。

D1.en: From August 15, 2004 to August 17, 2008, he endured four years of hardship that ended in a perfect day.
D1.zh: 从2004年8月15日到2008年8月17日，他经历了充满磨难的四年，最终换来了完美的一天。

D5.en: At 16, he made his debut as a professional badminton player.
D5.zh: 他16岁出道，成为职业羽毛球运动员。
